name: ab-to-adduction slide straight arms
description: The ab-to-adduction slide straight arms is a bodyweight exercise that combines core engagement with hip adductor activation. Performed on sliders, it challenges the abs and inner thighs through a controlled sliding motion with straight arms.
instructions: Begin in a plank position with your hands on sliders or a smooth surface, arms fully extended.
Slide one leg out to the side while simultaneously sliding your arms forward, extending your body.
Pause at the extended position, engaging your core and inner thighs.
Return to the starting plank position by pulling your arms and leg back in.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: alternate leg curl
description: The alternate leg curl is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings, performed lying face down. It involves curling one leg at a time toward the glutes, engaging the hamstrings and glutes while promoting unilateral leg strength.
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your legs extended straight behind you.
Keeping your hips pressed to the ground, curl one leg upward by bending the knee toward your glutes.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstring.
Slowly lower your leg back to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite leg, alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1244 - alternate leg curl.mp4

name: back row smith machine
description: The back row smith machine is a compound pulling exercise performed on a Smith machine that targets the lats, rhomboids, and traps. It involves rowing the body upward toward a fixed bar, building upper back strength and muscle thickness.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to approximately hip height and stand facing it.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet forward and lean back, allowing your body to hang at an angle with arms fully extended.
Pull your chest up toward the bar by driving your elbows back, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/back/lats/strength/beginner/1245 - back row smith machine.mp4

name: band lunges
description: Band lunges are a resistance band exercise targeting the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings. The band adds accommodating resistance throughout the lunge movement, increasing difficulty at the top and improving lower body strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with both feet on the center of a resistance band, holding the ends at shoulder height.
Step one foot forward into a lunge position, keeping your torso upright.
Lower your back knee toward the ground until both knees are at approximately 90 degrees.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite leg and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded back row
description: The banded back row is a resistance band exercise that targets the lats and upper back. By pulling the band toward the torso, it effectively engages the rhomboids, traps, and biceps, making it an accessible alternative to cable or machine rows.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at chest height or sit on the floor with the band looped around your feet.
Grasp both ends of the band with an overhand grip, arms fully extended in front of you.
Sit or stand tall with a slight forward lean and a neutral spine.
Pull the band toward your torso by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at full contraction, then slowly release back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded bench kickback
description: The banded bench kickback is a resistance band exercise targeting the glutes and hamstrings. Performed with hands on a bench for support, the band adds resistance to the kickback motion, intensifying glute activation and promoting hip extension strength.
instructions: Loop a resistance band just above your knees and kneel with your hands on a bench for support.
Engage your core and keep your spine neutral throughout the movement.
Kick one leg back and upward, extending it fully while squeezing your glute at the top.
Slowly return your leg to the starting position without touching the knee to the ground.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching to the other.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1248 - banded bench kickback.mp4

name: banded biceps curl
description: The banded biceps curl is a resistance band exercise targeting the biceps. Standing on the band and curling upward mimics the motion of a dumbbell curl while providing accommodating resistance throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Stand with both feet on the center of a resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold one end of the band in each hand with a supinated grip, arms hanging at your sides.
Keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso, curl your hands up toward your shoulders.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your hands back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1249 - banded biceps curl.mp4

name: banded biceps to shoulder raise
description: The banded biceps to shoulder raise is a resistance band exercise that combines a biceps curl with an overhead shoulder raise. It targets the deltoids and biceps simultaneously, improving shoulder mobility and upper arm strength in one fluid movement.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band, holding one end in each hand with a supinated grip.
Begin by performing a biceps curl, bringing your hands up toward your shoulders.
From the top of the curl, rotate your palms forward and press your arms overhead into a shoulder raise.
Slowly reverse the movement, lowering your arms back through the curl and returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1250 - banded biceps to shoulder raise.mp4

name: banded curls to high rows
description: Banded curls to high rows is a compound resistance band exercise that combines a biceps curl with an upright high row. It targets the lats, biceps, and upper traps, making it an efficient upper body pulling movement.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at a low point and stand facing the anchor, holding one end in each hand.
Begin by performing a biceps curl, bringing your hands toward your shoulders.
At the top of the curl, flare your elbows out and pull the band upward toward your chin in a high row.
Slowly reverse the movement, lowering back through the curl and returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/back/lats/strength/intermediate/1251 - banded curls to high rows.mp4

name: banded front squat
description: The banded front squat is a resistance band exercise targeting the quadriceps and glutes. Holding the band at shoulder height mimics the front rack position of a barbell, encouraging an upright torso and deep squat mechanics.
instructions: Stand with both feet shoulder-width apart on the center of a resistance band.
Hold the ends of the band at shoulder height with your elbows raised forward.
Keeping your chest tall and core engaged, lower your hips into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1252 - banded front squat.mp4

name: banded glute kick
description: The banded glute kick is a resistance band exercise performed on all fours, targeting the glutes and hamstrings. The band adds resistance to the kickback motion, intensifying glute activation and supporting hip extension development.
instructions: Loop a resistance band around your ankles and get on all fours on a mat.
Keep your core engaged and spine neutral throughout the movement.
Kick one leg straight back, extending it fully until it is parallel to the ground.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1253 - banded glute kick.mp4

name: banded hack squat
description: The banded hack squat is a resistance band exercise that targets the quadriceps with an emphasis on the lower quads. The band provides resistance through the full range of motion, simulating the mechanics of a hack squat machine.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart with a resistance band looped under your heels and held at your sides.
Position your heels slightly elevated if needed to mimic the hack squat angle.
Keeping your torso upright and core tight, lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position, extending your hips and knees fully.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1254 - banded hack squat.mp4

name: banded lat pulldown
description: The banded lat pulldown is a resistance band exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi. Anchoring the band overhead and pulling it downward mimics the cable lat pulldown, effectively engaging the lats, biceps, and rhomboids.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band overhead at a fixed point and kneel or sit below the anchor.
Grasp the band with both hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
With arms extended overhead, pull the band down toward your chest by driving your elbows toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly allow your arms to extend back overhead and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded one arm row
description: The banded one arm row is a unilateral resistance band exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Performing one arm at a time allows for greater focus on muscle activation and helps address any strength imbalances between sides.
instructions: Anchor one end of a resistance band at a low point and stand to the side facing the anchor.
Hold the other end of the band in one hand with your arm extended toward the anchor.
Brace your core and hinge slightly at the hips with a flat back.
Pull the band toward your hip by driving your elbow back, squeezing your lat at the top.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching arms.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/back/lats/strength/beginner/1256 - banded one arm row.mp4

name: banded squat
description: The banded squat is a resistance band exercise that targets the quads and glutes while activating the hip abductors. The band placed above the knees encourages proper knee tracking and adds an abductor challenge to the standard squat movement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and a resistance band looped just above your knees.
Hold your hands in front of your chest for balance and push your knees out against the band.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, maintaining outward knee pressure against the band throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded straight leg kickback
description: The banded straight leg kickback is a resistance band exercise that isolates the glutes through hip hyperextension. Keeping the leg straight throughout the movement increases the hamstring and glute demand, making it effective for posterior chain development.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at ankle height and loop the other end around one ankle.
Stand facing the anchor point with hands resting lightly on a stable surface for balance.
Keeping your leg straight and core engaged, kick the banded leg back as far as comfortable.
Squeeze your glute at the peak of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1258 - banded straight leg kickback.mp4

name: barbell deadlift
description: The barbell deadlift is a fundamental compound strength exercise targeting the glutes, hamstrings, and back. It involves lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing position, engaging nearly every major muscle group and building total-body strength.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart with a barbell resting over the middle of your feet.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, keep your chest tall, and ensure your back is flat before initiating the lift.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift the bar.
Stand fully upright at the top, then hinge at the hips to lower the bar back to the floor with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell glute bridge
description: The barbell glute bridge is a compound lower body exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings. Loading a barbell across the hips while lying on the floor and pressing upward maximizes glute activation through hip extension.
instructions: Sit on the floor with a barbell resting across your hips and your upper back against a bench.
Plant your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart, with knees bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Brace your core and drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1260 - barbell glute bridge.mp4

name: barbell hip thrust
description: The barbell hip thrust is a primary glute-building exercise performed with the upper back braced against a bench. It involves thrusting the hips upward against a loaded barbell, producing powerful glute contraction throughout the movement.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and a barbell resting across your hips.
Plant your feet flat on the ground shoulder-width apart, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Brace your core and drive through your heels to thrust your hips upward toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes at the top until your torso is parallel to the ground.
Slowly lower your hips back toward the floor without touching and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1261 - barbell hip thrust.mp4

name: barbell kickback
description: The barbell kickback is a glute isolation exercise performed using a landmine or anchored barbell. Loading resistance onto the foot during a kickback motion increases glute and hamstring activation compared to bodyweight alternatives.
instructions: Load a barbell in a landmine attachment or anchor it at one end against a stable surface.
Kneel on all fours and place one foot in the collar or sleeve of the barbell.
Engage your core and keep your spine neutral.
Drive your foot back and upward, extending your hip fully and squeezing your glute at the top.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.

name: barbell lunge to press
description: The barbell lunge to press is a compound exercise combining a lower body lunge with an overhead press. It simultaneously targets the quads, glutes, and shoulders, making it an efficient total-body movement for strength and coordination.
instructions: Stand with a barbell resting on your upper back in the squat rack position, feet hip-width apart.
Step forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground.
As you rise from the lunge, press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the barbell back to your shoulders as you step back to the starting position.
Alternate legs with each repetition and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/1263 - barbell lunge to press.mp4

name: barbell lunges
description: Barbell lunges are a compound lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings. Performing alternating forward lunges with a barbell increases the load demands on the lower body and improves unilateral leg strength and balance.
instructions: Stand with a barbell resting across your upper back and your feet hip-width apart.
Take a large step forward with one foot, planting it firmly on the ground.
Lower your back knee toward the ground until both knees are bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Alternate legs with each repetition and continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell static lunges
description: Barbell static lunges are a stationary lunge variation performed with a barbell across the back. Fixing the feet in a split stance isolates each leg independently, increasing focus on quad and glute strength without the balance demand of alternating lunges.
instructions: Stand with a barbell resting across your upper back and step one foot forward into a split stance.
Keep your torso upright and your core braced throughout the movement.
Lower your back knee straight down toward the ground without touching it.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all desired repetitions on one leg before switching to the other.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1265 - barbell static lunges.mp4

name: barbell stiff deadlift
description: The barbell stiff deadlift, also known as the Romanian deadlift, is a hip hinge exercise that targets the hamstrings and glutes. Keeping the legs relatively straight throughout the movement maximizes the stretch and load on the hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell at hip level with an overhand grip.
Keeping your legs nearly straight with a slight bend in the knees, hinge at the hips and lower the barbell toward the ground.
Maintain a flat back and feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings at the bottom.
Drive your hips forward to return to the standing position, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench abs crunch
description: The bench abs crunch is a bodyweight exercise performed at the edge of a bench, targeting the abdominal muscles. By combining a knee tuck with a torso crunch, it effectively engages the full rectus abdominis and hip flexors.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the sides for support.
Extend your legs out in front of you and lean back slightly to engage your core.
Bring your knees toward your chest while simultaneously crunching your torso forward.
Pause at the top, squeezing your abs.
Extend your legs back out and return to the starting position, repeating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench barbell squat
description: The bench barbell squat is a squat variation that uses a bench as a depth guide. It targets the quads and glutes while teaching proper squat depth and mechanics, making it useful for beginners and experienced lifters alike.
instructions: Set a bench behind you and position a barbell across your upper back.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out.
Lower yourself into a squat until you lightly touch the bench with your glutes, keeping your chest tall.
Pause briefly at the bench without fully resting your weight on it.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1268 - bench barbell squat.mp4

name: bench jump
description: The bench jump is a plyometric exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. It involves explosively jumping onto a bench or box, building lower body power, coordination, and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy bench or plyo box with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and swing your arms back to prepare for the jump.
Explosively jump forward and upward, landing softly on both feet on top of the bench.
Stand tall on the bench, then step back down one foot at a time.
Reset and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: biceps isolation curl
description: The biceps isolation curl is a dumbbell exercise that removes the contribution of the shoulder and core, placing full tension on the biceps. By bracing the upper arm against a surface, it effectively targets the biceps brachii for maximum isolation.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench or rest your upper arm on the inside of your thigh to isolate the bicep.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a supinated grip, arm fully extended.
Curl the dumbbell upward toward your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your bicep at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1270 - biceps isolation curl.mp4

name: bicycle
description: The bicycle is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs and obliques simultaneously. The alternating elbow-to-knee pedaling motion creates rotational tension across the entire core, making it one of the most effective abdominal exercises.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed behind your head and your knees bent.
Lift your shoulders off the ground and raise your legs to a 90-degree angle.
Bring your right elbow toward your left knee while simultaneously extending your right leg.
Switch sides in a controlled pedaling motion, bringing your left elbow toward your right knee.
Continue alternating in a smooth, rhythmic motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bosu elbow plank
description: The BOSU elbow plank is a core stability exercise performed with forearms on the unstable surface of a BOSU ball. The instability of the BOSU increases core muscle recruitment compared to a standard plank, challenging the abs, obliques, and stabilizing muscles.
instructions: Place a BOSU ball flat side up and position your forearms on the dome surface.
Extend your body into a plank position with your elbows beneath your shoulders and feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and glutes to maintain a straight line from head to heels.
Keep your hips level and avoid sagging or rising.
Hold the position for the desired duration.

name: cable glute kickback
description: The cable glute kickback is a cable machine exercise that isolates the gluteus maximus through hip hyperextension. The constant tension provided by the cable makes it highly effective for targeting the glutes throughout the entire range of motion.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to a low cable pulley and secure it around one ankle.
Stand facing the cable machine, holding the frame lightly for balance.
Keeping your core engaged and your leg straight, kick the cuffed leg back as far as comfortable.
Squeeze your glute firmly at the peak of the movement.
Slowly return your foot to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1273 - cable glute kickback.mp4

name: calf raise leg press
description: The calf raise leg press is a machine-based exercise that targets the gastrocnemius and soleus using the leg press platform. By pressing through the balls of the feet, it isolates calf strength and size development effectively.
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine and position the balls of your feet at the bottom edge of the footplate.
Unlock the machine and extend your legs fully, keeping a slight bend in the knees.
Press your toes forward by plantarflexing your ankles, raising your heels as high as possible.
Pause at the top to squeeze your calves.
Slowly lower your heels back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1274 - calf raise leg press.mp4

name: chest press in smith
description: The chest press in Smith machine is a guided barbell pressing exercise targeting the pectorals, triceps, and anterior deltoids. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine provides stability, making it suitable for beginners or those training without a spotter.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to a height just above chest level and lie flat on a bench beneath it.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart with an overhand grip.
Unrack the bar and lower it slowly to your mid-chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Press the bar back up to the starting position by fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and re-rack safely.

name: chin up
description: The chin up is a fundamental bodyweight pulling exercise that targets the lats and biceps. Using a supinated grip places greater emphasis on the biceps compared to an overhand pull-up, making it an effective upper body strength and muscle builder.
instructions: Grasp a pull-up bar with a supinated grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended and your body relaxed.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Pull yourself upward until your chin clears the bar, leading with your chest.
Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cross lat pulldown
description: The cross lat pulldown is a cable exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi by pulling from two opposing high cable pulleys. This bilateral variation increases the range of motion and lat stretch at the top, improving width and back muscle development.
instructions: Attach two single handles to high cable pulleys on opposite sides of a cable crossover machine.
Kneel or stand in the center and grasp the handles, one in each hand, with arms extended overhead.
Pull both handles down and toward your sides simultaneously, driving your elbows toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly return the handles overhead and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crunch hug
description: The crunch hug is a bodyweight abdominal exercise that combines a traditional crunch with a knee tuck. Wrapping the arms around the knees while crunching upward intensifies abdominal engagement and promotes core compression.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Wrap your arms around your knees, hugging them toward your chest.
Curl your upper body off the ground toward your knees, engaging your abs.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your core.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crunches
description: Crunches are a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the rectus abdominis. By curling the upper body off the ground, they isolate the abdominal muscles and build core strength and endurance.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outward.
Engaging your abs, curl your upper body off the ground toward your knees.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell cursty lunges
description: Dumbbell curtsy lunges are a lower body exercise that targets the glutes, particularly the gluteus medius, along with the quads and hip abductors. The crossover stepping pattern adds a rotational demand that increases glute activation compared to standard lunges.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot behind and across your body, as if performing a curtsy.
Lower your back knee toward the ground while keeping your chest tall and core engaged.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Alternate legs with each repetition and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1280 - dumbbell cursty lunges.mp4

name: dumbbell hammer curl
description: The dumbbell hammer curl is an upper arm exercise that targets the biceps brachii and brachioradialis using a neutral grip. This grip variation places greater emphasis on the outer bicep and forearm compared to a standard curl.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your torso throughout the movement.
Curl both dumbbells upward simultaneously, maintaining the neutral grip.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1281 - dumbbell hammer curl.mp4

name: dumbbell hammer to biceps curl
description: The dumbbell hammer to biceps curl is a combination curl exercise that works both the brachioradialis and biceps brachii. By transitioning from a neutral to a supinated grip within each repetition, it targets multiple heads of the bicep for complete development.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip.
Curl the dumbbells upward with palms facing each other, performing a hammer curl.
At the top of the hammer curl, rotate your palms to face upward and continue into a standard biceps curl.
Squeeze your biceps at the peak, then reverse the rotation and lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/1282 - dumbbell hammer to biceps curl.mp4

name: dumbbell leg curl
description: The dumbbell leg curl is a hamstring isolation exercise performed lying face down on a bench, using a dumbbell held between the feet. It effectively mimics the motion of a lying leg curl machine, targeting the hamstrings and calves.
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench, positioning a dumbbell between your feet by squeezing it with your ankles.
Keep your hips pressed into the bench and grip the bench for stability.
Curl your legs upward by bending your knees, bringing the dumbbell toward your glutes.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstrings.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell skullcrusher
description: The dumbbell skullcrusher is a triceps isolation exercise performed lying on a bench. Lowering dumbbells toward the sides of the head while keeping the upper arms fixed places maximum tension on the triceps brachii.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended straight above your chest.
Keep your upper arms stationary and perpendicular to the floor.
Slowly bend your elbows to lower the dumbbells toward the sides of your head.
Pause just before the dumbbells touch your temples, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: elevated plank
description: The elevated plank is a modified bodyweight plank exercise where the hands are placed on an elevated surface. This reduces the load on the core compared to a standard plank, making it suitable for beginners or those recovering from injury.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface such as a bench, box, or step.
Walk your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line from head to heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and shoulders to maintain the plank position.
Keep your hips level without sagging or raising them.
Hold the position for the desired duration.

name: elevated skii jumps with booty bands
description: Elevated ski jumps with booty bands are a plyometric resistance band exercise targeting the glutes, quads, and hamstrings. Incorporating a band above the knees increases hip abductor and glute activation during the lateral jumping movement.
instructions: Loop a resistance band just above your knees and stand with one foot on a low elevated surface.
Push your knees outward against the band and hinge slightly at the hips.
Explosively jump laterally from one side of the elevated surface to the other, landing softly.
Maintain a slight bend in your knees upon landing and immediately jump back to the other side.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions, keeping the band tension consistent throughout.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/plyometrics/intermediate/1286 - elevated skii jumps with booty bands.mp4

name: flutter
description: The flutter kick is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the lower abs and hip flexors. Performing rapid alternating leg kicks while hovering above the ground challenges core stability and endurance.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your hands placed beneath your lower back for support.
Lift both legs off the ground approximately six inches and engage your core.
Alternately raise and lower each leg in a small, rapid flutter kick motion.
Keep your lower back pressed down and your core braced throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: foot touch
description: The foot touch is a bodyweight abdominal exercise performed lying on the back with legs raised. By reaching the hands toward the elevated feet, it creates a full contraction of the rectus abdominis, effectively targeting the upper and lower abs.
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs raised vertically toward the ceiling.
Extend your arms straight up toward your feet.
Curl your upper body off the ground and reach your hands toward your feet.
Pause at the top, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: front squat
description: The front squat is a barbell compound exercise targeting the quadriceps with greater emphasis than a back squat due to the forward bar placement. The upright torso demands significant core and upper back engagement to maintain the front rack position.
instructions: Position the barbell in the front rack position, resting on your front delts with your elbows raised high.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly pointed out.
Keeping your elbows up and chest tall, lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position without dropping your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: glute bridge march
description: The glute bridge march is a bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes while challenging core stability and hip flexor control. Holding a sustained bridge while alternating knee lifts requires the glutes and core to work together dynamically.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge position.
Maintaining the bridge height with your hips, lift one knee toward your chest in a marching motion.
Lower the foot back to the ground and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1290 - glute bridge march.mp4

name: glute lift (leg curl)
description: The glute lift using a leg curl machine combines hamstring curling mechanics with intentional glute activation at the peak of the movement. By focusing on a glute squeeze at the top, it transforms a hamstring isolation machine exercise into a posterior chain movement.
instructions: Lie face down on a leg curl machine and position the pad just above your ankles.
Grip the handles for stability and press your hips into the bench.
Curl your legs upward toward your glutes, squeezing at the top of the movement.
Focus on driving through the glutes rather than only the hamstrings at the peak.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1291 - glute lift (leg curl).mp4

name: glute walk
description: The glute walk is a resistance band exercise that targets the gluteus medius and hip abductors through lateral banded walking. Maintaining a squat position while resisting the band encourages continuous glute activation and improves hip stability.
instructions: Loop a resistance band just above your knees and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a quarter squat position and push your knees outward against the band.
Take a step to one side with your right foot, then bring your left foot to follow.
Continue stepping laterally for the desired number of steps, then reverse direction.
Keep tension on the band throughout and maintain the squat position during all steps.

name: hip abduction
description: The hip abduction machine exercise targets the hip abductors and outer glutes. By pushing the legs outward against resistance, it isolates the gluteus medius and tensor fasciae latae, which are important for hip stability and injury prevention.
instructions: Sit on a hip abduction machine and adjust the pads to rest against the outside of your knees.
Sit upright with your back flat against the seat and your feet on the footrests.
Push your legs outward against the pads, spreading them as wide as comfortable.
Pause at the widest point and squeeze your outer glutes.
Slowly bring your legs back together and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/hip abductors/strength/beginner/1293 - hip abduction.mp4

name: hip adduction
description: The hip adduction machine exercise isolates the inner thigh muscles by squeezing the legs together against resistance. It targets the hip adductors and is commonly used to build inner thigh strength and improve hip stability.
instructions: Sit on a hip adduction machine and adjust the pads to rest against the inside of your knees.
Sit upright with your back flat against the seat and feet on the footrests.
Squeeze your legs together against the pads in a controlled motion.
Pause at the point of full adduction.
Slowly allow your legs to return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/hip adductors/strength/beginner/1294 - hip adduction.mp4

name: hip thrust on leg press
description: The hip thrust on leg press is a glute-dominant exercise that adapts the hip thrust movement to a leg press machine. By driving the hips upward against the resistance of the footplate, it heavily activates the gluteus maximus.
instructions: Position yourself on a leg press machine with only your upper back on the seat and feet low on the platform.
Slide your hips off the seat so your body is extended, using the footplate as resistance.
Drive through your heels to extend your hips upward, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1295 - hip thrust on leg press.mp4

name: in & out jump
description: The in and out jump is a plyometric bodyweight exercise that targets the quads, glutes, and hip abductors. The rapid inward and outward jumping motion elevates the heart rate and builds lower body power and agility.
instructions: Stand with feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump your feet outward to shoulder-width apart while simultaneously bringing your arms up.
Immediately jump your feet back together.
Continue alternating in and out at a rapid pace.
Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/beginner/1296 - in & out jump.mp4

name: incline pushup
description: The incline push-up is a bodyweight exercise that targets the lower pectorals and triceps. Elevating the hands reduces the percentage of body weight lifted compared to a standard push-up, making it a suitable progression exercise for beginners.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface such as a bench, box, or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line.
Lower your chest toward the elevated surface by bending your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause when your chest nearly touches the surface, then push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline treadmill walk
description: The incline treadmill walk is a low-impact cardio exercise that targets the lower body while elevating the heart rate. Walking at a steep incline significantly increases glute, quad, and calf activation compared to walking on a flat surface.
instructions: Set the treadmill to an incline of at least 10–15 percent and a moderate walking speed.
Stand on the belt and begin walking at the set speed without holding the handrails.
Maintain an upright posture and engage your core as you walk.
Drive through your heels with each step to engage your glutes and quads.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: knee lift crunch
description: The knee lift crunch is a standing bodyweight exercise that targets the abs and obliques. Alternating knee lifts combined with rotational crunches create a dynamic core movement that also engages the hip flexors.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your hands behind your head.
Lift your right knee toward your chest while simultaneously crunching your left elbow toward the rising knee.
Lower your leg and return to the upright position.
Repeat on the opposite side, lifting your left knee and crunching your right elbow toward it.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kneeling overhead tricep extension
description: The kneeling overhead tricep extension with a resistance band targets the triceps brachii in the fully stretched position. Kneeling provides a stable base while anchoring the band beneath the knee, allowing for effective overhead tricep loading without equipment.
instructions: Kneel on one knee and anchor a resistance band beneath the planted knee.
Hold the other end of the band with both hands and raise your arms overhead.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, bend your elbows to lower your hands behind your head.
Extend your arms fully overhead to return to the starting position, squeezing your triceps.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/1300 - kneeling overhead tricep extension.mp4

name: landmine barbell deadlift
description: The landmine barbell deadlift is a hip hinge exercise performed with a barbell anchored in a landmine attachment. The angled bar path places a unique stimulus on the glutes and lower back while reducing shear forces on the spine compared to a conventional deadlift.
instructions: Insert one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment and load the free end with plates.
Stand facing the loaded end with feet shoulder-width apart and hinge at the hips to grip the bar.
Brace your core, flatten your back, and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Drive through your heels to stand up, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Hinge back at the hips to lower the bar with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: landmine v squat
description: The landmine V squat uses a barbell anchored in a landmine attachment to create a guided squat path. The angled bar encourages an upright torso and is particularly effective for targeting the quads while reducing lower back strain.
instructions: Insert one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment and stand with the loaded end between your legs.
Grip the bar at hip height with both hands, feet shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your chest up and core tight, lower into a squat by bending your knees and hips.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1302 - landmine v squat.mp4

name: lat pulldown
description: The lat pulldown is a cable machine exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Pulling a weighted bar from overhead down toward the chest mimics the pulling pattern of a pull-up, making it effective for developing back width.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine and adjust the thigh pad to secure your legs.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar down toward your upper chest, driving your elbows toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lean forward hip abduction
description: The lean forward hip abduction is a variation of the machine hip abduction that shifts emphasis toward the gluteus maximus by leaning the torso forward. This positional change alters the angle of hip abduction and increases glute engagement.
instructions: Sit on a hip abduction machine and lean your torso forward slightly, resting your chest on your thighs.
Adjust the pads to rest against the outside of your knees.
Push your legs outward against the pads in a controlled motion.
Pause at the widest point and squeeze your outer glutes.
Slowly return your legs to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1304 - lean forward hip abduction.mp4

name: leg press frog stance
description: The leg press frog stance is a leg press variation with a wide, high, and externally rotated foot placement that targets the glutes and hip adductors. This position shifts emphasis away from the quads and onto the posterior and inner thigh muscles.
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine and position your feet wide and high on the platform, toes pointed outward.
Lower the weight by bending your knees and allowing them to track outward toward your elbows.
Push through your heels to extend your legs and return to the starting position without locking your knees.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1305 - leg press frog stance.mp4

name: leg press narrow stance
description: The leg press narrow stance is a leg press variation that places the feet close together on the platform, increasing the emphasis on the quadriceps. This foot positioning recruits the outer quad more than a wider stance.
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine and place your feet close together at the center of the platform.
Ensure your feet are directly below or slightly above center with toes pointed forward.
Lower the weight by bending your knees to approximately 90 degrees.
Press through the balls of your feet to extend your legs and return to the starting position without locking your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1306 - leg press narrow stance.mp4

name: lunges to hacksquat
description: Lunges to hack squat is a compound bodyweight sequence that combines a forward lunge with a hack squat to target the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. The movement transitions between two complementary exercises to maximize lower body engagement.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands at your sides.
Step forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground.
Push off your front foot to bring your feet together and stand upright.
From standing, lower into a hack squat by hinging at the hips and bending the knees with the torso leaning slightly back.
Return to standing and repeat the lunge on the opposite leg, continuing in an alternating sequence.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1307 - lunges to hacksquat.mp4

name: lying leg curl
description: The lying leg curl is a machine-based isolation exercise targeting the hamstrings. By curling the legs toward the glutes against resistance, it effectively develops hamstring strength and size.
instructions: Lie face down on a leg curl machine and position the pad just above your ankles.
Grip the handles for stability and press your hips firmly into the pad.
Curl your legs upward toward your glutes by bending your knees.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstrings.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1308 - lying leg curl.mp4

name: medicine ball abs crunch
description: The medicine ball abs crunch is a weighted abdominal exercise that targets the rectus abdominis. Holding a medicine ball against the chest adds load to the standard crunch, increasing the difficulty and muscle engagement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a medicine ball with both hands against your chest.
Engaging your abs, curl your upper body off the ground, bringing the ball toward your knees.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1309 - medicine ball abs crunch.mp4

name: medicine ball elbow to knee
description: The medicine ball elbow to knee is a weighted core exercise that targets the abs and obliques. Combining a knee tuck with a weighted crunch provides rotational and flexion demand across the entire core.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and holding a medicine ball with both hands.
Lift your shoulders off the ground and extend your legs slightly.
Bring one knee toward your chest while simultaneously bringing the medicine ball toward that knee.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1310 - medicine ball elbow to knee.mp4

name: medicine ball mountain climber
description: The medicine ball mountain climber is a dynamic core exercise that targets the abs and hip flexors while also challenging shoulder stability. Placing the hands on a medicine ball increases instability, amplifying core and stabilizer recruitment.
instructions: Begin in a plank position with your hands on a medicine ball, arms fully extended.
Engage your core and maintain a straight line from head to heels.
Drive one knee toward your chest as quickly as possible, then return it to the starting position.
Immediately repeat with the opposite knee in a running motion.
Continue alternating legs at a rapid pace for the desired duration or repetitions.

name: medicine ball plank
description: The medicine ball plank is a core stability exercise that increases the challenge of a standard plank by placing the hands on an unstable spherical surface. The instability demands greater shoulder and core muscle activation to maintain a neutral position.
instructions: Place a medicine ball on the floor and position your hands on top of it, shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet back to assume a plank position with your body forming a straight line.
Engage your core and stabilize your upper body on the rounded surface of the ball.
Keep your hips level and hold the position without letting the ball shift.
Hold for the desired duration.

name: medicine ball straight crunch
description: The medicine ball straight crunch is a weighted abdominal exercise performed with arms extended overhead holding a medicine ball. Reaching the ball upward during the crunch increases the lever arm, intensifying the demand on the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold a medicine ball with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Curl your upper body off the ground, reaching the ball straight up toward the ceiling.
Pause at the top and squeeze your abs.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1313 - medicine ball straight crunch.mp4

name: medicine ball toe tap
description: The medicine ball toe tap is an abdominal exercise that targets the upper and lower abs simultaneously. By holding a medicine ball between the feet and reaching to tap it at the top of the crunch, it creates a full-range core contraction.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and place a medicine ball between your feet, gripping it with your ankles.
Extend your legs upward to hold the ball above your hips.
Curl your upper body off the ground and reach your hands toward the ball.
Tap the ball with your fingertips at the top of the crunch.
Lower back to the starting position with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: medicine plank to toe tap
description: The medicine plank to toe tap combines a medicine ball plank with a rotational reach, targeting the abs and obliques. The movement challenges core stability and introduces a rotational element that increases the demand on the entire core.
instructions: Begin in a plank position with your hands on a medicine ball.
Maintain a straight body line and brace your core.
Lift one hand off the ball and reach it toward the opposite foot, rotating slightly through your torso.
Return your hand to the ball and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1315 - medicine plank to toe tap.mp4

name: narrow grip hack squat
description: The narrow grip hack squat is a machine squat variation performed with feet close together, placing greater emphasis on the outer quadriceps. The restricted stance increases the VMO and lateral quad activation compared to a standard wide stance hack squat.
instructions: Position yourself on a hack squat machine with a narrow foot stance, feet together or close.
Toes should face forward or slightly outward.
Disengage the safety and lower the sled by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the footplate.
Drive through your heels to extend your legs back to the starting position without locking your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1316 - narrow grip hack squat.mp4

name: one arm shoulder press
description: The one arm shoulder press is a unilateral dumbbell exercise targeting the deltoids and triceps. Pressing with one arm at a time requires significant core stabilization to resist lateral lean, making it effective for addressing shoulder strength imbalances.
instructions: Stand or sit with a dumbbell in one hand, raised to shoulder height with your palm facing forward.
Brace your core to stabilize your torso against the unilateral load.
Press the dumbbell straight overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to shoulder height.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching arms.

name: one leg deadlift smith machine
description: The one leg deadlift on a Smith machine is a single-leg hip hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings and glutes. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine provides stability, making this exercise accessible for developing unilateral posterior chain strength.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to hip height and stand facing it on one leg.
Grip the bar with both hands and hinge forward at the hip, extending the lifted leg behind you.
Lower the bar by hinging until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground.
Drive through your planted heel to return to the standing position.
Complete the desired repetitions on one leg before switching sides.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1318 - one leg deadlift smith machine.mp4

name: one leg hip thrust (leg press)
description: The one leg hip thrust on a leg press is a unilateral glute exercise that adapts the hip thrust to a leg press machine. Performing the movement with a single leg increases the glute load and helps address strength imbalances between sides.
instructions: Set up on a leg press machine with only your upper back supported and one foot on the lower portion of the platform.
Extend the non-working leg out or rest it on the platform.
Drive through the heel of the working foot to thrust your hips upward.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your hips and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1319 - one leg hip thrust (leg press).mp4

name: plank twist
description: The plank twist is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the obliques through rotational hip movement from a plank position. It challenges core stability while adding a dynamic element that specifically engages the lateral core muscles.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with your elbows beneath your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and rotate your hips to one side, lowering them toward the ground.
Pause just before touching the floor, then rotate to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled, rhythmic motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: push to toe-touch
description: Push to toe-touch is a compound bodyweight exercise that combines a push-up with a rotational toe-touch reach. It targets the chest, triceps, and core, while the toe-touch component activates the obliques and hamstrings in a flowing sequence.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest to the ground and perform a push-up.
As you push back up, transition into a downward-dog position by raising your hips.
Lift one hand and reach toward the opposite foot, touching or reaching toward your toe.
Return to the push-up position and repeat on the opposite side, continuing to alternate.

name: push-ups
description: Push-ups are a foundational bodyweight exercise that targets the pectorals, triceps, and anterior deltoids. They require no equipment and can be performed anywhere, making them one of the most versatile upper body pressing exercises.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels with your core engaged.
Lower your chest toward the ground by bending your elbows at approximately 45 degrees.
Pause when your chest is just above the floor, then push through your palms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rear flyes
description: Rear flyes are a dumbbell exercise targeting the rhomboids and posterior deltoids. Performed in a hinged position, the outward arc of the arms effectively activates the upper back and rear shoulder muscles, improving posture and shoulder health.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground.
Let the dumbbells hang in front of you with a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise both arms out to the sides simultaneously, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band deadlift
description: The resistance band deadlift mimics the hip hinge pattern of a conventional deadlift using a resistance band. It targets the glutes, hamstrings, and lower back, providing accommodating resistance that increases throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Stand with both feet on the center of a resistance band, feet hip-width apart.
Hold one end of the band in each hand and hinge at the hips with a flat back.
Brace your core and drive through your heels to stand upright, extending your hips and knees.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Slowly hinge back at the hips to return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse plank abs & adduction
description: The reverse plank abs and adduction is a bodyweight exercise that combines core stabilization with hip adductor engagement. Performing inner thigh squeezes from a reverse plank position challenges the abs, glutes, and inner thighs simultaneously.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands placed behind you, fingers pointing toward your feet.
Press through your palms and heels to lift your hips into a reverse plank position.
Engage your core and squeeze your inner thighs together in an adduction motion.
Hold the adduction briefly, then release slightly and repeat the squeeze.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining the reverse plank.

name: seated calf raise leg press
description: The seated calf raise on a leg press machine targets the calves with the knees bent, which shifts the emphasis to the soleus muscle. It is an effective machine-based calf exercise for building lower leg size and strength.
instructions: Sit on a leg press machine and place the balls of your feet at the lower edge of the footplate.
Extend your legs slightly and unlock the machine.
Lower your heels below the footplate to get a full stretch in the calves.
Press your toes upward by plantarflexing your ankles as high as possible.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your heels back to the stretched position and repeat.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1326 - seated calf raise leg press.mp4

name: side kneeling kick
description: The side kneeling kick is a bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteus medius and hip abductors through a lateral leg lift. Performing the movement from a kneeling position isolates the outer glute and promotes hip abductor strength and stability.
instructions: Begin on all fours with your hands below your shoulders and knees below your hips.
Lower onto one forearm and extend the opposite leg out to the side.
Lift the extended leg upward in a lateral kick, squeezing your outer glute at the top.
Slowly lower the leg back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1327 - side kneeling kick.mp4

name: side to side crunch
description: The side to side crunch is a bodyweight exercise that targets the obliques through lateral flexion. Alternating the crunch direction to each side creates a comprehensive oblique workout that improves lateral core strength.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing out.
Curl your upper body up and to the right, bringing your right elbow toward your right knee.
Lower back to the starting position and repeat to the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: single arm lat pulldown
description: The single arm lat pulldown is a unilateral cable exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Training one side at a time allows for greater focus on the working lat and helps correct strength and size imbalances in the upper back.
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high cable pulley and sit at the lat pulldown station.
Grasp the handle with one hand and extend your arm fully overhead.
Pull the handle down toward your shoulder by driving your elbow toward your hip.
Squeeze your lat at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly allow the handle to return overhead and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching arms.

name: smith machine donkey kick
description: The Smith machine donkey kick is a glute isolation exercise that uses the Smith machine bar as resistance during a kickback motion. The fixed bar path provides consistent resistance at the top of the hip extension, maximizing glute contraction.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to a low height and kneel facing away from the machine.
Place one foot under the bar and position it to hook or press against it.
Keep your hands on the floor for support and your core engaged.
Drive the foot upward and backward, extending your hip and pressing through the bar.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement and slowly return to the starting position, repeating for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1330 - smith machine donkey kick.mp4

name: stairmaster jog
description: The StairMaster jog is a high-intensity cardio exercise performed on a StairMaster machine at a jogging pace. It targets the quads, glutes, and calves while significantly elevating the heart rate, making it effective for cardiovascular conditioning and lower body endurance.
instructions: Set the StairMaster to a moderate to high speed setting.
Stand tall with a light grip on the handrails for balance only.
Drive your knees forward and upward at a jogging pace, stepping onto each step with full foot contact.
Maintain an upright posture and avoid leaning heavily on the rails.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: stairmaster kickback
description: The StairMaster kickback adds a glute activation element to stair climbing by exaggerating the trailing leg extension with each step. This variation increases glute and hamstring engagement beyond standard stair climbing.
instructions: Stand on the StairMaster and set it to a slow, controlled speed.
As you step onto each stair, kick the trailing leg back and upward in an exaggerated motion.
Squeeze your glute at the peak of the kickback.
Place your foot back on the next step and repeat the kickback with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating for the desired duration.

name: stairmaster walk
description: The StairMaster walk is a low-impact cardio exercise that mimics climbing stairs. It targets the quads, glutes, and calves while maintaining a steady cardiovascular pace, making it accessible for all fitness levels.
instructions: Set the StairMaster to a comfortable walking speed.
Stand upright on the machine with a light touch on the handrails for balance.
Step onto each stair with the full foot, pressing down through the heel.
Maintain a steady upright posture and avoid resting your weight on the rails.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: standing cable row
description: The standing cable row is a cable exercise targeting the lats and upper back from a standing position. The upright stance requires greater core engagement compared to a seated row, adding a functional stability component to the pulling movement.
instructions: Attach a handle to a cable pulley set at about waist height and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with one or both hands and take a slight step back to create tension.
Stand upright with your core braced and a slight hinge at the hips.
Pull the handle toward your torso by driving your elbow back and squeezing your shoulder blade.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: supinated triceps extension (one arm)
description: The supinated triceps extension with one arm is a cable isolation exercise that targets the triceps using a reverse grip. The supinated wrist position shifts the emphasis to the long head of the triceps, promoting full triceps development.
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high cable pulley and face away from the machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand with a supinated grip, palm facing upward.
Step away from the machine and raise your elbow overhead.
Extend your forearm downward and forward until your arm is fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching arms.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/1335 - supinated triceps extension (one arm).mp4

name: thrust to abduction
description: Thrust to abduction is a resistance band exercise that combines a glute bridge with a hip abduction movement. This compound glute exercise targets the gluteus maximus through the thrust and the gluteus medius through the outward knee press.
instructions: Loop a resistance band just above your knees and lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Drive through your heels to perform a glute bridge, thrusting your hips toward the ceiling.
At the top of the bridge, push your knees apart against the band in an abduction motion.
Bring your knees back together and lower your hips to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1336 - thrust to abduction.mp4

name: toe tap
description: The toe tap is a bodyweight abdominal exercise performed lying on the back with legs raised vertically. Reaching upward to tap the toes creates a strong contraction of the rectus abdominis and engages the hip flexors throughout.
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs raised vertically, forming a 90-degree angle at the hips.
Extend your arms straight up toward your feet.
Curl your upper body off the ground and reach your fingertips toward your toes.
Lightly tap your toes at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: treadmill jog
description: The treadmill jog is a moderate-intensity cardio exercise that targets the lower body while improving cardiovascular endurance. The controlled treadmill environment allows for consistent pacing and easy adjustment of speed and incline.
instructions: Set the treadmill to a jogging speed appropriate for your fitness level.
Begin walking and gradually increase the speed to a comfortable jog.
Maintain an upright posture with a slight forward lean and relaxed arm swing.
Land with a midfoot strike and keep your breathing controlled throughout.
Continue for the desired duration or distance.

name: treadmill run
description: The treadmill run is a high-intensity cardio exercise targeting the lower body and cardiovascular system. Running on a treadmill provides a controlled environment for speed training, interval work, and endurance development.
instructions: Set the treadmill to a running speed appropriate for your fitness level.
Warm up by walking or jogging before increasing to a run.
Maintain a tall posture with a slight forward lean and actively drive your arms.
Land with a midfoot strike beneath your hips and maintain an even breathing rhythm.
Continue for the desired duration or distance.

name: treadmill walk
description: The treadmill walk is a low-impact cardio exercise suitable for all fitness levels. It targets the lower body while improving cardiovascular health, and can be adjusted in speed and incline to match varying fitness goals.
instructions: Set the treadmill to a comfortable walking speed.
Step onto the belt and begin walking at a steady pace.
Maintain an upright posture with relaxed shoulders and a natural arm swing.
Breathe steadily and keep your gaze forward.
Continue for the desired duration or distance.

name: under knee ab crunch
description: The under knee ab crunch is a bodyweight exercise that combines a standard crunch with a knee tuck. Placing hands beneath the knees provides light feedback and assists in coordinating the simultaneous torso and knee movement, targeting the full abdominal wall.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands beneath your thighs or knees for light support.
Curl your upper body off the ground, bringing your chest toward your knees.
At the same time, draw your knees toward your chest to meet your rising torso.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1341 - under knee ab crunch.mp4

name: wide hack squat
description: The wide hack squat is a machine squat variation performed with a wide stance and externally rotated feet. This positioning shifts emphasis to the inner quads and hip adductors while still heavily recruiting the glutes, making it effective for overall lower body development.
instructions: Position yourself on a hack squat machine with a wide foot stance and toes pointed outward.
Place your shoulders under the pads and disengage the safety locks.
Lower the sled by bending your knees, tracking them in line with your toes until your thighs are parallel.
Drive through your heels to extend your legs back to the starting position without locking your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1342 - wide hack squat.mp4

name: agility ladder squat jump in out
description: The agility ladder squat jump in out is a plyometric drill that targets the quadriceps and challenges coordination, speed, and lower body power. It combines ladder footwork with explosive squat jumps to improve agility and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Set up an agility ladder flat on the ground.
Stand at one end of the ladder with your feet together.
Jump both feet into the first square, then jump them out to either side.
Immediately drop into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Explode upward and move to the next square, repeating the in-out squat pattern down the length of the ladder.
Continue for the desired number of passes.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1517 - agility ladder squat jump in out.mp4

name: balls slam on wall squat
description: The balls slam on wall squat is a dynamic compound exercise that combines a squat with an explosive wall throw, targeting the quadriceps while also engaging the shoulders and core. It builds lower body strength and upper body power simultaneously.
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy wall, holding a medicine ball at chest height.
Lower into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
As you rise from the squat, explosively throw the ball against the wall.
Catch the ball as it rebounds and immediately lower back into the squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1518 - balls slam on wall squat.mp4

name: band row to triceps
description: The band row to triceps is a resistance band exercise that combines a back row with a triceps extension, targeting the lats while also engaging the triceps and rear delts. This compound movement improves pulling strength and arm definition.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at chest height and stand facing the anchor point.
Grasp the band with both hands and step back to create tension.
Pull the band towards your torso in a rowing motion, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
At the end of the row, extend your arms back in a triceps kickback motion.
Return to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/beginner/1519 - band row to triceps.mp4

name: band single leg pistol squat
description: The band single leg pistol squat uses a resistance band for assistance, targeting the quadriceps while building single-leg strength, balance, and hip stability. It is an effective progression toward the unassisted pistol squat.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at waist height and hold it for support with both hands.
Stand on one leg with the other leg extended in front of you.
Slowly lower your body by bending the standing knee, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Descend until your thigh is parallel to the ground or as low as possible.
Drive through the heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1520 - band single leg pistol squat.mp4

name: barbell bench wide grip press
description: The barbell bench wide grip press is a chest exercise that uses a wider-than-normal hand placement to increase the stretch on the pectorals. It targets the chest with secondary emphasis on the triceps and front delts, making it effective for building chest width.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grasp the barbell with a grip wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly above your chest with arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell to your lower chest, keeping your elbows flared outward.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1521 - barbell bench wide grip press.mp4

name: barbell close grip bench press
description: The barbell close grip bench press is a compound pressing exercise that shifts emphasis onto the triceps by using a narrower grip. It also engages the pectorals and front delts, making it a popular movement for building arm and upper body pressing strength.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grasp the barbell with a grip shoulder-width or slightly narrower apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly above your chest with arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell to your lower chest, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/triceps/strength/intermediate/1522 - barbell close grip bench press.mp4

name: barbell deadlift to press
description: The barbell deadlift to press is a full-body compound exercise that combines a deadlift with an overhead press, targeting the traps and posterior chain while also developing shoulder strength. It is an efficient movement for building total-body power and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart with a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your back flat.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, performing a deadlift.
At the top, shrug the bar upward and transition into an overhead press, pushing the bar above your head.
Lower the bar back to your shoulders and then to the floor with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell elevated back squat
description: The barbell elevated back squat places the heels on an elevated surface to increase quad activation and allow for a more upright torso. It targets the quadriceps with secondary emphasis on the glutes and hamstrings, and is commonly used to improve squat depth and mechanics.
instructions: Position a barbell on your upper back and stand with your heels elevated on plates or a wedge.
