Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Keeping your chest up and back straight, lower your body by bending your knees.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1524 - barbell elevated back squat.mp4

name: barbell elevated front squats
description: The barbell elevated front squat combines heel elevation with a front rack position to maximize quadriceps engagement and encourage an upright torso. It targets the quads with secondary involvement of the glutes, core, and upper back for stabilization.
instructions: Position a barbell across the front of your shoulders with heels elevated on plates or a wedge.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and cross your arms to secure the bar or use a clean grip.
Keeping your elbows high and chest up, lower your body by bending your knees.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1525 - barbell elevated front squats.mp4

name: barbell good morning to behind neck shoulder press
description: The barbell good morning to behind neck shoulder press is an advanced compound movement combining a posterior chain stretch with an overhead press. It targets the hamstrings and lower back through the good morning while also developing shoulder and trap strength through the press.
instructions: Place a barbell across your upper back and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hinge forward at the hips with a slight bend in the knees, lowering your torso toward the ground.
Keep your back flat and lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Return to an upright position, then press the barbell overhead from behind the neck.
Lower the bar back to your upper back and repeat the sequence.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/hamstrings/strength/advanced/1526 - barbell good morning to behind neck shoulder press.mp4

name: barbell reverse lunge press
description: The barbell reverse lunge press is a compound lower and upper body exercise that combines a reverse lunge with an overhead press. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while simultaneously developing shoulder strength and core stability.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a barbell at shoulder height in a front rack position.
Step one foot back and lower your back knee toward the ground, entering a reverse lunge.
At the bottom of the lunge, press the barbell overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the bar back to your shoulders as you return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1527 - barbell reverse lunge press.mp4

name: barbell deadlift to curl
description: The barbell deadlift to curl is a compound exercise that targets the hamstrings and posterior chain through a deadlift while adding a biceps curl at the top of the movement. It is an efficient exercise for building back, glute, and arm strength in one fluid motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart with a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, keeping your back flat.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, performing a deadlift.
At the top of the deadlift, curl the barbell up toward your chest by bending your elbows.
Lower the bar back down with control and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: battle rope alternate slam in out
description: The battle rope alternate slam in out is a high-intensity conditioning exercise that combines alternating rope slams with lateral foot movement. It targets the biceps and shoulders while engaging the core and improving cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance holding one end of the battle rope in each hand.
Raise one arm overhead and slam the rope down to the ground while stepping that foot outward.
Immediately raise the opposite arm and slam, stepping the other foot outward.
Alternate sides in a continuous rhythmic motion, maintaining a stable athletic base.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/cardio/intermediate/1529 - battle rope alternate slam in out.mp4

name: battle rope russian twist
description: The battle rope russian twist is a core exercise that combines the rotational demands of a russian twist with the added resistance of a battle rope. It targets the abdominals and obliques while also engaging the shoulders for rope control.
instructions: Sit on the floor in a slight recline with your knees bent and feet elevated or flat on the ground.
Hold one end of a battle rope in both hands with arms extended in front of you.
Rotate your torso to one side, bringing the rope toward the ground beside your hip.
Return to center and rotate to the other side in a controlled twisting motion.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bench frog barbell glute
description: The bench frog barbell glute bridge is a weighted hip thrust variation that uses a frog stance to increase glute activation. Performed with a barbell across the hips and upper back on a bench, it primarily targets the glutes with secondary engagement of the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Position your upper back on a flat bench with a barbell across your hips.
Place your feet flat on the ground with knees bent and turned outward in a frog stance.
Drive your hips upward by squeezing your glutes until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Hold at the top for a moment, then lower your hips back down with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1531 - bench frog barbell glute.mp4

name: bench frog kick band
description: The bench frog kick band exercise uses a resistance band to add tension to a frog-stance hip thrust, increasing glute activation and hip abductor engagement. It targets the glutes while also working the hamstrings and outer hips.
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees and position your upper back on a flat bench.
Plant your feet on the ground with knees bent and turned outward in a frog stance.
Drive your hips upward against the resistance of the band, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Hold briefly, then lower your hips back toward the ground with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1532 - bench frog kick band.mp4

name: boating
description: Boating is a low-impact cardiovascular exercise that simulates the motion of rowing. It engages the upper back and shoulders while requiring core stability to maintain balance, making it a functional movement for endurance and postural strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your tailbone.
Simulate a rowing motion by extending your arms forward and then pulling them back toward your torso.
Maintain a stable core throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to curl press
description: The bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to curl press is a full-body compound exercise performed on an unstable surface. It targets the quads, glutes, and hamstrings through the deadlift while adding a biceps curl and overhead press to challenge the upper body and core balance simultaneously.
instructions: Stand on the flat side of a bosu ball holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the ground, performing a deadlift.
Drive through your hips to stand upright on the bosu ball.
Curl the dumbbells to shoulder height, then press them overhead until arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back down with control and return to the deadlift starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/1534 - bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to curl press.mp4

name: bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to lateral raises
description: The bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to lateral raises pairs a deadlift with lateral raises on an unstable bosu surface. It targets the quadriceps and posterior chain while adding a shoulder strengthening component, all while challenging core stability and balance.
instructions: Stand on the flat side of a bosu ball holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the ground in a deadlift motion.
Drive through your hips to return to a standing position on the bosu ball.
At the top, raise the dumbbells out to your sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Lower the dumbbells back down and return to the deadlift starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1535 - bosu ball dumbbell deadlift to lateral raises.mp4

name: bosu ball kneeling ball slam
description: The bosu ball kneeling ball slam is a core and shoulder exercise that challenges stability while developing explosive upper body power. Performed kneeling on a bosu ball, it targets the shoulders and core with each slam, requiring significant balance and coordination.
instructions: Kneel on the dome side of a bosu ball, maintaining a stable upright position.
Hold a medicine ball or slam ball with both hands overhead.
Engage your core and slam the ball forcefully down in front of you.
Retrieve the ball and return to the overhead starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1536 - bosu ball kneeling ball slam.mp4

name: bosu ball lateral battle rope shuffle
description: The bosu ball lateral battle rope shuffle is a high-intensity cardio drill combining lateral agility with upper body battle rope work. It challenges cardiovascular endurance, lateral quickness, and shoulder and core endurance simultaneously.
instructions: Place a bosu ball flat-side down and stand to one side of it holding battle ropes in each hand.
Shuffle laterally over the bosu ball to the opposite side, keeping a low athletic stance.
While shuffling, continuously wave the battle ropes in an alternating pattern.
Shuffle back over the bosu ball to the starting side and repeat.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/advanced/1537 - bosu ball lateral battle rope shuffle.mp4

name: bosu ball plank toe tap
description: The bosu ball plank toe tap is a core stability exercise performed on an unstable surface. It targets the abdominals and obliques while requiring shoulder stability, making it an effective movement for building core strength and anti-rotation control.
instructions: Begin in a plank position with your hands on the flat side of a bosu ball and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and tap your right foot out to the side, then return it to the starting position.
Repeat with your left foot, alternating sides in a controlled manner.
Avoid rotating your hips throughout the movement.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1538 - bosu ball plank toe tap.mp4

name: box lateral shuffle
description: The box lateral shuffle is an agility and lower body conditioning drill that targets the quadriceps and glutes. It develops lateral quickness and coordination by repeatedly stepping over a low box from side to side in a controlled shuffling pattern.
instructions: Stand to the side of a low box or platform in an athletic stance.
Step one foot onto the box, then bring the other foot up to meet it.
Step the leading foot down to the other side of the box, followed by the trailing foot.
Immediately reverse the direction, shuffling back to the starting side.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/cardio/beginner/1539 - box lateral shuffle.mp4

name: burpee to broad jump
description: The burpee to broad jump is a full-body plyometric exercise that combines the conditioning demands of a burpee with an explosive horizontal jump. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while also developing total-body power, cardiovascular endurance, and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Drop your hands to the ground and jump or step your feet back into a push-up position.
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back toward your hands.
From a crouched position, swing your arms back and then explosively jump forward as far as possible.
Land softly with bent knees and immediately reset for the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1540 - burpee to broad jump.mp4

name: burpee to tuck jump
description: The burpee to tuck jump is an advanced plyometric exercise combining the full-body conditioning of a burpee with an explosive vertical tuck jump. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while developing power, coordination, and cardiovascular fitness.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Drop your hands to the ground and jump or step your feet back into a push-up position.
Perform a push-up, then explosively jump your feet back toward your hands.
From a crouched position, leap vertically and drive your knees toward your chest in a tuck position.
Land softly with bent knees and immediately begin the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/1541 - burpee to tuck jump.mp4

name: cross country
description: Cross country is a dynamic core and cardio exercise performed in a plank position that mimics a cross-body running motion. It targets the cardiovascular system while engaging the core, hip flexors, and lower body for a challenging conditioning workout.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Drive your right knee toward your left elbow in a cross-body motion.
Return to the starting position and repeat with your left knee toward your right elbow.
Alternate sides in a continuous, controlled running motion.
Maintain a flat back and engaged core throughout.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: diamond cobra push up
description: The diamond cobra push up combines a close-grip diamond push-up with a cobra extension at the bottom of each rep. It targets the pectorals and triceps during the pressing phase while stretching the lower back and core through the cobra movement.
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands forming a diamond shape under your chest.
Lower your chest toward your hands, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
At the bottom, lower your hips to the ground and extend your arms, arching your back into a cobra stretch.
Return your hips upward and bend your elbows to lower back into the bottom of the push-up.
Push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dragon flag
description: The dragon flag is an advanced bodyweight core exercise that requires exceptional abdominal and full-body tension. It targets the abs by demanding that the entire body be held rigid while raising and lowering, also engaging the lower back, glutes, and shoulders for stabilization.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench and grip the bench behind your head with both hands.
Engage your core and lift your entire body off the bench, supported only by your upper back and shoulders.
Keep your body in a straight line from shoulders to feet as you slowly lower toward the bench.
Stop just before your body touches the bench, then raise back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell close chest press
description: The dumbbell close chest press is a chest exercise performed by keeping both dumbbells pressed together throughout the movement. This creates continuous adduction tension on the pectorals, making it an effective movement for building inner chest strength and definition.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
Press the dumbbells together and hold them touching throughout the movement.
Lower the dumbbells toward your chest while maintaining constant inward pressure.
Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position, keeping them in contact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell deadlift to biceps curl
description: The dumbbell deadlift to biceps curl is a compound exercise that pairs a posterior chain deadlift with a biceps curl. It targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back while adding an arm strengthening component to create an efficient full-body movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the floor, keeping your back flat.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, performing a deadlift.
At the top, curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells back down and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1546 - dumbbell deadlift to biceps curl.mp4

name: dumbbell drop step
description: The dumbbell drop step is a unilateral lower body exercise that involves stepping back diagonally to challenge the quads and glutes from a different angle. It improves single-leg strength, balance, and hip stability while also engaging the core.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot back and diagonally to the outside, lowering into a reverse diagonal lunge.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes and your torso upright.
Drive through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1547 - dumbbell drop step.mp4

name: dumbbell elevated goblet squats
description: The dumbbell elevated goblet squat uses heel elevation to increase quadriceps activation while the goblet grip encourages an upright torso and core engagement. It is an effective variation for building quad and glute strength with improved squat mechanics.
instructions: Stand with your heels elevated on plates or a wedge, holding a single dumbbell vertically at your chest in a goblet grip.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Keeping your chest up and elbows pointing down, lower your body by bending your knees.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1548 - dumbbell elevated goblet squats.mp4

name: dumbbell elevated squat front lunge
description: The dumbbell elevated squat front lunge combines an elevated squat with a forward lunge to target the quadriceps and glutes through two complementary movement patterns. The heel elevation increases quad recruitment during the squat phase, while the lunge challenges single-leg strength and balance.
instructions: Stand with heels elevated on plates or a wedge, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Perform a squat by lowering your hips until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Return to the standing position, then step one foot forward into a lunge.
Lower your back knee toward the ground, then push off the front foot to return.
Alternate legs with each lunge repetition and continue for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1549 - dumbbell elevated squat front lunge.mp4

name: dumbbell goblet squat to reverse lunge
description: The dumbbell goblet squat to reverse lunge is a compound lower body exercise that pairs a goblet squat with a reverse lunge. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while improving unilateral strength, balance, and core stability throughout both movements.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell vertically at your chest in a goblet grip, standing with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a goblet squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Return to standing, then step one foot back into a reverse lunge.
Lower your back knee toward the ground, then return to standing.
Alternate legs with each lunge and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1550 - dumbbell goblet squat to reverse lunge.mp4

name: dumbbell hammer press
description: The dumbbell hammer press is a chest pressing exercise performed with a neutral grip to reduce shoulder stress and increase triceps involvement. It targets the pectorals with secondary emphasis on the triceps and front delts, and is a joint-friendly alternative to traditional dumbbell bench press.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing each other.
Hold the dumbbells at chest level with your elbows at approximately a 45-degree angle from your torso.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell incline close chest press
description: The dumbbell incline close chest press combines an incline angle with the constant inward pressing of a close chest press. It targets the upper portion of the pectorals while the adduction tension engages the inner chest, making it effective for upper chest development.
instructions: Set a bench to a 30-45 degree incline and lie back holding dumbbells pressed together at your chest.
Maintain constant inward pressure on the dumbbells throughout the movement.
Lower the dumbbells toward your upper chest while keeping them in contact.
Press back up to the starting position without allowing the dumbbells to separate.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1552 - dumbbell incline close chest press.mp4

name: dumbbell kneeling hammer curl to hammer press
description: The dumbbell kneeling hammer curl to hammer press is a compound arm exercise performed in a kneeling position, eliminating lower body momentum. It targets the biceps through the curl and the shoulders through the press, while the kneeling position engages the core for stability.
instructions: Kneel on both knees on a mat with a dumbbell in each hand using a neutral grip.
Curl the dumbbells upward toward your shoulders in a hammer curl motion.
From the top of the curl, press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, then lower further to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1553 - dumbbell kneeling hammer curl to hammer press.mp4

name: dumbbell lateral raise to front raise
description: The dumbbell lateral raise to front raise is a shoulder exercise that targets all heads of the deltoid by combining lateral raises with front raises. Alternating between the two movements provides comprehensive shoulder development and improved muscular endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Raise both arms out to the sides in a lateral raise until they are parallel to the ground.
Lower the dumbbells back down, then raise both arms straight in front of you in a front raise.
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Continue alternating between lateral and front raises for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/beginner/1554 - dumbbell lateral raise to front raise.mp4

name: dumbbell reverse step
description: The dumbbell reverse step is a lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes by stepping backward onto an elevated platform. It develops unilateral leg strength and improves balance and coordination with the added resistance of dumbbells.
instructions: Stand facing a low step or platform holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step backward onto the platform with one foot, then bring the other foot up.
Step back down one foot at a time, returning to the starting position.
Repeat, leading with the opposite foot and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1555 - dumbbell reverse step.mp4

name: dumbbell single leg & arm squat press
description: The dumbbell single leg and arm squat press is a unilateral exercise that challenges balance and coordination by pairing a single-leg squat with a contralateral overhead press. It targets the quads and glutes while also developing shoulder strength and core stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand at shoulder height.
Lower into a single-leg squat by bending the standing knee, keeping your chest upright.
As you rise from the squat, press the dumbbell overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder height as you return to the starting position.
Complete the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/1556 - dumbbell single leg & arm squat press.mp4

name: dumbbell single leg deadlift to upright row
description: The dumbbell single leg deadlift to upright row combines a single-leg Romanian deadlift with an upright row. It targets the hamstrings and glutes through the hinge while adding trap and shoulder engagement through the row, also requiring significant core and balance stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the floor, extending the free leg behind you.
Return to upright, then pull the dumbbells up along your body toward your chin in an upright row.
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1557 - dumbbell single leg deadlift to upright row.mp4

name: dumbbell step up box
description: The dumbbell step up box is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes by stepping up onto an elevated platform with added dumbbell resistance. It builds single-leg strength, balance, and coordination.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or bench holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step one foot onto the box and drive through that heel to lift your body up.
Bring the other foot onto the box to stand fully upright.
Step back down one foot at a time to return to the starting position.
Repeat, leading with the opposite foot and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1558 - dumbbell step up box.mp4

name: dumbbell transverse lunges
description: Dumbbell transverse lunges involve stepping at an angle across the body, targeting the quadriceps and glutes while engaging the hip abductors and adductors. This multi-planar movement improves lower body strength, mobility, and dynamic stability.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot diagonally behind you in a transverse or curtsy lunge pattern.
Lower your back knee toward the ground while keeping your front knee stable.
Drive through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1559 - dumbbell transverse lunges.mp4

name: dumbbell windmill
description: The dumbbell windmill is a functional exercise that targets the obliques and core by rotating through the torso while maintaining an overhead dumbbell. It also stretches and strengthens the hamstrings and glutes, making it an excellent movement for mobility and rotational strength.
instructions: Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand pressed overhead.
Keep your eyes on the raised dumbbell and push your hip out to the side opposite the dumbbell.
Slowly bend at the waist and lower your free hand down your inner leg toward the ground.
Keep the dumbbell arm straight and perpendicular to the ground throughout the movement.
Return to the upright position by contracting your obliques and glutes.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.

name: dumbbell single leg & arm deadlift to upright row
description: The dumbbell single leg and arm deadlift to upright row is a unilateral compound exercise combining a single-leg hinge with a contralateral upright row. It targets the hamstrings and posterior chain while also building trap and shoulder strength, requiring balance and core stability throughout.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbell toward the floor, extending the free leg behind you.
Return to upright, then pull the dumbbell up along your body toward your chin.
Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position with control.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1561 - dumbbell single leg & arm deadlift to upright row.mp4

name: elevated kettlebell deadlift
description: The elevated kettlebell deadlift uses an elevated surface to increase the range of motion compared to a standard kettlebell deadlift. It targets the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back and core, making it effective for posterior chain development and hip hinge mechanics.
instructions: Place a kettlebell on elevated plates or a low platform and stand over it with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at the hips and grasp the kettlebell handle with both hands.
Keeping your back flat and chest up, drive through your heels to stand upright.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Lower the kettlebell back to the elevated surface with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1562 - elevated kettlebell deadlift.mp4

name: elevated kettlebell sumo deadlift
description: The elevated kettlebell sumo deadlift uses a wide stance and elevated surface to increase range of motion and target the glutes and inner thighs. It builds posterior chain strength with an emphasis on the glutes, adductors, and hamstrings.
instructions: Place a kettlebell on elevated plates or a low platform and stand over it in a wide sumo stance.
Turn your feet outward at approximately 45 degrees and hinge at the hips to grasp the kettlebell.
Keep your chest up, back flat, and knees tracking over your toes.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to stand upright.
Lower the kettlebell back to the elevated surface with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1563 - elevated kettlebell sumo deadlift.mp4

name: exercise ball chest press
description: The exercise ball chest press is performed on an unstable surface to increase core activation alongside traditional chest pressing. It targets the pectorals while requiring the abs and lower back to stabilize the body, making it an effective functional strength exercise.
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and walk your feet forward until your upper back is resting on the ball.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level with your feet flat on the ground.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining your hips in a raised bridge position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: exercise ball dumbbell press glute bridge
description: The exercise ball dumbbell press glute bridge combines a chest press with an isometric glute bridge on an exercise ball. It targets the pectorals and glutes simultaneously while also engaging the hamstrings and core for overall body stability and strength.
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and walk forward until your upper back rests on the ball.
Raise your hips into a glute bridge position and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest height.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Maintain the glute bridge throughout the entire set.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1565 - exercise ball dumbbell press glute bridge.mp4

name: exercise ball incline chest press
description: The exercise ball incline chest press uses the instability of an exercise ball to create an incline push-up angle that targets the upper pectorals. It also engages the core for balance and challenges the front delts and triceps as secondary muscles.
instructions: Place your feet on an exercise ball and your hands on the ground in a push-up position.
Walk your hands back toward the ball so your upper body is at an incline angle.
Lower your chest toward the ground by bending your elbows.
Push back up to the starting position while keeping your core tight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1566 - exercise ball incline chest press.mp4

name: exercise ball reverse superman
description: The exercise ball reverse superman targets the lower back and posterior chain by performing a back extension over an exercise ball. The unstable surface increases core activation, while the exercise primarily develops lower back strength and spinal erector endurance.
instructions: Lie face down with your hips on an exercise ball and your feet anchored against a wall or floor.
Place your hands behind your head or extend your arms in front of you.
Keeping your core engaged, lift your upper body and extend your back until your body is in a straight line.
Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lower back/strength/beginner/1567 - exercise ball reverse superman.mp4

name: exercise ball windmill
description: The exercise ball windmill is a rotational core exercise that targets the obliques by lowering the legs side to side while holding an exercise ball. The added weight and instability increase the demand on the obliques, lower back, and deep core stabilizers.
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms extended out to the sides and an exercise ball held between your feet.
Lift your legs to a 90-degree angle, squeezing the ball between your feet.
Slowly lower your legs to one side toward the ground, rotating at the hips.
Return to the center and lower to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: exercise oblique rotation exercise ball kettlebell
description: The exercise oblique rotation with exercise ball and kettlebell is a seated rotational exercise that targets the obliques and core. Performed on an unstable ball with a kettlebell for resistance, it develops rotational strength and spinal stability simultaneously.
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and hold a kettlebell with both hands at chest height.
Lean back slightly to engage your core and maintain a stable position on the ball.
Rotate your torso to one side, bringing the kettlebell toward your hip.
Return to center and rotate to the other side in a controlled manner.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/1569 - exercise oblique rotation exercise ball kettlebell.mp4

name: hex bar deadlift to shrug
description: The hex bar deadlift to shrug combines a deadlift with an end-range shrug to maximally target the traps and posterior chain. The hex bar reduces lower back stress compared to a straight bar, making it a safer option for developing the traps, hamstrings, and glutes together.
instructions: Step inside a hex bar with feet hip-width apart and grip the handles.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to lower to the bar, keeping your back flat.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, performing a deadlift.
At the top of the movement, perform a shrug by elevating your shoulders toward your ears.
Lower your shoulders and then lower the bar back to the ground with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/traps/strength/intermediate/1570 - hex bar deadlift to shrug.mp4

name: hex bar deadlift
description: The hex bar deadlift is a compound lower body and back exercise that uses a hexagonal bar to allow a more neutral grip and upright torso angle compared to a conventional barbell deadlift. It targets the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back, traps, and core.
instructions: Step inside a hex bar with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to grip the handles at your sides.
Keep your back flat, chest up, and shoulders retracted.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Lower the bar back to the ground with control, keeping your back flat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: icky shuffle ladder drill
description: The icky shuffle ladder drill is an agility footwork drill that targets the quads and hip abductors while developing coordination, balance, and lateral quickness. The alternating in-and-out pattern challenges neuromuscular control and lower body speed.
instructions: Set up an agility ladder flat on the ground and stand at one end.
Step your right foot into the first square, then your left foot, then step your right foot out to the right side of the ladder.
Step your left foot into the next square, then your right foot, then step your left foot out to the left side.
Continue down the ladder, alternating the outward step pattern with quick, light feet.
Repeat for the desired number of passes.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/cardio/intermediate/1572 - icky shuffle ladder drill.mp4

name: incline bench press alternate arm
description: The incline bench press alternate arm targets the upper chest by pressing one arm at a time on an incline bench. Alternating arms challenges core stability and anti-rotation strength while maintaining consistent upper chest and shoulder engagement.
instructions: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees and lie back holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest height.
Press one arm upward until fully extended while keeping the other dumbbell at chest level.
Lower the extended arm back to chest height, then press the opposite arm upward.
Alternate arms in a controlled manner for the desired number of repetitions.
Keep your core engaged and avoid rotating your torso throughout the movement.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1573 - incline bench press alternate arm.mp4

name: incline hammer press to hammer curl
description: The incline hammer press to hammer curl combines an incline neutral-grip chest press with a hammer curl, targeting the upper pectorals and biceps in one fluid sequence. The neutral grip reduces shoulder stress while adding biceps engagement at the end of each repetition.
instructions: Set a bench to a 30-45 degree incline and lie back holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip.
Press both dumbbells upward from your chest until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to your chest, then sit up slightly and perform a hammer curl by curling the dumbbells toward your shoulders.
Lower the dumbbells and return to the press starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1574 - incline hammer press to hammer curl.mp4

name: kettlebell elevated squat to front lunges
description: The kettlebell elevated squat to front lunges pairs heel-elevated goblet squats with alternating forward lunges for comprehensive quad and glute development. The heel elevation increases quad activation during the squat phase, while the lunge challenges single-leg strength and balance.
instructions: Stand with heels elevated on plates or a wedge, holding a kettlebell in a goblet grip at your chest.
Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Return to standing, then step one foot forward into a front lunge.
Lower your back knee toward the ground, then return to standing.
Alternate legs with each lunge and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1575 - kettlebell elevated squat to front lunges.mp4

name: kettlebell elevated squat
description: The kettlebell elevated squat uses heel elevation and a kettlebell in the goblet position to maximize quadriceps engagement and promote an upright torso. It is effective for building quad strength, improving squat depth, and developing core stability.
instructions: Stand with your heels elevated on plates or a wedge, holding a kettlebell in a goblet grip at your chest.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Keeping your chest up and elbows pointing down, lower your body by bending your knees.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1576 - kettlebell elevated squat.mp4

name: kettlebell goblet squat to front lunges
description: The kettlebell goblet squat to front lunges combines a goblet squat with alternating forward lunges to target the quadriceps and glutes. The goblet grip encourages an upright torso and engages the core throughout both movements.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a kettlebell in a goblet grip at your chest.
Lower into a goblet squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Return to standing, then step one foot forward into a lunge.
Lower your back knee toward the ground, then return to standing.
Alternate legs with each lunge and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1577 - kettlebell goblet squat to front lunges.mp4

name: kettlebell hold glute bridge with resistance band
description: The kettlebell hold glute bridge with resistance band adds a kettlebell for hip loading and a resistance band to increase hip abductor engagement during the bridge. It targets the glutes with secondary emphasis on the hamstrings and outer hips, making it an effective combined glute exercise.
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees and lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold a kettlebell on your hips with both hands.
Push your knees outward against the band and drive your hips upward by squeezing your glutes.
Hold at the top for a moment, then lower your hips back toward the ground with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1578 - kettlebell hold glute bridge with resistance band.mp4

name: kettlebell plyo deadlift to box jump
description: The kettlebell plyo deadlift to box jump pairs an explosive kettlebell deadlift with a box jump to develop posterior chain power and lower body explosiveness. It targets the hamstrings and glutes through the deadlift while the box jump builds plyometric power and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand with a kettlebell on the floor in front of a plyometric box.
Perform a kettlebell deadlift by hinging at the hips and driving upward explosively.
Set the kettlebell down and immediately transition into a box jump.
Explode upward and land softly on top of the box with both feet.
Step back down and repeat the sequence for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/plyometrics/advanced/1579 - kettlebell plyo deadlift to box jump.mp4

name: kettlebell pullover to glute raise
description: The kettlebell pullover to glute raise combines a lat-focused pullover with a glute bridge to target both the upper back and posterior chain in a single floor exercise. It develops lat flexibility and strength while also building glute and hamstring power.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a kettlebell with both hands above your chest.
Lower the kettlebell back over your head in a pullover motion until you feel a stretch in your lats.
Return the kettlebell above your chest, then simultaneously raise your hips off the ground into a glute bridge.
Lower your hips back to the ground, then repeat the pullover.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/intermediate/1580 - kettlebell pullover to glute raise.mp4

name: kettlebell staggered stance stiff deadlift
description: The kettlebell staggered stance stiff deadlift targets the hamstrings and glutes with a unilateral emphasis by splitting the stance to shift the load to the front leg. It builds posterior chain strength, improves hip hinge mechanics, and challenges core stability.
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance with one foot slightly ahead of the other, holding a kettlebell in each hand.
Keeping most of your weight on the front foot, hinge at the hips and lower the kettlebells toward the ground.
Keep your back flat and a slight bend in the front knee throughout.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching the staggered stance.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1581 - kettlebell staggered stance stiff deadlift.mp4

name: kettlebell walking lateral squat
description: The kettlebell walking lateral squat is a lateral lower body exercise that targets the quads and glutes while emphasizing the hip abductors and inner thighs. It improves frontal plane movement, lateral strength, and mobility in the hips and ankles.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a kettlebell at your chest in a goblet grip.
Step one foot out wide and lower into a lateral squat by shifting your weight over that foot.
Keep the other leg straight and both feet flat on the ground.
Drive through the squatting leg to return to standing, then bring your feet together.
Repeat the lateral squat to the same side for the desired reps, then switch sides.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1582 - kettlebell walking lateral squat.mp4

name: kettlebell reverse lunge alternating between leg
description: The kettlebell reverse lunge alternating between legs is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes. Alternating legs with each rep challenges balance and coordination while building single-leg strength with the added resistance of kettlebells.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a kettlebell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot back and lower your back knee toward the ground in a reverse lunge.
Keep your front knee directly over your ankle and your torso upright.
Drive through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1583 - kettlebell reverse lunge alternating between leg.mp4

name: kneeling ball slam
description: The kneeling ball slam is an upper body power exercise performed from a kneeling position, removing lower body involvement to isolate the shoulders, core, and lats. It develops explosive pressing strength and core stability with each slam.
instructions: Kneel on both knees on a mat with your hips stacked over your knees.
Hold a medicine ball or slam ball with both hands overhead.
Engage your core and forcefully slam the ball straight down in front of you.
Retrieve the ball and raise it back overhead.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kneeling hammer curl to hammer shoulder press
description: The kneeling hammer curl to hammer shoulder press is a compound arm and shoulder exercise that eliminates lower body momentum by removing the option to stand. It targets the biceps through the curl and the shoulders through the press, with core engagement required throughout.
instructions: Kneel on both knees with a dumbbell in each hand using a neutral grip, arms extended at your sides.
Curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height in a hammer curl motion.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, then lower to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1585 - kneeling hammer curl to hammer shoulder press.mp4

name: kneeling kettlebell around the world
description: The kneeling kettlebell around the world is a shoulder and core stability exercise that involves circling a kettlebell around the body while kneeling. It targets the deltoids and challenges the core to resist rotation with each pass, developing shoulder endurance and stability.
instructions: Kneel on both knees holding a kettlebell by the horns with both hands.
Begin passing the kettlebell around your body in a circular motion, transferring it from hand to hand.
Keep your core braced and avoid rotating your torso as the kettlebell moves.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Maintain upright posture throughout the movement.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1586 - kneeling kettlebell around the world.mp4

name: kneeling landmine oblique twist
description: The kneeling landmine oblique twist is a rotational core exercise that uses the arc of the landmine bar to challenge the obliques through a full range of motion. Performing the exercise kneeling eliminates lower body compensation and increases core activation.
instructions: Kneel in front of a landmine apparatus holding the barbell end with both hands at chest height.
Brace your core and rotate your torso to one side, lowering the bar toward the ground.
Return to the center and rotate to the opposite side in a controlled arc.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
Keep your hips square and only rotate through the thoracic spine and shoulders.

name: kneeling quad extension
description: The kneeling quad extension is an advanced bodyweight exercise that eccentrically loads the quadriceps by leaning backward from a kneeling position. It builds significant quad strength and is used as a progression toward exercises requiring knee flexion control.
instructions: Kneel on both knees on a mat with your body upright.
Slowly lean back by extending at the knee joint, lowering your torso toward the ground.
Keep your hips forward and your body in a straight line from head to knees.
Hold for a moment at the lowest comfortable point, then contract your quads to return to upright.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/1588 - kneeling quad extension.mp4

name: kneeling single arm arnold shoulder press
description: The kneeling single arm arnold shoulder press is a unilateral shoulder exercise that incorporates the rotational movement of the arnold press to target all three deltoid heads. Performing it kneeling removes lower body stability and increases core engagement.
instructions: Kneel on one knee with a dumbbell in the hand on the side of the raised knee.
Hold the dumbbell in front of your shoulder with your palm facing you and elbow bent.
Press the dumbbell upward while rotating your palm outward.
Fully extend your arm overhead with your palm facing forward.
Reverse the motion to return to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1589 - kneeling single arm arnold shoulder press.mp4

name: landmine sissy squat
description: The landmine sissy squat uses the landmine bar for balance assistance while targeting the quadriceps through a deep knee flexion squat pattern. It provides a controlled progression for developing quad strength and knee joint resilience.
instructions: Place a barbell in a landmine sleeve and stand holding the end of the bar in front of you.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and use the bar for balance.
Lean back slightly and bend your knees, allowing your body to lower toward the ground.
Keep your hips forward and back straight as you descend.
Return to the starting position by contracting your quadriceps.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1590 - landmine sissy squat.mp4

name: landmine kneeling hip thrust
description: The landmine kneeling hip thrust is a glute-focused exercise that uses the landmine to add resistance to a hip thrust performed from a kneeling position. It isolates the glutes and hamstrings while reducing lower back strain compared to a standard hip thrust.
instructions: Place a barbell in a landmine sleeve and kneel in front of it with the bar end positioned at your hips.
Sit back on your heels in a tall kneeling position with the bar resting at hip level.
Drive your hips forward explosively, pressing the bar upward through hip extension.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1591 - landmine kneeling hip thrust.mp4

name: landmine reverse lunges twist
description: The landmine reverse lunges twist combines a reverse lunge with a rotational core movement, targeting the quadriceps and glutes while adding oblique engagement. The landmine provides a guided arc for the twist, making it a functional movement for lower body and rotational strength.
instructions: Stand holding the end of a landmine barbell with both hands at chest height.
Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground.
At the bottom of the lunge, rotate your torso toward the front knee, bringing the bar to that side.
Return to center and drive through the front foot to stand.
Repeat on the other leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1592 - landmine reverse lunges twist.mp4

name: landmine sit ups
description: Landmine sit ups are a core exercise that uses the resistance of a landmine barbell to increase difficulty during the sit-up movement. They target the abdominals with secondary engagement of the hip flexors and shoulders throughout the controlled lowering and rising phases.
instructions: Anchor a barbell in a landmine sleeve and sit on the floor with knees bent and feet flat.
Hold the end of the bar with both hands and extend your arms forward at chest level.
Lower your torso toward the ground in a slow and controlled manner.
Return to the upright position by engaging your abs, pressing the bar forward.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: landmine squat to press
description: The landmine squat to press is a compound exercise that combines a squat with an overhead press using a landmine barbell. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while developing shoulder strength and core stability throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand holding the end of a landmine barbell with both hands at chest height.
Perform a squat by lowering your hips until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
As you drive upward from the squat, press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the bar back to chest height as you return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1594 - landmine squat to press.mp4

name: landmine stiff stance deadlift
description: The landmine stiff stance deadlift is a hip hinge exercise that uses the guided arc of a landmine barbell to target the hamstrings and posterior chain. The fixed range of motion makes it an accessible option for developing hip hinge mechanics and lower back strength.
instructions: Anchor a barbell in a landmine sleeve and stand facing away from the anchor with feet hip-width apart.
Hold the end of the bar with both hands behind you or from the side.
Hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, lowering the bar toward the ground.
Keep your back flat and core engaged throughout the movement.
Drive through your heels to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lateral burpee box step overs
description: The lateral burpee box step over is a high-intensity conditioning exercise that combines a burpee with a lateral movement over a plyo box. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while also challenging chest, shoulder, and core strength through the push-up component.
instructions: Stand beside a plyo box in a wide stance.
Place your hands on top of the box and jump your feet back into a plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet up and over the box to land on the other side.
Immediately place your hands on the box again and repeat the burpee in the opposite direction.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/1596 - lateral burpee box step overs.mp4

name: lateral shuffle battle rope
description: The lateral shuffle battle rope is a full-body conditioning exercise that combines lateral agility with upper body battle rope work. It targets the biceps and shoulders through the rope movement while engaging the quads and glutes for lateral locomotion and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance holding one end of a battle rope in each hand.
Begin alternating rope waves while simultaneously shuffling laterally in one direction.
After several steps, shuffle back in the opposite direction while continuing the rope waves.
Maintain a low athletic stance throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/cardio/intermediate/1597 - lateral shuffle battle rope.mp4

name: lateral walking lunges
description: Lateral walking lunges are a lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes through a side-to-side lunge pattern. They improve frontal plane strength, hip abductor activation, and dynamic balance.
instructions: Stand with feet together and hands on your hips or extended in front.
Step one foot out wide to the side and lower into a lateral lunge by shifting your weight over that foot.
Keep the opposite leg straight and both feet flat on the ground.
Push off the bent leg to bring your feet back together.
Repeat in the same direction for the desired steps, then switch sides.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1598 - lateral walking lunges.mp4

name: lunges alternate biceps curl
description: Lunges alternate biceps curl combines an alternating forward lunge with a simultaneous biceps curl to train the lower body and arms together. It targets the biceps while also developing quad, glute, and hamstring strength for an efficient compound exercise.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground.
As you lower into the lunge, curl both dumbbells toward your shoulders.
Drive through the front foot to return to standing and lower the dumbbells.
Repeat on the other leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1599 - lunges alternate biceps curl.mp4

name: med ball slam burpee
description: The med ball slam burpee combines an explosive medicine ball slam with a full burpee to create a demanding full-body conditioning exercise. It targets the quads and glutes through the burpee squat pattern while also developing shoulder power and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand holding a medicine ball with both hands overhead.
Slam the ball forcefully down to the ground, then immediately drop into a burpee.
Place your hands on the ground, jump your feet back, and perform a push-up.
Jump your feet forward, pick up the ball, and stand back up.
Raise the ball overhead and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/1600 - med ball slam burpee.mp4

name: med ball slam on floor in lunges
description: The med ball slam on floor in lunges combines a stationary lunge with an explosive medicine ball slam to challenge lower body strength and upper body power simultaneously. It targets the quadriceps and glutes through the lunge while developing shoulder explosiveness.
instructions: Stand in a lunge stance with one foot forward, holding a medicine ball overhead.
Lower into the lunge and simultaneously slam the medicine ball down to the ground.
Pick the ball up and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1601 - med ball slam on floor in lunges.mp4

name: med ball throw behind shoulder
description: The med ball throw behind shoulder is a rotational power exercise that targets the deltoids and traps through an explosive overhead throwing movement. It builds shoulder and upper back power while engaging the core for rotational strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a medicine ball in both hands at your chest.
Rotate your torso to one side and bring the ball beside your hip.
Explosively throw the ball back and over that shoulder toward a wall or partner.
Catch the ball on rebound or retrieve it and return to the starting position.
Repeat on the same side for the desired reps, then switch sides.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/plyometrics/intermediate/1602 - med ball throw behind shoulder.mp4

name: medicine ball squat throw
description: The medicine ball squat throw is a plyometric exercise combining a squat with an explosive vertical throw to develop lower body power and shoulder strength. It targets the quadriceps and glutes through the squat phase while the throw challenges the shoulders and engages the core.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a medicine ball at chest height.
Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Explosively stand up and throw the ball vertically as high as possible.
Catch the ball on its way down and immediately lower into the next squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1603 - medicine ball squat throw.mp4

name: medicine ball walking lunge twist
description: The medicine ball walking lunge twist combines forward lunges with a rotational core movement to target the quadriceps, glutes, and obliques. It builds functional lower body strength and rotational core power in a dynamic walking pattern.
instructions: Stand with feet together holding a medicine ball at chest height with arms extended.
Step one foot forward into a lunge and lower your back knee toward the ground.
At the bottom of the lunge, rotate your torso and the ball toward the front knee.
Return to center and step the back foot forward, entering the next lunge.
Continue walking forward, twisting at each lunge for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1604 - medicine ball walking lunge twist.mp4

name: plank ball slap
description: The plank ball slap is a core anti-rotation exercise that challenges the abdominals and obliques by requiring you to maintain a stable plank while reaching across the body to tap a medicine ball. It develops core stability, shoulder strength, and coordination.
instructions: Start in a plank position with your hands on the ground and a medicine ball placed just outside one hand.
Reach one hand across your body and tap or slap the ball, then return to the plank.
Alternate hands, tapping the ball from each side while maintaining a rigid plank position.
Avoid rotating your hips as you reach across.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank dumbbell drag
description: The plank dumbbell drag is a core stability exercise that challenges the abs and obliques to resist rotation while pulling a dumbbell from side to side. It develops anti-rotation strength, shoulder stability, and full core engagement.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell on the ground just outside one hand.
Reach the opposite hand under your torso and drag the dumbbell to the other side.
Return to the plank position and repeat with the other hand dragging the dumbbell back.
Maintain a rigid, level plank throughout the movement and avoid swaying your hips.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank in out
description: The plank in out is a dynamic core exercise that adds a jumping component to a standard plank by moving the feet in and out. It targets the abdominals and hip flexors while elevating the heart rate and improving core stability under movement.
instructions: Start in a forearm or high plank position with your core engaged and body in a straight line.
Jump both feet outward to a wide stance, then jump them back to the starting position.
Maintain a stable plank with your hips level throughout the movement.
Move at a consistent pace for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: plank pose sliding around the kettlebell
description: The plank pose sliding around the kettlebell is a dynamic core challenge that requires moving the entire body in a plank around a stationary kettlebell. It targets the abs and obliques while demanding significant shoulder stability and full-body coordination.
instructions: Place a kettlebell on the floor and start in a high plank position with the kettlebell to one side.
Move your body in a circular pattern around the kettlebell by stepping your hands and feet alternately.
Complete a full circle around the kettlebell while maintaining a plank.
Reverse direction and circle back to the starting position.
Continue for the desired number of circles.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1608 - plank pose sliding around the kettlebell.mp4

name: plank single arm dumbbell triceps
description: The plank single arm dumbbell triceps exercise combines a plank hold with a one-arm triceps kickback, targeting the triceps while requiring the core and shoulders to stabilize the plank position. It is an effective way to train the triceps with added core challenge.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with one hand on a dumbbell and the other flat on the ground.
From the plank, perform a triceps kickback by bending the elbow of the dumbbell arm to 90 degrees.
Extend the dumbbell back by straightening your elbow until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Slowly return the dumbbell to the bent position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching arms.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/1609 - plank single arm dumbbell triceps.mp4

name: plank t twist
description: The plank T twist is a rotational core exercise that targets the obliques by rotating from a plank position into a side plank T shape. It challenges spinal rotation, shoulder stability, and full core strength with each alternating twist.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Lift one hand off the ground and rotate your torso, opening your chest toward the ceiling.
Extend the raised arm straight up so your body forms a T shape.
Hold for a moment, then return to the plank and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plyo step up
description: The plyo step up is a plyometric lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes through explosive step-up movements on an elevated platform. It develops unilateral leg power, coordination, and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy plyo box or bench.
Step one foot onto the box and explosively drive through that heel to jump upward.
Switch feet in the air so the opposite foot lands on the box.
Immediately step or jump back down and repeat, alternating the leading foot.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1611 - plyo step up.mp4

name: plyometric push-ups
description: Plyometric push-ups are an explosive variation of the standard push-up that develops upper body power by requiring the hands to leave the ground. They target the pectorals with secondary emphasis on the triceps, front delts, and core through the explosive pressing motion.
instructions: Start in a standard push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest toward the ground, then explosively push yourself upward.
Generate enough force to lift your hands off the ground at the top of the movement.
Land with soft elbows and immediately lower into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band arms raise
description: The resistance band arms raise is a shoulder isolation exercise that targets the front and lateral deltoids using band resistance. It builds shoulder strength and endurance while providing a joint-friendly alternative to dumbbell raises.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, holding an end in each hand.
Keep your arms straight and raise them in front of you until they are parallel to the ground.
Hold briefly, then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band kettlebell swing
description: The resistance band kettlebell swing adds accommodating resistance to the traditional kettlebell swing, increasing peak load at the top of the movement. It targets the glutes and hamstrings through the hip hinge pattern while also engaging the shoulders, lower back, and core.
instructions: Attach a resistance band to a kettlebell and anchor the other end behind you.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hinge at the hips to grasp the kettlebell.
Drive your hips forward explosively to swing the kettlebell to shoulder height.
Allow the kettlebell to swing back down and hinge at the hips to absorb the load.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1614 - resistance band kettlebell swing.mp4

name: resistance band kneeling crunch
description: The resistance band kneeling crunch uses overhead band resistance to load the abdominals throughout the crunch motion. It targets the abs by requiring the core to flex against the constant tension of the band, making it more effective than a standard bodyweight crunch.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band overhead and kneel on a mat facing the anchor point.
Grasp the band with both hands behind your head.
Crunch your torso downward by flexing your spine, bringing your elbows toward your knees.
Hold for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1615 - resistance band kneeling crunch.mp4

name: resistance band row to triceps
description: The resistance band row to triceps is a compound pulling exercise that targets the lats through a rowing motion and adds a triceps extension at the end. It develops back width and arm strength while also engaging the rear delts and rhomboids.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at chest height and stand facing the anchor.
Grasp the band with both hands and step back to create tension.
Pull the band toward your torso in a rowing motion, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Extend your arms back into a triceps kickback at the end of the row.
Return to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/beginner/1616 - resistance band row to triceps.mp4

name: resistance band stepper in out jump
description: The resistance band stepper in out jump uses a resistance band above the knees to add hip abductor loading to an in-and-out jump drill on a stepper. It targets the quads and glutes while improving lateral hip strength and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees and stand on a low stepper or platform.
Jump both feet out to the sides of the stepper and back in, moving against the band tension.
Maintain a slight squat position throughout the movement.
Continue jumping in and out at a consistent tempo for the desired duration.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/cardio/intermediate/1617 - resistance band stepper in out jump.mp4

name: resistance band single leg glute
description: The resistance band single leg glute exercise isolates the glutes and hip abductors through a single-leg kickback pattern with band resistance. It targets the glutes while improving hip extension strength, balance, and stability of the standing leg.
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees and stand on one leg near a wall for balance.
Hinge slightly at the hips and kick the banded leg back and outward against the resistance.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Slowly return your leg to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1618 - resistance band single leg glute.mp4

name: reverse lunges slider lateral raise
description: The reverse lunges slider lateral raise pairs a reverse lunge with simultaneous lateral dumbbell raises to challenge the lower body and shoulders together. It targets the quads and glutes through the lunge while building shoulder strength and requiring core stability.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand, with sliders under your feet if available.
Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground.
As you lower into the lunge, raise both arms laterally to shoulder height.
Lower the arms as you drive back to standing.
Repeat on the other leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1619 - reverse lunges slider lateral raise.mp4

name: seated row machine
description: The seated row machine is a foundational back exercise targeting the lats and rhomboids through a horizontal pulling movement. It develops back width and thickness while also engaging the biceps and rear delts as secondary movers.
instructions: Sit at a cable row machine with your feet on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with an overhand or neutral grip and sit upright with your back straight.
Pull the handles toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the end of the row, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: single arm leg dumbbell deadlift to upright row
description: The single arm leg dumbbell deadlift to upright row is a unilateral compound exercise that combines a single-leg deadlift with an upright row. It targets the hamstrings and glutes through the hinge and the traps and shoulders through the row, all while demanding significant balance and core stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the hand on the same side.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbell toward the floor, extending the free leg behind you.
Return to upright, then pull the dumbbell up along your body to chin height.
Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1621 - single arm leg dumbbell deadlift to upright row.mp4

name: single leg & arm dumbbell shoulder press
description: The single leg and arm dumbbell shoulder press is a unilateral balance challenge that pairs a single-arm overhead press with a contralateral single-leg stance. It targets the deltoids while requiring significant core stability and balance to maintain position.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand at shoulder height.
Press the dumbbell overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder height with control.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs and arms.
Maintain balance on the standing leg throughout the movement.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1622 - single leg & arm dumbbell shoulder press.mp4

name: single leg box jump
description: The single leg box jump is an advanced plyometric exercise that develops unilateral lower body power by requiring an explosive jump from one leg onto a box. It targets the quads and glutes while also building ankle stability, coordination, and reactive strength.
instructions: Stand on one leg in front of a sturdy plyo box.
Bend your standing knee and swing your arms to generate momentum.
Explode off the single leg and land on top of the box with both feet.
Step back down carefully and reset on the same foot.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/1623 - single leg box jump.mp4

name: single leg dumbbell shoulder press
description: The single leg dumbbell shoulder press is a bilateral press performed while balancing on one leg to add a core and balance challenge. It targets the deltoids while requiring full-body stability, making it an effective functional shoulder strengthening exercise.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
Press both dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control.
Complete all repetitions while balancing on one leg before switching.
