Maintain a stable upright posture throughout the movement.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1624 - single leg dumbbell shoulder press.mp4

name: single leg glute raise holds & single arm dumbbell
description: The single leg glute raise holds and single arm dumbbell exercise combines a single-leg glute bridge hold with an upper body dumbbell movement to train the glutes and shoulders simultaneously. It targets the glutes through the bridge and challenges core stability throughout.
instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg extended, holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Drive the hips upward by contracting the glute of the bent leg into a single-leg bridge.
At the top, perform a dumbbell press or lateral raise with the arm holding the weight.
Lower the dumbbell and then lower your hips back to the ground.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1625 - single leg glute raise holds & single arm dumbbell.mp4

name: single leg glute raise holds with dumbbell chest press
description: The single leg glute raise holds with dumbbell chest press combines a single-leg glute bridge hold with a chest press to simultaneously develop the glutes and pectorals. The elevated hip position requires core and glute endurance throughout each pressing repetition.
instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent, the other leg extended, and a dumbbell in each hand.
Drive the hips upward into a single-leg glute bridge and hold the position.
With your hips elevated, press both dumbbells upward from your chest until arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to your chest and maintain the single-leg bridge throughout.
Complete the desired number of presses before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/chest/glutes/strength/intermediate/1626 - single leg glute raise holds with dumbbell chest press.mp4

name: single leg hop jump
description: The single leg hop jump is a plyometric exercise that develops unilateral lower body power, balance, and ankle stability by hopping on one leg. It targets the quadriceps and calves while building reactive strength and coordination in the standing leg.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other leg slightly bent and lifted.
Bend your standing knee slightly and hop forward, backward, or laterally on the single leg.
Land softly with a bent knee to absorb the impact.
Repeat the hop for the desired number of repetitions or directions.
Switch legs and repeat.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1627 - single leg hop jump.mp4

name: single leg ball slam
description: The single leg ball slam is a unilateral power exercise that combines the explosive force of a ball slam with a single-leg balance challenge. It targets the shoulders and core while requiring the standing leg and hip stabilizers to maintain balance throughout each slam.
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a medicine ball overhead with both hands.
Engage your core and forcefully slam the ball to the ground in front of you.
Maintain your balance on the single leg throughout the slam.
Retrieve the ball and raise it back overhead.
Complete all repetitions on one leg before switching.

name: slam ball hurdle squat
description: The slam ball hurdle squat combines a weighted squat with a lateral hurdle movement to challenge lower body power and coordination. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while the slam ball adds upper body load and the hurdle introduces agility demands.
instructions: Stand holding a slam ball at chest height with a hurdle or low obstacle in front of you.
Squat down and then explosively jump or step over the hurdle, maintaining control of the ball.
Land softly on the other side and squat again.
Jump or step back over the hurdle to return to the starting side.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/1629 - slam ball hurdle squat.mp4

name: sled drag
description: The sled drag is a strength and conditioning exercise that targets the quadriceps and lower body by pulling a weighted sled over a distance. It builds lower body power, muscular endurance, and cardiovascular fitness through resisted locomotion.
instructions: Load a weighted sled and attach a harness or rope to your body or hands.
Stand facing away from the sled and lean forward into the harness.
Drive your feet into the ground and walk or run forward, pulling the sled behind you.
Maintain a forward body lean and take short, powerful steps.
Continue for the desired distance.

name: slider split
description: The slider split is a unilateral lower body exercise that uses a slider disc to create a smooth split squat by sliding one foot backward. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while also stretching the hip flexors through a larger range of motion than a static split squat.
instructions: Stand with a slider disc under one foot and your feet together.
Slide the back foot backward as you lower into a split squat position.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes and your torso upright.
Pull the sliding foot back in by contracting your front leg to return to standing.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.

name: slider upright row
description: The slider upright row translates the traditional upright row into a plank-based movement using slider discs, targeting the deltoids and traps while requiring the core to stabilize. It is an effective and creative way to develop shoulder strength with added core engagement.
instructions: Place both hands on slider discs and start in a high plank position.
Pull both slider discs toward your chest simultaneously, bending your elbows outward as in an upright row.
Extend your arms back out to the starting plank position.
Maintain a rigid core and avoid sagging your hips throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: slow eccentric slider hamstring curl
description: The slow eccentric slider hamstring curl uses slider discs to eccentrically load the hamstrings by slowly extending the legs from a glute bridge position. The slow tempo during the lengthening phase maximizes hamstring strength and muscle development.
instructions: Lie on your back with your heels on slider discs and your arms flat on the ground.
Drive your hips upward into a bridge position by contracting your glutes.
Slowly slide both heels away from your body, extending your legs over a count of 3-5 seconds.
Once fully extended, use your hands to reset to the curled position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1633 - slow eccentric slider hamstring curl.mp4

name: stability ball deadbug
description: The stability ball deadbug is a core anti-extension exercise that uses an exercise ball between the hands and knees to create irradiation and maintain tension. It targets the abdominals while challenging the hip flexors and shoulders as opposite limbs are lowered in a controlled manner.
instructions: Lie on your back and hold an exercise ball between your hands and knees with your arms and hips at 90 degrees.
Brace your core and slowly lower one leg and the opposite arm toward the ground.
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor throughout the movement.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing dumbbell shoulder press
description: The standing dumbbell shoulder press is a fundamental overhead pressing exercise that targets all three heads of the deltoid. Standing rather than seated requires greater core activation, making it a functional movement for building shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward.
Brace your core and press both dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Avoid arching your lower back as you press.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing kettlebell around the world
description: Standing kettlebell around the world is a shoulder and core exercise that involves circling a kettlebell around the body while standing. It targets the deltoids and challenges the core to resist rotation with each pass of the bell.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a kettlebell by the horns with both hands.
Begin passing the kettlebell around your body in a circular motion, transferring it from hand to hand.
Keep your core engaged and your hips stable as the kettlebell moves around you.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Maintain upright posture and controlled breathing throughout.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/beginner/1636 - standing kettlebell around the world.mp4

name: stepper lateral shuffle
description: The stepper lateral shuffle is a cardio and agility drill that involves shuffling laterally over a stepper to improve lateral quickness and lower body conditioning. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while also developing hip abductor strength and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand to one side of a stepper or low platform in an athletic stance.
Shuffle laterally, stepping up onto the stepper with the lead foot and then bringing the trailing foot up.
Immediately step down on the opposite side and continue shuffling in the same direction.
Reverse direction and shuffle back across the stepper.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/cardio/beginner/1637 - stepper lateral shuffle.mp4

name: sumo kettlebell single arm upright row
description: The sumo kettlebell single arm upright row combines a sumo stance hip extension with a single-arm upright row, linking lower body drive with upper body pulling power. It targets the deltoids and traps while also engaging the quads and glutes through the wide-stance hip hinge.
instructions: Stand in a wide sumo stance with toes pointed outward, holding a kettlebell in one hand.
Lower into a slight sumo squat, allowing the kettlebell to hang in front of you.
Drive through your hips to stand while simultaneously pulling the kettlebell upward along your body to chin height.
Keep your elbow higher than your wrist throughout the row.
Lower the kettlebell and return to the sumo squat starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching hands.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1638 - sumo kettlebell single arm upright row.mp4

name: t-bar deadlift to row
description: The T-bar deadlift to row is a compound back exercise that combines a T-bar deadlift with a row to target the lats, rhomboids, and posterior chain. It efficiently develops back thickness and strength by pairing two pulling movements in a single fluid exercise.
instructions: Load a T-bar row machine and stand over the bar with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at the hips and grasp the handles, keeping your back flat.
Perform a deadlift by driving through your heels to stand upright.
At the top, row the bar toward your chest by driving your elbows back.
Lower the bar back toward your hips, then return to the deadlift starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/advanced/1639 - t-bar deadlift to row.mp4

name: trx band pistol squat
description: The TRX band pistol squat uses suspension straps for balance assistance to help athletes build toward the unassisted pistol squat. It targets the quadriceps and glutes while developing single-leg strength, stability, and mobility.
instructions: Hold a TRX band with both hands and stand on one leg, extending the other leg in front of you.
Lower your body into a single-leg squat by bending the standing knee.
Use the TRX for balance assistance, keeping your torso upright as you descend.
Drive through the standing heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1640 - trx band pistol squat.mp4

name: trx band seated in & out
description: The TRX band seated in and out is a core exercise that uses suspension straps to add instability and resistance to the in-and-out abdominal movement. It targets the abs and hip flexors while also requiring shoulder engagement to maintain a controlled position on the straps.
instructions: Sit facing the TRX anchor with your hands in the handles and lean back slightly.
Lift your feet off the ground and extend your legs outward, leaning back further.
Pull your knees toward your chest while bringing your torso upright.
Hold for a moment, then extend back out to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1641 - trx band seated in & out.mp4

name: trx band with glute raise curl
description: The TRX band with glute raise curl combines a glute bridge with a hamstring curl using suspension straps to challenge both muscle groups in a single movement. It targets the glutes through the bridge and the hamstrings through the curl, while the unstable straps increase core activation.
instructions: Lie on your back and place both heels in TRX straps with your legs extended.
Drive your hips upward into a glute bridge by pressing through your heels.
From the elevated position, curl both heels toward your glutes by bending your knees.
Extend your legs back to the straight position while keeping your hips elevated.
Lower your hips to the ground to complete the repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1642 - trx band with glute raise curl.mp4

name: trx kettlebell deadlift to curl
description: The TRX kettlebell deadlift to curl uses TRX straps for balance assistance while performing a deadlift-to-curl combination with a kettlebell. It targets the hamstrings and glutes through the deadlift and adds a biceps challenge through the curl.
instructions: Hold TRX handles for balance and stand with a kettlebell on the floor between your feet.
Hinge at the hips and grasp the kettlebell with both hands.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, performing a deadlift.
At the top, curl the kettlebell toward your chest by bending your elbows.
Lower the kettlebell and return to the starting deadlift position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1643 - trx kettlebell deadlift to curl.mp4

name: viking press trunk rotation
description: The viking press trunk rotation is a rotational pressing exercise that targets the obliques and core through a combination of upper body pressing and thoracic rotation. It develops rotational power and core stability while also engaging the shoulders and traps.
instructions: Stand at a viking press machine and grasp the handles at shoulder height.
Push the handles away from your body while rotating your torso in the direction of the press.
Return to the center and press in the opposite direction, rotating your torso the other way.
Maintain a stable lower body and only rotate through the trunk.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: vipr lateral shuffle
description: The ViPR lateral shuffle is a functional conditioning exercise that combines lateral shuffling with the added resistance of a ViPR tube. It targets cardiovascular fitness and lower body conditioning while also engaging the shoulders and core to stabilize the load during movement.
instructions: Hold a ViPR tube vertically or horizontally at chest level.
Begin shuffling laterally in one direction while maintaining the ViPR position.
After several steps, shuffle in the opposite direction.
Keep a low athletic stance and an engaged core throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/intermediate/1645 - vipr lateral shuffle.mp4

name: bird dog advanced (both hands raise)
description: The bird dog advanced (both hands raise) is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and core stabilizers. By simultaneously extending opposite limbs from the quadruped position, this advanced variation increases the balance and stability demand compared to the standard bird dog, developing spinal control and full-body coordination.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Brace your core and simultaneously extend both your right arm forward and left leg back.
Hold the extended position briefly, keeping your hips level and back flat.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side, extending your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a neutral spine throughout.
video_path: 130 Gym Workouts/back/lower back/strength/intermediate/1646 - bird dog advanced (both hands raise).mp4

name: band pushups
description: Band pushups are a resistance band variation of the standard pushup targeting the pectoral muscles. The band placed across the upper back adds progressive resistance at the top of the pressing movement, increasing muscle activation and overload compared to a bodyweight pushup alone.
instructions: Loop a resistance band across your upper back and hold one end in each hand, placing your hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you so your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows, keeping them at a 45-degree angle from your body.
Pause when your chest nearly touches the floor, then push back up against the band resistance.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: arm butterfly with bottle stretch
description: The arm butterfly with bottle stretch is a bodyweight exercise using bottles as light resistance. It targets the deltoids through a wide butterfly motion, warming up the shoulders and upper chest while improving range of motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a bottle in each hand at your sides.
Raise both arms out to the sides to shoulder height, palms facing down.
Slowly bring both arms forward and cross them in front of your chest in a butterfly motion.
Open the arms back out to the sides and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper arms/delts/stretching/beginner/0973 - arm butterfly with bottle stretch.mp4

name: arm stretch version 1
description: Arm stretch version 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary engagement of the deltoids and upper back. It involves an overhead elbow bend assisted by the opposite hand to maximize the stretch.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Raise one arm overhead and bend the elbow so your hand reaches toward the opposite shoulder blade.
Use your opposite hand to gently press the raised elbow downward, deepening the stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch arms.
Repeat on both sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: arm stretch version 2
description: Arm stretch version 2 is a bodyweight stretching exercise that targets the deltoids. It involves a horizontal cross-body arm pull to increase shoulder flexibility and relieve tension in the upper arms and upper back.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bring one arm straight across your chest at shoulder height.
Use the opposite hand or forearm to press the extended arm closer to your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull in the shoulder.
Switch arms and repeat on the other side.

name: ben knee v sit up beginner
description: The bent knee V sit-up beginner is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. It involves simultaneously raising the torso and bent knees to form a V shape, making it an accessible entry-level variation of the V sit-up.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms straight along your sides.
Engaging your core, simultaneously lift your upper body and bring your knees toward your chest.
Hold the V position for a moment, balancing on your tailbone.
Slowly lower your upper body and feet back to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0976 - ben knee v sit up beginner.mp4

name: bench dips upper arms
description: Bench dips is a bodyweight exercise primarily targeting the triceps, with secondary engagement of the chest and deltoids. It involves lowering and raising the body using a bench for support, making it an effective upper arm strengthening movement.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or sturdy chair with your hands gripping the edge beside your hips.
Slide your hips off the bench and extend your legs out in front of you.
Slowly lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach a 90-degree angle.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0977 - bench dips upper arms.mp4

name: bent knee glute bridge
description: The bent knee glute bridge is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes, with secondary activation of the hamstrings and lower back. It involves lying on your back and raising the hips to form a straight line, emphasizing hip extension and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms flat along your sides with palms facing down.
Pressing through your heels, lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold for a moment.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0978 - bent knee glute bridge.mp4

name: bent knee v sit up
description: The bent knee V sit-up is a bodyweight abdominal exercise that targets the abs by simultaneously raising the torso and bent knees to a balanced V shape. It develops core strength and hip flexor coordination.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead and knees bent.
In one motion, lift your torso and bring your knees toward your chest while reaching your hands forward.
Balance on your tailbone in the V position, keeping your core tight.
Hold briefly, then slowly lower both your torso and feet back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bent knee v sit up advanced
description: The bent knee V sit-up advanced is an intense bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. It builds on the standard variation by emphasizing explosive contraction and peak hold, demanding greater core strength and control.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Explosively contract your core to simultaneously raise your torso and bring your bent knees to your chest.
Reach your hands past your knees at the top, fully compressing the abs.
Hold the peak contraction for a moment before slowly lowering back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions with controlled movement throughout.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/advanced/0980 - bent knee v sit up advanced.mp4

name: bicycle jump rope
description: The bicycle jump rope is a cardio exercise combining jump rope with an alternating high-knee cycling motion. It elevates heart rate, improves coordination, and engages the core, quads, and calves simultaneously.
instructions: Stand with your feet together holding a jump rope handle in each hand.
Begin jumping rope and alternate lifting each knee in a cycling motion as if pedaling a bicycle.
Keep your core engaged and maintain a consistent rhythm with the rope.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with each rotation.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: bird dog hip stretch
description: The bird dog hip stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes, with secondary engagement of the lower back and abs. It involves extending opposite limbs from a tabletop position to improve balance, hip stability, and spinal alignment.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Simultaneously extend your right arm forward and your left leg straight back.
Hold the extended position for 2-3 seconds, keeping your hips square to the ground.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/0982 - bird dog hip stretch.mp4

name: bodyweight jump squat wide leg 1
description: The bodyweight jump squat wide leg 1 is a plyometric exercise targeting the quads, with secondary activation of the glutes, hamstrings, and calves. The wide stance emphasizes the inner thighs and increases the demand on the lower body during the explosive jump.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes turned slightly outward.
Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Explosively push through your heels to jump up off the ground.
Land softly with your knees slightly bent, absorbing the impact through your legs.
Immediately lower back into the squat and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/0983 - bodyweight jump squat wide leg 1.mp4

name: bodyweight squat with bottles hand
description: The bodyweight squat with bottles hand is a lower body exercise targeting the quads, using bottles as added resistance. The raised bottles engage the shoulders and improve overall coordination while performing the standard squat movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a bottle in each hand at your sides.
Raise the bottles to shoulder height as you begin to lower into the squat.
Bend your knees and push your hips back until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Press through your heels to return to the standing position, lowering the bottles back to your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0984 - bodyweight squat with bottles hand.mp4

name: bridge glutes with reverse crunch
description: The bridge glutes with reverse crunch is a bodyweight exercise combining a glute bridge and reverse crunch to target the glutes and abdominals simultaneously. It challenges posterior chain strength and core control in a single fluid movement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Press through your heels to lift your hips into a bridge position, squeezing your glutes at the top.
While holding the bridge, draw your knees toward your chest in a reverse crunch motion.
Extend your legs back to the bent position, then lower your hips back to the floor.
Repeat the combined movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bridge with reverse crunch
description: The bridge with reverse crunch is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abdominals while integrating a glute bridge. The combination movement promotes core stability, glute activation, and lower back engagement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lift your hips into a glute bridge and hold the position steady.
From the bridge, pull your knees in toward your chest while maintaining the elevated hips.
Slowly return your feet to the floor and lower your hips back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bulgarian split squat
description: The Bulgarian split squat is a bodyweight unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quads, with secondary activation of the glutes and hamstrings. Elevating the rear foot increases range of motion and places greater demand on the front leg.
instructions: Stand about two feet in front of a bench or elevated surface.
Place the top of your rear foot on the bench behind you.
Lower your body by bending your front knee until your rear knee nearly touches the ground.
Keep your torso upright and your front knee aligned with your toes throughout the movement.
Press through your front heel to return to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.

name: bulgarian split sumo squat
description: The Bulgarian split sumo squat is a bodyweight lower body exercise combining a split squat with a wide sumo stance. It targets the quads and glutes while increasing hip adductor engagement and improving hip mobility.
instructions: Stand in front of an elevated surface and place the top of your rear foot on it.
Position your front foot wider than shoulder-width and turn your toes outward in a sumo stance.
Lower your body into the squat, keeping your chest tall and front knee tracking over your toes.
Pause at the bottom when your rear knee nearly touches the floor.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position and repeat on both sides.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0988 - bulgarian split sumo squat.mp4

name: burpee
description: The burpee is a full-body bodyweight cardio exercise that combines a squat, plank, push-up, and jump into one continuous movement. It elevates heart rate rapidly and builds cardiovascular endurance, strength, and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Drop into a squat and place both hands on the floor in front of you.
Jump or step your feet back into a high plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump or step your feet forward toward your hands.
Explosively jump up with your arms overhead and land softly, then immediately repeat.

name: calf raise
description: The calf raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the calf muscles, particularly the gastrocnemius and soleus. It involves raising the heels off the ground in a standing position to build lower leg strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart near a wall or surface for balance if needed.
Press through the balls of your feet to rise up onto your toes as high as possible.
Hold the top position for a moment, squeezing the calves.
Slowly lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: calf raise walk
description: The calf raise walk is a bodyweight exercise targeting the calves that combines a sustained calf raise with forward walking. It develops calf endurance, ankle stability, and balance in a dynamic movement pattern.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Rise onto the balls of your feet and maintain that elevated position.
Begin walking forward slowly, keeping your heels raised with each step.
Engage your core for balance as you walk for the desired distance or duration.
Lower your heels to the floor and rest before repeating.

name: cat pose stretch
description: The cat pose stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the upper back. It involves rounding the spine from a tabletop position to release tension, improve spinal flexibility, and activate the core.
instructions: Begin on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Exhale and round your spine upward toward the ceiling, tucking your chin to your chest.
Hold the rounded position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the stretch across your upper back.
Slowly release back to a neutral spine position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cat pose stretch version 1
description: Cat pose stretch version 1 is a bodyweight spinal mobility exercise targeting the upper back. This variation focuses on a slower, more deliberate arch and hold to deepen the stretch through the thoracic spine.
instructions: Start on all fours in a tabletop position with a neutral spine.
Exhale and slowly arch your back upward, dropping your head and tailbone toward the floor.
Pause and hold the stretch for 3-5 seconds, focusing on the thoracic spine.
Inhale as you return to the neutral starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/0993 - cat pose stretch version 1.mp4

name: cat pose to forward fold
description: Cat pose to forward fold is a bodyweight mobility flow targeting the upper back and hamstrings. It transitions from the cat stretch into a forward fold to sequentially release the spine and posterior chain.
instructions: Begin on all fours in a tabletop position.
Round your spine upward into the cat pose, tucking your chin and tailbone.
From cat pose, push your hips back toward your heels into a child's pose or forward fold position.
Hold the forward fold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your back and hips.
Return to all fours and repeat the transition for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/0994 - cat pose to forward fold.mp4

name: cat pose upper back
description: The cat pose upper back is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the thoracic spine. It isolates the upper back rounding motion from a tabletop position to relieve tightness and improve thoracic mobility.
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Exhale and push your mid-back upward, focusing the rounding in the thoracic region.
Drop your head between your arms and hold for 3-5 seconds.
Slowly return to a flat back position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing each rep on the upper back.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/0995 - cat pose upper back.mp4

name: cat pose upper back 1
description: Cat pose upper back 1 is a bodyweight stretching variation emphasizing scapular separation and upper thoracic rounding. It targets the upper back and traps with a prolonged hold for deeper muscle release.
instructions: Begin on all fours in a neutral tabletop position.
Slowly round your upper back toward the ceiling while keeping your lower back relatively still.
Spread your shoulder blades apart to maximize the upper back stretch.
Hold the position for 5 seconds, breathing steadily.
Return to neutral and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/0996 - cat pose upper back 1.mp4

name: cat pose upper back advanced
description: Cat pose upper back advanced is a challenging bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the upper back and core. The advanced variation incorporates a deeper hold and active pressing to intensify spinal flexion and improve thoracic control.
instructions: Begin on all fours in a tabletop position.
As you round your upper back into cat pose, simultaneously tuck your chin deeply and draw your navel in.
Hold the peak contraction for 5-8 seconds, actively pressing the floor away with your hands.
Slowly return to neutral, then immediately flow into a deep arch for the cow pose.
Alternate between cat and cow for the desired number of repetitions, holding the cat position longer each round.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/advanced/0997 - cat pose upper back advanced.mp4

name: cat pose version 1
description: Cat pose version 1 is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the upper back with full spinal involvement. The version emphasizes simultaneous pelvic tuck and thoracic rounding for a comprehensive spinal flexion stretch.
instructions: Start on all fours with a neutral spine and hands beneath your shoulders.
Exhale slowly as you round your entire spine from neck to tailbone.
Draw your abs inward and tuck your pelvis under at the same time.
Hold the full spinal flexion for 3-5 seconds.
Inhale and slowly release back to a neutral position before repeating.

name: clap push ups
description: Clap push ups are an advanced plyometric bodyweight exercise targeting the chest. The explosive push-up followed by a mid-air hand clap develops upper body power, speed, and reactive strength.
instructions: Begin in a standard push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest toward the floor in a controlled manner.
Explosively push up with maximum force to lift your hands off the ground.
Clap your hands together quickly before placing them back on the floor.
Absorb the landing with slightly bent elbows and immediately lower into the next repetition.

name: close push ups
description: Close push ups are a bodyweight exercise that shifts the primary emphasis from the chest to the triceps by narrowing hand placement. They effectively build tricep strength and size alongside secondary chest and delt engagement.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor closer than shoulder-width apart, directly below your chest.
Extend your legs behind you into a plank position with your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Push through your palms to fully extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crossover high knee cardio
description: Crossover high knee cardio is a bodyweight cardio exercise combining high knees with a crossbody rotation. It elevates heart rate while engaging the core and hip flexors through the diagonal knee-to-elbow pattern.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to the sides.
Drive your right knee upward and across your body toward your left elbow simultaneously.
Return to the starting position and immediately drive your left knee toward your right elbow.
Continue alternating sides in a brisk, rhythmic motion.
Maintain an upright posture and keep your core engaged throughout.

name: decline close push ups
description: Decline close push ups are an advanced bodyweight exercise targeting the upper chest and triceps simultaneously. The elevated feet increase resistance on the upper pectoral region while the narrow hand placement intensifies tricep activation.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface such as a bench or step.
Position your hands on the floor closer than shoulder-width apart below your chest.
Keep your body in a straight diagonal line from heels to head.
Lower your chest toward the floor, keeping your elbows tucked close to your body.
Push explosively back to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions.

name: decline plank hold
description: The decline plank hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abs. Elevating the feet increases the angle and shifts greater load onto the upper abs and shoulders, making it a more challenging variation of the standard plank.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface such as a bench or step.
Place your hands on the floor directly under your shoulders.
Maintain a straight line from your heels to your head, engaging your core fully.
Hold the position without letting your hips sag or rise.
Breathe steadily and hold for the desired duration.

name: donkey kick bent
description: The donkey kick bent is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes with secondary activation of the hamstrings and lower back. Performed on all fours with a bent knee, it isolates the glute muscles through hip extension.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keeping your right knee bent at 90 degrees, lift your right foot toward the ceiling.
Drive your heel upward as high as possible while squeezing the glutes.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your knee back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching to the left leg.

name: donkey kick leg straight
description: The donkey kick leg straight is a bodyweight glute exercise performed on all fours with the leg fully extended. This variation increases the range of motion and the length-tension relationship of the glute muscles compared to the bent-knee version.
instructions: Begin on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Extend your right leg straight back so it is parallel to the floor.
Lift your straight leg upward toward the ceiling, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Lower the leg back to the parallel position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired repetitions before switching to the other leg.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1005 - donkey kick leg straight.mp4

name: donkey kick leg up
description: Donkey kick leg up is a bodyweight glute isolation exercise that combines a straight-leg kickback with a sustained top hold. The upward direction and isometric pause maximize glute activation and posterior chain endurance.
instructions: Start on all fours in a tabletop position.
Lift one knee off the floor and extend the leg up and back simultaneously.
At the top, hold the position for 2 seconds, feeling the glute fully contracted.
Lower the leg back toward the floor without letting the knee touch down.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1006 - donkey kick leg up.mp4

name: downward dog upper back stretch
description: The downward dog upper back stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the upper back. The inverted V position allows gravity to decompress the thoracic spine while simultaneously stretching the hamstrings, calves, and deltoids.
instructions: Begin on all fours in a tabletop position.
Tuck your toes under and lift your hips upward and back to form an inverted V shape.
Press your hands firmly into the floor and push your chest toward your thighs.
Hold the stretch, focusing on lengthening the upper back for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/1007 - downward dog upper back stretch.mp4

name: dynamic chest stretch
description: The dynamic chest stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. The rhythmic swinging motion warms up the chest and anterior deltoids by moving the shoulder joints through their full range of motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to your sides at shoulder height.
Swing both arms forward, crossing them in front of your chest in a controlled motion.
Immediately swing your arms back and out to the sides, opening the chest as wide as possible.
Continue the swinging motion rhythmically without pausing at either end.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: elbow hold side plank
description: The elbow hold side plank is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the obliques. Supporting the body on one forearm and foot challenges lateral core stability and strengthens the entire side of the trunk.
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs stacked and your body in a straight line.
Place your elbow directly beneath your shoulder and press your forearm into the floor.
Lift your hips off the ground so your body forms a straight diagonal line.
Hold the position with your core engaged and hips level.
Maintain the hold for the desired duration before switching sides.

name: elbow hold side plank with half hand raise
description: The elbow hold side plank with half hand raise is a bodyweight side plank variation that adds an arm raise to increase the demand on the obliques and shoulder stabilizers. The partial hold intensifies muscle engagement along the lateral core.
instructions: Set up in a side plank position on your forearm with your body in a straight line.
Raise your top arm straight toward the ceiling.
Slowly lower the raised arm halfway down and hold for 2-3 seconds.
Raise the arm back to the vertical position and repeat.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching to the other side.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/1010 - elbow hold side plank with half hand raise.mp4

name: elbow plank hold with hips dip
description: The elbow plank hold with hips dip is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the obliques. The lateral hip dip motion from a forearm plank position adds dynamic oblique engagement on top of the static core stability demand.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with elbows under shoulders and body in a straight line.
Keeping your feet in place, slowly rotate your hips and lower them to one side toward the floor.
Stop just before your hip touches the ground, then rotate and lower to the other side.
Continue alternating dips in a controlled, rhythmic manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/1011 - elbow plank hold with hips dip.mp4

name: elbow plank hold with hips up
description: The elbow plank hold with hips up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abs. It transitions from a flat forearm plank to a pike position, increasing spinal flexion demand on the abdominals and challenging shoulder stability.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with your elbows directly beneath your shoulders.
Keeping your legs straight, raise your hips upward toward the ceiling into an inverted V shape.
Hold the elevated hip position for 2-3 seconds.
Lower your hips back to the straight plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1012 - elbow plank hold with hips up.mp4

name: elbow plank to push ups
description: Elbow plank to push ups is a bodyweight compound exercise targeting the chest and core. It combines the stability demand of an elbow plank with the upper body strength challenge of a push-up, creating a dynamic full upper-body movement.
instructions: Start in a forearm plank position with elbows on the floor directly under your shoulders.
Press up onto your right hand, then your left hand, rising into a high plank position.
Perform one full push-up in the high plank position.
Lower back down to your right forearm, then your left forearm, returning to the elbow plank.
Alternate the leading arm each rep and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: forward lunges hold
description: Forward lunges hold is a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads. The isometric hold at the bottom of the lunge increases time under tension, improving quad and glute strength along with single-leg balance.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together and hands on your hips.
Step forward with your right foot and lower your body into a lunge until both knees are at 90 degrees.
Hold the bottom lunge position for 3-5 seconds, keeping your chest upright.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other leg and continue alternating for the desired repetitions.

name: glute bridge hold
description: The glute bridge hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the glutes. Sustaining the elevated hip position develops posterior chain endurance and deep glute strength while improving hip extension stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Press through your heels to raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Squeeze your glutes and hold the top position for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of holds.

name: glutes bridge lift the hip high
description: Glutes bridge lift the hip high is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes with an emphasis on achieving maximum hip extension. The exaggerated lift height and forceful glute squeeze intensify muscle activation compared to a standard glute bridge.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the ground, and arms at your sides.
Drive through your heels and lift your hips as high as possible, maximizing hip extension.
At the top, pause and forcefully squeeze the glutes for 2 seconds.
Lower your hips slowly in a controlled descent back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1016 - glutes bridge lift the hip high.mp4

name: good morning
description: The good morning is a bodyweight hip-hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings and lower back. It strengthens the posterior chain through a forward trunk lean while reinforcing proper hip hinge mechanics.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands placed behind your head.
With a soft bend in your knees and a flat back, hinge forward at the hips.
Lower your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Squeeze your glutes and drive your hips forward to return to the upright position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: good morning advanced
description: Good morning advanced is a deeper and more demanding variation of the good morning, targeting the hamstrings and lower back. The increased range of motion and pause at the bottom maximizes hamstring stretch and strengthens the spinal erectors.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hands behind your head.
Hinge aggressively forward at the hips, lowering your torso well past parallel to the floor.
Pause at the lowest point and hold for 2-3 seconds, feeling the deep hamstring stretch.
Drive your hips forward powerfully to return to the upright position.
Repeat with a controlled tempo for the desired number of repetitions.

name: half bent body rotate in circular
description: Half bent body rotate in circular is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the obliques. Performed in a half-bent position, the circular rotation of the torso engages the entire core and warms up the lumbar spine and obliques.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hinge forward at the hips about 45 degrees.
Extend both arms in front of you and clasp your hands together.
Begin rotating your torso and arms in a wide circular motion, leading with your hands.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Keep your hips stable and your knees softly bent throughout the movement.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/obliques/stretching/beginner/1019 - half bent body rotate in circular.mp4

name: head down on ground with knee bent
description: Head down on ground with knee bent is a bodyweight floor exercise targeting the glutes through lateral hip abduction from a prone position. The inclined upper body and bent knee create a unique angle for effective glute and hip activation.
instructions: Begin on all fours and lower your forearms to the floor, resting your forehead on the ground.
Keep one knee bent at 90 degrees and lift it out to the side in a hip abduction motion.
Pause at the top for 2 seconds, squeezing the glutes.
Lower the knee back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1020 - head down on ground with knee bent.mp4

name: high knee skip cardio fast
description: High knee skip cardio fast is a bodyweight cardio exercise combining skipping with exaggerated knee drives at a rapid pace. It elevates heart rate quickly and builds hip flexor strength, coordination, and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand upright with your arms relaxed at your sides.
Begin skipping forward, driving one knee up to hip height with each skip.
Pump your arms in opposition to your legs to maintain rhythm.
Increase the tempo to a fast pace, staying on the balls of your feet.
Continue for the desired distance or duration.

name: hindu push ups
description: Hindu push ups are a bodyweight compound movement that flows through a downward dog into an upward dog. The arcing motion targets the chest, triceps, and shoulders while mobilizing the spine and building full upper body strength.
instructions: Start in a downward dog position with hips high and hands and feet on the ground.
Swoop your head and chest forward and downward in an arc, moving toward the floor.
Continue the arc by pushing your chest through and up into an upward dog or cobra position.
Reverse the motion, pushing your hips back up to the starting downward dog position.
Repeat in a fluid, continuous motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hip circle
description: The hip circle is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the hip flexors and surrounding musculature. The circular hip rotation warms up the hip joints, improves pelvic mobility, and reduces stiffness in the lower back.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hands on your hips.
Begin rotating your hips in a wide, circular motion, moving clockwise.
Keep your upper body relatively still and let the movement come from your hips and pelvis.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse to counterclockwise.
Breathe steadily and increase the circle size progressively as you warm up.

name: hold bottle with toe raise
description: Hold bottle with toe raise is a bodyweight lower leg exercise using bottles for added load. The toe raise targets the tibialis anterior and calves through dorsiflexion, building lower leg balance and strength.
instructions: Stand upright holding a bottle in each hand at your sides for added resistance.
Keeping your legs straight, raise the toes of both feet off the ground, flexing the shins.
Hold the top position for 2 seconds, feeling the tibialis anterior and calf stretch.
Lower your toes back to the floor in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1024 - hold bottle with toe raise.mp4

name: incline push ups
description: Incline push ups are a beginner-friendly bodyweight chest exercise performed with hands elevated. The incline angle reduces the load compared to a standard push-up, targeting the lower chest while still engaging the triceps and deltoids.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface such as a bench, step, or wall.
Walk your feet back until your body forms a straight inclined line from heels to head.
Lower your chest toward the elevated surface by bending your elbows.
Push back up through your palms to fully extend your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: interlocked hand on her overhead hips bent
description: Interlocked hand on her overhead hips bent is a bodyweight stretch targeting the abs and obliques. The combination of overhead arm reach and hip hinge creates a full trunk stretch that decompresses the spine and engages the core.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and interlace your fingers overhead.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips.
Hold the bent hip position while reaching the interlocked hands as far overhead as possible.
Maintain the position for 20-30 seconds, engaging the core throughout.
Return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/stretching/beginner/1026 - interlocked hand on her overhead hips bent.mp4

name: interlock hand in front of chest hold
description: Interlock hand in front of chest hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the chest. Pressing the interlocked palms together activates the pectoral muscles without movement, building chest strength and endurance through sustained contraction.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Interlace your fingers in front of your chest with elbows bent at shoulder height.
Press your palms firmly together and engage your chest muscles isometrically.
Hold the contracted position for 10-15 seconds.
Release, rest briefly, and repeat for the desired number of holds.
video_path: 130 Workouts/chest/chest/strength/beginner/1027 - interlock hand in front of chest hold.mp4

name: interlocked hand in front of chest arm stretch
description: Interlocked hand in front of chest arm stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the chest and upper back. Extending the clasped hands forward with a rounded back creates a simultaneous pectoral and thoracic stretch.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and interlace your fingers in front of you.
Extend your arms straight forward at shoulder height with palms facing outward.
Round your upper back and push your hands away from your chest, feeling the stretch across the upper back.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/chest/chest/stretching/beginner/1028 - interlocked hand in front of chest arm stretch.mp4

name: jump for beginners
description: Jump for beginners is a bodyweight cardio exercise designed for those new to jumping movements. It introduces low-impact vertical jumping mechanics, improving cardiovascular fitness, calf strength, and landing technique.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Bend your knees slightly and swing your arms backward.
Push through both feet to jump a few inches off the ground.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with your knees slightly bent to absorb impact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions at a comfortable pace.

name: jump in air
description: Jump in air is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting cardio and lower body power. The maximal vertical jump develops explosive leg strength, coordination, and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and swing your arms back to prepare.
Explosively jump as high as possible, extending your hips, knees, and ankles fully.
Reach your arms overhead at the peak of the jump.
Land softly with bent knees and immediately reset for the next jump.

name: jump rope
description: Jump rope is a classic cardio exercise using a rope to train cardiovascular endurance, coordination, and calf strength. It is a high-efficiency exercise that burns calories and improves agility.
instructions: Hold the jump rope handles with one handle in each hand and the rope behind your heels.
Swing the rope up and over your head.
Jump with both feet together as the rope passes under your feet.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately repeat the swing.
Maintain a consistent rhythm and continue for the desired duration.

name: jump squat
description: The jump squat is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the quads and glutes. The explosive squat-to-jump movement builds lower body power, burns calories, and improves athletic performance.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Explosively drive through your heels to jump off the ground.
Land softly with your knees slightly bent, immediately sinking back into the squat position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kneeling side push-up stretch
description: The kneeling side push-up stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the chest. Performed in a kneeling position, the lateral lean creates a unilateral chest and shoulder stretch while the push-up element adds mild strength engagement.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and place one hand on the floor beside you at shoulder height.
Lean your body toward the hand on the floor, bending that elbow to lower your shoulder.
Feel the stretch across the chest of the opposite side.
Slowly push back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: l sit hold with leg bent close
description: The L sit hold with leg bent close is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. Lifting the hips and bent legs off the floor requires significant core and upper body strength to sustain the hold.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs bent and feet close to your hips.
Place your hands flat on the floor beside your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Press into your hands and engage your core to lift your hips and feet off the ground.
Hold the suspended position with your bent knees close to your chest.
Maintain the hold for the desired duration, keeping your arms straight.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1034 - l sit hold with leg bent close.mp4

name: l sit ups
description: L sit ups are a bodyweight abdominal exercise that involves simultaneously lifting the torso and straight legs to meet in the middle. This challenging movement maximizes rectus abdominis contraction and hip flexor strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs straight and arms extended overhead.
Simultaneously raise both straight legs and your torso toward each other.
Reach your hands toward your feet at the top, forming an L or V shape.
Hold the peak position for a moment, then slowly lower both your legs and torso.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: leg raise thigh stretch
description: Leg raise thigh stretch is a bodyweight standing exercise targeting the quads and hip flexors. Holding the raised straight leg challenges balance while providing a dynamic stretch along the front of the thigh.
instructions: Stand upright next to a wall or support for balance.
Raise one leg in front of you to hip height, keeping it straight.
Hold the raised leg position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the stretch in the quads and hip flexors.
Lower the leg slowly and switch sides.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each leg.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/1036 - leg raise thigh stretch.mp4

name: lying back and hips stretch
description: The lying back and hips stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back and hips. Pulling both knees to the chest from a supine position gently decompresses the lumbar spine and releases tension in the glutes and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended.
Draw both knees to your chest and wrap your arms around them.
Gently pull your knees closer to your chest, feeling the stretch in your lower back and hips.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Release and slowly lower your legs back to the floor.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/1037 - lying back and hips stretch.mp4

name: lying single bent knee hold stretch
description: The lying single bent knee hold stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise targeting the glutes and hip flexors. The single-leg pull toward the chest isolates the stretch to one side at a time, allowing greater control and depth.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended.
Bend one knee and draw it toward your chest, holding it with both hands.
Gently pull the knee closer until you feel a stretch in your glute and outer hip.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1038 - lying single bent knee hold stretch.mp4

name: lying straight leg up hold stretch
description: Lying straight leg up hold stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings. Raising a straight leg while lying on your back provides a passive hamstring stretch that improves posterior chain flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended on the floor.
Raise one straight leg toward the ceiling, keeping the knee locked.
Hold the leg at the point of comfortable tension for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly lower the leg back to the floor.
Repeat on the other side for the desired number of holds.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1039 - lying straight leg up hold stretch.mp4

name: lying with leg raise straight
description: Lying with leg raise straight is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. Raising and lowering both straight legs simultaneously creates constant abdominal tension and builds lower core strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended and hands placed beneath your lower back for support.
Keeping both legs straight, raise them together to a 90-degree angle with the floor.
Slowly lower your legs back down without letting them touch the floor.
Pause just above the ground, then raise them again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: mountain climber
description: Mountain climbers are a bodyweight exercise targeting the abs through dynamic knee drives from a plank position. They combine core stability with a cardio element, engaging the hip flexors, quads, and upper body.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
Drive your right knee toward your chest, keeping your hips level.
Quickly switch legs, extending the right leg back while driving the left knee forward.
Continue alternating in a running motion as quickly as possible.
Maintain a straight body line and keep your core engaged throughout.

name: mountain climber vertical
description: Mountain climber vertical is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abs in a standing incline position against a wall. The vertical variation reduces wrist pressure while maintaining the hip flexor and core engagement of the traditional mountain climber.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place your hands on it at shoulder height, arms fully extended.
Step your feet back until your body is in a slight incline, similar to a standing plank.
Drive one knee upward toward your chest while keeping the other foot planted.
Quickly alternate legs in a running motion against the wall.
Continue for the desired duration while keeping your core tight.

name: one leg balance
description: One leg balance is a bodyweight exercise targeting the calves and lower leg stabilizers. Balancing on a single leg challenges proprioception, ankle stability, and neuromuscular coordination throughout the leg and core.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together.
Shift your weight onto one foot and slowly lift the opposite foot off the ground.
Balance on the standing leg with a slight bend in the knee.
Hold the balance position for 20-30 seconds, keeping your core engaged.
Lower the raised foot and repeat on the other side.

name: pike push ups
description: Pike push ups are a bodyweight shoulder exercise performed in a downward dog position. The elevated hip angle shifts load from the chest to the deltoids, making it an effective progression toward handstand push-ups.
instructions: Begin in a downward dog position with your hips raised and body forming an inverted V.
Position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your elbows and lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank downward dog
description: Plank downward dog is a bodyweight exercise that alternates between a high plank and a downward dog. It targets the core in the plank phase and stretches the posterior chain in the downward dog phase, building both strength and mobility.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands beneath your shoulders.
Push your hips upward and back to transition into a downward dog position.
Hold the downward dog for 2-3 seconds, pressing your chest toward your thighs.
Return your hips back down to the high plank position.
Repeat the transition for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank hold with hips twist
description: Plank hold with hips twist is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the obliques. The lateral hip rotation from a forearm plank challenges rotational core strength while maintaining overall plank stability.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Keeping your shoulders square, rotate your hips and lower them to one side toward the floor.
Stop before your hip touches the ground, then rotate to the opposite side.
Continue alternating twists in a controlled and rhythmic manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank with hand and leg lift
description: Plank with hand and leg lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abs and lower back. Extending opposite limbs from a high plank demands anti-rotational core stability and challenges balance and coordination.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Simultaneously raise your right hand forward and your left leg straight back.
Hold the extended position for 2 seconds, keeping your hips level.
Lower and repeat on the opposite side, raising your left hand and right leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1047 - plank with hand and leg lift.mp4

name: plank with hand and leg up
description: Plank with hand and leg up is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. Raising the same-side arm and leg simultaneously challenges lateral stability and demands greater balance and core control than standard plank variations.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under your shoulders.
Raise your right arm straight up toward the ceiling while simultaneously raising your right leg.
Hold the position briefly with your arm and same-side leg elevated.
Return to the plank and repeat on the left side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1048 - plank with hand and leg up.mp4

name: plank with hips twist
description: Plank with hips twist is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the obliques. Performed from a high plank, the alternating hip rotations add dynamic oblique engagement while maintaining upper body stability.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and body straight.
Rotate your hips to the right, dropping your right hip toward the floor.
Return to the center plank position and rotate to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank with one hand lift
description: Plank with one hand lift is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and obliques. Removing one hand from the ground forces the core to resist rotation and maintain spinal alignment with greater anti-rotational strength.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands directly beneath your shoulders.
Shift your weight onto your left hand and lift your right hand off the ground.
Hold the single-arm plank position for 2-3 seconds while keeping your hips level.
Place the right hand back down and repeat on the left side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank with opposite hand leg lift
description: Plank with opposite hand leg lift is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. Extending diagonally opposite limbs from a high plank challenges anti-rotational stability and strengthens the entire posterior chain and core.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands beneath your shoulders.
Lift your right hand forward and your left leg back simultaneously, keeping both aligned with your torso.
Hold for 2 seconds, then return both limbs to the floor.
Repeat on the opposite side, lifting your left hand and right leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1051 - plank with opposite hand leg lift.mp4

name: prisoner hip stretch
description: The prisoner hip stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hip flexors. The kneeling lunge position combined with hands behind the head and upright torso promotes full hip flexor elongation and thoracic extension.
instructions: Kneel on your right knee with your left foot forward, in a lunge position.
Place your hands behind your head in a prisoner position with elbows wide.
Drive your hips forward to feel the stretch in your right hip flexor.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, keeping your torso upright.
Switch legs and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/1052 - prisoner hip stretch.mp4

name: prisoner one leg twist
description: Prisoner one leg twist is a bodyweight balance and core exercise targeting the obliques. Combining a single-leg stance with rotational trunk movements challenges balance, spinal rotation, and lateral core strength.
instructions: Stand on one leg with your hands clasped behind your head in a prisoner position.
Slowly rotate your torso to the right, bringing your right elbow back.
Hold the rotated position for 2 seconds, then rotate to the left.
Continue alternating twists while maintaining your balance on one leg.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching the standing leg.

name: push ups
description: Push ups are a fundamental bodyweight exercise targeting the chest. They build upper body strength and endurance through a pressing motion, with secondary engagement of the triceps, deltoids, and core for stability.
instructions: Place your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width on the floor.
Extend your legs behind you into a plank position, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest to the floor by bending your elbows at roughly a 45-degree angle to your body.
Press through your palms to fully extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: push up (chest touch on floor)
description: Push up with chest touch on floor is a bodyweight exercise that takes the push-up through its complete range of motion by touching the chest to the floor at the bottom. The full range of motion maximizes chest muscle engagement and builds greater pectoral strength.
instructions: Begin in a standard push-up position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your body slowly until your chest makes full contact with the floor.
Pause briefly with your chest on the ground, keeping your elbows at your sides.
Push explosively through your palms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: quadriceps hold stretch
description: The quadriceps hold stretch is a bodyweight standing stretch targeting the quads and hip flexors. Pulling the heel toward the glutes elongates the rectus femoris and other quad muscles, improving front-of-thigh flexibility.
instructions: Stand upright on one leg, using a wall or surface for balance if needed.
Bend the opposite knee and grasp the ankle with your hand behind you.
Pull the heel toward your glutes until you feel a stretch along the front of the thigh.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other side.

name: quadriceps stretch version 1
description: Quadriceps stretch version 1 is a bodyweight kneeling quad stretch targeting the quads and hip flexors. The half-kneeling position provides stability while allowing a deep stretch in the front of the thigh.
instructions: Kneel on one knee with the other foot forward in a half-kneeling position.
Reach back with your hand and grasp the ankle of the kneeling leg.
Pull the foot gently toward your glutes, stretching the quad of the kneeling leg.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds while keeping your torso upright.
Release and repeat on the other side.

name: reverse bridge pose ii
description: Reverse bridge pose II is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and glutes. By lifting the hips from a seated reverse position, it opens the chest, stretches the front of the body, and strengthens the posterior chain.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and hands placed on the floor behind your hips, fingers pointing away.
Press into your palms and heels to lift your hips off the ground as high as possible.
Squeeze your glutes and let your head drop back gently.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/1058 - reverse bridge pose ii.mp4

name: reverse crunch
description: The reverse crunch is a bodyweight abdominal exercise targeting the lower abs. By curling the hips off the floor rather than the torso, it isolates the lower portion of the rectus abdominis with minimal lower back strain.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides and legs raised to a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and curl your hips off the floor, drawing your knees toward your chest.
Pause at the top, feeling the lower abs contract.
Slowly lower your hips and legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse crunch advanced
description: Reverse crunch advanced is an intense bodyweight core exercise targeting the lower abs. The extended leg variation at the top of the movement increases the difficulty and maximizes lower abdominal contraction.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms at your sides and legs raised to 90 degrees.
Explosively curl your hips upward, extending your legs toward the ceiling at the peak.
Hold the fully extended position for a moment, balancing on your upper back.
Slowly lower your hips and bent knees back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions with full control.

name: reverse crunch advanced with straight leg
description: Reverse crunch advanced with straight leg is a demanding bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. Keeping the legs straight throughout the movement significantly increases the lever arm, intensifying the challenge on the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms at your sides and legs straight, raised slightly off the floor.
Without bending your knees, curl your hips off the floor and raise your straight legs toward the ceiling.
Continue lifting until your legs are perpendicular to the floor or beyond.
Hold briefly, then slowly lower your straight legs back to the starting position without touching the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/advanced/1061 - reverse crunch advanced with straight leg.mp4

name: reverse lunges
description: Reverse lunges are a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads. Stepping backward reduces knee stress compared to forward lunges while effectively targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together and hands on your hips.
Step one foot backward and lower your rear knee toward the floor.
Stop when both knees are at approximately 90-degree angles.
Push through your front heel to return to the standing position.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: running
description: Running is a bodyweight cardio exercise that targets cardiovascular endurance and the muscles of the lower body. It is one of the most fundamental and effective exercises for building aerobic fitness and burning calories.
instructions: Stand upright with a slight forward lean from the ankles, not the waist.
Begin by jogging at a comfortable pace, landing midfoot with each step.
Pump your arms forward and back in opposition to your legs.
Gradually increase your pace to the desired running speed.
