Continue for the desired distance or duration, maintaining a steady breathing pattern.

name: russian twist
description: The Russian twist is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the obliques. Rotating the torso from side to side in a reclined seated position challenges the lateral core muscles and improves rotational strength and stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean back slightly so your torso is at a 45-degree angle to the floor.
Clasp your hands together in front of your chest.
Rotate your torso to the right as far as comfortable, then rotate to the left.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: russian twist version 1
description: Russian twist version 1 is a more challenging bodyweight oblique exercise with feet elevated off the floor. Removing foot support increases the balance demand and intensifies core activation compared to the standard version.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet raised slightly off the ground.
Lean back to a 45-degree angle and clasp your hands together.
Rotate your torso to one side and tap your clasped hands to the floor beside your hip.
Rotate to the other side and repeat the tap.
Continue alternating sides, keeping your feet off the ground throughout.

name: scissor kick
description: Scissor kicks are a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. The alternating leg crossing motion creates continuous tension in the lower abdomen and improves core endurance.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed beneath your lower back for support.
Raise both legs off the ground to about a 45-degree angle.
Alternate raising and lowering each leg in a scissoring motion, passing them over each other.
Keep your legs straight and your lower back pressed into your hands throughout.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: scissor kick advanced
description: Scissor kick advanced is an intense bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. The wider range of motion and added crunch element increase the challenge and activate the obliques in addition to the lower abs.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with hands at your sides and legs fully extended.
Raise both legs off the floor by a few inches, keeping them straight.
Perform a large scissoring motion, raising one leg high while the other stays low.
Alternate quickly and add a slight torso crunch on each crossover.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions without letting your legs touch the floor.

name: scissor kick ii
description: Scissor kick II is a bodyweight core variation targeting the abs with legs starting from vertical. The vertical starting position reduces hip flexor involvement and places greater emphasis on lower abdominal contraction.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise both legs to a 90-degree angle.
Lower one leg toward the floor while keeping the other vertical.
Switch legs before the lowered leg touches the floor.
Continue alternating in a controlled scissoring pattern.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated abductor flex
description: The seated abductor flex is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the glutes and hip abductors. Sitting with knees bent and pressing them outward against resistance builds hip abductor strength and glute activation.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Engage your core and sit tall with your hands placed on the floor beside you for support.
Push both knees outward against imaginary resistance, activating the hip abductors.
Hold the abducted position for 5-10 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated abductor hip lift
description: Seated abductor hip lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hip abductors. Lifting the knee outward from a seated position isolates the abductor muscles, making it an accessible exercise for hip strengthening and rehabilitation.
instructions: Sit upright on the edge of a chair or bench with feet flat on the floor and hands on your thighs.
Lift one foot slightly off the ground and move the knee outward in a hip abduction motion.
Hold the abducted position for 2-3 seconds.
Return the foot to the floor and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/rehabilitation/beginner/1070 - seated abductor hip lift.mp4

name: seated ankle stretch
description: The seated ankle stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the calves and ankle mobility. The combination of pointing, flexing, and rotating the foot improves ankle range of motion and relieves calf tightness.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
Point your toes forward as far as possible, holding for 2-3 seconds.
Flex your feet back toward your shins, pulling your toes up, and hold for 2-3 seconds.
Rotate your ankles in circles, completing the desired number in each direction.
Repeat the point-flex-circle sequence for the desired duration.

name: seated bent knee forward fold (head to floor)
description: Seated bent knee forward fold (head to floor) is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings and lower back. The deep forward fold with bent knees allows the spine to decompress and the posterior chain to relax into a deep stretch.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean forward from the hips, reaching your torso toward your thighs.
Continue folding forward until your head reaches toward the floor in front of your feet.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Slowly roll up to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1072 - seated bent knee forward fold (head to floor).mp4

name: seated bent knee forward fold glutes
description: Seated bent knee forward fold glutes is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the glutes. The figure-four leg position during the forward fold isolates the piriformis and gluteal muscles for a deep hip and glute release.
instructions: Sit on the floor and cross one ankle over the opposite knee in a figure-four position.
Flex the foot of the crossed leg to protect the knee.
Lean forward gently from the hips until you feel a deep stretch in the outer glute of the crossed leg.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides.
Repeat on each side for the desired number of holds.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1073 - seated bent knee forward fold glutes.mp4

name: seated butterfly open stretch
description: The seated butterfly open stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hip flexors and inner thighs. Pressing the knees toward the floor from a butterfly position improves hip external rotation and groin flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together, letting your knees fall outward.
Hold your feet with both hands and sit as tall as possible.
Gently press your knees downward toward the floor using your elbows.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the pressure and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/1074 - seated butterfly open stretch.mp4

name: seated forward fold
description: Seated forward fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings. Folding the torso over straight legs from a seated position provides a deep stretch along the entire posterior chain, from hamstrings to calves.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended straight in front of you.
Sit tall and reach your arms forward toward your feet.
Hinge from the hips and fold your torso forward as far as comfortable.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Slowly return to the upright seated position and repeat.

name: seated forward fold with bent knees
description: Seated forward fold with bent knees is a bodyweight stretch targeting the lower back. Bending the knees reduces hamstring tension and allows a greater focus on releasing the lumbar spine and glutes in the folded position.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs slightly bent in front of you.
Reach your arms forward and fold your torso toward your thighs.
Allow your back to round gently and your head to drop.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the release in the lower back.
Slowly return upright and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/lower back/stretching/beginner/1076 - seated forward fold with bent knees.mp4

name: seated hamstring stretch (one leg bent inward)
description: The seated hamstring stretch (one leg bent inward) is a bodyweight flexibility exercise targeting the hamstrings. The single-leg focus allows a deeper and more targeted stretch on one hamstring at a time.
instructions: Sit on the floor and extend one leg straight while bending the other so the foot rests against the inner thigh of the extended leg.
Sit tall and reach both hands toward the foot of the extended leg.
Fold forward gently from the hips until you feel the stretch in the hamstring of the extended leg.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs.
Repeat on each side for the desired number of holds.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1077 - seated hamstring stretch (one leg bent inward).mp4

name: seated relax with folded leg
description: Seated relax with folded leg is a bodyweight restorative stretch targeting the glutes and hip flexors. The relaxed seated posture with folded legs gently opens the hips and relieves tightness in the glutes and outer hip.
instructions: Sit on the floor and fold one leg in front of you, bending the knee at a comfortable angle.
Allow the other leg to relax beside you or fold it in the opposite direction.
Sit upright and rest your hands on your knees.
Breathe deeply and hold for 30-60 seconds, releasing tension in the hips and glutes.
Switch the position of the folded legs and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1078 - seated relax with folded leg.mp4

name: seated side reach stretch
description: Seated side reach stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the obliques and lateral torso. The seated overhead side lean provides a deep stretch along the intercostals, obliques, and lateral back.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs crossed or extended in front of you.
Raise your right arm overhead and lean your torso to the left.
Reach the right arm over and feel the stretch along the right side of the torso.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides.
Repeat on each side for the desired number of holds.

name: seated with leg raise balance hold
description: Seated with leg raise balance hold is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. Raising and holding one leg while in a reclined seated position challenges balance and maintains continuous tension in the abdominals and hip flexors.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean back slightly and raise one leg, extending it straight out in front of you.
Hold the leg raise position while balancing on your tailbone.
Keep your core tight and hold for the desired duration.
Switch legs and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/balance/beginner/1080 - seated with leg raise balance hold.mp4

name: side lying leg raise
description: Side lying leg raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hip abductors. Raising the top leg against gravity while lying on your side isolates the gluteus medius, improving hip stability and lateral strength.
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs stacked and your body in a straight line.
Rest your head on your lower arm and place your top hand on the floor in front for support.
Keeping your top leg straight, raise it toward the ceiling as high as comfortable.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your leg back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching to the other side.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1081 - side lying leg raise.mp4

name: single leg glute bridge
description: The single leg glute bridge is a unilateral bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes. Performing the bridge on one leg increases the demand on the supporting glute, hamstring, and core for greater strength and stability development.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend one leg straight out, keeping your thighs parallel.
Press through the heel of the planted foot to lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulder to knee.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold for a moment.
Lower your hips back down and complete the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1082 - single leg glute bridge.mp4

name: single leg squat with wall support
description: Single leg squat with wall support is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the quads. The wall provides assistance for balance during the single-leg squat, making it an accessible progression toward unsupported pistol squats.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your fingertips resting lightly on it for support.
Lift one foot off the ground and hold it in front of you.
Slowly bend the standing leg and lower into a single-leg squat.
Descend as far as comfortable while keeping your heel flat and your knee tracking over your toes.
Push through your heel to return to standing and repeat before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1083 - single leg squat with wall support.mp4

name: sit ups
description: Sit ups are a classic bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. The full curl from flat to upright builds core strength and endurance, engaging both the upper and lower abs along with the hip flexors.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands behind your head.
Engage your core and curl your torso upward toward your knees.
Continue rising until your elbows nearly reach your thighs.
Slowly lower your torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: sit ups + twist sit ups
description: Sit ups + twist sit ups is a bodyweight compound core exercise combining a full sit-up with an alternating trunk rotation. The added twist recruits the obliques in addition to the rectus abdominis for complete abdominal training.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat, and hands behind your head.
Curl up into a full sit-up position.
At the top, rotate your torso to the right, bringing your left elbow toward your right knee.
Return to center, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Alternate the twist direction each rep and continue for the desired repetitions.

name: sit ups half
description: Sit ups half is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. By stopping the sit-up at the halfway point, the movement keeps constant tension on the abdominals and reduces hip flexor involvement compared to a full sit-up.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head with elbows pointing outward.
Curl your upper body up to a 45-degree angle, pausing before reaching vertical.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: squat knee dive
description: Squat knee dive is a bodyweight squat variation targeting the quads that emphasizes forward knee travel during the descent. This variation specifically challenges the quad muscles in a lengthened position and improves ankle dorsiflexion.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Begin lowering into a squat while intentionally driving your knees forward over your toes.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor, maximizing knee flexion.
Push through your full foot to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing side leg lift
description: Standing side leg lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hip abductors. Lifting the leg laterally in a standing position isolates the gluteus medius and improves hip stability and balance.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together, holding a wall or surface for balance if needed.
Keeping your upper body still and your standing leg slightly bent, raise one leg out to the side.
Lift the leg as high as comfortable, pausing at the top.
Slowly lower the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired repetitions before switching sides.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1088 - standing side leg lift.mp4

name: straight sitting with leg raise
description: Straight sitting with leg raise is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. Raising a straight leg from a tall-seated position creates abdominal tension and challenges hip flexor strength and upright posture.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor with legs extended straight in front of you and hands on the floor beside your hips.
Raise one leg off the ground to hip height, keeping it straight.
Hold the raised position for 2-3 seconds.
Lower the leg back to the floor and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1089 - straight sitting with leg raise.mp4

name: sumo squat
description: The sumo squat is a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads with an emphasis on the inner thighs and glutes. The wide, turned-out stance increases hip adductor engagement compared to a standard squat.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes turned outward at 45 degrees.
Place your hands together in front of your chest.
Lower into a squat by bending your knees outward over your toes and pushing your hips back.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position and repeat.

name: tunk jump
description: The tunk jump is a bodyweight plyometric cardio exercise that combines a squat with an explosive tuck jump. Bringing both knees toward the chest at the peak of the jump develops lower body power, core strength, and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Squat down quickly and then explosively jump up into the air.
At the peak of the jump, tuck both knees up toward your chest.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with knees bent to absorb the impact.
Repeat continuously for the desired number of repetitions.

name: tunk jump high
description: Tunk jump high is an advanced bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting cardio and explosive power. The maximum height jump combined with a deep knee tuck demands greater lower body power and neuromuscular coordination than the standard tunk jump.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees deeply and swing your arms back to load the jump.
Jump as high as possible, driving both knees up toward your chest forcefully.
At the peak, compress tightly into the tuck position.
Land softly, absorb through your legs, and immediately jump again.

name: twist sit ups
description: Twist sit ups are a bodyweight core exercise targeting the obliques. Adding a rotational element to the sit-up increases lateral abdominal engagement and promotes rotational core strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands behind your head.
As you curl up, rotate your torso and bring your right elbow toward your left knee.
Return to the starting position and repeat, this time bringing your left elbow toward your right knee.
Continue alternating the twist direction each repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v sit hold
description: The V sit hold is an isometric bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. Sustaining the V position by balancing on the tailbone with straight legs and elevated torso demands intense abdominal and hip flexor endurance.
instructions: Sit on the floor and lean back slightly.
Raise both legs off the ground, keeping them straight and together.
Balance on your tailbone with your torso and legs forming a V shape.
Hold the V position for the desired duration with your arms extended forward for balance.
Breathe steadily and maintain the position without letting your legs or torso drop.

name: v sit ups
description: V sit ups are a challenging bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs. The simultaneous leg and torso raise into a V shape maximizes contraction throughout the entire abdominal region and requires significant hip flexor strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously raise your legs and torso toward each other, reaching your hands toward your feet.
Meet in the V position at the top, balancing momentarily on your tailbone.
Slowly lower both your legs and torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall facing squat with wide legs
description: Wall facing squat with wide legs is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quads with a wide sumo stance. Facing the wall forces an upright torso and encourages proper knee tracking, making it an excellent technique drill and hip mobility exercise.
instructions: Stand facing a wall about one foot away, with your feet wider than shoulder-width and toes turned out.
Raise your arms overhead to touch the wall for support.
Slowly lower into a wide squat, keeping your torso upright and knees tracking over your toes.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Press through your heels to return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1096 - wall facing squat with wide legs.mp4

name: wall forward lunges
description: Wall forward lunges are a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads. The wall provides balance support, allowing the performer to focus on depth and control of the lunge without worrying about stability.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your hands resting lightly on it for support.
Step one foot forward into a lunge position, lowering your rear knee toward the floor.
Keep your front knee aligned over your toes and your torso upright.
Hold the bottom lunge position for 2-3 seconds.
Push through the front foot to return to standing and repeat on the other side.

name: wall hinge stretcher
description: The wall hinge stretcher is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back. Using the wall for hand support during a standing hip hinge allows a deep stretch along the posterior chain without the instability of a freestanding forward fold.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart, about arm's length away.
Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height.
Hinge at the hips and push them back as you lower your torso until your back is flat and parallel to the floor.
Hold the hip hinge stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull in your lower back and hamstrings.
Return to standing and repeat.

name: wall hinge stretcher 1
description: Wall hinge stretcher 1 is a bodyweight variation of the wall hinge performed from a side-facing stance. The single-arm wall support and free hand on the back promotes proper spinal alignment and a focused lower back release.
instructions: Stand with your side facing a wall, placing one hand on it for support.
Hinge at the hips with a flat back, extending your torso forward and out.
Place the other hand on your lower back to monitor spinal alignment.
Hold the hinge position for 20-30 seconds, focusing on the stretch in the lower back.
Return upright and repeat on the other side.

name: wall one leg squat
description: Wall one leg squat is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the quads. The wall provides back support during the single-leg squat, reducing balance demands and allowing a focus on quad strength and knee tracking.
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and feet hip-width apart a foot from the wall.
Lift one foot off the ground and extend that leg in front of you.
Slide your back down the wall by bending your standing leg into a single-leg squat.
Descend as far as comfortable while maintaining control.
Press through your standing heel to return to the starting position before switching legs.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1100 - wall one leg squat.mp4

name: wall squats
description: Wall squats are a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the quads. Maintaining a seated position against the wall builds quad endurance, improves knee stability, and is a low-impact strengthening option for all fitness levels.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and feet shoulder-width apart, a step away from the wall.
Slide your back down the wall until your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Hold the seated wall squat position for the desired duration.
Slide back up the wall to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of holds or repetitions.

name: wall squats with wide leg
description: Wall squats with wide leg is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the quads with a wider sumo stance. The wide foot position increases inner thigh and hip adductor engagement while the wall provides back support for proper alignment.
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes turned out.
Slide your back down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Hold the wide-leg wall squat position with your knees tracking over your toes.
Breathe steadily and maintain the position for the desired duration.
Slide back up the wall and reset before repeating.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1102 - wall squats with wide leg.mp4

name: wide-leg lateral stretch with floor support
description: Wide-leg lateral stretch with floor support is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hip flexors and inner thighs. Leaning toward each leg from a wide-seated position provides a deep unilateral hip and groin stretch.
instructions: Sit on the floor and spread your legs as wide as comfortable.
Place both hands on the floor in front of you for support.
Lean your torso toward one leg, reaching your hands along the floor.
Hold the lateral stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull in the inner thigh and hip.
Return to center and repeat toward the other leg.
video_path: 130 Workouts/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/1103 - wide-leg lateral stretch with floor support.mp4

name: y t w hand leg raise
description: Y T W hand leg raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the upper back and rear deltoids. Combining the Y, T, and W arm positions with opposite leg raises challenges posterior chain stability, scapular control, and overall back endurance.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your arms and legs extended.
Simultaneously raise your arms into a Y shape and your opposite leg off the floor.
Transition to a T shape by extending your arms straight out to the sides, then a W shape by bending your elbows.
Lower back to the starting position and repeat the sequence.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/strength/intermediate/1104 - y t w hand leg raise.mp4

name: y t w hand raise
description: Y T W hand raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the upper back and traps. The sequential arm positions activate different portions of the scapular stabilizers and posterior deltoids, improving posture and shoulder health.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your forehead resting on the ground and arms extended overhead.
Raise your arms into a Y shape, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower your arms and raise them into a T shape, then into a W shape by bending your elbows.
Lower your arms after each position before moving to the next.
Complete all three positions for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 130 Workouts/back/upper back/strength/beginner/1105 - y t w hand raise.mp4

name: y-t-w beginners
description: Y-T-W beginners is a bodyweight exercise targeting the upper back in a standing position. The beginner variation uses a slight forward hinge instead of lying prone, making it accessible while still activating the scapular stabilizers and rear deltoids.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hinge forward slightly at the hips.
Extend your arms in a Y shape overhead, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Lower and extend your arms into a T position at shoulder height.
Lower and bring your arms into a W position by bending the elbows.
Return to the starting position and repeat the Y-T-W sequence for the desired repetitions.

name: 90-90 rotation
description: The 90-90 rotation is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and obliques. It involves lying on your back with legs bent at 90 degrees and rotating them side to side, improving spinal rotation and core stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips and knees bent at 90 degrees, feet off the ground.
Extend your arms out to the sides for stability, palms facing down.
Keeping your knees together, slowly rotate your legs to one side until they are just above the floor.
Pause for a moment, then rotate your legs back through center and to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: angle mobility
description: Angle mobility is a bodyweight exercise targeting the deltoids and improving shoulder joint range of motion. It involves controlled circular arm movements to increase flexibility, reduce stiffness, and warm up the shoulder muscles.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height.
Slowly rotate your arms in small circular motions, gradually increasing the size of the circles.
After the desired number of repetitions, reverse the direction of the circles.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions in each direction.

name: arnold press
description: The arnold press is a dumbbell shoulder exercise that targets the deltoids through a combined pressing and rotation movement. Named after Arnold Schwarzenegger, it provides greater muscle activation across all three deltoid heads compared to a standard overhead press.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing you.
Begin pressing the dumbbells overhead while simultaneously rotating your palms away from you.
Fully extend your arms overhead with palms now facing forward at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, reversing the rotation so palms face you again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: back squat
description: The back squat is a barbell compound exercise that primarily targets the quadriceps, with significant engagement of the glutes, hamstrings, and lower back. It is one of the fundamental lower body strength movements used to build overall leg mass and functional strength.
instructions: Position a barbell on your upper traps and step back from the rack with feet shoulder-width apart.
Brace your core, keep your chest up, and begin lowering your hips by bending your knees.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to or just below the floor, keeping your knees in line with your toes.
Drive through your heels to press the floor away and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell glute bridge
description: The barbell glute bridge is a barbell exercise targeting the glutes, with secondary activation of the hamstrings and lower back. It involves thrusting the hips upward against the load of the barbell to develop glute strength and muscle mass.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and roll a barbell over your hips.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Brace your core and drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement and hold for a moment.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1347 - barbell glute bridge.mp4

name: barbell romanian deadlift
description: The barbell romanian deadlift is a barbell hinge exercise that primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes. By maintaining a soft knee bend and hinging at the hips, this movement develops posterior chain strength and flexibility.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Keeping a slight bend in your knees and your back straight, hinge at the hips and push them back.
Lower the barbell along the front of your legs until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Squeeze your glutes and drive your hips forward to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell row
description: The barbell row is a barbell compound pulling exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi and engages the biceps, rhomboids, and traps. It is a foundational back-building movement performed by hinging forward and rowing the bar toward the torso.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, keeping your back flat.
Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: battle ropes
description: Battle ropes is a rope-based cardio exercise that drives full-body metabolic conditioning while primarily targeting the cardiovascular system. The alternating wave motion also builds endurance in the shoulders, arms, and core.
instructions: Anchor a heavy rope to a fixed point at ground level and hold one end in each hand.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent in an athletic stance.
Rapidly alternate raising and lowering each arm to create waves along the rope.
Maintain a strong core and keep your back straight throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration or number of intervals.

name: bear hold
description: The bear hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abs and core stabilizers. By hovering the knees just above the ground in an all-fours position, this movement builds deep core strength, shoulder stability, and body awareness.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees directly under your hips.
Tuck your toes under and lift your knees just a few centimeters off the ground.
Keep your back flat, core braced, and hold this position.
Maintain steady breathing and avoid letting your hips rise or sag.
Hold for the desired duration.

name: bicycle crunch
description: The bicycle crunch is a bodyweight exercise that targets the abdominal muscles with a strong emphasis on the obliques. The alternating rotation and leg extension mimic a cycling motion, making it one of the most effective core exercises for muscle activation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed lightly behind your head and legs lifted off the ground.
Bend your right knee toward your chest while simultaneously rotating your left elbow toward it.
Extend your right leg and bring your left knee in, rotating your right elbow toward the left knee.
Continue alternating sides in a smooth pedaling and twisting motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bird dog
description: The bird dog is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and core stabilizers. By extending opposite limbs while maintaining a neutral spine, it develops balance, coordination, and spinal stability.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Brace your core and simultaneously extend your right arm forward and your left leg back.
Hold the extended position for a moment, keeping your hips level and back flat.
Slowly return your arm and leg to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side, alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: burpee
description: The burpee is a full-body bodyweight exercise that primarily targets the cardiovascular system while engaging the chest, quads, abs, and shoulders. It combines a squat, push-up, and jump into one dynamic movement, making it highly effective for conditioning.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Drop your hands to the floor and jump your feet back into a high plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back toward your hands.
Explosively jump up into the air, reaching your arms overhead.
Land softly and immediately go into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable crossover
description: The cable crossover is a cable exercise targeting the pectoral muscles through a fly-pattern movement. The constant cable tension throughout the arc provides consistent resistance, making it effective for chest isolation and muscle definition.
instructions: Set the pulleys on a cable machine to the highest position and attach a handle to each side.
Stand in the center of the machine, grasp one handle in each hand, and step forward slightly.
With a slight bend in your elbows, bring both handles downward and together in a wide arc in front of your body.
Squeeze your chest at the peak of the movement, holding briefly.
Slowly return the handles back to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable crunch
description: The cable crunch is a cable exercise that targets the abdominal muscles using weighted resistance from a high pulley. Kneeling and crunching downward against the cable allows for progressive overload and strong muscle activation throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to the high pulley of a cable machine and kneel facing the machine.
Grasp the rope and hold it at the sides of your head, elbows pointing down.
Brace your core and flex your spine to crunch downward, bringing your elbows toward your knees.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly return to the starting position, maintaining tension in the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable face pull up
description: The cable face pull up is a cable exercise targeting the traps and rear deltoids. By pulling the rope toward the face with elbows flared high, it strengthens the upper back and promotes healthy shoulder mechanics.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to upper chest height and attach a rope handle.
Grasp the rope with both hands and step back to create tension in the cable.
Pull the rope toward your face, separating your hands and driving your elbows back and out to the sides.
Pause when your hands are beside your ears and elbows are in line with your shoulders.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/back/traps/strength/beginner/1357 - cable face pull up.mp4

name: cable kickback
description: The cable kickback is a cable isolation exercise targeting the glutes. By extending the leg backward against resistance, it effectively isolates the gluteal muscles and helps develop shape and strength in the posterior chain.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley of a cable machine and secure it around one ankle.
Stand facing the machine and hold onto it for balance with a slight bend in your standing knee.
Keeping your core engaged and back flat, extend your cuffed leg straight back as far as comfortable.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement and hold briefly.
Slowly return your leg to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

name: cable woodchopper
description: The cable woodchopper is a cable rotational exercise targeting the obliques. The diagonal pulling motion from high to low mimics a chopping pattern, developing rotational core strength and power used in athletic movements.
instructions: Set the cable pulley to a high position and attach a single handle.
Stand sideways to the machine and grasp the handle with both hands, arms extended.
Keeping your arms mostly straight, pull the handle diagonally downward and across your body toward the opposite hip.
Rotate through your core and hips during the movement.
Slowly return the handle back to the high starting position with control.
Complete the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.

name: curtsy lunge
description: The curtsy lunge is a bodyweight lower body exercise that targets the glutes with additional emphasis on the quads and hip abductors. The crossing-behind step increases lateral glute engagement and challenges balance and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your hands on your hips or extended in front for balance.
Step your right foot behind and to the left of your left foot, crossing it like a curtsy.
Bend both knees and lower your hips until your front thigh is nearly parallel to the floor.
Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the movement.
Push through your front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dead bug
description: The dead bug is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abdominal muscles by requiring coordinated opposite-limb extension while maintaining spinal stability. It is widely used in rehabilitation and core conditioning programs.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended straight up toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees with feet off the floor.
Brace your core and press your lower back firmly into the floor.
Simultaneously lower your right arm overhead and extend your left leg toward the floor, keeping both just above the surface.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell lateral raise
description: The dumbbell lateral raise is a dumbbell isolation exercise targeting the lateral head of the deltoids. Raising the arms out to the sides against gravity effectively develops shoulder width and definition.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise both arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top and avoid shrugging your shoulders.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell reverse lunges
description: Dumbbell reverse lunges are a dumbbell lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps, with engagement of the glutes and hamstrings. Stepping backward rather than forward reduces knee stress and challenges balance and unilateral leg strength.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot straight back and lower your hips until both knees are bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Keep your front knee directly above your front ankle and your torso upright.
Push through your front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other leg, alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1363 - dumbbell reverse lunges.mp4

name: elbow wall plank
description: The elbow wall plank is a bodyweight isometric exercise that targets the abdominal muscles in a modified upright position. It is commonly used as a beginner-friendly alternative to the floor plank, building core endurance and stability.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place your forearms against it at shoulder height, elbows bent at 90 degrees.
Step your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line from head to heels.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes, keeping your hips level.
Avoid letting your lower back sag or your hips rise.
Hold for the desired duration.

name: elliptical trainer
description: The elliptical trainer is a low-impact cardio machine exercise that targets the cardiovascular system. It simulates running and stair climbing while minimizing joint stress, making it suitable for endurance training and active recovery.
instructions: Step onto the elliptical machine and grip the handles firmly.
Begin pedaling in a smooth, elliptical motion, pushing and pulling the handles in coordination with your legs.
Maintain an upright posture with a slight forward lean and keep your core engaged.
Adjust the resistance and incline as needed to reach your desired intensity.
Continue for the desired duration, maintaining a consistent pace.

name: cable kneeling row (meadow alternate)
description: The cable kneeling row in the meadow alternate style is a unilateral cable exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Kneeling creates a stable base while the alternating arm and knee position encourages a strong lat stretch and full range of motion.
instructions: Attach a single handle to a low cable pulley and kneel on one knee facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with the opposite hand to your planted knee, arm fully extended.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright with a slight forward lean.
Pull the handle toward your hip by driving your elbow back and squeezing your lat.
Pause at full contraction, then slowly return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: frog pumps
description: Frog pumps are a bodyweight glute isolation exercise performed with feet together and knees flared outward. The external rotation of the hips shifts the focus to the gluteal muscles, making it an effective activation and warm-up movement.
instructions: Lie on your back with the soles of your feet pressed together and knees dropped out to the sides in a butterfly position.
Place your arms flat on the floor beside you for stability.
Squeeze your glutes and drive your hips up toward the ceiling.
Pause at the top, holding the contraction for a moment.
Lower your hips back down to just above the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: front squat
description: The front squat is a barbell compound exercise targeting the quadriceps with the load held in front of the body. The forward bar position demands greater torso uprightness and core stability compared to the back squat.
instructions: Position a barbell across the front of your shoulders, resting on your fingertips or crossed arms, with elbows pointed forward.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes turned slightly outward.
Keeping your elbows high and torso upright, descend by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or below, maintaining an upright chest.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: glute bridge hold
description: The glute bridge hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the glutes. Holding the bridge position develops glute endurance and strength while also engaging the hamstrings and core stabilizers.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms flat on the floor beside you, palms facing down.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Squeeze your glutes firmly at the top.
Hold this position for the desired duration, maintaining steady breathing.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1369 - glute bridge hold.mp4

name: glute bridge marching
description: Glute bridge marching is a bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes while adding a dynamic challenge by alternating knee lifts in the bridge position. This movement improves hip stability and challenges the core to prevent rotation.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips into a glute bridge position.
Maintaining the bridge, lift one foot off the floor and bring your knee toward your chest.
Lower that foot back down and repeat on the opposite side in an alternating marching pattern.
Keep your hips level throughout and continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1370 - glute bridge marching.mp4

name: glute drive machine
description: The glute drive machine is a machine-based exercise specifically designed to isolate and overload the glutes through a hip thrust pattern. The fixed pad provides direct resistance to the hips, allowing for heavy loading and consistent muscle contraction.
instructions: Sit in the glute drive machine and adjust the pads so they rest comfortably across your hips.
Place your feet flat on the foot platform, hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels and thrust your hips forward into the pad, fully extending your hips at the top.
Squeeze your glutes at the peak contraction and hold for a moment.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1371 - glute drive machine.mp4

name: goblet squat
description: The goblet squat is a kettlebell lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps and glutes. Holding the weight at the chest promotes an upright torso and is commonly used as a teaching tool for proper squat mechanics.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell vertically at chest height with both hands cupping the bell.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes turned slightly outward.
Keeping your chest up and elbows inside your knees, descend into a deep squat.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to or below the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: good morning
description: The good morning is a barbell hip hinge exercise that targets the lower back and hamstrings. The movement closely mimics a bow, challenging posterior chain strength and spinal erector endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and position a barbell across your upper traps.
Hinge at the hips and push them back while lowering your torso toward the floor, maintaining a neutral spine.
Lower until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor or until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings.
Reverse the movement by driving your hips forward to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hanging raise up
description: The hanging raise up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles by requiring you to lift your legs while hanging from a bar. This movement builds core strength and hip flexor endurance while also challenging grip and shoulder stability.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms fully extended.
Engage your core and raise your legs in front of you, keeping them straight or with a slight knee bend.
Continue raising until your legs are parallel to the floor or higher if possible.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: heel slides
description: Heel slides are a bodyweight rehabilitation and activation exercise targeting the hamstrings. The controlled sliding motion through the full range of knee flexion and extension is commonly used to restore leg strength after injury.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and a smooth surface or sliders under your heels.
Brace your core and slowly slide both heels toward your glutes by bending your knees.
Once your heels are close to your glutes, slowly slide them back out to the starting position.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: heel tap
description: The heel tap is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, particularly the obliques. By lying on your back and alternately reaching to tap each heel, it creates a lateral crunch motion that strengthens the sides of the core.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lift your shoulders slightly off the ground, engaging your abs.
Reach your right hand down to tap your right heel, crunching to the side.
Return to center and repeat on the left side, reaching your left hand to your left heel.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hip abduction machine
description: The hip abduction machine is a machine-based exercise targeting the hip abductors and outer glutes. Seated isolation of the abductor muscles helps improve hip stability and is commonly used in rehabilitation and lower body conditioning.
instructions: Sit in the hip abduction machine with your back flat against the pad and feet on the footrests.
Position your legs inside the padded arms and adjust the range of motion.
Push your legs outward against the pads as far as comfortable, squeezing your outer glutes.
Pause at the widest point, then slowly bring your legs back together with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/abductors/strength/beginner/1377 - hip abduction machine.mp4

name: hip adduction machine
description: The hip adduction machine is a machine-based exercise targeting the hip adductor muscles of the inner thigh. This isolation movement builds inner thigh strength and stability and is commonly used in lower body and rehabilitation programs.
instructions: Sit in the hip adduction machine with your back flat against the pad and legs positioned outside the padded arms.
Adjust the machine to your range of motion.
Squeeze your inner thighs and bring your legs together against the pads.
Pause at the closest point, then slowly allow your legs to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/1378 - hip adduction machine.mp4

name: hip thrust
description: The hip thrust is a barbell compound exercise targeting the glutes through a powerful hip extension movement. It is one of the most effective exercises for maximizing gluteal muscle activation and building lower body strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench and a barbell across your hips.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels and thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Squeeze your glutes firmly at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back toward the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: inch worm
description: The inch worm is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles while also stretching the hamstrings and challenging shoulder stability. The forward and backward hand-walking pattern develops core strength and full-body coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hinge forward at your hips to place your hands on the floor.
Walk your hands forward until you are in a high plank position, keeping your core tight.
Pause briefly in the plank, then walk your hands back toward your feet.
Return to the standing position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline push-up
description: The incline push-up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower portion of the pectoral muscles. Using an elevated surface reduces the load compared to a standard push-up, making it suitable for beginners or as a warm-up variation.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface such as a bench or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you so your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending your elbows, keeping them at a 45-degree angle from your body.
Pause when your chest nearly touches the surface, then push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline treadmill walk
description: The incline treadmill walk is a low-impact cardio machine exercise that elevates heart rate and targets the glutes, hamstrings, and calves more than flat walking. The incline adds resistance to the walking motion, increasing caloric expenditure.
instructions: Set the treadmill to a comfortable walking speed and increase the incline to your desired level.
Step onto the belt and begin walking at a steady, controlled pace.
Maintain an upright posture, avoid holding the handrails, and keep your core engaged.
Pump your arms naturally with each step.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: jump lunges
description: Jump lunges are a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the quadriceps and developing explosive lower body power. The airborne switch between lunge positions increases cardiovascular demand and challenges balance and coordination.
instructions: Start in a lunge position with your right foot forward and left foot back, both knees bent at 90 degrees.
Explosively jump upward, switching the positions of your feet mid-air.
Land softly with your left foot forward and right foot back in a lunge position.
Immediately lower into the lunge and jump again.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jumping jack
description: The jumping jack is a classic bodyweight cardio exercise that elevates the heart rate through repeated jumping and arm raises. It is used as a warm-up, in conditioning circuits, or as a standalone cardio movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump your feet out to the sides while simultaneously raising your arms overhead.
Jump back to the starting position, bringing your feet together and arms back to your sides.
Maintain a steady rhythm and keep your knees slightly bent to absorb the impact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kettle bell swing
description: The kettlebell swing is a kettlebell ballistic exercise targeting the glutes and posterior chain. The explosive hip hinge movement develops power, builds cardiovascular endurance, and strengthens the hamstrings, lower back, and core.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell with both hands in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and swing the kettlebell back between your legs, maintaining a flat back.
Drive your hips forward explosively to swing the kettlebell up to chest height.
Allow the bell to float at the top, then hinge back at the hips to guide it back between your legs.
Continue the swinging motion for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1385 - kettle bell swing.mp4

name: lat pulldown
description: The lat pulldown is a cable machine exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Pulling the bar from overhead to the chest through a vertical pulling motion is fundamental for developing back width and upper body pulling strength.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine and adjust the thigh pad to secure your legs.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, wider than shoulder-width apart.
Pull the bar down toward your upper chest, driving your elbows toward your sides and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the bottom, then slowly extend your arms to lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lateral raise
description: The cable lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise that targets the lateral deltoid using a low cable pulley. The cable provides constant tension throughout the range of motion, making it more effective at the bottom position compared to a dumbbell lateral raise.
instructions: Attach a single handle to a low cable pulley and stand sideways to the machine.
Grasp the handle with the hand furthest from the machine, arm hanging at your side.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbow, raise your arm out to the side until it is parallel to the ground.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your lateral deltoid.
Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position and repeat for the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: leg extension
description: The leg extension is a machine-based isolation exercise that targets the quadriceps. Extending the knee against resistance from the seated position provides direct stimulation to the quad muscles, commonly used for hypertrophy and rehabilitation.
instructions: Sit in a leg extension machine with your back flat against the pad and knees aligned with the machine's pivot point.
Hook your ankles under the roller pad and grip the handles on the sides.
Extend your legs upward until they are fully straight, squeezing your quads at the top.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position, stopping just before it touches the stack.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: leg press
description: The leg press is a machine-based compound exercise targeting the quadriceps, with significant engagement of the glutes and hamstrings. It allows for heavy loading of the lower body in a controlled, supported position.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your back flat against the pad and feet hip-width apart on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform by bending your knees toward your chest.
Stop when your knees are at approximately a 90-degree angle, keeping your lower back pressed to the pad.
Press the platform away by driving through your heels to fully extend your legs without locking your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying leg curl
description: The lying leg curl is a machine-based isolation exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performing the curl in a prone position places the hamstrings under full tension through the entire range of motion for effective muscle development.
instructions: Lie face down on the lying leg curl machine with your knees aligned with the machine's pivot point.
Hook your ankles under the roller pad and grip the handles.
Curl your legs upward by bending your knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstrings.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1390 - lying leg curl.mp4

name: mountain climber
description: Mountain climbers are a bodyweight dynamic exercise targeting the abdominal muscles while elevating the heart rate. The rapid alternating knee drives provide a challenging core and cardio stimulus in a plank position.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your arms fully extended, hands directly under your shoulders.
Drive your right knee toward your chest, then quickly switch, driving your left knee in as you extend the right leg back.
Continue alternating legs rapidly in a running-like motion.
Keep your hips level and your core braced throughout.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: nordic curl
description: The nordic curl is an advanced bodyweight exercise that targets the hamstrings eccentrically. It is considered one of the most effective movements for hamstring strength and injury prevention, requiring significant eccentric control.
instructions: Kneel on a padded surface and secure your ankles under a fixed bar or have a partner hold them.
With your torso upright and arms crossed over your chest, slowly lower your body toward the floor by extending your knees.
Control the descent using your hamstrings as long as possible.
When you can no longer hold yourself, lower to the ground and use your hands to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: over under
description: The over under is a bodyweight agility and core exercise targeting the obliques through lateral bending and rotation. Ducking alternately to each side challenges lateral core strength, balance, and coordination.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and place a cone or obstacle beside you.
Hinge to one side to duck under the obstacle by bending your knees and lowering your torso.
Shift your weight and rise up on the other side.
Return in the opposite direction, alternating the ducking pattern.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pelvic tilt
description: The pelvic tilt is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and lower back. This gentle movement is commonly used to reduce lower back pain, improve lumbopelvic stability, and develop core awareness.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Flatten your lower back against the floor by tightening your abdominal muscles and tilting your pelvis slightly upward.
Hold this position for a few seconds, keeping your glutes relaxed.
Release and allow your lower back to return to its natural arch.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pendulum squat
description: The pendulum squat is a machine-based exercise targeting the quadriceps through a swinging arc motion. The pendulum mechanism reduces spinal compression while allowing a deep range of motion, making it effective for quad development.
instructions: Step into the pendulum squat machine and position your shoulders under the pads.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart on the platform and release the safety.
Descend by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, lowering through a full range of motion.
Drive through your heels to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank
description: The plank is a foundational bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and overall core stability. Holding a rigid straight-body position trains the deep stabilizing muscles of the spine and is widely used across fitness disciplines.
instructions: Lie face down and lift yourself onto your forearms and toes, with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Form a straight line from your head to your heels, keeping your hips level.
Brace your core, squeeze your glutes, and keep your neck in a neutral position.
Breathe steadily and avoid allowing your hips to sag or rise.
Hold for the desired duration.

name: pull-ups
description: Pull-ups are a bodyweight compound exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi and is one of the most effective movements for developing upper body pulling strength. The movement requires significant coordination of the back, biceps, and core.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and depress your shoulder blades.
Pull your body upward by driving your elbows toward your hips until your chin clears the bar.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the hanging position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: push-up
description: The push-up is a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. It simultaneously engages the triceps, shoulders, and core, making it one of the most versatile and widely used upper body exercises.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width and body forming a straight line.
Lower your chest to the floor by bending your elbows at a 45-degree angle from your body.
Stop just before your chest touches the ground.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: quadruped knee lifts
description: Quadruped knee lifts are a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and core stabilizers. Hovering the knees off the floor while on all fours builds core endurance and hip stability, and is commonly used in rehabilitation and activation routines.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Brace your core and lift one knee slightly off the floor while maintaining a flat back.
Hold the position for a moment, then lower that knee and lift the other.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
Avoid allowing your hips to rotate or shift during the movement.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/rehabilitation/beginner/1399 - quadruped knee lifts.mp4

name: reach to w
description: The reach to W is a bodyweight exercise targeting the rhomboids and lower traps. Alternating between an overhead reach and a W position effectively activates the mid-back muscles and promotes healthy scapular movement and posture.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended overhead in a Y position.
Lift your arms off the ground and pull your elbows down and back toward your sides, forming a W shape with your arms.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly return your arms to the extended overhead position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rear delt fly
description: The rear delt fly is a dumbbell isolation exercise targeting the posterior head of the deltoids and upper back. Performed in a hinged position, the fly motion develops shoulder balance and postural integrity.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down with a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise both arms out to the sides in a wide arc until they are parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your rear delts and rhomboids at the top.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse hack squat
description: The reverse hack squat is a machine-based exercise that places emphasis on the glutes and hamstrings by reversing the body orientation on the hack squat machine. The forward lean of the torso shifts load to the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand facing the hack squat machine and place your chest against the back pad, with your shoulders under the pads.
Position your feet low on the platform, hip-width apart.
Release the safety and lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Descend until your thighs are below parallel, feeling a stretch in your glutes and hamstrings.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1402 - reverse hack squat.mp4

name: reverse hyperextension
description: Reverse hyperextension is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hamstrings by lifting the legs in a prone hanging position. It develops posterior chain strength and spinal decompression simultaneously.
instructions: Lie face down on a bench or elevated surface with your hips at the edge and legs hanging down.
Grip the sides of the bench for stability.
Keeping your legs together and slightly bent, raise them upward by squeezing your glutes.
Lift until your body forms a straight line or slightly above parallel.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse lunges
description: Reverse lunges are a bodyweight unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps. Stepping backward reduces anterior knee stress and develops balance, stability, and strength in each leg independently.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips or extended in front for balance.
Step one foot directly back and lower your hips until both knees reach approximately 90 degrees.
Keep your front knee aligned over your front ankle and your torso upright.
Push through your front foot to return to the standing position.
Repeat on the opposite side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse crunch
description: The reverse crunch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, particularly the lower abs. By lifting the hips off the floor rather than the shoulders, it shifts emphasis to the inferior portion of the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended at your sides, palms facing down.
Bend your knees and lift your feet off the floor, bringing your knees toward your chest.
Continue to curl your hips upward off the floor using your lower abs.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower your hips and legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated calf raise
description: The seated calf raise is a machine-based isolation exercise targeting the calves, with particular emphasis on the soleus. The seated position bends the knee and places greater stress on the soleus compared to standing variations.
instructions: Sit at a seated calf raise machine with the knee pads positioned over your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the platform, allowing your heels to hang.
Lower your heels as far as comfortable to get a full stretch in the calves.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1406 - seated calf raise.mp4

name: seated leg curl
description: The seated leg curl is a machine-based isolation exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed in a seated position, the movement provides consistent resistance throughout the range of motion for hamstring development.
instructions: Sit in the seated leg curl machine with your back against the pad and the roller positioned just above your heels.
Grip the handles and align your knees with the machine's pivot point.
Curl your legs downward as far as possible, squeezing your hamstrings.
Pause at the fully curled position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1407 - seated leg curl.mp4

name: seated machine row
description: The seated machine row is a machine-based pulling exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi. The chest-supported design stabilizes the torso and isolates the back muscles for consistent muscle activation.
instructions: Sit at a seated row machine with your chest against the chest pad and feet on the platform.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with an overhand or neutral grip.
Pull the handles toward your torso, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated row
description: The seated row is a cable exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi and mid-back. Pulling the cable toward the torso from a seated position builds back thickness and promotes proper scapular retraction.
instructions: Sit at a low cable row station with feet on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip and sit upright.
Pull the handles toward your lower abs, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
Hold the contraction briefly, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: side knee plank
description: The side knee plank is a modified bodyweight side plank that targets the obliques with reduced difficulty. Keeping the knees bent shortens the lever arm, making it accessible as a progression toward the full side plank.
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on your forearm with your elbow under your shoulder.
Stack your knees on top of each other with your legs bent at 90 degrees.
Lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from head to knees.
Brace your core and avoid letting your hips drop.
Hold for the desired duration, then switch sides.

name: side plank
description: The side plank is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the obliques and lateral core. Holding the body in a rigid side-lying position develops lateral stability, which is essential for spinal protection and athletic performance.
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on your forearm with your elbow directly under your shoulder.
Stack your feet on top of each other and lift your hips off the floor.
Maintain a straight line from your head to your feet, keeping your core tight.
Avoid letting your hips sag or rotate.
Hold for the desired duration, then switch sides.

name: single arm row
description: The single arm row is a dumbbell unilateral pulling exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Working one side at a time allows for greater range of motion and helps identify and correct strength imbalances between sides.
instructions: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a bench for support, holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Keep your back flat and parallel to the floor.
Pull the dumbbell toward your hip, driving your elbow straight back.
Squeeze your lat at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch sides.

name: single-leg hip thrust
description: The single-leg hip thrust is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the glutes through a one-legged hip extension. It develops glute strength asymmetrically and helps correct imbalances between sides.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench, one foot flat on the floor and the other leg extended.
Drive through the heel of your grounded foot to thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line.
Squeeze your glute firmly at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your hips back toward the floor.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1413 - single-leg hip thrust.mp4

name: smith squat
description: The smith squat is a machine-guided barbell squat targeting the quadriceps. The fixed vertical bar path of the smith machine provides stability and allows for focused leg training with reduced balance demands.
instructions: Position a smith machine bar on your upper traps and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Unhook the bar and brace your core.
Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to or below the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position and re-rack.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: spiderman lunges
