description: Spiderman lunges are a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the hip flexors. Stepping forward from a plank position into a deep lunge opens the hips and groin, and is commonly used in warm-up and mobility routines.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Step your right foot forward to land just outside your right hand.
Hold the position for a moment, feeling the stretch in your hip flexors and groin.
Return your foot to the starting plank position.
Repeat on the left side, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/beginner/1415 - spiderman lunges.mp4

name: stairmaster walk
description: The stairmaster walk is a cardio machine exercise that mimics continuous stair climbing. It elevates the heart rate while engaging the glutes, quads, and calves, making it effective for cardiovascular conditioning and lower body toning.
instructions: Step onto the stairmaster and grip the handles lightly for balance.
Set the speed to a comfortable level and begin stepping on the rotating stairs.
Maintain an upright posture and avoid leaning heavily on the handles.
Drive through the full step with each foot, engaging your glutes and quads.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: standing calf raise
description: The standing calf raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the gastrocnemius of the calf complex. Raising the heels against gravity builds calf size, strength, and ankle stability.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart on a flat surface or the edge of a step for increased range.
Rise up onto the balls of your feet as high as possible, squeezing your calves at the top.
Hold briefly at the peak contraction.
Slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1417 - standing calf raise.mp4

name: standing pallof press
description: The standing pallof press is a cable anti-rotation exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and obliques. Resisting the rotational pull of the cable develops core stability and is widely used in sports performance and rehabilitation programs.
instructions: Set a cable pulley to chest height and attach a single handle.
Stand sideways to the machine and hold the handle with both hands at your chest.
Step away to create tension in the cable, feet shoulder-width apart.
Press the handle straight out in front of you, fully extending your arms.
Hold for a moment, resisting rotation, then bring the handle back to your chest.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch sides.

name: step-up
description: The step-up is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the quadriceps. Stepping up onto an elevated surface develops single-leg strength, balance, and coordination, while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or box and place one foot flat on the surface.
Drive through the heel of your raised foot to step up onto the box, bringing your trailing foot up beside it.
Step back down with the trailing foot first, returning to the starting position.
Repeat on the same leg for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

name: suitcase carry
description: The suitcase carry is a dumbbell loaded carry exercise targeting the forearms and grip strength. Carrying the weight on one side creates an anti-lateral flexion demand on the obliques and core, making it an effective stability and strength exercise.
instructions: Hold a heavy dumbbell in one hand at your side, arm fully extended.
Brace your core firmly to resist lateral tilting toward the weighted side.
Walk forward in a controlled manner, keeping your torso upright and shoulders level.
Maintain your grip and resist the urge to lean away from the weight.
Complete the desired distance or duration, then switch hands.

name: sumo tap
description: The sumo tap is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hip adductors in a wide-stance squat position. Alternating reaches add a lateral core challenge and improve hip mobility and dynamic balance.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes turned outward.
Lower into a sumo squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
From the squat, reach one hand across to tap the opposite foot or the floor beside it.
Return to the squat and repeat on the other side in an alternating tapping motion.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: t-bar row
description: The t-bar row is a barbell compound pulling exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi and mid-back. The narrow grip angle and chest-supported variation allow for heavy loading and strong back muscle contraction.
instructions: Straddle a t-bar row machine or loaded barbell and grasp the handles with a neutral grip.
Hinge at the hips until your torso is at roughly a 45-degree angle, keeping your back flat.
Pull the bar toward your chest, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: t-spine rotation
description: T-spine rotation is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the thoracic spine and lower back. The controlled rotation improves thoracic mobility, reduces stiffness, and is commonly used in warm-up routines and injury prevention protocols.
instructions: Start in a quadruped position with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Place one hand behind your head with your elbow pointing outward.
Rotate your upper body, opening your elbow toward the ceiling as far as comfortable.
Hold briefly at the end range, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

name: toe tap
description: The toe tap is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles through a vertical crunch motion. Reaching up toward vertical legs increases the range of motion and demands strong upper ab engagement throughout.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and legs raised to 90 degrees.
Brace your core and curl your upper body off the floor, reaching your hands toward your feet.
Tap your toes at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your upper body back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: toe touch
description: The toe touch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings and lower back. The standing forward fold is a fundamental flexibility movement used in warm-up and cool-down routines to improve posterior chain mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet together and legs straight.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach both hands toward your toes.
Hold the stretch briefly at the lowest point you can reach.
Slowly return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: treadmill run
description: Treadmill running is a cardio machine exercise that targets the cardiovascular system and lower body musculature. It provides a controlled, consistent running surface for aerobic conditioning, interval training, and endurance development.
instructions: Step onto the treadmill and set your desired speed and incline.
Begin jogging and gradually increase to your running pace.
Maintain an upright posture with a slight forward lean and relaxed arms.
Breathe rhythmically and keep a comfortable stride length.
Continue for the desired duration, then gradually decrease speed to cool down.

name: treadmill walk
description: Treadmill walking is a low-impact cardio machine exercise that targets the cardiovascular system. It is suitable for all fitness levels and is commonly used for warm-up, active recovery, and steady-state aerobic conditioning.
instructions: Step onto the treadmill and set a comfortable walking speed.
Begin walking at a steady pace, maintaining an upright posture.
Swing your arms naturally and keep your gaze forward.
Breathe steadily throughout the walk.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: triceps pushdown
description: The triceps pushdown is a cable isolation exercise targeting the triceps. Extending the elbow against resistance from the high pulley effectively develops tricep size and strength through the full range of motion.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to the high pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine and grasp the attachment with an overhand grip, elbows at your sides.
Push the bar or rope downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight.
Pause at the bottom and squeeze your triceps.
Slowly allow the weight to rise back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: tuck jump
description: The tuck jump is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the quadriceps and lower body power. The explosive jump combined with a high knee tuck demands significant force production and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms at your sides.
Bend your knees slightly and then explosively jump as high as possible.
At the peak of the jump, bring your knees up toward your chest.
Extend your legs back out before landing and absorb the impact by bending your knees.
Immediately go into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v-up
description: The v-up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles by requiring simultaneous elevation of the upper and lower body to form a V shape. This movement challenges full abdominal strength and hip flexor coordination.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously raise your legs and upper body off the floor, reaching your hands toward your feet.
Form a V shape with your body at the peak of the movement.
Slowly lower your arms and legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: captain's chair leg raise
description: The captain's chair leg raise is a bodyweight exercise that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. Suspended between the arm pads of a captain's chair, raising the knees toward the chest creates strong core engagement and lower ab activation.
instructions: Mount a captain's chair apparatus and press your back firmly against the back pad.
Grip the handles and allow your legs to hang straight down.
Engage your core and raise your legs by bending your knees toward your chest.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back to the hanging position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: warm up lateral lunges
description: Warm up lateral lunges are a bodyweight exercise targeting the hip adductors and inner thighs. The lateral stepping pattern dynamically stretches and activates the groin and hips, making it effective as a lower body warm-up movement.
instructions: Stand with feet together and hands on your hips.
Take a wide step to the right and shift your body weight over your right leg, bending the right knee.
Keep your left leg straight as you sink into the lateral lunge.
Push off your right foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the left side and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/adductors/mobility/beginner/1432 - warm up lateral lunges.mp4

name: warm up squat+goodmorning
description: The warm up squat and good morning is a bodyweight combination exercise targeting the quadriceps and hamstrings. Alternating between a squat and a hip hinge pattern activates the entire lower body and prepares the posterior chain for training.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hands clasped behind your head.
Perform a bodyweight squat by bending your knees and lowering your hips until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Rise back to standing, then immediately hinge forward at the hips into a good morning, lowering your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor.
Return to standing and repeat the squat to begin the next cycle.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/mobility/beginner/1433 - warm up squat+goodmorning.mp4

name: warm up wiper
description: The warm up wiper is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the obliques and spinal rotators. The side-to-side leg rotation from the lying position loosens the lower back and activates the core before training.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended out to the sides for stability.
Bend your knees and bring them together above your hips at a 90-degree angle.
Rotate your knees slowly to one side, lowering them toward the floor.
Bring your knees back to center and rotate to the other side.
Continue alternating sides in a windshield wiper motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: weighted russian twist
description: The weighted russian twist is a dumbbell rotational exercise targeting the obliques. Adding a dumbbell to the twisting motion increases resistance, developing rotational core strength and stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly off the ground, leaning back at a 45-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands at your chest.
Rotate your torso to the right, bringing the dumbbell toward the floor beside your hip.
Return to center and rotate to the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wrist circle
description: Wrist circles are a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the forearms and wrist joint. The circular motion improves wrist flexibility, reduces stiffness, and is used as a warm-up before exercises involving grip and pressing.
instructions: Extend your arms in front of you at shoulder height.
Make fists with both hands and begin rotating your wrists in slow, full circles.
Perform the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Keep the movement smooth and controlled.

name: ab wheel rollout
description: The ab wheel rollout is an ab wheel exercise targeting the abdominal muscles through an extended anti-extension movement. Rolling the body out against gravity demands significant core strength and is considered one of the most demanding ab exercises.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and grip the ab wheel handles with both hands directly under your shoulders.
Brace your core and slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body toward the floor.
Extend as far as you can while maintaining a flat back and without letting your hips sag.
Pause briefly, then use your core to pull the wheel back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crossbody arm swing
description: The crossbody arm swing is a dynamic bodyweight exercise targeting the deltoids and upper back. The rhythmic swinging motion improves shoulder mobility, increases blood flow to the shoulder joint, and serves as an effective warm-up or active recovery movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to your sides at shoulder height.
Swing both arms inward across your body simultaneously, crossing them in front of your chest.
Return your arms back out to the starting position with control.
Continue the swinging motion in a rhythmic pattern for the desired number of repetitions.
Keep your core engaged and your torso stable throughout the movement.

name: dips
description: Dips are a compound bodyweight exercise that primarily targets the triceps with secondary engagement of the pectorals and deltoids. Performed on parallel bars, they are one of the most effective upper body pressing movements for building arm and chest strength.
instructions: Grip the parallel bars with both hands and push yourself up to the starting position, arms fully extended.
Cross your ankles and keep your legs slightly in front of you for balance.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chest supported dumbbell rows
description: Chest supported dumbbell rows are a dumbbell pulling exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. The chest-supported position eliminates torso momentum and isolates the back muscles for consistent, controlled pulling.
instructions: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees and lie face down with your chest against the pad.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging straight down.
Pull both dumbbells upward by driving your elbows toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/back/lats/strength/beginner/1440 - chest supported dumbbell rows.mp4

name: dumbbell single-leg romanian deadlift
description: The dumbbell single-leg romanian deadlift is a dumbbell unilateral hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings and glutes. The single-leg stance demands significant balance and core stability in addition to posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand on one foot and hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells toward the floor while extending the free leg behind you.
Keep your back flat and your hips square as you descend.
Once you feel a strong stretch in your hamstring, drive your hips forward to return to standing.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/1441 - dumbbell single-leg romanian deadlift.mp4

name: dumbbell bench press
description: The dumbbell bench press is a compound chest exercise targeting the pectorals, triceps, and anterior deltoids. Using dumbbells allows for a greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared to a barbell, improving chest development and correcting imbalances.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward.
Plant your feet flat on the floor and arch your lower back slightly.
Press both dumbbells upward simultaneously until your arms are fully extended above your chest.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell chest support front raise
description: The dumbbell chest support front raise is a shoulder isolation exercise performed lying face down on an incline bench. The chest-supported position eliminates momentum and body swing, placing strict tension on the anterior deltoids throughout the movement.
instructions: Set an incline bench to approximately 45 degrees and lie face down with your chest against the pad.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms hanging straight down.
Raise both arms forward to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause at the top, squeezing your anterior deltoids.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the hanging position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/1443 - dumbbell chest support front raise.mp4

name: cable overhead triceps extension
description: The cable overhead triceps extension is a cable isolation exercise targeting the triceps in a fully stretched overhead position. The overhead angle places emphasis on the long head of the tricep for maximum muscle development.
instructions: Attach a rope to the low pulley of a cable machine.
Grasp the rope and face away from the machine, holding the attachment overhead with elbows bent.
Extend your elbows to straighten your arms overhead, pressing the rope forward and upward.
Pause at full extension, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/1444 - cable overhead triceps extension.mp4

name: sit-ups
description: Sit-ups are a foundational bodyweight abdominal exercise that involves fully raising the torso from the floor. Unlike crunches, the full sit-up range of motion also engages the hip flexors and is commonly used in fitness testing and core conditioning.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands behind your head.
Engaging your core, curl your entire torso up toward your knees.
Continue until your upper body is fully upright.
Slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell farmer carry
description: The dumbbell farmer carry is a functional strength exercise that targets the forearms and grip while also challenging the traps, core, and lower body. Carrying heavy loads for distance builds full-body stability, endurance, and practical carrying strength.
instructions: Stand with a heavy dumbbell in each hand at your sides, arms fully extended.
Brace your core, retract your shoulder blades, and stand tall.
Walk forward in a straight line at a controlled pace, maintaining an upright posture.
Keep your grip firm and your steps deliberate throughout the carry.
Walk for the desired distance or duration, then set the dumbbells down carefully.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/lower arms/forearms/strength/intermediate/1446 - dumbbell farmer carry.mp4

name: single-leg leg press
description: The single-leg leg press is a machine-based unilateral exercise targeting the quadriceps. Working one leg at a time allows for targeted strength development and helps address imbalances between sides.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with one foot in the center of the platform and the other foot off to the side.
Release the safety and lower the platform by bending your working knee toward your chest.
Stop when your knee is at approximately a 90-degree angle.
Press through your heel to fully extend your leg without locking the knee.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch legs.

name: wide stance leg press
description: The wide stance leg press is a machine-based lower body exercise that shifts emphasis to the glutes and inner thighs compared to a standard leg press. The wider foot placement and outward toe angle recruit the adductors and gluteal muscles more effectively.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your feet placed wide apart on the platform and toes turned outward.
Release the safety and lower the platform by bending your knees toward your chest.
Descend until your knees are at roughly 90 degrees, keeping your lower back pressed to the pad.
Drive through your heels to press the platform away and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1448 - wide stance leg press.mp4

name: dumbbell concentration bicep curl
description: The dumbbell concentration bicep curl is a dumbbell isolation exercise targeting the biceps. Bracing the arm against the inner thigh eliminates swinging and maximizes bicep isolation for focused muscle development.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet flat on the floor and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Rest the back of your upper arm against the inside of your thigh.
Curl the dumbbell upward toward your shoulder, focusing on squeezing your bicep at the top.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions, then switch arms.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/1449 - dumbbell concentration bicep curl.mp4

name: close grip triceps push-up
description: The close grip triceps push-up is a bodyweight exercise that targets the triceps by using a narrow hand placement. The reduced hand width shifts emphasis away from the chest and increases demand on the triceps.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands placed close together, directly under your chest.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides throughout the movement.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the floor.
Push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: knee push-ups
description: Knee push-ups are a beginner bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. By supporting the body on the knees rather than the toes, the movement reduces difficulty and allows those building upper body strength to practice proper push-up mechanics.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and place your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your hips so your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the floor.
Push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell shrugs
description: Dumbbell shrugs are an isolation exercise that targets the upper trapezius muscles. The upward shrugging motion against loaded dumbbells builds trap size and thickness, improving shoulder and neck aesthetics and upper body stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Let your arms hang naturally and keep your chest tall and core engaged.
Shrug your shoulders straight upward toward your ears as high as possible.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your traps.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lateral raise
description: The cable lateral raise is a cable isolation exercise targeting the lateral deltoids. The constant cable tension throughout the full range of motion provides more consistent resistance than dumbbell variations, improving shoulder width.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley of a cable machine.
Stand sideways to the machine and grasp the handle with the far hand.
Raise your arm out to the side in a wide arc until it is parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch sides.

name: dumbbell front raise
description: The dumbbell front raise is a dumbbell isolation exercise targeting the anterior deltoid. Lifting the arms forward against gravity develops frontal shoulder strength and is commonly used in shoulder conditioning routines.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs, palms facing down.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise one or both arms forward to shoulder height.
Pause at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell walking lunges
description: Dumbbell walking lunges are a dynamic lower body exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. Moving continuously forward with dumbbells adds load and increases the demand on the core and lower body compared to stationary lunges.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step forward with one foot and lower your back knee toward the ground.
Ensure both knees are at approximately 90 degrees at the bottom of the lunge.
Push through your front heel and step the rear foot forward into the next lunge.
Continue walking forward in a lunge pattern for the desired number of steps.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1455 - dumbbell walking lunges.mp4

name: hammer curl
description: The hammer curl is an upper arm exercise performed with a neutral grip that targets the biceps brachii and brachioradialis. The neutral hand position places greater emphasis on the outer bicep and forearm compared to a standard supinated curl.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your torso throughout the movement.
Curl both dumbbells upward simultaneously, maintaining the neutral grip.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: assisted pistol squat
description: The assisted pistol squat is a bodyweight unilateral squat targeting the quadriceps. Using external support to help balance and control the descent allows trainees to build the strength and mobility needed for the full pistol squat.
instructions: Stand facing a pole, TRX strap, or doorframe and hold on with both hands for support.
Shift your weight onto one leg and extend the other leg straight out in front of you.
Using the support to assist you, lower your hips toward the floor on the standing leg.
Descend as low as possible, keeping your extended leg off the floor.
Drive through your heel to return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch legs.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1457 - assisted pistol squat.mp4

name: med ball sit-up
description: The med ball sit-up is a medicine ball exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. Adding a weighted ball increases the resistance of a standard sit-up and can be combined with an overhead press at the top for additional shoulder engagement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hold a medicine ball at your chest.
Engage your core and perform a full sit-up, raising your torso all the way up.
At the top of the movement, extend your arms and press the ball overhead or toward the ceiling.
Lower back to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank with reach
description: The plank with reach is a bodyweight exercise that adds dynamic challenge to the standard plank by requiring alternating arm extensions. This movement demands strong anti-rotation core stability and shoulder control.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands under your shoulders and body forming a straight line.
Brace your core and slowly lift one arm off the ground, extending it directly in front of you.
Hold briefly while maintaining a stable plank position with minimal hip rotation.
Return your hand to the floor and repeat on the other arm.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying leg raises (bent knee)
description: Lying leg raises with bent knees are a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. The bent knee position reduces the lever arm and decreases demand on the hip flexors compared to straight-leg raises, making it more accessible.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your glutes for support and knees slightly bent.
Keeping your lower back pressed into the floor, raise your legs toward the ceiling.
Stop when your legs are perpendicular to the floor or when you feel your lower back lift.
Slowly lower your legs back toward the floor without touching it.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1460 - lying leg raises (bent knee).mp4

name: hanging leg raise
description: The hanging leg raise is an advanced bodyweight core exercise targeting the lower abs and hip flexors. Hanging from a bar while raising the legs requires significant core strength and grip endurance, making it one of the most demanding abdominal exercises.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms fully extended and body relaxed.
Brace your core and keep a slight bend in your knees.
Raise your legs upward by flexing at the hips until they are parallel to the ground or higher.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back to the hanging position with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline bench press (barbell)
description: The incline barbell bench press is a compound chest exercise that places greater emphasis on the upper pectorals and anterior deltoids compared to a flat bench press. The incline angle shifts the pressing load upward, developing upper chest thickness and strength.
instructions: Set an incline bench to 30–45 degrees and rack a barbell at the appropriate height.
Lie back on the bench and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it above your upper chest with arms fully extended.
Lower the bar slowly to your upper chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Press the bar back up to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable tricep kickback
description: The cable tricep kickback is a cable isolation exercise targeting the triceps. The cable provides consistent resistance during the extension, making it effective for tricep isolation and muscle endurance.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley of a cable machine.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor and grasp the handle.
Keeping your upper arm stationary at your side, extend your elbow to kick the handle straight back.
Pause when your arm is fully extended, squeezing your tricep.
Slowly bend your elbow to return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch arms.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/1463 - cable tricep kickback.mp4

name: cable standing chest press
description: The cable standing chest press is a cable compound exercise targeting the pectoral muscles in a standing position. Standing rather than lying on a bench increases core demand and allows a more natural pressing arc.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to chest height and attach handles to each side.
Stand in the center of the machine and grasp one handle in each hand at chest level.
Step forward slightly for stability and brace your core.
Press both handles forward until your arms are fully extended in front of you.
Slowly return the handles back to chest level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: inverted row
description: The inverted row is a bodyweight pulling exercise targeting the lats, rhomboids, and biceps. Performed beneath a fixed bar, it is an accessible and scalable alternative to pull-ups, with difficulty adjustable by changing the angle of the body.
instructions: Set a barbell in a rack or use a Smith machine bar at approximately hip height.
Lie underneath the bar and grip it with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your body in a straight line with your heels on the ground, arms fully extended.
Pull your chest up toward the bar by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position and repeat.

name: plate front raise
description: The plate front raise is a plate isolation exercise targeting the anterior deltoids. Holding the plate with both hands in a fixed position maintains shoulder-width tension and builds frontal shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight plate with both hands at the bottom, arms extended downward.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise the plate forward and upward to shoulder height.
Pause at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the plate back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell stiff deadlifts
description: Dumbbell stiff deadlifts are a dumbbell hip hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings. Maintaining nearly straight legs during the hinge maximizes the stretch and contraction of the hamstrings, building posterior chain strength and flexibility.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
With a slight bend in your knees, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells along your legs.
Keep your back flat and lower until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings.
Squeeze your glutes and drive your hips forward to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jump lunges
description: Jump lunges are a plyometric exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. The explosive jumping and mid-air leg switch intensify the lunge movement, building lower body power, cardiovascular fitness, and coordination.
instructions: Start in a lunge position with your right foot forward and left knee near the ground.
Explosively jump upward, driving both knees toward the ceiling.
Switch your legs in the air so your left foot lands forward and right foot lands back.
Land softly in a lunge position, immediately lowering into the next repetition.
Continue alternating legs with each jump for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kas hip thrust
description: The KAS hip thrust is a barbell glute exercise popularized for its constant-tension partial repetition style. By avoiding full lockout at the top and staying in the mid-range, the glutes remain under continuous load, maximizing time under tension and muscle activation.
instructions: Set up for a hip thrust with your upper back against a bench and a barbell across your hips.
Plant your feet flat on the ground close to your glutes, hip-width apart.
Instead of performing a full hip thrust, perform small and controlled partial repetitions, never fully locking out at the top.
Keep constant tension on the glutes throughout by staying in the mid-range of the movement.
Continue pulsing for the desired number of repetitions without resting at the bottom.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1469 - kas hip thrust.mp4

name: flutter kicks
description: Flutter kicks are a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. The rapid alternating leg movement in a supine position creates sustained core tension, making it effective for lower ab endurance.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and hands under your glutes for support.
Lift both legs a few inches off the floor and brace your core.
Alternate raising and lowering each leg in small, rapid kicks.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: leg swing
description: Leg swings are a dynamic bodyweight mobility exercise that targets the hip flexors and improves range of motion through the hip joint. They are commonly used as a warm-up movement to prepare the hips and legs for more demanding exercises.
instructions: Stand next to a wall or sturdy surface and lightly place one hand on it for balance.
Shift your weight onto one foot and allow the other leg to hang freely.
Swing the free leg forward as high as comfortable, then swing it back behind you.
Continue in a smooth, controlled arc without rotating your hips excessively.
Perform the desired repetitions on one leg before switching sides.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/beginner/1471 - leg swing.mp4

name: lying leg raise (bent knees)
description: The lying leg raise with bent knees is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the lower abs and hip flexors. Bending the knees reduces the lever arm compared to a straight-leg raise, making it more accessible while still effectively loading the abdominal muscles.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your hands placed beneath your lower back or flat at your sides.
Bend your knees to approximately 90 degrees and lift your feet slightly off the ground.
Raise your bent legs upward, bringing your knees toward your chest.
Pause at the top, engaging your abs.
Slowly lower your feet back toward the ground without fully resting them and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1472 - lying leg raise (bent knees).mp4

name: bulgarian split squat (forward lean glute focus)
description: The bulgarian split squat with a forward lean is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the glutes. The forward torso lean shifts the load from the quadriceps to the glutes and hamstrings, making it a highly effective posterior chain movement.
instructions: Stand a few feet in front of a bench and place the top of your rear foot on it.
Lean your torso forward slightly to shift emphasis to the glutes.
Lower your hips by bending your front knee until your rear knee nearly touches the floor.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch legs.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1473 - bulgarian split squat (forward lean glute focus).mov

name: seated dumbbell press
description: The seated dumbbell press is a compound shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids and triceps. The seated position stabilizes the torso and eliminates leg drive, placing the full load on the shoulders and making it effective for building overhead pressing strength.
instructions: Sit on an upright bench with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Keep your back flat against the bench and feet planted firmly on the floor.
Press both dumbbells overhead simultaneously until your arms are fully extended.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cross-body knee crunch
description: The cross-body knee crunch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the obliques through a diagonal knee drive from a plank position. The cross-body movement pattern challenges rotational core stability and hip flexor strength simultaneously.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with hands under your shoulders and body straight.
Bring your right knee across your body toward your left elbow.
Return your foot to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, bringing your left knee toward your right elbow.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell farmer carry squat
description: The dumbbell farmer carry squat is a dumbbell compound exercise targeting the quadriceps while also challenging grip strength and core stability. Holding heavy dumbbells at the sides increases spinal loading compared to a goblet squat.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a heavy dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Brace your core and descend into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor, keeping your chest up.
Drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining your grip on the dumbbells.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1476 - dumbbell farmer carry squat.mp4

name: trap bar deadlift
description: The trap bar deadlift is a compound pulling exercise performed with a hexagonal trap bar that allows the lifter to stand inside the implement. The neutral grip and centered load reduce spinal shear forces compared to a straight bar deadlift while effectively targeting the glutes, quads, and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand inside a trap bar with feet hip-width apart, centered between the handles.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to grip both handles at your sides.
Brace your core, flatten your back, and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Drive through your heels to stand upright, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Lower the bar back to the floor with a controlled hip hinge and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell curl
description: The barbell curl is a fundamental upper arm exercise that targets the biceps brachii. Using a barbell allows for heavier loading compared to dumbbells, making it effective for building overall biceps size and strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with a supinated grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of you with your elbows tucked close to your torso.
Curl the barbell upward toward your chest by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell skull crusher
description: The barbell skull crusher is a triceps isolation exercise performed lying on a bench. Lowering the barbell toward the forehead while keeping the upper arms fixed places maximum tension on all three heads of the triceps brachii, making it highly effective for building arm size.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a barbell with an overhand grip, arms extended straight above your chest.
Keep your upper arms perpendicular to the floor and stationary throughout the movement.
Slowly bend your elbows to lower the barbell toward your forehead or just above it.
Pause just before the bar reaches your head, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/1479 - barbell skull crusher.mov

name: b-stance squat hack squat machine
description: The b-stance squat on a hack squat machine is a unilateral variation that places the majority of the load on one leg at a time. The staggered foot position increases single-leg quad and glute demand while the machine provides stability, making it an effective progression toward single-leg training.
instructions: Position yourself on a hack squat machine with one foot flat on the platform and the other foot slightly behind it, resting on the toes only.
Ensure the majority of your weight is loaded through the front foot.
Disengage the safety and lower the sled by bending the front knee until your thigh is parallel to the footplate.
Drive through the heel of your front foot to extend back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching the foot position.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1480 - b-stance squat hack squat machine.mp4

name: goblet cyclist squat
description: The goblet cyclist squat is a kettlebell squat variation that combines a heel elevation with front-loaded weight to maximize quadriceps activation. The elevated heels allow for deep knee flexion and keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
instructions: Hold a kettlebell at chest height and stand with your heels elevated on a plate or wedge, feet close together.
Keeping your chest upright and knees tracking forward, descend into a deep squat.
Lower until your hips are below your knees.
Drive through your elevated heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1481 - goblet cyclist squat.mp4

name: hollow body hold
description: The hollow body hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abdominal muscles through a full-body tension pattern. Maintaining the hollowed position develops deep core strength and is a fundamental gymnastic conditioning exercise.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Press your lower back into the floor and raise your arms, shoulders, and legs simultaneously off the ground.
Hold your arms and legs a few inches above the floor, creating a curved, banana-like body shape.
Keep your chin tucked and core braced.
Hold for the desired duration.

name: seated knee tucks
description: Seated knee tucks are a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles from a bench-seated position. Drawing the knees to the chest while keeping the feet elevated challenges the lower abs and hip flexors.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the sides for support and legs extended in front of you.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor.
Bring your knees toward your chest by contracting your abs.
Pause at the top, then extend your legs back out without letting your feet touch the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable straight arm pulldown
description: The cable straight arm pulldown is a cable isolation exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Performing the pulldown with straight arms removes bicep involvement and directly isolates the lats through the shoulder extension movement.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine and grasp the bar with an overhand grip, arms extended overhead.
Keeping your arms straight, pull the bar down toward your thighs in a wide sweeping arc.
Pause at the bottom of the movement and squeeze your lats.
Slowly return the bar overhead to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: captain's chair leg raise
description: The captain's chair leg raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles using a vertical knee raise station. The supported arm position stabilizes the upper body while the core works to lift the lower body.
instructions: Mount a captain's chair apparatus and support your body on your forearms with your back against the back pad.
Let your legs hang straight down.
Brace your core and raise both knees toward your chest or lift your legs until parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hanging windshield wipers
description: Hanging windshield wipers are an advanced bodyweight exercise targeting the obliques. The demanding side-to-side leg sweep while hanging requires significant core rotational strength and grip endurance.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms fully extended.
Raise your legs until they are parallel to the floor or higher.
Rotate both legs together to the right as far as possible.
Sweep your legs back through center and to the left side.
Continue alternating sides in a windshield wiper motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank jacks
description: Plank jacks are a bodyweight exercise that combines the core stability demand of a plank with the cardiovascular challenge of a jumping jack. This movement targets the abdominal muscles while elevating heart rate.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands under your shoulders and body forming a straight line.
Keeping your core braced, jump both feet out wide simultaneously.
Jump your feet back together.
Continue the jumping jack motion with your legs while maintaining the plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: starfish crunch
description: The starfish crunch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles through a diagonal cross-body crunch from an X-shaped lying position. The movement develops full abdominal and oblique engagement with a wide range of motion.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms and legs extended outward in an X shape.
Simultaneously lift your right arm and left leg, crunching your torso to bring them toward each other.
Touch or reach toward your foot at the top of the movement.
Lower back to the starting X position.
Repeat on the opposite side, lifting your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: back extension (glute focus)
description: The back extension with a glute focus is a machine-based exercise targeting the glutes. By rounding the upper back and consciously driving through the glutes during hip extension, the emphasis shifts from the lower back to the gluteal muscles.
instructions: Position yourself on a hyperextension bench with your hips at the edge of the pad and feet secured.
Cross your arms over your chest or extend them in front.
Round your upper back slightly and focus on driving through your glutes as you extend your hips.
Rise until your body forms a straight line or slightly above parallel.
Slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pistol squat
description: The pistol squat is an advanced bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the quadriceps. The single-leg full-depth squat requires exceptional strength, balance, and mobility and is considered one of the most demanding bodyweight leg exercises.
instructions: Stand on one leg and extend the other leg straight out in front of you.
Brace your core and slowly lower your hips by bending your standing knee.
Descend as deeply as possible while keeping your extended leg off the floor and heel flat.
Drive through your heel to stand back up to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch legs.

name: cossack squat
description: The cossack squat is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hip adductors and inner thighs. The deep lateral squat with an extended opposite leg develops hip mobility, inner thigh flexibility, and single-leg strength.
instructions: Stand with your feet in a wide stance and toes pointed slightly outward.
Shift your weight to your right side and bend your right knee, lowering your hips toward the floor.
Extend your left leg out to the side, keeping it straight with your foot flat on the floor.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your inner thigh.
Drive through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the left side and continue alternating.

name: skater jump
description: The skater jump is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the glutes through a lateral bounding movement. The side-to-side explosive motion develops lateral power, single-leg stability, and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Stand on your right foot with your left foot lifted slightly off the ground.
Bend your right knee and push off explosively to jump laterally to the left.
Land softly on your left foot, swinging your right leg behind you and reaching your right hand toward your left foot.
Immediately push off your left foot and jump back to the right.
Continue alternating sides in a continuous skating motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell single leg step up
description: The dumbbell single leg step up is a dumbbell unilateral exercise targeting the quadriceps. The added load of the dumbbells increases lower body strength demand and helps develop balanced muscular strength between legs.
instructions: Stand in front of a box or bench holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Place one foot flat on the box.
Drive through the heel of your elevated foot to step up, bringing your other foot onto the box.
Step back down with the trailing foot first.
Repeat on the same leg for the desired number of repetitions, then switch.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/1510 - dumbbell single leg step up.mp4

name: split squat jump
description: The split squat jump is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the quadriceps in a stationary lunge stance. Exploding vertically from the lunge develops single-leg power and challenges lower body endurance.
instructions: Stand in a lunge position with one foot forward and one foot back.
Lower into the lunge by bending both knees to 90 degrees.
Explosively jump straight up from the lunge position.
Land softly back in the same lunge stance and immediately lower into the next repetition.
Complete the desired repetitions and switch legs.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/1511 - split squat jump.mp4

name: arm swings (cross body)
description: Cross-body arm swings are a bodyweight dynamic mobility exercise targeting the deltoids and chest. The rhythmic crossing and opening of the arms warms up the shoulder joints, improves flexibility, and activates the chest and upper back.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and extend your arms straight out to the sides.
Swing both arms forward and across your body, crossing them in front of your chest.
Open your arms back out wide to the sides.
Continue the cross and open motion in a controlled swinging rhythm.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pilates ball sit up
description: The pilates ball sit up is a medicine ball core exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. Performing the sit-up on an unstable ball surface increases core activation and also develops balance and spinal stabilizer engagement.
instructions: Sit on a pilates or stability ball with feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Walk your feet forward and lean back until your lower back is supported by the ball.
Place your hands behind your head with elbows pointed out.
Engage your core and curl your upper body off the ball into a crunch or full sit-up.
Slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Gym Female/waist/abs/strength/beginner/1516 - pilates ball sit up.mp4

name: ab wheel rollout (kneeling)
description: The ab wheel rollout (kneeling) is a core strengthening exercise performed from a kneeling position using an ab wheel. It challenges the abs, lower back, and shoulders by requiring full-body tension during a controlled forward and backward rolling motion.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and grip an ab wheel with both hands directly below your shoulders.
Engage your core and slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body toward the ground.
Continue rolling until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor without touching it.
Pause briefly, then use your abs to pull the wheel back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: assisted pull up
description: The assisted pull up is a machine-based upper body exercise that targets the lats and involves secondary engagement of the biceps, rhomboids, and traps. The counterweight support makes it ideal for building pull-up strength progressively.
instructions: Set the desired counterweight on the assisted pull-up machine and kneel or stand on the platform.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your body upward until your chin rises above the bar.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position with full arm extension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable crunch
description: The cable crunch is a weighted core exercise performed at a cable machine using a rope attachment. It targets the abs with constant tension throughout the movement, making it highly effective for building abdominal strength and definition.
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and kneel facing the machine.
Hold the rope behind your head with both hands and position your hips back slightly.
Engage your core and curl your torso downward, bringing your elbows toward your knees.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, fully contracting your abs.
Slowly return to the starting position, keeping tension on the cable throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cross-body mountain climbers
description: Cross-body mountain climbers are a dynamic bodyweight exercise targeting the abs and obliques. By driving each knee to the opposite elbow, the movement adds a rotational element that intensifies core engagement and raises the heart rate.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and drive your right knee toward your left elbow.
Return your right foot to the starting position, then immediately drive your left knee toward your right elbow.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled, rhythmic motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: dead bug
description: The dead bug is a bodyweight core stability exercise that targets the abs while teaching proper spinal bracing. By moving opposite limbs simultaneously, it challenges coordination and deep core control while minimizing lower back stress.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended straight toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees above your hips.
Engage your core, pressing your lower back firmly into the floor.
Slowly lower your right arm overhead and extend your left leg toward the floor simultaneously.
Pause just above the floor, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side, lowering your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: decline bench crunch
description: The decline bench crunch is a bodyweight abdominal exercise performed on a decline bench, which increases the range of motion and intensity compared to a standard crunch. It primarily targets the abs with secondary engagement of the obliques and hip flexors.
instructions: Position yourself on a decline bench with your feet secured under the pads and hands placed behind your head.
Engage your core and curl your upper body upward toward your knees.
Pause at the top of the movement, fully contracting your abs.
Slowly lower your torso back to the starting position without fully relaxing your core.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: decline elbow plank
description: The decline elbow plank is an advanced bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs by elevating the feet above the shoulders. The inclined position increases the demand on the abdominal muscles compared to a standard forearm plank.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and position your feet on an elevated surface such as a bench.
Align your body in a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core fully.
Keep your hips level and avoid raising or dropping your lower back.
Hold this position for the desired duration.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: dumbbell chopper
description: The dumbbell chopper is a rotational core exercise that targets the abs and obliques through a diagonal chopping motion. It also engages the shoulders and legs, making it an effective full-body stabilization movement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell with both hands.
Raise the dumbbell diagonally overhead to one side, rotating your torso slightly.
In a controlled chopping motion, bring the dumbbell diagonally down toward the opposite hip, rotating your torso.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, then return to the starting position.
Complete the desired number of repetitions on one side before switching.

name: dumbbell drag
description: The dumbbell drag is a plank-based core exercise that targets the abs through anti-rotation resistance. By dragging a dumbbell beneath the body, it challenges core stability and shoulder strength simultaneously.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell placed outside your right hand.
Engage your core and keep your hips level throughout the movement.
Reach under your body with your left hand and drag the dumbbell to the left side.
Return your left hand to the plank position and stabilize.
Repeat in the opposite direction, dragging the dumbbell back with your right hand.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: flutter kick
description: The flutter kick is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors through continuous alternating leg movements. Performed lying on the back, it builds lower abdominal endurance and overall core stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and hands placed beneath your lower back for support.
Lift both legs a few inches off the ground, engaging your core.
Alternately raise and lower each leg in a small, controlled fluttering motion.
Keep your lower back pressed toward the floor throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: glute bridge
description: The glute bridge is a foundational bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings through a hip extension movement. It also activates the lower back and abs, making it effective for improving posterior chain strength and hip stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor hip-width apart, and arms at your sides.
Engage your core and press through your heels to lift your hips off the ground.
Drive your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Pause at the top, squeezing your glutes tightly.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: half reverse crunch
description: The half reverse crunch is a bodyweight ab exercise that focuses on the lower abdominal region by curling the hips toward the chest. It provides a controlled range of motion suitable for building foundational core strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed beneath your lower back and legs bent at 90 degrees.
Engage your core and slowly draw your knees toward your chest, lifting your hips slightly off the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement, contracting your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back to the 90-degree starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: half sit ups
description: Half sit ups are a beginner-friendly bodyweight ab exercise that targets the abs through a partial range of motion. By stopping midway, the movement maintains continuous tension on the abdominal muscles while reducing strain on the lower back.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands placed behind your head.
Engage your core and curl your upper body upward until your torso reaches a 45-degree angle.
Pause briefly at the halfway point, keeping your lower back in contact with the floor.
Slowly lower your torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hanging knee raise (captain's chair)
description: The hanging knee raise on a captain's chair is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs and hip flexors. It involves lifting the knees toward the chest from a supported hanging position, building lower abdominal strength and hip flexor control.
instructions: Position yourself on a captain's chair with your forearms resting on the pads and your back against the support.
Hang freely with your legs extended downward.
Engage your core and raise your knees upward toward your chest.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hanging leg raise
description: The hanging leg raise is an advanced bodyweight core exercise performed from a pull-up bar with straight legs. It intensely targets the abs and hip flexors, requiring significant core strength and grip endurance to maintain controlled form.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms fully extended and legs together.
Engage your core and keep a slight bend in your knees throughout the movement.
Raise your straight legs upward until they are parallel to the floor or higher.
Pause at the top, contracting your abs fully.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position without swinging.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hollow body hold
description: The hollow body hold is a foundational bodyweight gymnastics exercise that targets the abs through sustained isometric tension. It requires the entire anterior core to work together to maintain the hollow position while keeping the lower back flat.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Press your lower back firmly into the floor and engage your core.
Lift your shoulders, arms, and legs a few inches off the ground simultaneously.
Create a banana-like curve in your body while keeping your lower back in contact with the floor.
Hold this position for the desired duration, breathing steadily.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: jumping jack
description: The jumping jack is a classic full-body cardio exercise performed using body weight. It elevates the heart rate by combining a lower body jumping movement with a lateral arm raise, making it effective for cardiovascular conditioning and warm-up routines.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump and simultaneously spread your feet wider than shoulder-width while raising both arms overhead.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with your arms above your head.
Immediately jump again, returning your feet together and arms back to your sides.
Continue at a steady rhythm for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: kneeling cable crunch
description: The kneeling cable crunch with a rope attachment behind the neck is a weighted abdominal exercise that provides constant resistance throughout the movement. It targets the abs through a loaded flexion pattern, helping to develop strength and muscle definition in the core.
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and kneel facing the machine.
Hold the rope behind your neck with both hands and position your hips back over your heels.
Engage your core and crunch downward, bringing your elbows toward the floor.
Pause at the fully contracted position, isolating the abs.
Slowly return to the upright starting position while maintaining tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: mountain climber
description: The mountain climber is a dynamic bodyweight exercise that targets the abs while also elevating the heart rate. Performed in a plank position, it combines core stabilization with hip flexor drive, making it an effective conditioning and core strengthening movement.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly below your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and drive your right knee toward your chest.
Quickly return your right foot to the starting position and simultaneously drive your left knee toward your chest.
Continue alternating legs in a running-like motion at a controlled or fast pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: plank hip dip
description: The plank hip dip is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs and obliques through controlled lateral hip rotation. Performed from the forearm plank position, it adds rotational challenge to standard plank stability, increasing oblique activation.
instructions: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows beneath your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your hips to the right, dipping toward the floor.
Return to the center plank position before dipping your hips to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a smooth, controlled motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: russian twist dumbbell
description: The russian twist with a dumbbell is a weighted core exercise targeting the obliques and abs through rotational movement. Holding the dumbbell increases the resistance compared to the bodyweight version, making it effective for building rotational core strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet slightly elevated, and lean back to a 45-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of your chest.
Rotate your torso to the right, bringing the dumbbell beside your right hip.
Return to center and rotate to the left, bringing the dumbbell beside your left hip.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: russian twist
description: The russian twist is a classic bodyweight ab exercise that targets the obliques and core through rotational trunk movement. Performed seated with feet elevated, it challenges balance and rotational strength simultaneously.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly off the ground.
Lean back slightly to a 45-degree angle, keeping your back straight.
Clasp your hands together in front of your chest.
Rotate your torso to the right as far as comfortable, then return to center.
Rotate to the left and return to center.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: scissor kick
description: The scissor kick is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs and hip flexors through continuous alternating leg movement. It builds lower abdominal endurance and hip flexor strength while challenging core stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended and hands placed beneath your lower back for support.
Lift both legs a few inches off the floor and engage your core.
Raise your right leg upward while lowering your left leg, then switch in a scissoring motion.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor and your legs as straight as possible.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: single leg glute bridge
description: The single leg glute bridge is a unilateral bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings through single-leg hip extension. It helps identify and correct strength imbalances between sides while improving hip stability and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Extend one leg straight, keeping your thighs parallel.
Engage your core and press through the heel of the grounded foot to lift your hips.
Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to the raised knee.
Pause at the top, squeezing your glute, then slowly lower your hips back down.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0124 - single leg glute bridge.mp4

name: standard crunch
description: The standard crunch is a foundational bodyweight abdominal exercise that targets the abs through spinal flexion. It is widely used as a core strength builder and can be performed by individuals of all fitness levels.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands placed lightly behind your head.
Engage your core and curl your upper body off the floor toward your knees.
Pause at the top of the movement, contracting your abs.
Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standard plank
description: The standard plank is a fundamental isometric bodyweight exercise targeting the abs and core. Held on the forearms, it simultaneously engages the shoulders, lower back, and glutes, making it one of the most effective exercises for full core stability.
instructions: Place your forearms on the ground with elbows directly below your shoulders.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet.
Align your body in a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core.
Keep your hips level and avoid sagging or raising your lower back.
Hold this position for the desired duration.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: toe touch crunch
description: The toe touch crunch is a bodyweight ab exercise performed with legs vertical. Reaching toward the feet from this elevated position increases the range of motion and demands greater abdominal contraction than a standard crunch.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise your legs straight up perpendicular to the floor.
Extend your arms toward the ceiling.
Engage your core and reach your hands toward your feet by lifting your shoulders off the ground.
Pause at the top of the movement, contracting your abs.
Slowly lower your upper body back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v-up
description: The v-up, also known as a jackknife, is an advanced bodyweight core exercise that simultaneously engages the upper and lower abs. By lifting both the torso and legs together, it creates a full abdominal contraction and challenges hip flexor strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously lift your legs and upper body off the ground, reaching your hands toward your feet.
Form a V-shape with your body at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly, then slowly lower both your arms and legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: alternating dumbbell curl
description: The alternating dumbbell curl is a classic upper arm exercise targeting the biceps. By curling one arm at a time, it allows for focused contraction and better mind-muscle connection while also engaging the forearms as secondary stabilizers.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms fully extended at your sides.
Keeping your elbow stationary, curl the right dumbbell upward toward your shoulder.
Pause at the top and squeeze your bicep, then slowly lower the weight.
Repeat the movement with your left arm.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: archer push up
description: The archer push up is an advanced bodyweight exercise that targets the pectorals by placing more load on one arm at a time. The extended arm provides balance and mild assistance, making it a powerful stepping stone toward single-arm push-up strength.
instructions: Start in a wide push-up position with your hands significantly wider than shoulder-width.
Extend one arm straight out to the side, parallel to the ground.
Lower your body toward the bent arm, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Push back up to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, extending the opposite arm.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: arms stretch
description: The arms stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the deltoids and upper back. It involves a cross-body shoulder stretch that improves flexibility and range of motion in the shoulder joint, commonly used as part of a warm-up or cool-down routine.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm straight across your chest at shoulder height.
Use the opposite hand to gently pull the extended arm closer to your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20 to 30 seconds, feeling the tension in your shoulder.
Release and repeat on the opposite side.

name: barbell curl narrow grip
description: The barbell curl with a narrow grip targets the biceps with emphasis on the long head of the bicep. The close hand placement shifts focus to the outer portion of the biceps, helping to build peak and overall upper arm thickness.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell with a supinated grip narrower than shoulder-width.
Hold the barbell with arms fully extended in front of your thighs.
Keeping your elbows tucked in, curl the barbell upward toward your chest.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0132 - barbell curl narrow grip.mp4

name: barbell upright row
description: The barbell upright row is a compound shoulder and trap exercise targeting the lateral deltoids and upper trapezius. By pulling the bar vertically toward the chin, it effectively develops upper body width and shoulder thickness.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly narrower than shoulder-width.
Hold the barbell in front of your thighs with arms extended.
Pull the barbell upward toward your chin, leading with your elbows.
