Keep the barbell close to your body and raise your elbows above shoulder height.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: biceps curl
description: The biceps curl is a fundamental upper arm exercise targeting the biceps through a controlled flexion movement. Using dumbbells, it allows equal strength development on both sides and is foundational for building arm size and strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended and palms facing forward.
Keeping your elbows close to your torso, curl both dumbbells upward toward your shoulders.
Pause at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bicycle crunches
description: Bicycle crunches are a bodyweight ab exercise targeting the abs and obliques through a combined crunch and rotation movement. The pedaling action adds dynamic engagement to both the upper and lower abdominal regions.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands lightly placed behind your head and knees bent.
Lift your shoulders off the ground and raise your knees to a 90-degree angle.
Extend your right leg while simultaneously rotating your torso to bring your right elbow toward your left knee.
Switch sides, extending the left leg and bringing the left elbow toward the right knee.
Continue alternating in a smooth pedaling motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable face pull
description: The cable face pull with a rope attachment is a shoulder health and strength exercise targeting the rear deltoids, traps, and rhomboids. It improves shoulder stability, posture, and external rotation strength, making it a staple in balanced upper body training.
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and set the pulley at face height.
Grasp the rope with both hands and step back to create tension in the cable.
Pull the rope toward your face, separating your hands and driving your elbows out to the sides.
Pause at the end of the movement, squeezing your rear delts and upper back.
Slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable lateral raise
description: The cable lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise that targets the lateral deltoids using constant cable tension. Unlike dumbbell raises, the cable maintains resistance at the bottom of the movement, providing more consistent muscle stimulation throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Stand next to a low cable pulley and attach a single handle.
Grasp the handle with the hand farthest from the machine and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
With a slight bend in your elbow, raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions on one side before switching.

name: cable pull
description: The cable pull is a versatile back exercise targeting the lats through a pulling motion using a cable machine. The adjustable pulley angle allows for various angles of lat activation, making it effective for comprehensive back development.
instructions: Attach a handle to the cable machine and set the pulley at the desired height.
Grasp the handle and position yourself with feet shoulder-width apart and slight knee bend.
Pull the handle toward your torso by driving your elbow back and down.
Pause at the fully contracted position, squeezing your back muscles.
Slowly return to the starting position with a controlled extension.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar attachment)
description: The cable triceps pushdown with a v-bar attachment is a classic upper arm exercise targeting the triceps. The v-bar allows a natural wrist position, reducing joint stress while delivering consistent tension throughout the pushdown movement.
instructions: Attach a v-bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, elbows tucked close to your sides.
With your upper arms stationary, push the bar downward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause at the bottom, squeezing your triceps.
Slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0139 - cable triceps pushdown (v-bar attachment).mp4

name: cable overhead triceps extension
description: The cable overhead triceps extension targets the long head of the triceps through a full stretch at the bottom position. Using a low cable provides constant tension, making it highly effective for overall triceps development and arm thickness.
instructions: Attach a rope to the low pulley of a cable machine and face away from the machine.
Hold the rope overhead with both hands, elbows bent and pointing toward the ceiling.
Extend your arms fully overhead while keeping your elbows stationary.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Slowly lower the rope back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0140 - cable overhead triceps extension.mp4

name: close grip barbell bench press
description: The close grip barbell bench press is a compound exercise that emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the pectorals and shoulders. The narrow grip shifts the majority of the pressing load onto the triceps, making it effective for building upper arm pressing strength.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grip the barbell with hands slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and hold it directly above your chest with arms extended.
Lower the barbell slowly toward your lower chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: concentration curl
description: The concentration curl is a seated dumbbell exercise that isolates the biceps by bracing the elbow against the inner thigh. This position eliminates momentum and forces pure biceps contraction, making it highly effective for developing bicep peak and definition.
instructions: Sit on a bench and lean forward slightly, resting your right elbow against the inside of your right thigh.
Hold a dumbbell in your right hand with your arm fully extended.
Curl the dumbbell upward toward your shoulder by flexing your elbow.
Pause at the top and squeeze your bicep fully.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.

name: cross-body hammer curl
description: The cross-body hammer curl is a dumbbell exercise that targets the biceps and brachialis through a neutral-grip diagonal curl. The cross-body path increases forearm and brachialis engagement, contributing to overall arm thickness and grip strength.
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended and palms facing your torso.
Curl the right dumbbell diagonally across your body toward your left shoulder.
Pause at the top and squeeze the bicep.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat with the left arm curling toward the right shoulder.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: diamond push-up
description: The diamond push-up is an advanced bodyweight exercise that shifts emphasis onto the triceps by placing the hands in a narrow diamond formation. It also targets the inner chest and shoulders, making it a highly efficient upper-body movement.
instructions: Start in a push-up position and place your hands together beneath your chest, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward your hands by bending your elbows.
Pause just above the floor, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dips
description: Dips are a bodyweight compound exercise primarily targeting the triceps, with significant secondary activation of the chest and shoulders. They are one of the most effective movements for building upper body pressing strength and overall arm development.
instructions: Grip the parallel bars and support your body weight with arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Keep your elbows close to your body to focus the movement on the triceps.
Pause at the bottom of the movement.
Press yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell triceps kickback
description: The dumbbell triceps kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps through elbow extension in a hinged position. By keeping the upper arm stationary and fixed, it forces the triceps to perform the full range of motion without assistance.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in your right hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Brace your right elbow against your side with the upper arm parallel to the floor.
Extend your forearm back and upward until your arm is fully straight.
Pause at the top, squeezing your tricep.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: dumbbell biceps curl up
description: The dumbbell biceps curl up is a standard bilateral biceps curl exercise targeting the biceps through simultaneous elbow flexion. It is effective for building bicep size and symmetry while also engaging the forearms for stability.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in both hands with palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Curl both dumbbells simultaneously upward toward your shoulders.
Keep your elbows stationary and close to your sides throughout the movement.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0147 - dumbbell biceps curl up.mp4

name: dumbbell forearms curl
description: The dumbbell forearms curl is a wrist flexion exercise targeting the forearm muscles. Performed seated with forearms braced on the thighs, it effectively isolates the wrist flexors to build forearm strength and grip endurance.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench and rest your forearms on your thighs, palms facing upward.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your wrists just beyond your knees.
Curl the dumbbells upward by flexing your wrists.
Pause at the top of the movement, contracting your forearm muscles.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell hammer curl
description: The dumbbell hammer curl is a bicep and brachialis exercise using a neutral grip, which places equal emphasis on the biceps and the brachialis. The neutral wrist position also reduces stress on the elbow joint while building overall arm thickness.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells in each hand with palms facing your torso in a neutral grip.
Keeping your elbows stationary and close to your sides, curl the dumbbells upward simultaneously.
Pause at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps and forearms.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell skull crusher
description: The dumbbell skull crusher is a triceps isolation exercise performed lying on a bench. By lowering the dumbbells toward the temples with controlled elbow flexion, it provides a deep stretch and full contraction of the triceps, making it highly effective for arm development.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended directly above your chest.
Keeping your upper arms perpendicular to the floor, bend your elbows to lower the dumbbells toward your temples.
Pause just above your head, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Keep your elbows pointed at the ceiling throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell waiter curl
description: The dumbbell waiter curl is a biceps exercise in which both hands hold a single dumbbell in a waiter-carry position. The supinated grip and fixed wrist angle intensify the squeeze at the top and increase long head biceps activation.
instructions: Stand holding one dumbbell with both hands, cupping the upper weight plate with palms facing upward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides.
Curl the dumbbell upward toward your chin by flexing your elbows.
Pause at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell weighted arm circle
description: Dumbbell weighted arm circles are a shoulder endurance and mobility exercise targeting the deltoids through circular rotational motion. Adding light dumbbells increases the challenge on the shoulder stabilizers and rotator cuff, improving joint health and muscular endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a light dumbbell in each hand, arms extended at your sides.
Raise your arms to shoulder height and make small circular motions forward.
Gradually increase the size of the circles over several repetitions.
Reverse the direction and perform the same number of circles backward.
Lower your arms and rest before repeating.

name: dynamic stretch
description: The dynamic stretch is a movement-based warm-up exercise targeting the deltoids and upper back. Unlike static stretching, it uses momentum to increase range of motion and blood flow, making it ideal for preparing the shoulders for exercise.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and extend your arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
Swing both arms forward across your chest, then immediately swing them back out to the sides.
Continue the swinging motion in a controlled, fluid manner.
Gradually increase the range of motion over several repetitions.
Repeat for the desired duration.

name: ez bar biceps curl
description: The ez bar biceps curl is a classic upper arm exercise that targets the biceps using an angled bar to reduce wrist and elbow strain. The ergonomic grip allows for a more comfortable curl pattern while maintaining effective bicep activation.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the ez bar at the inner angled grips with a supinated grip.
Let the bar hang at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Curl the bar upward toward your shoulders, keeping your elbows stationary.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0154 - ez bar biceps curl.mp4

name: ez bar curl
description: The ez bar curl is a biceps exercise using the angled ez bar for a neutral wrist-friendly grip. It builds bicep mass and strength while reducing stress on the wrists compared to a straight barbell curl.
instructions: Stand upright holding an ez bar with a shoulder-width semi-supinated grip.
Allow the bar to hang at arm's length in front of your hips.
Curl the bar toward your shoulders while keeping your elbows close to your torso.
Pause at the top, contracting your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar back to the fully extended position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: high plank
description: The high plank is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the abs and core while performed on extended arms. Compared to the forearm plank, it places additional demand on the shoulders and triceps, making it a versatile full-body stability movement.
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands directly below your shoulders and arms fully extended.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and quads to maintain a rigid position.
Avoid sagging your hips or raising your lower back.
Hold for the desired duration.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: machine seated row
description: The machine seated row is a back exercise targeting the lats and rhomboids using a cable or plate-loaded row machine. The seated position allows for controlled loading and consistent tension, making it effective for developing mid-back thickness and strength.
instructions: Sit at the seated row machine with your feet on the footpads and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip and sit upright.
Pull the handles toward your torso, driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the fully contracted position.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: meadows row
description: The meadows row is a unilateral landmine exercise that targets the lats through a single-arm rowing movement. The angled pulling direction allows for a greater range of motion and stretch on the lat at the bottom of the movement compared to traditional rows.
instructions: Position a barbell in a landmine attachment or corner of the room.
Stand perpendicular to the bar and grip the end of the barbell with one hand using an overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips to a roughly 45-degree torso angle with a slight bend in the knees.
Pull the end of the bar upward toward your hip, driving your elbow back.
Pause at the top, squeezing your lat and upper back.
Slowly lower the bar to the starting position and repeat before switching sides.

name: preacher curl machine
description: The preacher curl machine is a biceps isolation exercise that uses a preacher pad to eliminate shoulder involvement. The fixed arm position maximizes bicep contraction throughout the movement and is particularly effective for developing lower bicep fullness.
instructions: Sit at the preacher curl machine and adjust the seat so your upper arms rest flat on the angled pad.
Grasp the handles with a supinated grip and arms fully extended.
Curl the handles upward toward your shoulders by flexing your elbows.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the handles back to the fully extended starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: push up arm lift
description: The push up arm lift combines a standard push-up with an alternating forward arm raise, targeting the triceps and pectorals while adding a balance challenge. The single-arm lift phase engages the core and shoulders for anti-rotation stability.
instructions: Start in a standard push-up position with hands below your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Perform a push-up, lowering your chest to the floor and pressing back up.
At the top of the push-up, shift your weight to your left hand and lift your right arm forward.
Hold for a moment, then return your right hand to the floor.
Perform another push-up and repeat the arm lift on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0160 - push up arm lift.mp4

name: resistance band biceps curl
description: The resistance band biceps curl is a portable and joint-friendly biceps exercise using an elastic band for resistance. The increasing band tension as the arm curls creates a unique strength curve that effectively builds bicep strength and endurance.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, holding one end in each hand.
Start with arms fully extended at your sides and palms facing forward.
Curl both handles upward toward your shoulders by flexing your elbows.
Pause at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0161 - resistance band biceps curl.mp4

name: resistance band overhead triceps extension
description: The resistance band overhead triceps extension targets the long head of the triceps through an overhead extension movement. The band provides progressive resistance, making it an accessible and effective option for triceps development.
instructions: Stand on one end of a resistance band and hold the other end overhead with both hands.
Position your elbows bent and pointing toward the ceiling behind your head.
Extend your arms fully overhead by straightening your elbows.
Pause at the top and squeeze your triceps.
Slowly lower the band back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0162 - resistance band overhead triceps extension.mp4

name: seated cable row
description: The seated cable row is a foundational back exercise targeting the lats and mid-back through a horizontal pulling motion. The cable provides constant tension, making it effective for building back thickness and improving posture.
instructions: Sit on the seated cable row bench with feet on the platform and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the v-bar or cable handles and sit upright with a neutral spine.
Pull the handles toward your lower abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the fully contracted position.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated lateral raise
description: The seated lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise targeting the lateral deltoids while seated to reduce the temptation to use momentum. It develops shoulder width and is a key movement for well-rounded shoulder development.
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs, palms facing inward.
Engage your core and maintain an upright torso.
Raise both arms out to the sides until they are at shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to your thighs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated overhead dumbbell extension
description: The seated overhead dumbbell extension is a triceps isolation exercise performed seated to minimize momentum. By lowering the dumbbell behind the head, it stretches the long head of the triceps for a full range of motion and maximizes muscle activation.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and hold a single dumbbell with both hands overhead.
Grip the dumbbell by cupping the upper weight plate with both palms.
Keeping your upper arms stationary and elbows pointing forward, lower the dumbbell behind your head.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your triceps.
Press the dumbbell back to the overhead starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0165 - seated overhead dumbbell extension.mp4

name: seated shoulder press machine
description: The seated shoulder press machine is a guided overhead pressing exercise targeting the deltoids. The machine provides a fixed movement path, making it accessible for beginners and ideal for isolating shoulder pressing strength with reduced stabilization demands.
instructions: Adjust the seat height on the shoulder press machine so the handles are at shoulder level.
Sit with your back firmly against the pad and grasp the handles with an overhand grip.
Press the handles upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated triceps dip machine
description: The seated triceps dip machine is a guided exercise targeting the triceps through a downward pressing motion. The machine eliminates the need for full body weight support, allowing users to isolate and load the triceps with controlled resistance.
instructions: Sit at the triceps dip machine and adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest level.
Grip the handles and position your arms with elbows bent at 90 degrees.
Press the handles downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Slowly allow the handles to rise back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0167 - seated triceps dip machine.mp4

name: side plank one arm lift
description: The side plank one arm lift is an advanced core exercise targeting the obliques through a rotational movement from a lateral plank position. Threading the arm under the body increases oblique and shoulder engagement while maintaining lateral stability.
instructions: Start in a side plank position, resting on your right forearm with your body forming a straight diagonal line.
Extend your left arm straight toward the ceiling.
Keeping your hips elevated, lower your left arm down and thread it underneath your body.
Return your left arm back to the raised position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.

name: single arm high cable curl
description: The single arm high cable curl is a biceps isolation exercise performed at a high pulley to target the short head of the biceps. The elevated cable angle creates a unique tension profile that differs from standard curls, providing a strong peak contraction.
instructions: Set a single handle on the high pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine and grasp the handle with one hand, arm fully extended at shoulder height.
Curl your forearm toward your bicep while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause at the fully contracted position, squeezing your bicep.
Slowly extend your arm back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0169 - single arm high cable curl.mp4

name: standing barbell extension
description: The standing barbell extension is a triceps mass-building exercise targeting the long head through a full overhead range of motion. Performing the movement standing engages the core for stabilization while maximizing triceps stretch and contraction.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell overhead with a close overhand grip.
Keep your upper arms stationary and elbows pointing forward.
Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause at the bottom with a full stretch in your triceps.
Press the barbell back to the overhead starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing barbell overhead extension
description: The standing barbell overhead extension is a compound triceps exercise that emphasizes the long head through a full overhead range of motion. The standing position requires core engagement for stability, making it an effective multi-muscle movement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and press a barbell overhead with both hands using a grip slightly narrower than shoulder-width.
Keep your core braced and upper arms perpendicular to the floor.
Slowly bend your elbows to lower the barbell behind your head.
Pause at the bottom, feeling the stretch in your triceps.
Extend your arms back to the overhead starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0171 - standing barbell overhead extension.mp4

name: standing cable concentration curls
description: Standing cable concentration curls isolate the biceps using a low cable pulley, mimicking the mechanics of a dumbbell concentration curl with the added benefit of constant cable tension. The cross-body pulling angle increases inner bicep activation.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley of a cable machine.
Stand beside the machine and grip the handle with one hand, allowing your arm to fully extend across your body.
Curl the handle upward toward your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause at the top and squeeze your bicep.
Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0172 - standing cable concentration curls.mp4

name: standing single arm overhead extension
description: The standing single arm overhead extension is a unilateral triceps exercise that isolates each arm individually using a dumbbell. The overhead position stretches the long head of the triceps, making it effective for developing arm length and size.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Press the dumbbell overhead, then bend your elbow to lower it behind your head.
Keep your upper arm stationary and close to your head.
Extend your arm back to the starting overhead position.
Pause at the top and squeeze your tricep.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0173 - standing single arm overhead extension.mp4

name: standing supinated bicep curl
description: The standing supinated bicep curl uses a fully supinated grip to maximize biceps brachii activation. This grip position places the biceps in the strongest mechanical position throughout the curl, making it one of the most effective bicep strengthening exercises.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with a fully supinated grip, palms facing upward.
Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your hips.
Curl the barbell upward toward your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Lower the barbell back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0174 - standing supinated bicep curl.mp4

name: straight barbell curl
description: The straight barbell curl is a classic mass-building biceps exercise that allows for heavy loading through a standard curl pattern. The straight bar provides a challenging wrist supination position that maximizes bicep engagement.
instructions: Stand holding a straight barbell with an underhand grip at shoulder-width.
Allow the bar to hang at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Curl the bar upward toward your chest, keeping your elbows stationary.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: tate press
description: The tate press is a triceps isolation exercise performed lying on a bench with dumbbells. The wide elbow position distinguishes it from standard presses, placing a unique demand on the lateral and medial heads of the triceps.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above your chest.
Point your elbows out to the sides and lower the dumbbells toward your chest by bending your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells until they touch your chest with elbows flared wide.
Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: triceps cable pushdown (rope attachment)
description: The triceps cable pushdown with a rope attachment targets all three heads of the triceps through a downward pressing motion. Spreading the rope ends at the bottom increases the range of contraction and develops triceps thickness and definition.
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley of a cable machine and stand facing it.
Grasp the rope with both hands and position your elbows at your sides at 90 degrees.
Push the rope downward by extending your elbows, spreading the rope ends apart at the bottom.
Pause with your arms fully extended and triceps contracted.
Slowly return to the starting position allowing your elbows to flex.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0177 - triceps cable pushdown (rope attachment).mp4

name: triceps cable pushdown (straight bar attachment)
description: The triceps cable pushdown with a straight bar attachment is a classic triceps exercise providing consistent resistance through the full range of motion. The pronated grip emphasizes the medial and lateral tricep heads, contributing to arm definition.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine and grip the bar with an overhand grip, elbows tucked to your sides.
Push the bar downward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause at the bottom, squeezing your triceps.
Slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0178 - triceps cable pushdown (straight bar attachment).mp4

name: triceps bench dip
description: The triceps bench dip is a bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps through a vertical dipping motion with hands on a bench. It is an accessible exercise that builds triceps strength and muscle endurance for beginners and can be progressed by elevating the feet.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench and place your hands beside your hips with fingers pointing forward.
Slide your hips off the bench and extend your legs in front of you.
Lower your body by bending your elbows to roughly 90 degrees.
Pause at the bottom, then press back up by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wide grip barbell curl
description: The wide grip barbell curl targets the short head of the biceps by using a wider-than-shoulder-width grip. This inner bicep emphasis contributes to overall bicep width and is an effective complement to close-grip curl variations.
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with a supinated grip wider than shoulder-width.
Let the bar hang at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Curl the bar upward toward your shoulders, keeping your elbows stationary.
Pause at the top and contract your biceps fully.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0180 - wide grip barbell curl.mp4

name: wide grip seated cable row
description: The wide grip seated cable row uses a straight bar with a wide overhand grip to emphasize the upper lats and rear deltoids. The wide grip changes the pulling angle compared to a narrow grip row, targeting the outer back for improved width.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley cable and sit with feet on the platform, knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder-width.
Sit upright and pull the bar toward your lower chest, driving your elbows wide.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the contracted position.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: yates row
description: The yates row is a bent-over row variation using an underhand grip performed at a more upright torso angle than a standard row. The underhand grip and angle shift emphasis to the lower lats and biceps, making it effective for overall back thickness.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and grip a barbell with an underhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hinge forward at the hips to approximately a 70-degree upright angle, more upright than a standard bent over row.
Pull the barbell toward your lower abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Pause at the contracted position, squeezing your lats.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: zottman curl
description: The zottman curl is a dumbbell exercise that targets the biceps on the way up and the forearm extensors and brachialis on the way down by rotating the wrist at the top. It is one of the most efficient exercises for developing complete arm thickness.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward.
Curl the dumbbells upward toward your shoulders as in a standard curl.
At the top of the movement, rotate your wrists so your palms face downward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells in this pronated position.
At the bottom, rotate your wrists back to the supinated starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: 45-degree hyperextension machine
description: The 45-degree hyperextension machine is a back extension exercise targeting the lower back erectors and glutes. The angled pad supports the hips at 45 degrees, allowing a full range of motion for strengthening the posterior chain and improving spinal extension strength.
instructions: Position yourself on the hyperextension machine with your hips resting at the top of the pad and feet secured under the footrests.
Cross your arms over your chest or place them behind your head.
Lower your torso downward toward the floor by hinging at the hips.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, feeling the stretch in your lower back.
Raise your torso back to the starting position by contracting your lower back and glutes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded overhead press
description: The banded overhead press is a shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids using resistance band tension for progressive resistance. It builds overhead pressing strength and shoulder stability while being gentle on the joints compared to barbell or dumbbell variations.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band at shoulder height with palms facing forward and elbows bent.
Press the band overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your hands back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded pull-apart
description: The banded pull-apart is a resistance band exercise targeting the rhomboids and rear deltoids. It improves shoulder health, posture, and upper back strength by training the scapular retractors through a horizontal pulling motion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height, arms extended in front of you.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and pull the band apart by moving your hands outward toward your sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the fully extended position.
Slowly return your hands back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded seated row
description: The banded seated row is a resistance band back exercise targeting the lats and rhomboids through a horizontal pulling motion. With the band anchored to the feet, it replicates a cable row without requiring any equipment, making it ideal for home workouts.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and loop a resistance band around your feet.
Hold the band with both hands, arms extended and back upright.
Pull the band toward your lower abdomen by driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the contracted position.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bent over barbell row
description: The bent over barbell row is one of the most effective compound back exercises, targeting the lats and upper back while also engaging the lower back as a stabilizer. The overhand grip emphasizes the upper and outer portions of the back.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor, keeping a neutral spine.
Allow the barbell to hang at arm's length below your chest.
Pull the barbell toward your lower ribcage by driving your elbows upward.
Pause at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position and repeat.

name: bent over dumbbell row
description: The bent over dumbbell row is a bilateral back exercise targeting the lats and rhomboids through a two-arm pulling motion. Using dumbbells allows for a greater range of motion and helps correct strength imbalances between sides.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Hinge forward at the hips to bring your torso close to parallel with the floor, maintaining a neutral spine.
Let the dumbbells hang at arm's length below your chest.
Pull both dumbbells toward your hips simultaneously by driving your elbows back.
Pause at the contracted position, squeezing your lats.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat.

name: cat cow back stretch
description: The cat cow back stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back and spine through alternating extension and flexion. It improves spinal flexibility, reduces tension, and is commonly used in warm-up and rehabilitation routines.
instructions: Start on all fours with your wrists directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Inhale and drop your belly toward the floor while lifting your chest and tailbone upward (cow position).
Exhale and round your back toward the ceiling, tucking your chin and tailbone (cat position).
Continue alternating between cat and cow positions in a slow, controlled rhythm.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.
video_path: 200 Workouts/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0190 - cat cow back stretch.mp4

name: conventional deadlift
description: The conventional deadlift is a fundamental compound movement targeting the lower back, glutes, and hamstrings. It is one of the most effective exercises for developing total posterior chain strength and is a cornerstone of strength training programs.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart with the barbell over your mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and grip the bar just outside your legs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Engage your core, brace your back flat, and push the floor away to begin the lift.
Drive your hips forward as the bar passes your knees, finishing in a standing position.
Pause at the top with hips fully extended.
Lower the bar back to the floor under control and repeat.

name: good morning
description: The good morning is a barbell hip hinge exercise targeting the lower back erectors and hamstrings. It builds posterior chain strength and reinforces proper hip hinge mechanics, making it valuable for athletic performance and deadlift improvement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and position a barbell across your upper back.
Keep your back flat and engage your core.
With a slight bend in your knees, hinge at the hips and lower your torso toward parallel with the floor.
Pause at the bottom, feeling the stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to return to the upright starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: gorilla row
description: The gorilla row is a bilateral hinged dumbbell rowing exercise that alternates between arms while using the opposite dumbbell for balance. It effectively targets the lats and builds back thickness while challenging core stability throughout the movement.
instructions: Place two dumbbells on the floor and hinge forward, gripping both dumbbells while maintaining a flat back.
Row the right dumbbell up toward your hip while using the left dumbbell as a support handle.
Lower the right dumbbell and immediately row the left dumbbell upward.
Continue alternating rows in a rhythmic manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: inverted row
description: The inverted row is a bodyweight back exercise targeting the lats and rhomboids through a horizontal pulling motion. It is a highly scalable movement that builds pulling strength and upper back thickness without requiring a pull-up bar.
instructions: Set a barbell or rings at waist height and lie underneath it.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip, shoulder-width apart, and hang with arms fully extended.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Pull your chest up to the bar by driving your elbows back.
Pause at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower yourself back to the starting position and repeat.

name: pendlay row
description: The pendlay row is a strict barbell row variation where the bar returns to the floor between each repetition, eliminating momentum. This dead-stop technique demands greater power and full lat engagement on each pull, making it highly effective for back strength development.
instructions: Stand over a barbell with feet shoulder-width apart and hinge forward until your torso is parallel to the floor.
Grip the barbell with an overhand grip at shoulder-width with arms fully extended.
From a dead stop, explosively row the barbell to your lower chest.
Lower the barbell completely back to the floor between each repetition.
Reset your position, then pull again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank to downward dog
description: Plank to downward dog is a bodyweight mobility and strength exercise that transitions between a plank and an inverted V position. It stretches the lower back and hamstrings while strengthening the shoulders and core through the transition.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Push your hips up and back into a downward dog position, forming an inverted V shape.
Press your heels toward the floor and lengthen through your spine.
Pause at the top, feeling the stretch through your back and hamstrings.
Return to the plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0196 - plank to downward dog.mp4

name: prone back extension
description: The prone back extension is a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back erectors through a spinal extension movement. Performed lying face down, it is commonly used in rehabilitation and warm-up routines to strengthen the posterior chain.
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with arms extended overhead or placed beside your body.
Engage your glutes and lower back to lift your chest and upper body off the floor.
Keep your neck neutral and avoid overextending.
Pause at the top, squeezing your lower back and glutes.
Slowly lower yourself back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rear delt dumbbell fly
description: The rear delt dumbbell fly is a back and shoulder exercise targeting the rhomboids and posterior deltoids through a wide pulling motion. Performed in the bent-over position, it improves upper back posture and shoulder health.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Let the dumbbells hang below your chest with palms facing each other.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise both arms out to the sides until they are at shoulder height.
Pause at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: renegade row
description: The renegade row is a challenging plank-position dumbbell exercise that targets the lats while simultaneously demanding core anti-rotation stability. It combines upper back strength with full-body tension, making it an efficient compound movement.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with a dumbbell in each hand, hands under your shoulders.
Keep your feet wider than hip-width for stability and engage your core.
Row the right dumbbell toward your right hip, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Lower the dumbbell and repeat the row on the left side.
Continue alternating rows while maintaining a stable plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band deadlift
description: The resistance band deadlift replicates the hip hinge pattern of a conventional deadlift using band resistance. It targets the lower back, glutes, and hamstrings while being accessible for home workouts or warm-up activation work.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the band at your sides with palms facing inward and hinge forward at the hips.
Keep your back flat and chest tall throughout the movement.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips to stand upright.
Pause at the top with hips fully extended.
Slowly hinge back down to the starting position and repeat.

name: resistance band lat pulldown
description: The resistance band lat pulldown replicates the cable lat pulldown using an overhead-anchored band. It targets the lats and biceps through a vertical pulling motion and is effective for building back width at home or as a warm-up exercise.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band to a high point overhead.
Kneel or sit facing the anchor and grasp the band with both hands wider than shoulder-width.
Pull the band down toward your upper chest while driving your elbows toward your sides.
Pause at the bottom, squeezing your lats.
Slowly allow the band to rise back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band w-raise
description: The resistance band w-raise is an upper back and shoulder health exercise targeting the rhomboids and rear deltoids. The W arm position maximizes scapular retraction and external rotation, improving posture and rotator cuff stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ends of a resistance band with both hands.
Raise your arms in front of you at shoulder height with elbows slightly bent.
Pull the band apart and back, bending your elbows to form a W shape with your arms.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: single arm dumbbell row
description: The single arm dumbbell row is a foundational unilateral back exercise targeting the lats through a supported one-arm pulling motion. The bench support allows heavier loading and better range of motion while minimizing lower back strain.
instructions: Place your right knee and right hand on a bench for support and hold a dumbbell in your left hand.
Let the dumbbell hang directly below your shoulder with arm fully extended.
Row the dumbbell upward toward your left hip by driving your elbow back.
Pause at the top, squeezing your lat.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: single leg deadlift
description: The single leg deadlift is a unilateral hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings and glutes while challenging balance and hip stability. It is effective for correcting strength imbalances between legs and improving proprioception.
instructions: Stand on one foot with a slight bend in the supporting knee.
Hinge forward at the hip, extending your free leg behind you as your torso descends.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the floor while keeping your back flat.
Pause at the bottom, feeling the stretch in your hamstring.
Return to the upright position by driving your hip forward.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching legs.

name: superman hold
description: The superman hold is a bodyweight posterior chain exercise targeting the lower back erectors through a prone hyperextension hold. Lifting both arms and legs simultaneously engages the entire back and glutes, building spinal extension endurance.
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor.
Squeeze your glutes and lower back at the top of the movement.
Hold the position for 2 to 3 seconds.
Slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: t-bar row
description: The t-bar row is a landmine barbell exercise targeting the lats, rhomboids, and traps through a close-grip rowing motion. The angled pulling direction and neutral grip provide a unique stimulus for building back thickness and density.
instructions: Position a barbell in a landmine attachment and load weight plates on the free end.
Straddle the bar and grip the handle attachment with both hands.
Hinge forward until your torso is at approximately a 45-degree angle.
Pull the bar toward your chest by driving your elbows back.
Pause at the contracted position, squeezing your back muscles.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position and repeat.

name: upper back kick
description: The upper back kick is a bodyweight stability exercise targeting the rhomboids and upper back through simultaneous opposite limb extension. It also improves coordination, balance, and core stability by requiring full-body tension on all fours.
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Extend your right arm forward and your left leg back simultaneously.
Hold for a moment, squeezing your upper back muscles.
Return to the starting position and switch sides.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wide grip pull-up
description: The wide grip pull-up is an advanced bodyweight exercise targeting the lats through a vertical pulling motion with a wide overhand grip. The wider grip increases lat activation and reduces biceps contribution, making it highly effective for developing back width.
instructions: Grip a pull-up bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder-width.
Hang with arms fully extended and engage your core.
Pull your body upward by driving your elbows down toward your hips.
Continue pulling until your chin rises above the bar.
Pause at the top, squeezing your lats.
Slowly lower yourself back to the dead hang position and repeat.

name: bear crawl
description: The bear crawl is a full-body cardio and strength exercise performed in a low quadruped position. It elevates heart rate while engaging the shoulders, core, and legs simultaneously, making it an effective conditioning and functional movement exercise.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees hovering an inch above the floor.
Engage your core and keep your back flat.
Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously.
Follow with your left hand and right foot, maintaining a low, controlled crawling position.
Continue forward for the desired distance or duration.

name: box jump
description: The box jump is a plyometric exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings through an explosive jumping movement. It builds lower body power, reactive strength, and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box with feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and swing your arms back to prepare for the jump.
Explosively jump upward and forward onto the box, landing softly with knees bent.
Stand fully upright on the box.
Step down carefully and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: burpee
description: The burpee is a high-intensity full-body cardio exercise that combines a squat, plank, push-up, and jump into one fluid movement. It rapidly elevates heart rate and builds muscular endurance across the entire body.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Squat down and place your hands on the floor in front of your feet.
Jump or step your feet back into a high plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back toward your hands.
Explosively jump upward and raise your arms overhead.
Land softly and immediately repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: butt kick
description: Butt kicks are a cardio and hamstring activation exercise performed by jogging in place with an exaggerated heel-to-glute kick. They are commonly used as a dynamic warm-up or cardio drill to engage the hamstrings and elevate the heart rate.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and begin jogging in place.
Increase the range of motion by kicking your heels up toward your glutes with each step.
Pump your arms in coordination with your legs.
Maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

name: high knee
description: High knees are a dynamic cardio exercise that combines running in place with exaggerated knee lifts. They engage the hip flexors and core while building cardiovascular endurance and lower body coordination.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and begin jogging in place.
Drive your right knee upward toward your chest as high as possible.
Quickly switch legs, driving your left knee upward.
Pump your arms in coordination with each knee drive.
Continue alternating at a brisk pace for the desired duration.

name: jump rope
description: Jump rope is a classic cardio exercise that targets cardiovascular endurance while also engaging the calves, shoulders, and forearms. It improves footwork, coordination, and timing while providing an efficient full-body workout.
instructions: Hold the jump rope handles with one in each hand and position the rope behind your feet.
Begin rotating the rope overhead and jump as it passes beneath your feet.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with a slight bend in your knees.
Maintain a consistent rhythm and keep your wrists doing most of the rotation work.
Continue for the desired duration or number of jumps.

name: kick boxing
description: Kickboxing is a high-intensity cardio workout that combines boxing punches and martial arts kicks. It develops cardiovascular fitness, coordination, agility, and total body conditioning through fast-paced offensive and defensive combinations.
instructions: Stand in a fighting stance with one foot slightly forward and hands raised to guard position.
Throw a series of punches including jabs, crosses, hooks, and uppercuts.
Alternate with front kicks, side kicks, and roundhouse kicks.
Maintain footwork and movement throughout the combination.
Continue for rounds of the desired duration.

name: lateral lunge
description: The lateral lunge is a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads through a sideways stepping and squatting motion. It also stretches the adductors and strengthens the glutes, making it effective for developing frontal plane movement and hip mobility.
instructions: Stand with feet together and hands on your hips.
Take a wide step out to your right side and bend your right knee as you lower your body.
Keep your left leg straight and your right knee tracking over your right foot.
Lower until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push off your right foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the left side and continue alternating for the desired repetitions.

name: medicine ball slam
description: The medicine ball slam is a high-intensity power exercise targeting the full body through an explosive overhead throwing motion. It builds power, cardiovascular conditioning, and core strength while serving as an effective stress-relief workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a medicine ball with both hands.
Raise the ball overhead, extending your body fully.
Explosively slam the ball into the floor as hard as possible.
Catch the ball as it rebounds or pick it up from the floor.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: place jog
description: Place jogging is a simple cardio exercise performed in a stationary position. It elevates the heart rate and warms up the lower body by simulating the movement pattern of running without requiring space or equipment.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Begin jogging in place by alternating lifting each foot off the ground.
Maintain a light, bouncy rhythm on the balls of your feet.
Pump your arms naturally to match your leg movement.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: plank jack modification
description: The plank jack modification is a cardio and core exercise that combines the stability demands of a plank with the heart rate elevation of a jumping jack. By keeping the upper body fixed, it targets the shoulders and abs while conditioning the lower body.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under your shoulders and body in a straight line.
Engage your core and jump both feet out wider than hip-width.
Immediately jump your feet back together.
Continue the in-and-out jumping motion at a controlled pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: run
description: Running is a fundamental cardiovascular exercise targeting the heart and lungs while engaging the lower body muscles. It builds aerobic endurance, burns calories, and improves cardiovascular health, making it one of the most accessible forms of exercise.
instructions: Stand upright with a slight forward lean from the ankles.
Begin by pushing off with one foot and landing on the midfoot of the opposite foot.
Drive your arms in coordination with your legs, bending your elbows at 90 degrees.
Maintain a steady breathing rhythm and relaxed shoulders.
Continue at the desired pace for the target distance or duration.

name: tuck jump
description: The tuck jump is a plyometric exercise targeting the quads and developing explosive lower body power. The knee tuck at the peak of the jump challenges hip flexor strength and cardiovascular conditioning simultaneously.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and bend slightly at the knees.
Explosively jump upward as high as possible.
At the peak of the jump, tuck your knees toward your chest.
Release your knees and land softly with a slight bend in the knees.
Immediately perform the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: clap push-up
description: The clap push-up is an advanced plyometric chest exercise targeting the pectorals through explosive upper body power. The mid-air hand clap requires maximal force production, making it effective for developing chest power and upper body reactiveness.
instructions: Start in a standard push-up position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your chest to the floor with a controlled descent.
Explosively push upward with maximum force to lift your hands off the floor.
Clap your hands together quickly before returning your hands to the ground.
Land with soft elbows and immediately lower into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: close grip (diamond) push-up
description: The close grip diamond push-up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps with secondary pectoral involvement. The narrow hand placement intensifies triceps engagement and inner chest activation compared to a standard push-up.
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands placed close together beneath your chest, forming a diamond with your thumbs and index fingers.
Keep your body in a straight line and engage your core.
Lower your chest toward your hands by bending your elbows.
Pause just above the floor.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: decline push-up
description: The decline push-up is a bodyweight chest exercise that targets the upper portion of the pectorals by elevating the feet above the hands. The inclined angle shifts the pressing emphasis to the upper chest and anterior deltoids.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface such as a bench and position your hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Align your body in a straight line from head to feet.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Pause at the bottom, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell glute bridge chest press
description: The dumbbell glute bridge chest press is a compound exercise that simultaneously targets the pectorals and glutes. Pressing from the bridge position requires hip stability throughout the movement, adding a functional lower body element to a standard chest press.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest height.
Press through your feet to lift your hips into a glute bridge position.
While holding the bridge, press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to your chest while maintaining the bridge.
Repeat the press for the desired repetitions, then lower your hips.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: fingers push-up
description: The fingers push-up is an advanced bodyweight variation of the standard push-up that shifts weight onto the fingertips. This modification dramatically increases forearm and grip strength demands while maintaining the chest and triceps activation of a standard push-up.
instructions: Begin in a standard push-up position but support your weight on your fingertips instead of your palms.
Spread your fingers wide for maximum stability.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Pause at the bottom, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: flat dumbbell bench press
description: The flat dumbbell bench press is a foundational chest exercise targeting the pectorals through a horizontal pressing motion. Dumbbells allow for a greater range of motion than a barbell and require independent stabilization of each arm, improving muscular balance.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest height with palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended above your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level, elbows flaring slightly outward.
Pause at the bottom, then press back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: handstand push-up
description: The handstand push-up is an advanced bodyweight shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids through an inverted pressing movement. It requires significant upper body strength and balance, making it one of the most demanding overhead pressing exercises.
instructions: Kick up into a handstand against a wall with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line.
Slowly lower your head toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Pause when your head lightly touches the floor.
Press back up to the starting handstand position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline barbell bench press
description: The incline barbell bench press targets the upper portion of the pectorals and anterior deltoids through an inclined pressing movement. It is a cornerstone exercise for developing upper chest fullness and overall pressing strength.
instructions: Set a bench to a 30 to 45-degree incline and lie back with a barbell racked above.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width and unrack it.
Lower the barbell slowly toward your upper chest.
Pause at the bottom, then press the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline dumbbell bench press
description: The incline dumbbell bench press targets the upper pectorals through an inclined pressing motion using dumbbells. The independent arm movement provides greater range of motion and requires bilateral stabilization, promoting chest development and symmetry.
instructions: Set a bench to a 30 to 45-degree incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand.
Lie back and press the dumbbells to chest height with palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to chest level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline push-up
description: The incline push-up is a beginner-friendly bodyweight exercise targeting the lower portion of the pectorals. Elevating the hands reduces the body weight load, making it ideal for beginners building toward a standard push-up.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface such as a bench or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Step your feet back so your body forms a straight diagonal line.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending your elbows.
Pause at the bottom, then press back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: machine pec deck fly
description: The machine pec deck fly is a chest isolation exercise targeting the pectorals through a controlled adduction movement. The guided machine path allows beginners to safely overload the chest while reducing shoulder joint stress compared to free weight flyes.
instructions: Sit at the pec deck machine and adjust the seat so the handles are at shoulder height.
Grasp the handles with elbows slightly bent and upper arms parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your pectorals and bring your arms toward each other in a hugging motion.
Pause at the fully contracted position, holding the contraction briefly.
Slowly allow your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: one hand push-up
description: The one hand push-up is an elite bodyweight exercise targeting the pectorals and triceps with maximum load on a single arm. It requires exceptional upper body strength, core stability, and balance to perform with proper form.
instructions: Start in a push-up position and widen your feet for extra stability.
Place one hand on the floor beneath your shoulder and place the other hand behind your back.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your working elbow.
Keep your body as square as possible throughout the movement.
Press back up to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.

name: parallettes push-up
description: The parallettes push-up is an advanced bodyweight chest exercise using raised handles that allow the chest to descend below the floor level. The increased range of motion creates a deeper stretch of the pectorals and greater muscle activation than a standard push-up.
instructions: Set up parallettes on the floor and assume a push-up position gripping the handles.
Keep your body in a straight line and engage your core.
Lower your chest below the level of the handles by bending your elbows.
Pause at the bottom with a deep range of motion.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band chest press
description: The resistance band chest press targets the pectorals through a horizontal pressing motion using elastic band resistance. It is a portable and accessible alternative to machine or barbell pressing, providing increasing resistance throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band behind you at chest height and hold one end in each hand.
Step forward to create tension and stand in a staggered stance.
Press both hands forward until your arms are fully extended in front of your chest.
Pause at the extended position, squeezing your pectorals.
Slowly bring your hands back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band push-up
description: The resistance band push-up is a progressive bodyweight exercise that adds external resistance to a standard push-up. The band increases the difficulty at the top of the movement, demanding greater force from the pectorals and triceps to complete the extension.
instructions: Loop a resistance band across your upper back and hold one end under each hand.
Assume a standard push-up position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Press back up against the added resistance of the band.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated machine chest press
description: The seated machine chest press is a guided pressing exercise targeting the pectorals. The fixed movement path provides stability and reduces the need for stabilizer muscles, making it effective for beginners or those focused on chest isolation.
instructions: Adjust the seat of the chest press machine so the handles are at chest level.
Sit with your back flat against the pad and grip the handles with an overhand grip.
Press the handles forward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly, then slowly bring the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standard push-up
description: The standard push-up is a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the pectorals, triceps, and shoulders. It requires no equipment and can be performed anywhere, making it the cornerstone of upper body bodyweight training.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor slightly wider than shoulder-width and extend your legs behind you.
Align your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest to the floor by bending your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause just above the floor.
Press back up to the starting position by fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wide grip push-up
description: The wide grip push-up is a bodyweight chest exercise that emphasizes the outer pectorals by using a wider-than-standard hand placement. The wider grip reduces triceps involvement and increases the stretch and activation of the chest muscles.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor significantly wider than shoulder-width.
Extend your legs behind you and align your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows outward.
Pause at the bottom, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: air squat
description: The air squat is a foundational bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. It teaches proper squat mechanics and builds lower body strength, making it the basis for all squat progressions.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Engage your core and push your hips back as you bend your knees.
Lower your body until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Keep your chest tall and your knees tracking over your toes.
Press through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: band squat
description: The band squat adds a resistance band around the knees to increase glute activation and correct knee cave during a standard squat pattern. It is effective for reinforcing proper squat mechanics and building lower body strength.
instructions: Place a resistance band just above your knees and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Push your knees outward against the band to activate your glutes.
Lower into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Keep your chest tall and knees tracking outward against the band resistance.
Press through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded seated abduction
description: The banded seated abduction is a resistance band exercise targeting the glutes and hip abductors through a seated hip opening motion. It is a joint-friendly option for activating the outer glutes and is commonly used in warm-up and rehabilitation routines.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair and place a resistance band around both thighs just above the knees.
Sit with feet flat on the floor and knees at 90 degrees.
Push both knees outward against the resistance of the band.
Pause at the widest position, contracting your outer glutes.
Slowly bring your knees back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell back squat
description: The barbell back squat is the king of lower body exercises, targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings under heavy load. It builds comprehensive lower body and core strength and is foundational to strength and power training programs.
instructions: Position a barbell across your upper back and step away from the rack with feet shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and push your hips back to begin the squat.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the floor or deeper.
Keep your chest tall, knees out, and back neutral throughout.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell front squat
description: The barbell front squat is a quad-dominant squat variation where the bar is held on the front of the shoulders. The upright torso required places greater emphasis on the quads and upper back while reducing lower back stress compared to a back squat.
instructions: Position a barbell on your front deltoids and collarbone, holding it with a clean grip or crossed-arm grip.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and elbows raised high.
Descend into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Keep an upright torso and elbows elevated throughout.
Drive through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell hip thrust
description: The barbell hip thrust is the premier glute isolation exercise targeting the gluteus maximus through a loaded hip extension movement. Research consistently shows it produces the highest glute activation of any lower body exercise.
instructions: Sit with your upper back against a bench and place a barbell across your hips.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to thrust your hips upward.
Raise until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Pause at the top, holding the glute contraction.
Lower your hips back to the starting position and repeat.

name: barbell march standing
description: The barbell standing march is a loaded hip flexion exercise that targets the glutes and hip flexors under barbell resistance. The marching pattern challenges core stability and single-leg balance while improving hip mobility and strength.
instructions: Stand with a barbell positioned across your upper back as in a back squat.
Engage your core and stand tall.
Lift your right knee upward toward your chest in a marching motion.
Lower your right foot and immediately lift your left knee.
Continue alternating in a controlled marching cadence.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell romanian deadlift
description: The barbell romanian deadlift is a hip hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings through an eccentric stretch-and-contract movement. The controlled descent under load builds hamstring length, strength, and posterior chain resilience.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Push your hips back and hinge forward while keeping your back flat and bar close to your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings.
Pause at the bottom, then drive your hips forward to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell standing calf raise
description: The barbell standing calf raise is a loaded calf exercise targeting the gastrocnemius through a full range of plantar flexion. The barbell allows for progressive overload, making it highly effective for building calf muscle size and strength.
