instructions: Place a barbell across your upper back and stand with the balls of your feet on a raised surface.
Let your heels drop below the platform to get a full calf stretch.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Pause at the top, squeezing your calf muscles.
Slowly lower your heels back below the platform.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/lower legs/calves/strength/intermediate/0248 - barbell standing calf raise.mp4

name: barbell sumo deadlift
description: The barbell sumo deadlift is a wide-stance deadlift variation that places greater emphasis on the quads, adductors, and glutes compared to a conventional deadlift. The more upright torso position reduces lower back stress, making it suitable for those with mobility limitations.
instructions: Stand with feet significantly wider than shoulder-width and toes pointed outward.
Grip the barbell with hands inside your legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Engage your core, brace your back flat, and push the floor outward to begin the lift.
Drive your hips forward as the bar rises, finishing in a standing position with hips fully extended.
Slowly lower the bar back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: bulgarian split squat
description: The bulgarian split squat is a demanding unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quads and glutes. The elevated rear foot increases the range of motion and shifts the load predominantly onto the front leg, building single-leg strength and correcting imbalances.
instructions: Stand a few feet in front of a bench and place the top of your right foot on it.
Lower your body by bending your left knee until your left thigh is parallel to the floor.
Keep your front knee tracking over your toes and your torso upright.
Press through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching legs.

name: cable hip abduction
description: Cable hip abduction is an isolation exercise targeting the outer glutes and hip abductors through a lateral leg raise against cable resistance. It builds glute medius strength and hip stability, which are important for knee alignment and functional movement.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley of a cable machine and fasten it to your right ankle.
Stand sideways with your left side closest to the machine and hold the machine for balance.
Keeping your leg straight, raise your right leg out to the side against the cable resistance.
Pause at the top, squeezing your outer glute.
Slowly lower your leg back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching legs.

name: curtsy lunge
description: The curtsy lunge is a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the glutes through a cross-behind stepping pattern. The diagonal step increases adductor involvement and glute medius activation compared to a standard lunge.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
Step your right foot diagonally behind and to the left of your left foot.
Bend both knees and lower your right knee toward the floor.
Keep your front knee tracking over your front foot.
Press through your left heel to return to standing.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating for the desired repetitions.

name: donkey single leg kick
description: The donkey single leg kick is a bodyweight glute exercise performed on all fours. By kicking one heel upward with the knee bent, it isolates the gluteus maximus and is commonly used as a glute activation exercise in warm-up and lower body routines.
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keep your right knee bent at 90 degrees and flex your right foot.
Push your right heel upward toward the ceiling while squeezing your glute.
Pause at the top of the movement, fully contracting your glute.
Lower your knee back toward the floor without touching it.
Repeat for the desired repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0253 - donkey single leg kick.mp4

name: dumbbell calf raise
description: The dumbbell calf raise is a lower leg exercise targeting the gastrocnemius through loaded plantar flexion. Holding dumbbells adds resistance beyond body weight, making it effective for progressive calf development.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Position the balls of your feet on the edge of a raised surface if available.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Pause at the top, squeezing your calf muscles.
Slowly lower your heels back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell march
description: The dumbbell march is a loaded hip flexion exercise targeting the glutes and hip flexors while adding core stability challenge through the added weight. It is effective for developing hip strength and single-leg balance simultaneously.
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Engage your core and maintain an upright posture.
Lift your right knee upward toward your hip level.
Lower it and immediately lift your left knee.
Continue alternating in a controlled marching cadence.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell romanian deadlift
description: The dumbbell romanian deadlift targets the hamstrings through a hip hinge movement with dumbbells. It is a highly effective exercise for building hamstring length and strength while also engaging the glutes and lower back.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Push your hips back and hinge forward, lowering the dumbbells along your legs.
Keep your back flat and lower the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell shoulder press
description: The dumbbell shoulder press is a compound overhead exercise targeting the deltoids and building overall shoulder strength. Using dumbbells allows for a natural pressing path and unilateral correction, making it a versatile alternative to barbell pressing.
instructions: Sit or stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward.
Engage your core and press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Avoid locking out your elbows at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell sumo deadlift
description: The dumbbell sumo deadlift uses a wide stance to target the quads and adductors through a vertical pulling pattern. Holding a single dumbbell between the legs provides a comfortable range of motion and is accessible for all training levels.
instructions: Stand with feet wide apart and toes pointed outward, holding a single dumbbell with both hands between your legs.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to lower the dumbbell toward the floor.
Keep your back flat and chest tall throughout the movement.
Press through your heels and inner feet to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell thruster
description: The dumbbell thruster is a full-body compound exercise combining a squat and overhead press into one fluid movement. It targets the quads, glutes, and shoulders simultaneously, making it highly efficient for building strength and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
Perform a full squat, lowering until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
As you drive upward from the squat, press the dumbbells overhead.
Fully extend your legs and arms at the top of the movement.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height as you descend into the next squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: fire hydrant
description: The fire hydrant is a bodyweight glute exercise performed on all fours that targets the glute medius and outer glutes through a lateral hip abduction movement. It is widely used for glute activation, hip mobility, and rehabilitation purposes.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keep your right knee bent at 90 degrees and lift it out to the side.
Raise your knee as high as your hip level while keeping your back flat.
Pause at the top, squeezing your outer glute.
Slowly lower your knee back to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: goblet squat
description: The goblet squat is a beginner-friendly dumbbell squat variation targeting the quads and glutes. Holding the weight at chest height promotes an upright torso and reinforces proper squat mechanics, making it an ideal introductory loading pattern.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell vertically at chest height with both hands.
Push your hips back and lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Keep your chest tall, elbows inside your knees, and knees tracking over your toes.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hammer raise front
description: The hammer raise front is a shoulder exercise targeting the anterior and medial deltoids through a neutral-grip front raise. The neutral grip reduces wrist and elbow stress while maintaining effective shoulder muscle activation.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and raise both dumbbells in front of you to shoulder height.
Pause at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hamstring stretch
description: The hamstring stretch is a foundational flexibility exercise targeting the hamstrings through a seated forward fold. It improves hamstring length and flexibility, reduces injury risk, and is commonly used as part of cool-down and mobility routines.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach your hands toward your feet.
Keep your back as straight as possible and avoid rounding your spine.
Hold the stretch when you feel tension in your hamstrings.
Hold for 20 to 30 seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: jefferson squat
description: The jefferson squat is an asymmetric barbell squat targeting the quads and adductors from a straddled stance. It provides a unique training stimulus and is valued for its ability to load the lower body through a wide range of motion with reduced spinal compression.
instructions: Straddle a loaded barbell with one foot in front and one behind, perpendicular to the bar.
Grip the bar with one hand in front and one hand behind your body.
Lower your body by bending both knees until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
Keep your torso upright and drive through both feet to stand.
Pause at the top, then lower for the next repetition.
Alternate your lead foot between sets.

name: jump squat
description: The jump squat is a plyometric exercise that combines a bodyweight squat with an explosive jump. It develops lower body power, fast-twitch muscle fiber activation, and cardiovascular conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Perform a squat by lowering your hips until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Explosively jump upward from the bottom of the squat.
Extend your arms overhead or keep them at your sides.
Land softly with knees slightly bent and immediately lower into the next squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jumping jack with resistance band
description: The jumping jack with resistance band adds lateral resistance to a standard jumping jack, increasing adductor and abductor engagement. The band amplifies the cardio conditioning and lower body strengthening effects of the exercise.
instructions: Place a resistance band around your ankles and stand with feet together.
Jump and spread your feet wide against the resistance of the band while raising your arms overhead.
Jump your feet back together.
Continue at a steady, controlled rhythm.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/quads/cardio/beginner/0266 - jumping jack with resistance band.mp4

name: kettlebell dumbbell swing
description: The kettlebell or dumbbell swing is a ballistic hip hinge exercise targeting the glutes and hamstrings through explosive hip extension. It combines strength and cardio conditioning in one movement, making it highly time-efficient for full-body training.
instructions: Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width holding a kettlebell or dumbbell with both hands.
Hinge at the hips and swing the weight back between your legs.
Explosively drive your hips forward to propel the weight up to shoulder height.
Let the weight swing back down and immediately hinge into the next repetition.
Maintain a flat back and engaged core throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kettlebell swing
description: The kettlebell swing is a foundational ballistic movement targeting the glutes and posterior chain through explosive hip extension. It builds power, cardiovascular endurance, and core strength simultaneously, making it one of the most effective exercises in kettlebell training.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and a kettlebell on the floor between your feet.
Hinge at the hips to grip the kettlebell with both hands.
Swing it back between your legs to create momentum.
Drive your hips explosively forward, propelling the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
Allow gravity to bring it back down and immediately hinge for the next rep.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: leg extension machine
description: The leg extension machine is a quad isolation exercise that targets the quadriceps through a knee extension movement. It provides direct and focused stimulation to the quads, making it effective for building quad size and definition.
instructions: Sit on the leg extension machine and adjust the pad so it rests on the top of your shins.
Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for support.
Extend your legs upward until they are fully straight.
Pause at the top, squeezing your quads.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: leg press machine
description: The leg press machine is a compound lower body exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings in a seated position. The machine allows for heavy loading without the spinal loading demands of barbell squats, making it accessible for most fitness levels.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine and place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform toward your chest by bending your knees.
Pause at the bottom when your thighs are just above parallel to the platform.
Press the platform away by extending your knees.
Re-engage the safety when complete.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying leg curl machine
description: The lying leg curl machine is a hamstring isolation exercise performed in a prone position. It provides direct loading through knee flexion, making it effective for building hamstring mass and strength while reducing lower back involvement.
instructions: Lie face down on the leg curl machine and position the pad just above your heels.
Grip the handles for stability and engage your core.
Curl your legs upward by bending your knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes.
Pause at the top, squeezing your hamstrings.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/0271 - lying leg curl machine.mp4

name: lying leg lift up
description: The lying leg lift up is a bodyweight ab and hip flexor exercise targeting the lower abdominals through a vertical leg raise. It builds lower core strength and hip flexor endurance while requiring the upper abs to stabilize the spine.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs extended and hands beneath your lower back or flat on the floor.
Keep your legs together and raise them upward until they are perpendicular to the floor.
Pause at the top, contracting your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back toward the floor without fully touching it.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying leg raise
description: The lying leg raise is a foundational lower abdominal exercise performed on the floor. By controlling the lowering phase, it maintains constant tension on the abs and hip flexors throughout the full range of motion.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and hands placed beneath your lower back.
Raise both legs off the ground until they are perpendicular to the floor.
Slowly lower your legs toward the floor, stopping a few inches above it.
Pause, then raise your legs again for the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band good morning
description: The resistance band good morning is a hip hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings using band resistance. It teaches proper posterior chain loading mechanics and is a gentler alternative to barbell good mornings for building hamstring and lower back strength.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band and place the looped ends over your shoulders.
Stand with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Hinge at your hips and lower your torso toward parallel while keeping your back flat.
Pause at the bottom, feeling the stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to return to the upright position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/0274 - resistance band good morning.mp4

name: resistance band monster walk
description: The resistance band monster walk is a glute activation exercise that targets the glute medius through lateral and forward stepping against band resistance. It improves hip abductor strength, knee stability, and is commonly used as a warm-up or corrective exercise.
instructions: Place a resistance band around your ankles and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Slightly bend your knees and get into an athletic quarter-squat position.
Take a large step forward and to the right, maintaining tension in the band.
Follow with your left foot, keeping feet apart against the band resistance.
Continue walking forward for the desired distance, then reverse direction.
Repeat for the desired number of steps or duration.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0275 - resistance band monster walk.mp4

name: resistance band squat with lateral raise
description: The resistance band squat with lateral raise combines a lower body squat with an upper body lateral raise into a single movement. It simultaneously targets the quads and shoulders, making it a time-efficient full-body exercise.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the ends in each hand.
Lower into a squat as you hold the band handles at your sides.
As you press back up to standing, raise both arms out to the sides to shoulder height against the band.
Lower your arms as you descend into the next squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 200 Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0276 - resistance band squat with lateral raise.mp4

name: resistance band squat
description: The resistance band squat is a beginner-friendly squat variation using elastic band resistance. With handles held at shoulder height, it combines an overhead stability demand with the squat movement, providing a full-body strengthening effect.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band handles at shoulder height with palms facing forward.
Lower into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Keep your chest tall and knees tracking over your toes.
Press through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: skater hops
description: Skater hops are a plyometric bodyweight exercise targeting the quads and glutes through explosive lateral jumping. The single-leg landing improves balance, coordination, and lateral movement mechanics while building lower body power.
instructions: Stand on your right leg with a slight knee bend.
Push off your right foot and hop laterally to land on your left foot.
Land softly on your left foot with a bent knee to absorb the impact.
Immediately push off to the right and land on your right foot.
Continue hopping side to side in a skating motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: smith machine squat
description: The smith machine squat is a guided barbell squat variation targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings. The fixed bar path provides additional stability compared to a free barbell, making it suitable for beginners learning squat mechanics or for isolated muscle focus.
instructions: Set the bar height on the smith machine to shoulder level and step under the bar.
Position the bar across your upper back and release the safety latches.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Lower into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Press through your heels to return to standing.
Re-engage the safety when complete.

name: squat machine
description: The squat machine provides a guided squatting motion targeting the quads and glutes. The shoulder-supported design allows for heavier loading with reduced spinal stress, making it a useful tool for leg development.
instructions: Adjust the squat machine pads to fit your shoulder width and step into the machine.
Position the shoulder pads on your shoulders and release the safety.
Lower into a squat by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Press through your feet to return to the starting position.
Re-engage the safety and step out when finished.

name: standing calf raise
description: The standing calf raise is a foundational bodyweight calf exercise targeting the gastrocnemius through plantar flexion. It builds calf muscle endurance and strength and can be progressed by adding weight or increasing range of motion.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart on a flat surface or with the balls of your feet on a raised edge.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Pause at the top, squeezing your calves.
Slowly lower your heels back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: sumo squat
description: The sumo squat is a wide-stance bodyweight squat targeting the quads and adductors. The wider stance and externally rotated foot position shift emphasis to the inner thighs and glutes compared to a standard squat.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointed significantly outward.
Place your hands on your hips or clasp them in front of your chest.
Lower into a squat by bending both knees, keeping them tracking over your toes.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Press through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: walking lunge
description: Walking lunges are a dynamic bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings through continuous forward lunging steps. They build single-leg strength, coordination, and balance simultaneously.
instructions: Stand upright with feet together and hands on your hips or at your sides.
Step your right foot forward and lower your body by bending both knees.
Lower your back knee toward the floor without touching it.
Push through your right foot and bring your left foot forward to step into the next lunge.
Continue walking forward for the desired number of steps or distance.

name: weighted step-up
description: The weighted step-up is a unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quads and glutes through a stepping and single-leg press movement. Adding dumbbells increases the loading for progressive strength development and functional leg training.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or bench holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Step your right foot onto the surface and press through your right heel to lift your body up.
Bring your left foot up to stand fully on the box.
Step your right foot back down, followed by your left.
Continue leading with the same leg or alternate sides for the desired repetitions.

name: banded lateral raise
description: The banded lateral raise is a resistance band shoulder exercise targeting the lateral deltoids. The band provides progressive resistance as the arms rise, offering a unique stimulus compared to dumbbells and making it an effective portable shoulder exercise.
instructions: Stand on the center of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold one end of the band in each hand with arms at your sides.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise both arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: banded pull-apart (rear delt focus)
description: The banded pull-apart with a rear delt focus targets the posterior deltoids and rhomboids through a horizontal pulling motion. By consciously engaging the rear delts at the end range, it improves shoulder health, posture, and upper back strength.
instructions: Hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height, arms extended in front of you.
Maintain a shoulder-width grip and slight elbow bend.
Pull the band apart horizontally by moving both hands outward toward your sides.
Focus on squeezing your rear deltoids and shoulder blades at the end of the pull.
Slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable upright row
description: The cable upright row targets the lateral deltoids and upper trapezius through a vertical pulling motion with constant cable tension. The cable provides a smoother resistance curve than a barbell, reducing joint stress throughout the movement.
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to the low pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine and grip the attachment with an overhand grip slightly narrower than shoulder-width.
Pull the attachment straight up toward your chin, leading with your elbows.
Keep the bar close to your body and raise your elbows above shoulder height.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the attachment back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumb waiter dumbbell
description: The dumb waiter dumbbell exercise targets the posterior deltoids and rotator cuff through an external rotation movement with arms fixed at the sides. It is highly effective for shoulder health and is commonly used for injury prevention and postural correction.
instructions: Stand with elbows bent at 90 degrees and tucked close to your sides, forearms parallel to the floor.
Hold a light dumbbell in each hand with palms facing upward.
Keeping your elbows at your sides, externally rotate your shoulders to open your forearms outward.
Pause at the widest point, feeling the contraction in your rear delts.
Slowly rotate back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell shrug
description: The dumbbell shrug is a trapezius isolation exercise targeting the upper traps through a vertical shrugging motion. It builds upper back and neck thickness and is a foundational exercise for developing trap mass and strength.
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with arms fully extended.
Keep your arms straight and shrug your shoulders upward toward your ears as high as possible.
Pause at the top, squeezing your traps.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell y-raise
description: The dumbbell y-raise is a shoulder health exercise targeting the lower trapezius and posterior deltoids through an inclined overhead Y-pattern raise. It improves scapular stability and shoulder girdle strength, making it valuable for injury prevention.
instructions: Lie prone on an incline bench set to approximately 45 degrees, holding light dumbbells in each hand.
Let your arms hang straight down toward the floor.
Raise both arms upward and outward at a diagonal, forming a Y shape with your body.
Pause at the top, squeezing your upper back and rear delts.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: elevated pike push-up
description: The elevated pike push-up is an advanced bodyweight shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids through a vertical pressing movement. Elevating the feet increases the body angle to be more vertical, placing greater demand on the shoulders compared to a standard pike push-up.
instructions: Place your feet on a bench and walk your hands toward the bench so your hips are elevated and your body forms an inverted V.
Position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower the top of your head toward the floor by bending your elbows outward.
Pause when your head is near the floor.
Press back up to the inverted V starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline bench rear delt fly
description: The incline bench rear delt fly is a prone dumbbell exercise that isolates the posterior deltoids and upper back. The incline bench provides a supported position that eliminates lower back involvement, allowing for focused rear delt development.
instructions: Lie prone on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand hanging toward the floor.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise both arms out to the sides until they are at shoulder height.
Squeeze your shoulder blades and rear deltoids at the top.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the hanging starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rear delt fly machine
description: The rear delt fly machine targets the posterior deltoids through a horizontal abduction movement. The machine provides a guided path for consistent muscle activation and is ideal for isolating the rear delts to improve shoulder balance and posture.
instructions: Sit at the rear delt fly machine facing the pad with arms extended and hands gripping the handles.
Set the handles at shoulder height.
Pull the handles outward and backward in a wide arc, squeezing your rear delts.
Pause at the end of the range of motion.
Slowly return the handles to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: resistance band high side pull
description: The resistance band high side pull targets the deltoids and upper back through a diagonal pulling motion. The upward and lateral pulling path simultaneously engages the medial and posterior deltoids, improving shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Anchor a resistance band at a low point and stand sideways to the anchor.
Hold the band with the hand farthest from the anchor and stand tall.
Pull the band upward and across your body to shoulder height.
Pause at the top and squeeze your deltoid.
Slowly return to the starting position.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching sides.

name: resistance band shoulder roll
description: The resistance band shoulder roll is a mobility and warm-up exercise targeting the deltoids and rotator cuff through circular shoulder rotation. The band adds light tension to increase activation and improve blood flow to the shoulder joint.
instructions: Hold a resistance band with both hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms at your sides.
Roll your shoulders forward in a circular motion while maintaining gentle tension on the band.
Complete several forward circles, then reverse the direction.
Keep the movements slow and controlled.
Repeat for the desired duration or repetitions.

name: reverse grip barbell upright row
description: The reverse grip barbell upright row uses an underhand grip to target the deltoids and biceps while reducing shoulder impingement risk compared to the standard overhand variation. The grip change shifts more load onto the biceps and front deltoids.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell with an underhand grip slightly narrower than shoulder-width.
Hold the barbell in front of your thighs with arms extended.
Pull the barbell upward toward your chin by leading with your elbows.
Keep the bar close to your body throughout the movement.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated behind-the-neck press
description: The seated behind-the-neck press is an advanced barbell shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids through a vertical pressing motion with the bar behind the head. It places significant stress on the shoulder joint and requires adequate flexibility and mobility to perform safely.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and position a barbell across your upper traps behind your neck.
Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip.
Press the bar upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Slowly lower the bar back behind your neck to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: shoulder landmine press
description: The shoulder landmine press is a shoulder exercise using the landmine setup that provides an arcing pressing path, reducing shoulder joint stress compared to a straight overhead press. It targets the deltoids and builds pressing strength in a shoulder-friendly range of motion.
instructions: Position a barbell in a landmine attachment and load the free end.
Stand perpendicular to the bar and hold the end of the barbell at shoulder height with one hand.
Press the bar upward and forward in an arc until your arm is fully extended.
Pause at the top, then lower the bar back to shoulder height.
Complete the desired repetitions before switching arms.

name: standing arnold press
description: The standing arnold press is a compound shoulder exercise created by Arnold Schwarzenegger that targets all three deltoid heads through a combined rotation and overhead press. The rotating motion increases the range of motion and shoulder muscle activation compared to a standard press.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your shoulders with palms facing you.
As you press the dumbbells overhead, rotate your palms to face forward.
Extend your arms fully overhead.
Reverse the rotation as you lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with palms facing you.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing overhead military press
description: The standing overhead military press is the gold standard barbell shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids and overhead pressing strength. Performed standing, it demands significant core stability and full-body tension, making it one of the most functional upper body strength movements.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hold the barbell at shoulder height with elbows just in front of the bar.
Engage your core and press the bar straight overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the bar back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Arm Circle Stretch 1
description: Arm Circle Stretch 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids. It involves rotating both arms in circular motions to warm up the shoulder joints, improve mobility, and increase blood flow to the upper body.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to your sides.
Begin making small circular motions with both arms, gradually increasing the size of the circles.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions, then reverse the direction of the circles.
Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the movement.

name: Arm Raise Front Stretch
description: Arm Raise Front Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids and chest. It involves lifting both arms forward to shoulder height to improve shoulder flexibility and warm up the upper body.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Slowly raise both arms straight in front of you to shoulder height, palms facing down.
Hold the position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the stretch in your shoulders and chest.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Arm Stretching Hug Yourself
description: Arm Stretching Hug Yourself is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. It involves wrapping the arms around the body to stretch the posterior shoulder and upper back muscles, improving flexibility and relieving tension.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Cross your arms over your chest, wrapping each hand around the opposite shoulder as if hugging yourself.
Gently pull your shoulders forward and hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat as desired.

name: Arm Up Down Stretch
description: Arm Up Down Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids and traps. It involves raising and lowering both arms in a controlled manner to improve shoulder mobility and warm up the upper body.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Raise both arms overhead as high as possible, stretching upward.
Slowly lower your arms back down to your sides in a controlled motion.
Repeat the up-and-down movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Bench Dips
description: Bench Dips is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. It involves lowering and raising the body using a bench or chair, making it an effective movement for building upper arm pushing strength.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a sturdy bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge beside your hips.
Slide your hips off the bench and extend your legs in front of you with your heels on the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body toward the ground until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Bench Dips High
description: Bench Dips High is an advanced variation of bench dips performed with the feet elevated, increasing the load on the triceps and shoulders. This bodyweight strength exercise develops upper arm pushing power and requires greater stability than standard bench dips.
instructions: Place both hands on a bench behind you and elevate your feet on a surface of equal or greater height in front of you.
With your body suspended between the two surfaces, bend your elbows to lower your hips toward the ground.
Lower until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0306 - Bench Dips High.mp4

name: Boxing Beginners
description: Boxing Beginners is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the triceps and shoulders. It introduces basic punching mechanics including jabs and crosses, improving coordination, upper body endurance, and cardiovascular fitness.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hands raised in a guard position near your face.
Extend your lead hand forward in a straight jab, rotating your fist so the palm faces down at full extension.
Retract the jab and follow with a cross punch from your rear hand, rotating your hips and shoulders into the punch.
Continue alternating jabs and crosses at a controlled pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or time.

name: Chest and Arm Stretch
description: Chest and Arm Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectorals and anterior deltoids. It involves clasping the hands behind the back and opening the chest to relieve tightness and improve upper body flexibility.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and interlace your fingers behind your back.
Straighten your arms and gently pull them downward and away from your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and lift your chest, feeling a deep stretch across your chest and the front of your shoulders.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat as desired.

name: Clap Push Up Version II
description: Clap Push Up Version II is an advanced plyometric bodyweight exercise targeting the pectorals. It involves an explosive push-up with a mid-air hand clap, developing upper body power, speed, and coordination.
instructions: Begin in a standard push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest toward the floor in a controlled manner.
Explosively push up with maximum force so that your hands leave the ground.
Clap your hands together in mid-air before landing back in the push-up position.
Absorb the landing by bending your elbows and immediately moving into the next repetition.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/plyometrics/advanced/0309 - Clap Push Up Version II.mp4

name: Close Half Push Ups
description: Close Half Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals with emphasis on the triceps. The close hand position and partial range of motion increase tricep activation while reducing the demands of a full push-up, making it suitable for beginners.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands placed close together, just inside shoulder-width.
Lower your chest halfway toward the ground, stopping when your elbows reach a 45-degree bend.
Hold the halfway position for a moment, then push back up to the starting position.
Keep your core tight and back flat throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Close Push Ups
description: Close Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. By positioning the hands close together, this variation increases tricep engagement compared to standard push-ups, also working the chest and shoulders as secondary muscles.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands placed directly under your chest, closer than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides as you lower your chest toward the floor.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the ground.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0311 - Close Push Ups.mp4

name: Close Push Ups Advanced(Nose touch On Floor)
description: Close Push Ups Advanced is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. The full range of motion nose-to-floor variation maximizes tricep and chest engagement, requiring significant upper body strength and control.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands placed close together directly under your chest.
Keep your elbows tightly against your sides throughout the movement.
Lower your body all the way down until your nose touches the floor.
Press through your palms explosively to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/advanced/0312 - Close Push Ups Advanced(Nose touch On Floor).mp4

name: Decline Close Push Ups
description: Decline Close Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps with secondary emphasis on the upper chest and shoulders. Elevating the feet shifts the load upward while the close grip increases tricep activation.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface such as a bench or chair and assume a push-up position with your hands close together under your chest.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows close to your sides.
Push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0313 - Decline Close Push Ups.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Pike Plank
description: Decline Elbow Pike Plank is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. Performed with feet elevated, the piking motion engages the deep core and hip flexors while the decline position increases overall difficulty.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and position your feet on an elevated surface behind you.
From a decline elbow plank position, pike your hips upward toward the ceiling.
Hold the pike position briefly, squeezing your core.
Lower your hips back to the decline plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Decline Elbow Plank Knee Drive Leg on Table
description: Decline Elbow Plank Knee Drive Leg on Table is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. Performed in a decline elbow plank with feet elevated, the knee drive adds dynamic core engagement to the static plank position.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface such as a table.
Engage your core and maintain a straight body line.
Drive one knee forward toward your chest in a controlled motion.
Return your foot to the elevated surface and repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0315 - Decline Elbow Plank Knee Drive Leg on Table.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Plank Leg Lift
description: Decline Elbow Plank Leg Lift is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and glutes. The decline position and single-leg lift increase core stability demands and posterior chain engagement.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight diagonal line.
Lift one leg off the elevated surface, raising it as high as possible while keeping your hips level.
Hold briefly, then lower the leg back to the surface.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0316 - Decline Elbow Plank Leg Lift.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Plank Side Touch
description: Decline Elbow Plank Side Touch is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and obliques. By reaching to the side from a decline plank position, this movement challenges core stability and anti-rotational strength.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface.
Hold a decline elbow plank with your core tight and hips level.
Reach one hand out to tap the floor to your side, then return it to the plank position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating with control.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0317 - Decline Elbow Plank Side Touch.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Plank – Hip Dips
description: Decline Elbow Plank Hip Dips is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. Performed in a decline elbow plank with feet elevated, the hip dipping motion increases rotational core activation and overall difficulty compared to the standard variation.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface to form a decline elbow plank.
Keeping your core engaged, rotate your hips to one side, dipping them toward the floor.
Return to the center and dip to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/0318 - Decline Elbow Plank – Hip Dips.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Plank – Knee Hover
description: Decline Elbow Plank Knee Hover is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. The knee hover from a decline elbow plank position intensifies core and shoulder stability demands.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface.
From the decline elbow plank position, bend both knees slightly and hover them just above the elevated surface.
Hold the knee hover position while maintaining a tight core and level hips.
Keep breathing steadily throughout the hold.
Return to the starting position and repeat as desired.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0319 - Decline Elbow Plank – Knee Hover.mp4

name: Decline Elbow Plank – Rocking
description: Decline Elbow Plank Rocking is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and shoulders. The rocking motion from a decline plank position dynamically challenges core stability and anterior shoulder endurance.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface to form a decline elbow plank.
Shift your body forward by pushing onto your toes, moving your shoulders past your elbows.
Rock back to the starting position in a smooth, controlled motion.
Continue rocking forward and backward for the desired number of repetitions.
Maintain a flat back and tight core throughout.

name: Decline Elbow Plank – Side Step-Out
description: Decline Elbow Plank Side Step-Out is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and obliques. Adding lateral foot movement to the decline elbow plank challenges core stability and introduces a dynamic element to the exercise.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and rest your feet on an elevated surface to form a decline elbow plank.
Step one foot out to the side, then step the other foot to meet it.
Step back in the opposite direction to return to center.
Maintain a tight core and level hips throughout the movement.
Continue stepping side to side for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0321 - Decline Elbow Plank – Side Step-Out.mp4

name: Decline Pike Push Up Hold
description: Decline Pike Push Up Hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the deltoids. By holding the lowered position of a decline pike push-up, this movement builds shoulder strength and stability under sustained tension.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface and walk your hands in so your hips are raised high, forming an inverted V shape.
Position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the ground, stopping at the bottom of the pike push-up.
Hold this lowered position for the desired duration.
Press back up to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/shoulders/delts/strength/intermediate/0322 - Decline Pike Push Up Hold.mp4

name: Decline Push Ups
description: Decline Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the upper pectorals. Elevating the feet shifts load toward the upper chest and places greater demand on the anterior deltoids, increasing the difficulty over standard push-ups.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface and assume a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width on the floor.
Keep your body in a straight diagonal line from head to feet.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Decline Spider Push Ups
description: Decline Spider Push Ups is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals and obliques. Combining a decline push-up with a lateral knee drive engages the chest, core, and hip flexors simultaneously.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface and assume a push-up position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
As you lower your chest toward the floor, bring one knee out to the side toward your elbow.
Press back up and return the leg to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating legs with each repetition.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Decline Wide Push Ups
description: Decline Wide Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. The combination of a decline angle and wide grip maximizes chest activation, particularly in the outer and upper pectoral region.
instructions: Place your feet on an elevated surface and assume a push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your body in a straight diagonal line.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows outward.
Press through your palms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Diamond Push Ups
description: Diamond Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. The diamond hand position places maximum emphasis on the triceps while the chest and shoulders serve as supporting muscles.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position and bring your hands together under your chest, forming a diamond shape with your index fingers and thumbs.
Keep your elbows close to your body as you lower your chest toward your hands.
Lower until your chest nearly touches your hands.
Press back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0326 - Diamond Push Ups.mp4

name: Diamond Push Ups Close
description: Diamond Push Ups Close is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps with a tighter hand position than standard diamond push-ups. This variation further isolates the triceps, increasing difficulty and range of motion demands.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position and place your hands very close together, fingers nearly touching, directly under your chest.
Form a narrow diamond shape with your index fingers and thumbs.
Lower your chest toward your hands, keeping elbows pointing back.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0327 - Diamond Push Ups Close.mp4

name: Fast Punches (Shadow Boxing)
description: Fast Punches is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the triceps and shoulders. This shadow boxing variation involves rapidly alternating punches to elevate heart rate, improve upper body endurance, and develop punching speed and coordination.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hands raised in a guard position near your face.
Rapidly alternate punching forward with each hand, rotating your wrists so palms face down at extension.
Keep your core tight and shoulders relaxed as you maintain a fast punching pace.
Breathe rhythmically and stay light on your feet.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or time.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/cardio/beginner/0328 - Fast Punches (Shadow Boxing).mp4

name: Fast Punches (Shadow Boxing) Advanced
description: Fast Punches Advanced is an advanced bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the triceps and shoulders. This shadow boxing variation adds footwork and combination punching to the standard fast punches, increasing cardiovascular demand and full-body coordination.
instructions: Stand in a boxing stance with your hands raised and knees slightly bent.
Begin rapidly alternating punches while simultaneously moving your feet, stepping forward, backward, and laterally.
Incorporate combinations of jabs, crosses, and hooks at maximum speed.
Engage your core and rotate your hips into each punch.
Continue for the desired duration or number of rounds.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/cardio/intermediate/0329 - Fast Punches (Shadow Boxing) Advanced.mp4

name: Half Way Push Ups
description: Half Way Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. The partial range of motion focuses tension on the chest and triceps at mid-range, making it a useful variation for beginners or as an isometric challenge.
instructions: Begin in a standard push-up position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower your chest halfway to the floor, stopping when your elbows reach a 90-degree angle.
Hold the halfway position briefly.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Handstand Push Up
description: Handstand Push Up is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the deltoids. Performed in an inverted position against a wall, this movement builds elite shoulder pressing strength, stability, and body control.
instructions: Kick up into a handstand against a wall with your hands shoulder-width apart and arms fully extended.
Slowly bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Stop when your head lightly touches the ground.
Press through your palms to push back up to the full handstand position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Handstand Close Pushups
description: Handstand Close Pushups is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the deltoids and triceps. The close hand position in the inverted pressing position increases tricep activation and demands greater shoulder stability than standard handstand push-ups.
instructions: Kick up into a handstand against a wall with your hands positioned closer than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a rigid body position.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Press through your palms to return to the full handstand position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Handstand Wall Support Hold
description: Handstand Wall Support Hold is a bodyweight balance exercise targeting the deltoids and core. Holding an inverted position with wall support builds shoulder strength and endurance, body awareness, and prepares athletes for freestanding handstands.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor approximately 6 inches from a wall, shoulder-width apart.
Kick up into a handstand, allowing your heels to rest gently against the wall for support.
Engage your core, squeeze your glutes, and point your toes to maintain a straight body line.
Hold the position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Come down carefully and repeat as desired.

name: Incline Close Grip Half Push Up
description: Incline Close Grip Half Push Up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. The incline position reduces overall difficulty while the close grip and partial range of motion keep focused tension on the triceps, making it ideal for beginners.
instructions: Place your hands close together on an elevated surface such as a bench, just inside shoulder-width.
Walk your feet back to form a straight body line, creating an incline push-up position.
Lower your chest halfway toward the surface, stopping at a 45-degree bend in the elbows.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0334 - Incline Close Grip Half Push Up.mp4

name: Incline Elbow Mountain Climber
description: Incline Elbow Mountain Climber is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the abs. Performed from an incline elbow plank position, the alternating knee drives elevate heart rate and dynamically engage the core and hip flexors.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface and walk your feet back into an incline elbow plank position.
Engage your core and maintain a straight body line.
Drive one knee rapidly toward your chest, then quickly switch legs in a running motion.
Continue alternating legs at a fast pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or time.

name: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Dips
description: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Dips is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. Performed in an incline elbow plank, the hip dipping motion engages the lateral core muscles while the incline reduces overall difficulty compared to the flat variation.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface and step your feet back into an incline elbow plank.
Keep your core engaged and body in a straight line.
Rotate your hips and dip them to one side toward the floor.
Return to center and dip to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/beginner/0336 - Incline Elbow Plank Hip Dips.mp4

name: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Rotate
description: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Rotate is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques and lower back. The circular hip rotation from an incline plank position challenges rotational core stability in a low-impact manner.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface and walk your feet back into an incline elbow plank.
Keeping your core tight, slowly rotate your hips in a circular motion.
Complete the circle in one direction for the desired number of repetitions.
Reverse the direction and repeat.
Maintain a controlled pace throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/beginner/0337 - Incline Elbow Plank Hip Rotate.mp4

name: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Twist
description: Incline Elbow Plank Hip Twist is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. The twisting hip motion from an incline elbow plank engages the rotational core muscles while the incline makes the exercise accessible to beginners.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface and step your feet back into an incline elbow plank.
Keeping your shoulders stable, twist your hips to the right, bringing them toward the floor.
Return to the center and twist to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a smooth, controlled motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/beginner/0338 - Incline Elbow Plank Hip Twist.mp4

name: Incline Elbow Plank Hold
description: Incline Elbow Plank Hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The incline position reduces loading compared to a flat plank, making it an effective starting point for building core endurance and proper plank mechanics.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface such as a bench and step your feet back to form a straight body line.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain the incline plank position.
Keep your hips level and avoid letting them sag or rise.
Hold for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Rest and repeat as needed.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0339 - Incline Elbow Plank Hold.mp4

name: Incline Elbow Plank Knee Drive
description: Incline Elbow Plank Knee Drive is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. The controlled knee drive from an incline plank position adds dynamic core engagement while the incline reduces overall difficulty for beginners.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface and step your feet back into an incline elbow plank.
Engage your core and maintain a straight body line.
Drive one knee forward toward your chest in a slow, controlled motion.
Return the foot to the starting position and repeat with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0340 - Incline Elbow Plank Knee Drive.mp4

name: Incline Mountain Climber
description: Incline Mountain Climber is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the abs. Performing the mountain climber on an incline reduces the intensity compared to the flat version, making it accessible to beginners while still effectively engaging the core and elevating the heart rate.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface and step your feet back into an incline push-up position.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line.
Drive one knee toward your chest quickly, then switch legs in a running motion.
Continue alternating legs at a rapid pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or time.

name: Incline Push Up One Hand
description: Incline Push Up One Hand is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Performing a push-up on one arm against an incline challenges unilateral chest and shoulder strength, core stability, and balance.
instructions: Place one hand on an elevated surface and extend your body into an incline push-up position, with the other arm held behind your back.
Keep your core tight and body aligned throughout.
Lower your chest toward the elevated surface by bending the working elbow.
Press back up to the starting position.
Complete all reps on one side before switching arms.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/strength/advanced/0342 - Incline Push Up One Hand.mp4

name: Incline Push Ups
description: Incline Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Using an elevated surface reduces the relative bodyweight load, making this variation ideal for beginners developing upper body pressing strength and push-up technique.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface slightly wider than shoulder-width and step your feet back to form a straight body line.
Engage your core and keep your back flat.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending your elbows.
Press back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Incline Push-Up – Close
description: Incline Push-Up Close is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. The close grip on an incline surface reduces load while increasing tricep engagement, making it a beginner-friendly variation for building upper arm pushing strength.
instructions: Place your hands close together on an elevated surface and step your feet back to form a straight incline body line.
Keep your elbows close to your sides throughout the movement.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending your elbows.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0344 - Incline Push-Up – Close.mp4

name: Incline Push-Up – Wide Foot Stance
description: Incline Push-Up Wide Foot Stance is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. The wide foot stance increases base of support and stability during the incline push-up, making it a suitable starting point for those learning proper push-up mechanics.
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface slightly wider than shoulder-width and step your feet back wider than hip-width.
Engage your core and maintain a straight body line.
Lower your chest toward the surface by bending your elbows.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/strength/beginner/0345 - Incline Push-Up – Wide Foot Stance.mp4

name: Incline Table Mountain Climber
description: Incline Table Mountain Climber is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the abs. Performed with hands on a table, this variation reduces the demand on the wrists and upper body compared to floor mountain climbers, making it accessible for beginners and those with wrist sensitivity.
instructions: Place your hands on a table and step your feet back to form a straight incline body line.
Engage your core and maintain a neutral spine.
Drive one knee forward toward your chest, then quickly return it and switch legs.
Continue alternating legs in a controlled running motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or time.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/cardio/beginner/0346 - Incline Table Mountain Climber.mp4

name: Knee Plank Hold
description: Knee Plank Hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The knee variation reduces load compared to a full plank, making it an effective entry point for building core endurance and learning proper plank alignment.
instructions: Begin on all fours and lower your forearms to the floor or keep your arms extended.
Lower your knees to the floor and align your body from knees to shoulders in a straight line.
Engage your core and avoid letting your hips sag.
Hold the position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Rest and repeat as needed.

name: Knee Push Up
description: Knee Push Up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Performing the push-up from the knees reduces the load, making it ideal for beginners developing chest, tricep, and shoulder pushing strength.
instructions: Kneel on the floor with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width in front of you.
Lower your knees to the floor and maintain a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Bend your elbows to lower your chest toward the floor.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Knuckle Push Ups
description: Knuckle Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Performing push-ups on the knuckles strengthens the wrists and forearms while keeping the wrists in a neutral position, making it a common variation in martial arts training.
instructions: Form fists with your hands and place your knuckles on the floor, shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you to form a straight push-up position.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Press through your knuckles to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: McGill Curl Up
description: McGill Curl Up is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the abs. Based on spine research, this low-load curl minimizes spinal compression while effectively activating the abdominal muscles, making it ideal for core rehabilitation and injury prevention.
instructions: Lie on your back with one leg straight and the other knee bent, foot flat on the floor.
Place your hands beneath the natural curve of your lower back for support.
Gently lift your head and shoulders off the floor without flattening your lower back.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching the leg position.

name: One Hand Push Ups
description: One Hand Push Ups is an advanced bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Performing a push-up on a single arm demands exceptional unilateral chest and shoulder strength, core stability, and body control.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position and place one hand behind your back, balancing on the other hand with feet spread wide.
Engage your core and keep your hips square to the floor.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending the working elbow.
Press back up to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.

name: Pike Plank Hold
description: Pike Plank Hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the deltoids. Holding the inverted V position places sustained load on the shoulders and upper back, making it an effective movement for building shoulder endurance and preparing for handstand training.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet in toward your hands, raising your hips high to form an inverted V shape.
Keep your arms straight and press through your palms.
Hold the pike position for the desired duration.
Walk your feet back to the starting position and repeat.

name: Pike Push Up
description: Pike Push Up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the deltoids. The inverted V position shifts the push-up load from the chest to the shoulders, making it an effective movement for building shoulder pressing strength.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position and walk your feet in toward your hands until your hips are raised high, forming an inverted V.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Stop when your head nearly touches the ground.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Plank Hold With Hand Join
description: Plank Hold With Hand Join is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The brief hand-joining motion challenges shoulder stability and core anti-rotation strength while maintaining the plank position.
instructions: Begin in a full push-up plank position with arms extended and hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and maintain a straight body line.
Bring both hands together beneath your chest, touching palms or fingertips.
Hold briefly, then return your hands to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0354 - Plank Hold With Hand Join.mp4

name: Plank Hold With One Hand and Leg Lift
description: Plank Hold With One Hand and Leg Lift is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and glutes. The simultaneous arm and opposite leg lift significantly challenges core anti-rotation stability and posterior chain engagement.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended.
Simultaneously lift one arm off the ground and the opposite leg, extending both straight.
Hold the extended position briefly while keeping your hips level.
Lower back to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0355 - Plank Hold With One Hand and Leg Lift.mp4

name: Plank Hold(On Elbow)
description: Plank Hold on Elbow is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The forearm plank distributes load across the core and reduces wrist strain compared to a high plank, making it a foundational core endurance exercise.
instructions: Lie face down and lift your body onto your forearms and toes, keeping your elbows directly beneath your shoulders.
Engage your core and maintain a straight line from head to heels.
Keep your hips level and avoid letting them sag or pike upward.
Hold the position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Rest and repeat as needed.

name: Plank Knee To Chest
description: Plank Knee To Chest is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and hip flexors. The slow, controlled knee drive from the plank position dynamically engages the core while maintaining upper body stability.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended and hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and draw one knee toward your chest in a controlled motion.
Hold briefly, then extend the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0357 - Plank Knee To Chest.mp4

name: Plank Knee To Elbow Hip Up
description: Plank Knee To Elbow Hip Up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. The combination of a hip rise and same-side knee drive from the plank position intensely engages the lateral core and requires dynamic stability.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended.
Simultaneously raise your hips slightly and drive one knee diagonally toward the same-side elbow.
Hold briefly, engaging your obliques.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/0358 - Plank Knee To Elbow Hip Up.mp4

name: Plank Push Up Elbow
description: Plank Push Up Elbow is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs and shoulders. Transitioning between the forearm and full plank positions builds core stability, shoulder strength, and muscular endurance.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with your elbows beneath your shoulders.
Press up onto one hand, then the other, transitioning to a full push-up plank.
Lower back down to one forearm, then the other, returning to the elbow plank.
Alternate the leading arm with each repetition.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Plank Reach
description: Plank Reach is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The single-arm extension from the plank position challenges core anti-rotation stability and shoulder girdle control.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended.
While keeping your hips level, slowly extend one arm directly in front of you at shoulder height.
Hold the reached position briefly, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Plank To Push-Ups
description: Plank To Push-Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs, chest, and shoulders. This dynamic movement combines the core endurance of a plank with a full push-up, building both upper body strength and core stability.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Press up with one hand, then the other, to transition into a full push-up position.
Perform one push-up, then lower back down to one forearm, then the other, returning to the elbow plank.
Alternate the leading arm each round.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Plank With Hips Lift on Elbow
description: Plank With Hips Lift on Elbow is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The hip lift from the elbow plank dynamically engages the core and challenges the transition between the plank and pike positions.
instructions: Begin in a forearm plank position with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and slowly lift your hips toward the ceiling into a pike position.
Hold briefly at the top, feeling the tension in your abs.
Lower your hips back to the straight plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0362 - Plank With Hips Lift on Elbow.mp4

name: Prayer Forward Fold
description: Prayer Forward Fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and lower back. Folding forward with hands in prayer position gently lengthens the posterior chain and opens the upper back.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and bring your palms together in a prayer position in front of your chest.
Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat as you lower your torso toward the ground.
Allow your arms to extend forward or rest in front of you as you deepen the stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Slowly rise back to standing and repeat as desired.

name: Prayer Stretch
