description: Prayer Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the forearms and wrists. Pressing the palms together and lowering the hands gently stretches the flexor muscles of the forearms, helping to relieve tension from repetitive gripping or typing.
instructions: Bring both palms together in front of your chest in a prayer position.
Slowly lower your hands toward your waist while keeping your palms pressed together.
Hold the stretch when you feel tension in your forearms and wrists.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat as desired.

name: Reverse Plank
description: Reverse Plank is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the glutes and lower back. Holding the body in a supine bridge position builds posterior chain strength, shoulder stability, and core endurance.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you and place your hands flat on the floor behind your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Press through your palms and heels to lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line.
Engage your glutes and core to maintain the position.
Hold for the desired duration.
Lower back down and repeat.

name: Reverse Prayer
description: Reverse Prayer is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the forearms and wrists. Pressing the palms together behind the back in a reverse prayer position deeply stretches the wrist extensors and opens the chest and shoulders.
instructions: Stand or sit tall and bring your hands behind your back, pressing the backs of your hands together.
Rotate your fingers upward and attempt to press your palms together behind your back.
Hold the stretch when you feel tension in your forearms, wrists, and chest.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat as desired.

name: Reverse Prayer Forward Fold
description: Reverse Prayer Forward Fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and lower back. Combining the reverse prayer arm position with a forward fold simultaneously stretches the wrist extensors, posterior shoulder, and entire posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and bring your hands into a reverse prayer position behind your back.
Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and neck neutral.
Lower your torso toward the ground, deepening the stretch in your back and wrists.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily.
Slowly rise back to standing and repeat.

name: Reverse Push-Ups
description: Reverse Push-Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps. Performed in a reverse tabletop position, the dipping motion loads the triceps and posterior shoulder in the opposite pressing plane from standard push-ups.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat, placing your hands flat on the floor behind your hips with fingers pointing forward.
Press through your palms to lift your hips off the floor.
Bend your elbows to lower your hips toward the floor in a controlled dip motion.
Press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Seated Arm Circles Leg Folded
description: Seated Arm Circles Leg Folded is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids. Performing arm circles from a seated position isolates shoulder mobility and reduces lower body involvement, making it ideal for warm-up or cool-down routines.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs crossed or folded in front of you.
Extend both arms out to your sides at shoulder height.
Begin making small circular motions with both arms, gradually increasing the size of the circles.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions, then reverse the direction.
Keep your back straight and core gently engaged throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/shoulders/delts/stretching/beginner/0369 - Seated Arm Circles Leg Folded.mp4

name: Shoulder Tap Plank
description: Shoulder Tap Plank is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abs. The alternating shoulder tap from the plank position challenges core anti-rotation stability and requires significant shoulder girdle strength and balance.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended and feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your hips as stable as possible.
Lift one hand off the ground and tap the opposite shoulder.
Return the hand to the floor and repeat with the other hand.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Shoulder Tap Push Ups
description: Shoulder Tap Push Ups is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. Combining a full push-up with an alternating shoulder tap adds core anti-rotation demand to the traditional push-up, building both upper body strength and stability.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with arms extended and feet hip-width apart.
Perform a full push-up, lowering your chest to the floor and pressing back up.
At the top of the push-up, lift one hand and tap the opposite shoulder.
Return the hand to the floor and perform the next push-up.
Alternate the shoulder tap after each push-up.

name: Side Elbow Plank Hold
description: Side Elbow Plank Hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. Holding the side plank position on the forearm builds lateral core stability, shoulder endurance, and hip abductor strength.
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on one forearm, with your elbow directly beneath your shoulder.
Stack your feet or place the lower knee on the ground for support.
Lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight diagonal line.
Hold the position for the desired duration, keeping your core tight.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: Side Plank Hip Backward Dip
description: Side Plank Hip Backward Dip is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques and glutes. The backward hip dip from the side plank position adds a rotational and posterior chain element to the standard side plank, increasing overall core demand.
instructions: Assume a side plank position on one hand or forearm with your body in a straight line.
Keeping your shoulder stable, dip your hips backward and slightly downward behind you.
Return to the straight side plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
Maintain control throughout the movement.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/0373 - Side Plank Hip Backward Dip.mp4

name: Side Plank Hip Dip 1
description: Side Plank Hip Dip 1 is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. The dipping and lifting motion from the side elbow plank position dynamically engages the lateral core and challenges hip and shoulder stability.
instructions: Assume a side elbow plank position with your elbow beneath your shoulder and body in a straight line.
Lower your hips toward the floor in a controlled motion.
Lift your hips back up to the straight plank position, squeezing your obliques.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: Side Plank Kneeling
description: Side Plank Kneeling is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. The modified kneeling position reduces load compared to a full side plank, making it an effective entry point for building lateral core strength and shoulder stability.
instructions: Lie on your side and prop yourself up on one forearm with your bottom knee bent and resting on the ground.
Stack your top leg straight and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from knee to shoulder.
Engage your core and hold the position for the desired duration.
Lower your hips and switch sides.
Repeat as desired.

name: Side Plank Leg Lift Touch With Hand
description: Side Plank Leg Lift Touch With Hand is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques and glutes. Combining a leg lift with a hand touch from the side plank position challenges lateral core stability, hip abduction, and balance simultaneously.
instructions: Assume a full side plank position on one hand with your body in a straight line.
Lift your top leg upward and simultaneously reach your top hand forward to touch your toes.
Return to the starting position in a controlled motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
Maintain a stable base and engaged core throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/0376 - Side Plank Leg Lift Touch With Hand.mp4

name: Side Plank With Knee Dive
description: Side Plank With Knee Dive is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques and hip flexors. The dynamic knee drive from the side plank position adds core flexion and hip engagement to the lateral stability demands of the traditional side plank.
instructions: Assume a full side plank on one hand with your body straight.
Draw your top knee toward your chest in a controlled diving motion.
Extend the leg back out to the straight starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
Keep your hips lifted and core engaged throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/obliques/strength/intermediate/0377 - Side Plank With Knee Dive.mp4

name: Side Plank – March
description: Side Plank March is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques. The marching leg motion from the side plank position adds dynamic hip flexion and challenges lateral core and hip stability simultaneously.
instructions: Assume a full side plank position on one hand with your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and lift your top leg slightly.
Bend your top knee and drive it toward your chest as if marching, then extend it back.
Continue the marching motion for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: Spider Plank Elbow Touch
description: Spider Plank Elbow Touch is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the obliques and hip flexors. The lateral knee drive toward the elbow from the plank position engages the core, hip flexors, and rotational muscles in a challenging compound movement.
instructions: Begin in a full plank position with arms extended.
Drive one knee out to the side and bring it toward the same-side elbow in a wide arc.
Touch or nearly touch the knee to the elbow.
Return the leg to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Standard Push-Ups
description: Standard Push-Ups is a fundamental bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectorals. It is one of the most widely used upper body exercises, effectively building chest, tricep, and shoulder strength while engaging the core for stability.
instructions: Begin with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width on the floor, arms extended.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Stop when your chest nearly touches the ground.
Press through your palms to push back up to the starting position and repeat.

name: Standing Arm Straight Stretch
description: Standing Arm Straight Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids and posterior shoulder. Gently pulling the arm across the chest stretches the rear deltoid and upper back, commonly used for shoulder warm-up and cool-down.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm straight across your chest at shoulder height.
Use your other hand to gently pull the extended arm closer to your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the tension in your shoulder.
Switch arms and repeat.

name: Standing Bow and Arrow
description: Standing Bow and Arrow is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. The pulling motion mimics drawing a bow, stretching the chest and anterior shoulder while activating the posterior shoulder and back muscles.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and extend both arms in front of you at shoulder height.
Bend one elbow and pull it back as if drawing a bowstring, rotating your torso toward that side.
Hold briefly, feeling the stretch across your chest, shoulder, and back.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Standing Deltoid Stretch (Across Upper Arm)
description: Standing Deltoid Stretch Across Upper Arm is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the posterior deltoid. Pulling the arm across the body stretches the rear shoulder and upper back, improving shoulder flexibility and range of motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm straight across your body at shoulder height, palm facing down.
Use the opposite hand or forearm to press the extended arm gently against your chest.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the back of the shoulder.
Switch sides and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/shoulders/delts/stretching/beginner/0383 - Standing Deltoid Stretch (Across Upper Arm).mp4

name: Standing Deltoid Stretch (Across Upper Arm) Twist
description: Standing Deltoid Stretch Across Upper Arm Twist is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids and upper back. Adding a torso rotation to the cross-body deltoid stretch deepens the stretch through the posterior shoulder and thoracic spine.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and extend one arm across your body at shoulder height.
Use the opposite hand to hold the extended arm.
Rotate your torso gently in the direction of the extended arm to deepen the stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch across the shoulder and back.
Switch sides and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/shoulders/delts/stretching/beginner/0384 - Standing Deltoid Stretch (Across Upper Arm) Twist.mp4

name: Standing Wrist Flexor Stretch
description: Standing Wrist Flexor Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the forearm flexors. Extending and bending the wrist backward gently lengthens the muscles and tendons of the inner forearm, commonly used to prevent or relieve repetitive strain injuries.
instructions: Extend one arm in front of you with your palm facing upward.
Use your other hand to gently pull your fingers downward and back toward your body.
Hold the stretch when you feel tension along the inside of your forearm and wrist.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Switch hands and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower arms/forearms/stretching/beginner/0385 - Standing Wrist Flexor Stretch.mp4

name: Towel Biceps Curl
description: Towel Biceps Curl is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the biceps. Using a towel looped under the thigh creates resistance for curling, allowing bicep training without equipment and making it accessible for home or travel workouts.
instructions: Sit on a sturdy chair and place a rolled towel under one thigh.
Grip both ends of the towel with the hand of the same side.
Curl your hand upward against the resistance of the towel, engaging your bicep.
Slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0386 - Towel Biceps Curl.mp4

name: Wrist Extension Stretch Fingers Down
description: Wrist Extension Stretch Fingers Down is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the forearm extensors. Pressing the fingers downward gently lengthens the extensor muscles along the top of the forearm, helping to relieve tension and prevent injuries such as tennis elbow.
instructions: Extend one arm in front of you with your palm facing downward.
Use your other hand to gently press your fingers downward and toward your body.
Hold when you feel tension along the top of your forearm.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Switch hands and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower arms/forearms/stretching/beginner/0387 - Wrist Extension Stretch Fingers Down.mp4

name: Back Stretch
description: Back Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Reaching forward with rounded shoulders separates the shoulder blades and gently stretches the posterior upper body, relieving tension and improving thoracic mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and reach both arms forward at shoulder height.
Round your upper back and gently press your arms forward, allowing your shoulder blades to spread apart.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the tension across your upper back.
Release and return to an upright standing position.
Repeat as desired.

name: Bodyweight Half Standing Row With Towel
description: Bodyweight Half Standing Row With Towel is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the lats. Using a towel and fixed anchor point to perform an inclined rowing motion builds upper back, bicep, and rear deltoid strength without equipment.
instructions: Hold both ends of a towel looped around a sturdy post or door handle.
Lean back at roughly a 45-degree angle with your arms extended and feet close to the anchor point.
Pull your chest toward the anchor point by bending your elbows and drawing them back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/strength/beginner/0389 - Bodyweight Half Standing Row With Towel.mp4

name: Bodyweight Roman Deadlift
description: Bodyweight Roman Deadlift is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the lower back and posterior chain. The hip hinge movement pattern strengthens the erector spinae, glutes, and hamstrings without external load, making it ideal for learning proper deadlift mechanics.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Hinge at your hips, pushing them backward while keeping your back flat and knees slightly bent.
Lower your torso until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/strength/beginner/0390 - Bodyweight Roman Deadlift.mp4

name: Child Pose
description: Child Pose is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and lower back. This restorative yoga-derived stretch gently elongates the spine, opens the hips, and relieves tension across the entire back.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back on your heels with your toes together and knees apart.
Reach your arms forward on the floor and lower your forehead toward the ground.
Stretch your arms out as far as possible and relax into the position.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Return to kneeling and repeat as desired.

name: Child Pose Upper Back Slightly Bent Stretch
description: Child Pose Upper Back Slightly Bent Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. The slight elbow bend encourages greater rounding in the thoracic spine, deepening the upper back stretch compared to a standard child pose.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back toward your heels.
Reach your arms forward but keep a slight bend in your elbows, allowing your upper back to round gently.
Let your chest lower toward the floor, feeling a stretch across your upper back.
Hold for 20-30 seconds while breathing deeply.
Return to the kneeling position and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0392 - Child Pose Upper Back Slightly Bent Stretch.mp4

name: Child Pose Upper Back Straight Stretch
description: Child Pose Upper Back Straight Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and shoulders. Extending the arms with straight elbows actively lengthens the latissimus dorsi and provides a strong stretch through the entire posterior upper body.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back toward your heels.
Reach your arms fully forward with your elbows straight and palms flat on the floor.
Maintain a straight spine and press your arms into the ground to actively lengthen your back.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily.
Return to kneeling and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0393 - Child Pose Upper Back Straight Stretch.mp4

name: Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Bent Forward Stretch
description: Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Bent Forward Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and chest. Folding forward with fingers interlaced behind the back simultaneously opens the chest and stretches the upper and lower back.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and interlace your fingers behind your back.
Straighten your arms and gently pull them away from your back.
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso and allowing your clasped hands to lift overhead.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch across your chest, shoulders, and back.
Slowly return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0394 - Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Bent Forward Stretch.mp4

name: Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Stretch
description: Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectorals and anterior deltoids. Clasping the hands behind the back and opening the chest relieves tightness from prolonged sitting or pressing exercises.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and interlace your fingers behind your back.
Straighten your arms and gently pull them downward and away from your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and lift your chest toward the ceiling.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat as desired.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/stretching/beginner/0395 - Interlaced Fingers Behind Back Stretch.mp4

name: Interlaced Fingers Folded Behind Back Stretch
description: Interlaced Fingers Folded Behind Back Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectorals. Pressing the folded hands together behind the back maximizes the chest and anterior shoulder stretch, improving postural alignment and thoracic extension.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and fold your hands behind your back, pressing the palms together if possible.
Squeeze your elbows toward each other behind your back.
Lift your chest and draw your shoulder blades together, feeling a deep opening in the front of your chest.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/stretching/beginner/0396 - Interlaced Fingers Folded Behind Back Stretch.mp4

name: Prisoner Standing Upper Body Bent
description: Prisoner Standing Upper Body Bent is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Folding forward from a prisoner hand position opens the thoracic spine and stretches the back extensors in a controlled, accessible manner.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place both hands behind your head, fingers interlaced.
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso until it is roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep your back flat and elbows wide.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your upper back and lats.
Return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0397 - Prisoner Standing Upper Body Bent.mp4

name: Seated Upper Back Pull (W-Squeeze)
description: Seated Upper Back Pull W-Squeeze is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the traps and rear deltoids. The W-shaped arm position during the pulling motion effectively activates the mid-trapezius and rhomboids, improving posture and upper back strength.
instructions: Sit upright with your feet flat on the floor and arms extended forward at shoulder height.
Bend your elbows and pull them back, forming a W shape with your arms.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together firmly at the end of the movement.
Hold the squeeze for 2-3 seconds, then return your arms to the extended position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/traps/strength/beginner/0398 - Seated Upper Back Pull (W-Squeeze).mp4

name: Standing Back Extension Stretch
description: Standing Back Extension Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back. Arching backward from a standing position gently extends the lumbar spine, relieving tightness from prolonged sitting and improving spinal mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place both hands on your lower back with fingers pointing downward.
Gently press your hips forward and arch backward, extending your spine.
Hold the position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the stretch along your lower back.
Return to an upright position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0399 - Standing Back Extension Stretch.mp4

name: Standing Back Fly Stretch
description: Standing Back Fly Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the traps and rear deltoids. The reverse fly motion from a slightly hinged position opens the chest, activates the upper back, and improves shoulder blade mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hinge slightly forward at your hips.
Extend both arms in front of you at chest height, palms facing each other.
Sweep both arms back and out to the sides in a fly motion, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Return to the starting position in a controlled motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Standing Back Stretch(Interlaced Fingers Behind Ba
description: Standing Back Stretch with Interlaced Fingers Behind Back is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats. Clasping the hands behind the back while standing upright opens the chest and gently stretches the upper back and posterior shoulder.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and interlace your fingers behind your back.
Straighten your arms and press your knuckles downward.
Simultaneously lift your chest and pull your shoulders back, feeling a stretch across your lats and chest.
Hold for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0401 - Standing Back Stretch(Interlaced Fingers Behind Ba.mp4

name: Standing Back "Hug Yourself" Stretch (Upper Back)
description: Standing Back Hug Yourself Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Crossing the arms and rounding the upper back stretches the posterior shoulder and thoracic spine, providing quick relief from upper back tension.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Cross your arms over your chest, wrapping each hand around the opposite shoulder.
Pull your shoulders gently forward and round your upper back.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Release and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0402 - Standing Back -Hug Yourself- Stretch (Upper Back).mp4

name: Standing Cat–Cow (Spine Wave)
description: Standing Cat-Cow is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back and spine. Alternating between spinal flexion and extension while standing improves thoracic and lumbar mobility and is commonly used as a warm-up or cool-down movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place your hands on your thighs.
Inhale and arch your back, lifting your chest and tailbone into a cow position.
Exhale and round your spine, tucking your chin and pelvis into a cat position.
Continue flowing smoothly between the two positions.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/mobility/beginner/0403 - Standing Cat–Cow (Spine Wave).mp4

name: Standing Cat–Cow (Spine Wave) 1
description: Standing Cat-Cow Spine Wave 1 is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back. Adding an overhead arm position to the spinal wave increases thoracic mobility demands and promotes full spinal articulation from cervical to lumbar.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms extended overhead.
Begin a spinal wave from the top, first arching the upper back, then the mid-back, then the lower back.
Continue the wave motion downward and then reverse it back upward.
Keep the movement fluid and coordinated with your breath.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/mobility/beginner/0404 - Standing Cat–Cow (Spine Wave) 1.mp4

name: Standing Chest Opener Backbend
description: Standing Chest Opener Backbend is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectorals. The combination of a forward arm press and chest lift creates a deep opening through the anterior chest and shoulder, counteracting the effects of rounded posture.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and interlace your fingers in front of your chest.
Press your palms forward and invert your interlaced hands, pushing them away from you.
Simultaneously lift your chest and gently drop your head back into a mild backbend.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch across your chest.
Return to upright and repeat.

name: Standing Rib Intercostal Stretch
description: Standing Rib Intercostal Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lats and intercostal muscles. Reaching overhead and leaning to the side elongates the lateral trunk, opens the ribcage, and improves thoracic side-bending mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and raise one arm overhead.
Lean your torso to the opposite side, creating a long arc from your raised hand to your hip.
Hold the side stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch along your ribs and lat.
Return to an upright position and switch sides.
Repeat as desired.

name: Standing Scapular W Stretch
description: Standing Scapular W Stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the traps and rhomboids. The W arm position activates and stretches the mid and lower trapezius, improving scapular mobility and upper body posture.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and raise your arms to shoulder height.
Bend your elbows to form a W shape, with forearms pointing upward.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold for 2-3 seconds.
Release and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Standing Shoulder Blade Clock
description: Standing Shoulder Blade Clock is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the traps and scapular stabilizers. Moving the shoulder blades through a full circular range of motion improves scapular mobility, relieves upper back tension, and enhances shoulder joint health.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Slowly move your shoulder blades through a full circular range of motion, beginning with an upward shrug.
Continue by rolling the shoulder blades back, then down, then forward in a clockwise circle.
Complete the desired number of circles before reversing direction.
Keep your neck relaxed and breathe steadily throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/traps/mobility/beginner/0408 - Standing Shoulder Blade Clock.mp4

name: Standing Shoulder Mobility Circle
description: Standing Shoulder Mobility Circle is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the deltoids. Performing large arm circles improves glenohumeral joint mobility, warms up the rotator cuff, and increases blood flow to the shoulder complex.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Raise one arm forward and begin making large, slow circles in one direction.
Complete the desired number of circles, then switch direction.
Repeat with the other arm.
Keep your movements smooth and controlled throughout.

name: Standing Shoulder Stretch Hold (Hands Over Head)
description: Standing Shoulder Stretch Hold Hands Over Head is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids and lats. Pressing both hands overhead and leaning side to side provides a deep stretch through the shoulder and lateral trunk.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and raise both arms overhead.
Interlace your fingers and press your palms toward the ceiling.
Lean slightly to one side to deepen the stretch through the shoulder and lat.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then lean to the other side.
Return to center and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/shoulders/delts/stretching/beginner/0410 - Standing Shoulder Stretch Hold (Hands Over Head).mp4

name: Standing Y-T-W Stretch Series
description: Standing Y-T-W Stretch Series is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the trapezius and upper back muscles. Moving through the Y, T, and W arm positions improves shoulder mobility, posture, and scapular stability while stretching the shoulders and lats.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hinge forward slightly at the hips.
Raise both arms overhead in a Y position, squeezing the shoulder blades upward and inward.
Move your arms out to the sides at shoulder height to form a T, squeezing the mid-back.
Bend your elbows and pull them back into a W position, drawing the shoulder blades together.
Flow slowly between each position for 20-30 seconds while maintaining controlled breathing.

name: aeroplan balance
description: The aeroplan balance is a bodyweight exercise that targets the core and challenges overall balance and stability. It involves hinging forward on one leg while extending the opposite leg and arms, mimicking an airplane shape. This movement develops proprioception, hip stability, and core control.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and arms extended out to your sides.
Shift your weight onto one foot and slowly hinge forward at the hips, lifting the opposite leg behind you.
Extend your arms forward as your torso and raised leg come parallel to the ground.
Hold the position for the desired duration, keeping your core engaged and your body in a straight line.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.

name: aeroplan balance advanced
description: The aeroplan balance advanced is a challenging bodyweight variation that increases the duration and difficulty of the standard aeroplan balance. It demands greater core stability, hip control, and proprioception, making it suitable for intermediate to advanced trainees.
instructions: Stand upright on one foot with your arms extended out to your sides.
Hinge forward at the hips while simultaneously lifting the opposite leg behind you until your torso and leg are parallel to the floor.
Reach your arms forward and hold the position for a longer duration than the standard version.
Engage your core and glutes throughout to maintain stability and control.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat on the other side for the desired repetitions.

name: boat
description: The boat is a bodyweight core exercise performed from a seated position. It involves balancing on the tailbone with the legs and torso elevated, forming a V shape. The movement targets the abs and hip flexors while building core endurance and stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor, bringing your shins parallel to the ground.
Extend your arms forward, parallel to the floor, with your palms facing inward.
Hold this position, keeping your core engaged and your spine long.
Breathe steadily and maintain the pose for the desired duration.

name: boat hold with hand clapping
description: The boat hold with hand clapping is a bodyweight core exercise that adds an upper body coordination element to the standard boat pose. Clapping the hands while maintaining the V-sit position increases the challenge to core stability and engages the shoulder muscles dynamically.
instructions: Sit on the floor and assume the boat position by leaning back and lifting your legs until your shins are parallel to the ground.
Balance on your tailbone with your arms extended forward.
While maintaining the boat hold, clap your hands together in front of your chest.
Return your arms to the extended position after each clap.
Continue clapping for the desired number of repetitions while keeping your core engaged throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/balance/intermediate/0415 - boat hold with hand clapping.mp4

name: boat rocking
description: Boat rocking is a dynamic bodyweight core exercise based on the boat pose. It involves rocking the torso back and forth while keeping the legs elevated, creating continuous tension through the abs and hip flexors. This movement builds core endurance and control.
instructions: Sit on the floor and assume the boat position with your legs lifted and shins parallel to the ground.
Extend your arms forward with palms facing inward.
Slowly rock your torso back, lowering your upper body toward the floor while keeping your legs raised.
Return to the upright boat position by engaging your abs to pull your torso forward.
Continue rocking in a controlled manner for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated in-and-outs hold
description: The seated in-and-outs hold is a bodyweight isometric core exercise performed from a seated position. It involves holding the legs extended while leaning back, placing sustained demand on the abs and hip flexors. The movement builds core endurance and lower abdominal strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your hands placed beside your hips for support.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground, extending your legs straight out in front of you.
Hold this extended position with your core fully engaged and your spine neutral.
Keep your legs elevated and together throughout the hold duration.
Maintain steady breathing and sustain the position for the desired time.

name: single leg balance
description: The single leg balance is a foundational bodyweight exercise that challenges balance, proprioception, and lower body stability. By standing on one leg, the exercise activates the core and glutes while improving ankle and knee joint stability.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the opposite foot off the ground.
Bend the lifted knee to approximately 90 degrees and hold the position.
Keep your standing leg slightly soft and your core engaged to maintain stability.
Hold for the desired duration, then switch legs and repeat.

name: single leg balance standing straight
description: The single leg balance standing straight is a bodyweight balance exercise that increases the demand compared to the basic single leg balance by requiring the raised leg to be held straight. This challenges core stability, hip flexor strength, and overall balance control.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet together and arms extended out to your sides.
Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the other leg straight out in front of you, keeping the knee fully extended.
Hold the raised leg at hip height or as high as comfortable.
Keep your core engaged and your standing leg firm throughout the hold.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/balance/intermediate/0419 - single leg balance standing straight.mp4

name: single leg balance with front hand
description: The single leg balance with front hand is a bodyweight balance exercise that adds an arm position challenge to the standard single leg balance. Extending the arms forward shifts the center of gravity and increases the demand on core stability and proprioception.
instructions: Stand upright on one foot with your core engaged and your body tall.
Lift the opposite leg off the ground, bending the knee to 90 degrees.
Extend both arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
Hold this position, maintaining balance on the standing leg without allowing your torso to sway.
Hold for the desired duration, then switch legs and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/balance/intermediate/0420 - single leg balance with front hand.mp4

name: burpee (low impact)
description: The low impact burpee is a modified full-body cardio exercise that removes jumping to reduce stress on the joints. It retains the core and upper body engagement of the traditional burpee while making the movement accessible for beginners or those with joint concerns.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and place your hands on the floor in front of you.
Step one foot back, then the other, to reach a plank position rather than jumping.
Perform a controlled push-up or simply hold the plank briefly, then step your feet back toward your hands.
Rise back to standing by straightening your legs and returning to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions at a steady, controlled pace.

name: burpee cardio
description: The burpee cardio is a high-intensity full-body exercise that combines a squat, plank, push-up, and jump into one fluid movement. It elevates the heart rate rapidly and engages multiple muscle groups simultaneously, making it a highly effective cardiovascular and strength conditioning exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Drop your hands to the floor and jump your feet back to a plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump your feet back toward your hands.
Explosively jump up, extending your arms overhead at the top of the movement.
Land softly and immediately transition into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions at a high intensity.

name: calf place jog
description: The calf place jog is a low-intensity cardio exercise that emphasizes calf activation while jogging in place. It is performed by focusing on the push-off phase through the forefoot and heel raise, targeting the calf muscles and providing a gentle cardiovascular stimulus.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your core engaged.
Begin jogging in place at a controlled pace.
Focus on pushing through the balls of your feet and raising your heels with each step.
Keep your arms bent at 90 degrees and swing them naturally in coordination with your legs.
Continue for the desired duration, maintaining a steady rhythm.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/calves/cardio/beginner/0423 - calf place jog.mp4

name: fast feet run
description: The fast feet run is a high-intensity cardio drill that involves rapid, short steps in place to elevate the heart rate and improve foot speed. It primarily targets the cardiovascular system while engaging the upper and lower leg muscles throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Lower into a slight squat position and begin rapidly shuffling your feet in place.
Keep your steps short and quick, staying on the balls of your feet.
Pump your arms in rhythm with your feet to maintain speed and coordination.
Continue for the desired duration at maximum speed.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/intermediate/0424 - fast feet run.mp4

name: hand raise front jumping jack
description: The hand raise front jumping jack is a cardio variation of the traditional jumping jack where the arms are raised forward to shoulder height rather than overhead. This modification engages the anterior deltoids more directly while providing an effective cardiovascular workout.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together and your arms at your sides.
Jump your feet out to shoulder-width while simultaneously raising both arms straight in front of you to shoulder height.
Jump your feet back together and lower your arms to the starting position.
Repeat the movement continuously at a brisk pace.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0425 - hand raise front jumping jack.mp4

name: high knee
description: The high knee is a dynamic cardio exercise that involves driving the knees up toward the chest in a rapid alternating pattern while jogging in place. It elevates the heart rate, engages the hip flexors and core, and improves running mechanics and coordination.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your arms bent at 90 degrees.
Begin jogging in place, driving your right knee up toward your chest.
As you lower your right leg, drive your left knee up in an alternating pattern.
Pump your arms in opposition to your legs to maintain rhythm and balance.
Continue at a brisk pace for the desired duration.

name: knee tuck jump
description: The knee tuck jump is a plyometric cardio exercise that combines an explosive vertical jump with a double knee tuck at the peak. It develops lower body power, cardiovascular endurance, and core control while engaging the hip flexors and leg muscles dynamically.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Swing your arms down and bend your knees to prepare for the jump.
Explosively jump upward, driving both knees toward your chest at the peak of the jump.
Quickly extend your legs to land softly on the balls of your feet with your knees slightly bent.
Immediately transition into the next repetition and continue for the desired number of jumps.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/plyometrics/advanced/0427 - knee tuck jump.mp4

name: modified superman hold (legs grounded)
description: The modified superman hold with legs grounded is a beginner-friendly back strengthening exercise. By keeping the legs flat on the floor, the focus shifts entirely to the upper back and lower back extensors, reducing difficulty while still effectively targeting the posterior chain.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your legs extended and your arms stretched overhead.
Keep your legs flat on the ground and focus on lifting only your chest and arms off the floor.
Squeeze your glutes and engage your lower back muscles as you lift.
Hold the raised position for the desired duration.
Slowly lower your chest and arms back to the floor and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/strength/beginner/0428 - modified superman hold (legs grounded).mp4

name: mountain climber
description: The mountain climber is a dynamic bodyweight exercise that targets the abs while providing a significant cardiovascular challenge. Performed in a plank position, it involves rapidly alternating knee drives toward the chest, engaging the core, hip flexors, and upper body stabilizers simultaneously.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and drive your right knee toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs, extending the right leg back while driving the left knee forward.
Continue alternating legs in a rapid, running motion.
Keep your hips level and your core tight throughout the movement.

name: mountain climber hold
description: The mountain climber hold is an isometric variation of the mountain climber that involves holding the knee-to-chest position rather than alternating rapidly. This modification increases the time under tension for the abs and hip flexors while reinforcing plank stability.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Drive your right knee toward your chest and hold it in the tucked position.
Keep your hips level and your core engaged throughout the hold.
Hold for the desired duration, then return the leg to the start and repeat on the left side.
Alternate legs as directed, focusing on maintaining a rigid plank position.

name: place jog cardio
description: The place jog cardio is a simple low-impact cardio exercise performed by jogging in place. It raises the heart rate with minimal equipment or space, making it effective as a warm-up, active rest period, or standalone cardiovascular conditioning movement.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Begin jogging in place by alternating lifting your feet slightly off the ground.
Keep a relaxed but engaged posture with your arms bent at 90 degrees and swinging naturally.
Maintain a consistent pace throughout the exercise.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0431 - place jog cardio.mp4

name: prisoner high knee cardio
description: The prisoner high knee cardio is a bodyweight cardio exercise that combines the high knee drill with the prisoner arm position. Placing the hands behind the head eliminates arm momentum and increases core and upper body engagement while sustaining cardiovascular demand.
instructions: Stand upright and place both hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outward.
Begin lifting your knees alternately toward your chest in a rapid high-knee jogging pattern.
Keep your chest up and your elbows back throughout the movement.
Pump your legs continuously at a brisk pace.
Continue for the desired duration while maintaining the prisoner hand position.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/intermediate/0432 - prisoner high knee cardio.mp4

name: run in place
description: Run in place is a fundamental cardio exercise that simulates outdoor running without requiring space or equipment. It elevates the heart rate efficiently and engages the legs and arms in a natural running pattern, making it ideal for warm-ups, cardio intervals, or home workouts.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Begin running in place by alternating lifting each foot off the ground.
Bend your arms at 90 degrees and swing them in natural opposition to your legs.
Gradually increase your pace to a comfortable running speed.
Continue for the desired duration, maintaining consistent form.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0433 - run in place.mp4

name: seated-to-floor cardio drop (burpee-style landing)
description: The seated-to-floor cardio drop is a burpee-style movement that involves dropping from standing to the floor and returning upright in a continuous cardio sequence. It challenges the cardiovascular system while engaging the full body, including the core, chest, and legs, through the rapid transitions.
instructions: Begin standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Quickly lower yourself into a squat, place your hands on the floor, and jump or step your feet back to reach a plank position.
Lower your body to the floor in a controlled manner.
Push up from the floor and jump or step your feet back toward your hands.
Return to standing and repeat immediately for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/intermediate/0434 - seated-to-floor cardio drop (burpee-style landing).mp4

name: speed march
description: The speed march is a brisk marching-in-place cardio exercise that elevates the heart rate while reducing joint impact compared to running. Driving the knees to hip height and swinging the arms vigorously engages the hip flexors, abs, and legs in a coordinated, rhythmic pattern.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
Begin marching in place at a brisk, controlled pace.
Drive your knees up to hip height with each step.
Swing your arms in opposition to your legs, bent at 90 degrees.
Increase your pace to the highest speed you can sustain with good form and continue for the desired duration.

name: tunk jump
description: The tunk jump is a plyometric cardio exercise involving explosive squat jumps performed in rapid succession. It develops lower body power, cardiovascular endurance, and landing mechanics, making it effective for both athletic conditioning and high-intensity interval training.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Bend your knees into a partial squat position.
Explosively jump upward, extending through your hips and knees at the peak.
Land softly with your knees bent to absorb the impact.
Immediately lower into the squat and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chair arm stretch
description: The chair arm stretch is a seated bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps and shoulder muscles. Performed while seated in a chair for support, it involves bending the elbow overhead and applying gentle pressure to achieve a deep stretch along the back of the upper arm.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Extend one arm overhead and bend the elbow, dropping your hand behind your head.
Use your opposite hand to gently press down on the elbow to deepen the stretch.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds, feeling the elongation through the tricep and shoulder.
Release and repeat on the opposite arm.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/stretching/beginner/0437 - chair arm stretch.mp4

name: chair back hand raise up stretch
description: The chair back hand raise up stretch is a seated bodyweight exercise that stretches the lats and upper back by reaching the arms overhead. It gently decompresses the spine and improves shoulder mobility, making it ideal as a recovery or mobility exercise.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Extend both arms straight overhead, reaching toward the ceiling.
Lengthen through your spine as you reach, allowing a gentle stretch through the sides and back.
Hold the position for 20–30 seconds.
Lower your arms and repeat as needed.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0438 - chair back hand raise up stretch.mp4

name: chair back overhead stretch
description: The chair back overhead stretch is a seated stretch that targets the lats and upper back through an overhead arm raise combined with a lateral lean. It improves thoracic mobility and reduces upper back tension, suitable for office or rehabilitation settings.
instructions: Sit tall in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Interlace your fingers and raise your arms overhead, palms facing upward.
Gently lean to one side to create a lateral stretch along the back and side.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then return to center and lean to the opposite side.
Repeat on each side for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chair back stretch - hand behind back
description: The chair back stretch with hands behind back is a seated bodyweight stretch that opens the chest and stretches the lats and shoulders. By clasping the hands behind the back and drawing the shoulder blades together, this exercise counteracts rounded posture and improves thoracic extension.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair and place both hands behind your back, interlacing your fingers.
Gently draw your shoulders back and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Lift your chest and hold the position for 20–30 seconds.
Release the hands and relax before repeating.
Perform for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0440 - chair back stretch - hand behind back.mp4

name: chair full neck circle up down
description: The chair full neck circle up down is a seated mobility exercise that moves the neck through its full range of circular motion. It targets the neck muscles and upper trapezius, relieving tension and improving cervical mobility, making it suitable for desk-based or rehabilitation routines.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your spine tall and shoulders relaxed.
Gently lower your chin toward your chest.
Slowly roll your head to one side, bringing your ear toward your shoulder.
Continue the circular motion by tilting your head back and then to the opposite side.
Complete a full slow circle in each direction for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/mobility/beginner/0441 - chair full neck circle up down.mp4

name: chair inner thigh push-out
description: The chair inner thigh push-out is a seated bodyweight exercise that uses isometric resistance to engage the hip abductors. By pressing the knees outward against the hands, the glutes and outer thigh muscles are activated, making it an accessible seated strengthening exercise.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor and knees together.
Place your hands on the outside of your knees.
Press your knees outward against the resistance of your hands.
Hold the contraction for 2–3 seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0442 - chair inner thigh push-out.mp4

name: chair neck extension
description: The chair neck extension is a seated mobility exercise that targets the posterior neck muscles and stretches the anterior neck. By gently tilting the head back while seated, it improves cervical extension range of motion and can help relieve neck stiffness from prolonged sitting.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Slowly tilt your head backward, looking up toward the ceiling.
Hold the extended position for a few seconds, feeling the stretch along the front of the neck.
Return your head to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chair seated bike
description: The chair seated bike is a seated cardio exercise that mimics cycling motion while seated in a chair. It targets the quads and hip flexors while engaging the core, offering a low-impact cardiovascular option suitable for those with mobility limitations or as a rehabilitation exercise.
instructions: Sit toward the front edge of a chair with your hands gripping the sides for support.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the floor.
Begin pedaling your legs in a circular cycling motion, alternating extending and bending each knee.
Keep your core engaged and your back from rounding excessively.
Continue for the desired duration at a smooth, controlled pace.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/cardio/beginner/0444 - chair seated bike.mp4

name: hamstring stretch chair
description: The hamstring stretch chair is a seated bodyweight stretch that targets the hamstring muscles using a chair for support. By extending one leg and hinging forward at the hips, a deep stretch is achieved along the back of the thigh, improving flexibility and reducing lower back tension.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with one leg bent normally and the other leg extended straight in front of you.
Sit tall and hinge forward at the hips over the extended leg.
Reach your hands toward your foot, feeling a stretch along the back of the extended leg.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0445 - hamstring stretch chair.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out – chair seated version
description: The inner thigh push-out chair seated version is a seated isometric exercise targeting the hip abductors. By positioning the hands on the inside of the knees and pushing outward against them, the outer thigh and glute muscles are effectively activated with minimal joint stress.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Open your knees wide apart, allowing your thighs to spread outward.
Place your hands on the inside of your knees and gently press inward as resistance.
Push your knees outward against the hand resistance and hold for 2–3 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0446 - inner thigh push-out – chair seated version.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out – chair wide-knee seated version
description: The inner thigh push-out chair wide-knee seated version is a seated isometric exercise that targets the adductors by pressing the knees inward against hand resistance from a wide-legged position. This variation places the adductors in a more lengthened position, increasing their activation.
instructions: Sit on a chair with your knees spread wide apart and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands on the outside of your knees.
Press your knees inward against the resistance of your hands.
Hold the contraction for 2–3 seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0447 - inner thigh push-out – chair wide-knee seated version.mp4

name: seated arm stretch chair
description: The seated arm stretch chair is a seated overhead tricep stretch performed while sitting in a chair for stability. It targets the tricep and posterior shoulder, promoting upper arm flexibility and reducing tightness in the overhead position.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Raise one arm overhead and place the hand behind your head.
Use the opposite hand to grasp the raised elbow and gently pull it toward your head.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds, feeling a deep stretch in the tricep.
Release and repeat on the other arm.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/triceps/stretching/beginner/0448 - seated arm stretch chair.mp4

name: seated arms & chest stretch chair
description: The seated arms and chest stretch chair is a seated posture and flexibility exercise that opens the chest and stretches the pectoral muscles. By clasping the hands behind the back and lifting the arms, the chest and anterior shoulder muscles are gently elongated.
instructions: Sit tall in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Clasp your hands behind your back with fingers interlaced.
Straighten your arms and lift them slightly, drawing your shoulder blades together.
Open your chest upward and hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/chest/stretching/beginner/0449 - seated arms & chest stretch chair.mp4

name: seated chair forward fold leg touch hips up
description: The seated chair forward fold leg touch hips up is a seated hamstring and lower back stretch that combines a forward fold with a mild hip lift to intensify the stretch. It targets the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and decompressing the lower spine.
instructions: Sit at the front edge of a chair with your legs extended and feet together on the floor.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach your hands toward your feet or the floor.
Press through your heels and engage your glutes as you fold forward.
Hold the lowest point briefly, then press your hips slightly upward to deepen the stretch.
Return to an upright position and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0450 - seated chair forward fold leg touch hips up.mp4

name: seated chair leg bent hold
description: The seated chair leg bent hold is a seated exercise targeting the hip flexors and quads through a sustained hold of one raised bent leg. It can be used for gentle hip mobilization and stretching or as an accessible core stability drill.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Lift one foot off the ground and bend the knee, bringing it toward your chest.
Hold the shin or knee with both hands to assist in raising the leg.
Hold the position for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the hip flexor and glute.
Lower the leg and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0451 - seated chair leg bent hold.mp4

name: seated chair march
description: The seated chair march is a low-impact cardio exercise performed while seated. It mimics marching by alternately lifting the knees, raising the heart rate, and engaging the hip flexors and core without placing load on the joints, making it ideal for seniors or those with limited mobility.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your thighs.
Begin lifting one knee at a time in a marching motion.
Drive each knee up to hip height alternately, pumping your arms in sync.
Maintain a brisk, consistent pace throughout.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0452 - seated chair march.mp4

name: seated chair punch
description: The seated chair punch is a seated cardio and strength exercise that alternates forward punching motions while seated. It targets the shoulders and triceps while engaging the core for stability, providing an upper body cardio stimulus suitable for chair-based or low-impact workouts.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your core engaged and feet flat on the floor.
Make fists with both hands and hold them at chest height.
Extend one arm forward in a controlled punching motion.
Retract that arm and immediately extend the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms at a brisk pace for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper arms/shoulders/cardio/beginner/0453 - seated chair punch.mp4

name: seated chair twist upper back
description: The seated chair twist upper back is a seated rotational mobility exercise targeting the thoracic spine and upper back muscles. By rotating the torso while keeping the hips still, the traps and lats are stretched while spinal rotation and flexibility are improved.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms crossed over your chest.
Keeping your hips facing forward and stationary, rotate your upper body to one side.
Hold the rotated position for a moment, feeling the stretch across your upper back.
Return to center and rotate to the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/traps/mobility/beginner/0454 - seated chair twist upper back.mp4

name: seated chest opener(chair cardio)
description: The seated chest opener is a chair-based cardio exercise that dynamically opens and closes the arms to engage the chest and shoulders. The rapid arm movement increases heart rate while stretching the pectoral muscles, making it an effective seated cardio and mobility drill.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Extend both arms out to your sides at shoulder height.
Rapidly draw your arms in front of you, crossing them at the wrists, then open them back out to the sides.
Continue the opening and closing motion at a brisk pace.
Perform for the desired duration to elevate the heart rate.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/chest/cardio/beginner/0455 - seated chest opener(chair cardio).mp4

name: seated fast toe taps chair
description: The seated fast toe taps chair is a seated cardio drill that targets the calves and improves foot speed while seated. Rapidly alternating toe taps elevates the heart rate and engages the lower leg muscles, providing an accessible cardiovascular stimulus with minimal joint load.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Begin rapidly tapping your toes on the floor, alternating left and right.
Keep the movement quick and light, staying on the balls of your feet.
Pump your arms in rhythm with your feet if desired.
Continue for the desired duration at maximum speed.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/calves/cardio/beginner/0456 - seated fast toe taps chair.mp4

name: seated flutter kicks – chair version
description: The seated flutter kicks chair version is a seated core and hip flexor exercise that replicates the lying flutter kick in an upright position. By alternating rapid leg kicks while seated, the hip flexors and lower abs are engaged, making it suitable for chair-based workouts.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair and grip the sides of the seat for support.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the floor.
Begin rapidly alternating small kicks up and down with your legs, keeping them relatively straight.
Keep your core engaged and your lower back from arching excessively.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0457 - seated flutter kicks – chair version.mp4

name: seated half squat
description: The seated half squat is a beginner-friendly squat variation that uses a chair as a depth guide. By stopping the descent just above the seat, the quads, glutes, and hamstrings are engaged through a partial range of motion, making it ideal for those building squat strength or mobility.
instructions: Stand in front of a chair with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your hips toward the chair, bending your knees and hinging at the hips.
Stop when your hips are just above the chair seat, hovering without fully sitting.
Hold the position for a moment, then push through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0458 - seated half squat.mp4

name: seated high knees (chair cardio)
description: Seated high knees is a chair-based cardio exercise that mimics the standing high knee drill from a seated position. It raises the heart rate and engages the hip flexors and abs while remaining accessible to those with lower extremity limitations or those performing chair-based workouts.
instructions: Sit upright on the edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Begin lifting one knee toward your chest as high as comfortable.
Lower that leg and immediately lift the opposite knee in an alternating fashion.
Pump your arms in coordination with your legs to increase intensity.
Continue at a brisk pace for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0459 - seated high knees (chair cardio).mp4

name: seated knee-to-chest crunch
description: The seated knee-to-chest crunch is a chair-based core exercise that targets the abs through a knee drive from a seated position. It engages the hip flexors and lower abdominals, making it an accessible crunch variation for those seeking a seated core workout.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with your hands gripping the sides of the seat for support.
Lean back slightly and extend one leg out in front of you.
Draw your knee toward your chest, contracting your abs at the top.
Extend the leg back out and repeat on the same side for the desired reps before switching.
Keep your core engaged throughout the movement.

name: seated knee-to-chest crunch v hold
description: The seated knee-to-chest crunch V hold is an isometric chair-based core exercise that holds the knees toward the chest from a seated position. The sustained contraction intensifies the demand on the abs and hip flexors compared to the standard knee-to-chest crunch variation.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair and grip the sides of the seat for support.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the floor, drawing your knees toward your chest.
Hold the knees-to-chest position for the desired duration.
Keep your core fully contracted and your lower back from rounding excessively.
Lower your feet and return to the starting position before repeating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0461 - seated knee-to-chest crunch v hold.mp4

name: seated shoulder press
description: The seated shoulder press is a bodyweight overhead pressing exercise performed while seated. It targets the deltoid muscles and engages the triceps and upper traps through the pressing motion, providing an effective seated upper body strengthening drill.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor and your core engaged.
Raise both arms to shoulder height with your elbows bent at 90 degrees, palms facing forward.
Press your arms upward until they are fully extended overhead.
Slowly lower your arms back to the 90-degree position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: air bike sit on hand support floor
description: The air bike sit on hand support floor is a seated bodyweight exercise that simulates a cycling motion on the floor using hand support for stability. It targets the abs and hip flexors through the continuous pedaling action, serving as an accessible core and cardio drill.
instructions: Sit on the floor and place your hands flat on the ground behind you for support.
Lift your feet off the floor and begin pedaling your legs in a cycling motion.
Alternate bending and extending each knee in a smooth, controlled bicycle pattern.
Keep your core engaged and your chest upright throughout the movement.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0463 - air bike sit on hand support floor.mp4

name: air twisting crunch
description: The air twisting crunch is a bodyweight core exercise that combines a crunch with a rotational twist and alternating leg extension. It targets the abs and obliques with added involvement of the hip flexors, making it an effective compound abdominal movement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed behind your head and your legs raised to a 90-degree angle.
Lift your shoulder blades off the floor and rotate your torso, bringing your right elbow toward your left knee.
At the same time, extend your right leg straight out.
Return to center and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: air twisting crunch advanced
description: The air twisting crunch advanced is an intensified version of the air twisting crunch performed with the legs held lower and at a faster tempo. The lower leg position increases the demand on the lower abs while the rotational component continues to target the obliques.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands behind your head and your legs fully extended and raised slightly above the floor.
Simultaneously crunch upward and rotate your torso, bringing one elbow toward the opposite knee as you bend that knee in.
Extend the leg back out and return your torso toward center before immediately repeating on the other side.
Maintain a faster tempo than the standard version with the legs kept lower for increased difficulty.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/advanced/0465 - air twisting crunch advanced.mp4

name: bicycle crunch advanced
description: The bicycle crunch advanced is a challenging version of the standard bicycle crunch performed with the legs held low and at a controlled, deliberate pace. Slowing the movement and keeping the legs lower increases the time under tension for the abs and obliques significantly.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands behind your head and your legs fully extended, hovering just above the floor.
Bring your right elbow toward your left knee while simultaneously extending your right leg straight out close to the floor.
Reverse the movement, switching to the left elbow toward the right knee.
Perform the movement at a slow, controlled tempo, pausing at each elbow-to-knee contact.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crunch
description: The crunch is a foundational bodyweight core exercise that targets the rectus abdominis. By lifting the shoulder blades off the floor with the knees bent, the movement isolates the abs through spinal flexion, forming the basis of most abdominal training routines.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outward.
Engage your abs and lift your shoulder blades off the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: crunch elbow to knee touch
description: The crunch elbow to knee touch is a rotational crunch variation that targets the abs and obliques by bringing the opposite elbow and knee together at the top of each crunch. The twisting motion increases oblique engagement compared to a standard crunch.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head and lift your shoulder blades off the floor.
As you crunch upward, bring your right elbow and left knee toward each other until they touch.
Lower back down and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0468 - crunch elbow to knee touch.mp4

name: crunch head to knee touch
description: The crunch head to knee touch is a bodyweight core exercise that combines a crunch with a knee drive to bring the head and knee toward each other. This movement intensifies the abdominal contraction and increases hip flexor involvement compared to a standard crunch.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with one knee bent and the opposite leg extended straight.
Place your hands behind your head and crunch upward.
Simultaneously drive the bent knee toward your head while lifting your head toward the knee.
Touch or bring your head as close to your knee as possible.
Lower back down and repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0469 - crunch head to knee touch.mp4

name: crunch with slow upward
description: The crunch with slow upward is a tempo-based crunch variation that slows the concentric phase of the movement. Pausing the ascent increases time under tension for the rectus abdominis, promoting greater muscle activation and strength development compared to standard-speed crunches.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows wide.
Slowly lift your shoulder blades off the floor over a count of 3–4 seconds.
Pause at the top of the crunch for a moment.
Lower back down at a normal pace and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0470 - crunch with slow upward.mp4

name: dead bug leg only
description: The dead bug leg only is a core stability exercise that isolates the leg lowering component of the full dead bug. By alternating slow leg extensions from the tabletop position, the lower abs and hip flexors are challenged while the lower back remains pressed into the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise your arms straight up toward the ceiling.
Lift your legs so your knees are bent at 90 degrees and your shins are parallel to the floor.
Slowly lower one leg toward the floor, extending it straight while keeping the opposite leg still.
Return the leg to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0471 - dead bug leg only.mp4

name: dead bug crunch
description: The dead bug crunch combines the dead bug movement pattern with an added crunch, increasing the demand on the rectus abdominis. The simultaneous limb extension and torso crunch require significant core coordination, anti-extension control, and abdominal strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling and legs at a 90-degree tabletop position.
Simultaneously lower one leg toward the floor and reach the opposite arm overhead.
As the limbs extend, crunch your torso slightly upward to engage the abs more deeply.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: half way crunch
