description: The halfway crunch is a bodyweight core exercise that holds the crunch at its midpoint and performs small pulsing contractions. This technique keeps the abs under continuous tension throughout the set, increasing muscle endurance and the burn compared to full-range crunches.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head and lift your shoulder blades off the floor.
Hold the crunch at the midpoint, approximately halfway between flat and fully upright.
Perform small pulsing crunches from this midpoint position.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: hollow hold 1
description: The hollow hold 1 is a foundational gymnastics-based isometric core exercise. By holding the body in a shallow hollow position with arms and legs raised, the entire core is placed under sustained tension, building deep abdominal strength and anti-extension stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight and arms extended overhead.
Press your lower back firmly into the floor and engage your core.
Lift your arms, shoulders, and legs slightly off the floor simultaneously.
Hold this hollow body position for the desired duration.
Focus on keeping the lower back in contact with the floor and breathing steadily.

name: hollow hold advanced
description: The hollow hold advanced is a more demanding variation of the hollow hold performed with the arms and legs held lower, closer to the floor. This position increases the mechanical disadvantage and demands significantly greater core strength and endurance to maintain.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Press your lower back firmly into the floor and brace your core maximally.
Lift your arms, shoulders, head, and legs to a lower position than the basic version, bringing them closer to the floor while maintaining spinal contact.
Hold the position for an extended duration without allowing your lower back to arch.
Breathe steadily throughout the hold and repeat as desired.

name: incline sit up plus
description: The incline sit-up plus is an advanced bodyweight core exercise performed on an inclined surface that adds an extra movement at the top of the sit-up to increase range of motion and abs activation. The incline increases resistance compared to a flat floor sit-up.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your head at the lower end and feet secured at the top.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Perform a full sit-up by curling your torso upward until you are fully upright.
At the top, add an additional forward reach or rotation for extra range.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat.

name: incline sit-up
description: The incline sit-up is a bodyweight core exercise performed on an inclined bench with the head lower than the feet. The incline increases the range of motion and gravity-based resistance compared to floor sit-ups, placing greater demand on the rectus abdominis and hip flexors.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your head at the lower end and your feet secured at the top.
Place your hands behind your head or cross your arms over your chest.
Engage your abs and curl your torso upward until you are fully seated.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline sit-up + forward reach
description: The incline sit-up with forward reach is a variation that adds an arm reach at the top of the sit-up to increase range of motion and core engagement. The forward reach extends the abdominal contraction and engages the shoulder flexors as a secondary movement.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your head lower and feet secured at the top.
Begin with your arms extended overhead.
Curl your torso upward into a full sit-up while simultaneously reaching your arms forward toward your feet.
Pause at the top with arms extended forward.
Slowly lower back to the starting position and repeat.

name: incline sit-up + rotation
description: The incline sit-up with rotation is a rotational core exercise combining an incline sit-up with an alternating torso twist at the peak. The rotation increases the oblique engagement alongside the primary rectus abdominis work from the incline sit-up.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your head at the lower end and your feet secured.
Place your hands behind your head.
Curl your torso up and rotate to one side at the top of the movement.
Lower back to the starting position and repeat with a rotation to the other side.
Continue alternating rotations for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline sit-up + rotation 1
description: The incline sit-up plus rotation 1 is a variation of the incline sit-up with rotation that emphasizes a momentary hold at the rotated top position. The pause increases time under tension in the obliques and abs, enhancing the muscle activation compared to a continuous-flow version.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your feet secured and your hands behind your head.
Curl your torso upward into the sit-up.
At the top, hold the rotation to one side for a beat before lowering.
Alternate the direction of rotation each repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: incline sit-up
description: The incline sit-up is a bodyweight exercise performed on an angled bench that leverages gravity to increase the resistance of the sit-up movement. It builds rectus abdominis strength through a full range of motion, with the incline adding challenge beyond a flat floor version.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your head at the lower end and feet anchored at the top.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands beside your temples.
Engage your abs and curl upward to a fully seated position.
Hold briefly at the top, then lower yourself in a controlled manner back to the start.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying vertical crunches (legs straight up)
description: The lying vertical crunch with legs straight up is a bodyweight core exercise that performs the crunch with the legs extended vertically. The raised leg position increases the difficulty of the crunch by removing hip flexor assistance and demanding greater rectus abdominis activation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise both legs straight up toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the floor.
Place your hands behind your head or cross your arms over your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your shoulder blades off the floor, reaching your hands toward your feet.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0482 - lying vertical crunches (legs straight up).mp4

name: lying vertical crunches (legs straight up) advance
description: The lying vertical crunches advance is an intensified version of the vertical crunch that incorporates an explosive concentric phase and an extended hold at the top. The combination of speed and peak contraction maximizes abs activation and builds greater abdominal power.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise both legs straight up toward the ceiling.
Place your hands behind your head.
Crunch upward explosively, bringing your chest as close to your knees as possible.
Hold the top position for a moment, squeezing the abs hard.
Lower with control and repeat at a challenging pace for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/advanced/0483 - lying vertical crunches (legs straight up) advance.mp4

name: reverse crunch ii
description: The reverse crunch II is a bodyweight lower abdominal exercise performed by curling the hips off the floor from a tabletop leg position. It targets the lower portion of the rectus abdominis and hip flexors while the arms and palms provide stability on the floor.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides and palms flat on the floor.
Lift your legs to a 90-degree tabletop position.
Engage your lower abs and curl your hips off the floor, bringing your knees toward your chest.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse crunch
description: The reverse crunch is a fundamental bodyweight exercise targeting the lower abdominals. By lifting the hips from a bent-knee position rather than raising the torso, the lower portion of the rectus abdominis is emphasized, making it a staple lower core movement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides.
Raise your legs and bend your knees to 90 degrees.
Using your lower abs, curl your knees toward your chest by lifting your hips off the floor.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower your hips back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse crunch i
description: The reverse crunch I is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the lower abs through a hip curl movement. It is a beginner variation with a standard bent-knee position and controlled hip lift, focusing on developing lower abdominal strength and body awareness.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hands pressing flat on the floor at your sides.
Bring your knees toward your chest with your legs bent.
Curl your hips off the ground toward your ribcage in a controlled motion.
Lower your hips slowly back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: reverse crunch with glute bridge
description: The reverse crunch with glute bridge is a compound bodyweight exercise that combines a glute bridge with a reverse crunch. It targets the abs and glutes simultaneously, providing a comprehensive core and posterior chain workout within a single flowing movement.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Press through your feet to lift your hips into a glute bridge.
From the bridge position, draw your knees toward your chest to perform a reverse crunch.
Return your feet to the floor and lower your hips to complete one repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0487 - reverse crunch with glute bridge.mp4

name: reverse crunch with glute bridge beginners
description: The reverse crunch with glute bridge beginners is a modified combination of the glute bridge and reverse crunch suited for those new to core training. The reduced range of motion makes the exercise accessible while still engaging the glutes and lower abdominals.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Gently press your feet into the floor and lift your hips a few inches into a beginner glute bridge.
From this position, slowly draw your knees toward your chest in a small reverse crunch.
Lower your feet back down and release the bridge.
Repeat at a controlled pace for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0488 - reverse crunch with glute bridge beginners.mp4

name: reverse crunch with straight leg
description: The reverse crunch with straight leg is an advanced bodyweight core exercise that performs the reverse crunch with legs fully extended. The straight-leg position increases the lever arm, significantly raising the demand on the lower abs and hip flexors compared to the bent-knee variation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides and legs extended straight toward the ceiling.
Engage your lower abs and curl your hips off the floor, pressing your legs upward.
Hold at the top briefly, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
Keep your legs straight throughout the entire movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/advanced/0489 - reverse crunch with straight leg.mp4

name: standing chin to chest
description: The standing chin to chest is a gentle neck stretch performed while standing. It targets the posterior neck muscles and upper trapezius by flexing the cervical spine, relieving tension accumulated from prolonged forward head posture or desk work.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your shoulders relaxed.
Slowly lower your chin toward your chest, feeling a stretch along the back of the neck.
Hold the position for 20–30 seconds.
Gently raise your head back to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v-sit up
description: The V-sit up is a bodyweight core exercise that alternates between a boat-like position and a V-shape, challenging the abs and hip flexors through a dynamic range of motion. It builds core strength, balance, and hip flexor endurance.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor, bringing your shins parallel to the ground.
Simultaneously extend your legs straight out and lean your torso back to form a V shape.
Hold the position for a beat, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v-sit up advanced
description: The V-sit up advanced is a demanding full-body V-sit where the movement begins from a fully flat lying position and rises to a balanced V-shape. The increased range of motion from the floor requires greater core power and hip flexor strength than the standard variation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously lift your torso and legs off the floor, reaching your hands toward your feet to form a full V shape.
Balance on your tailbone at the top position for a moment.
Slowly lower back to the flat starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: v-sit ups half
description: The V-sit ups half is a modified V-sit exercise that uses a partial leg extension rather than a full stretch. This intermediate variation reduces the demand on the hip flexors and lower back while still engaging the abs in a held position, serving as a progression between the boat hold and full V-sit.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor into the boat position.
Partially extend your legs to about a 45-degree angle rather than fully straight.
Hold the half V position for the desired duration.
Lower and repeat as needed.

name: v-up crunches
description: The V-up crunch is a challenging bodyweight exercise that targets the full core by simultaneously raising the legs and torso from a flat position. It requires coordinated core strength and hip flexor power, making it one of the most effective total abdominal movements.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Simultaneously raise your torso and both legs toward each other, keeping your legs straight.
Reach your hands toward your feet at the top of the movement.
Lower both your torso and legs back to the floor in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: adductor stretch forward
description: The adductor stretch forward is a bodyweight inner thigh stretch performed in a wide-stance position. By shifting the weight to one side, the adductors of the opposite straight leg are elongated, improving hip mobility and inner thigh flexibility.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart.
Shift your weight to one side, bending that knee and keeping the other leg straight.
Lower your hips toward the bent knee side, feeling a stretch along the inner thigh of the straight leg.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then shift to the other side.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0495 - adductor stretch forward.mp4

name: advanced frog side -shift version 2
description: The advanced frog side-shift version 2 is a dynamic hip mobility exercise performed from the frog position. Side-shifting the hips opens the inner thighs and hip capsule progressively, improving adductor flexibility and deep hip range of motion.
instructions: Begin in a frog position on all fours with your knees spread wide and feet turned outward.
Shift your hips to one side and then rock back to center.
Move further to each side with each repetition to explore a greater range.
Keep your core engaged and your lower back from excessively arching.
Continue for the desired number of side shifts in each direction.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/mobility/advanced/0496 - advanced frog side -shift version 2.mp4

name: advanced frog side-shift version
description: The advanced frog side-shift is a bodyweight hip mobility drill that uses lateral hip shifting from the frog position to stretch the adductors and hip joint. It is used to develop deep inner thigh flexibility and improve overall hip range of motion.
instructions: Start in a frog position on hands and knees with your knees wide and feet turned out.
Shift your hips slowly to one side, lowering toward the floor.
Return to the center and shift to the opposite side.
Maintain a controlled, deliberate movement pace throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/mobility/advanced/0497 - advanced frog side-shift version.mp4

name: advanced frog side-shift version 1
description: The advanced frog side-shift version 1 is a hip mobility variation of the frog side-shift that incorporates a brief hold at the end range of each lateral shift. The paused position allows the adductor and hip flexor muscles to relax into a deeper stretch.
instructions: Begin in a frog position on all fours with your knees wide and toes pointed outward.
Gently shift your hips toward one side, sinking closer to the floor on that side.
Hold the shifted position briefly before returning to center.
Shift to the opposite side and hold.
Continue alternating for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/mobility/advanced/0498 - advanced frog side-shift version 1.mp4

name: advanced frog side-shift version 3
description: The advanced frog side-shift version 3 incorporates a continuous flowing transition from side to side in the frog position. The smooth arc of motion and progressive amplitude challenge the adductor mobility and hip joint control at a more dynamic level than the held variations.
instructions: Begin in the frog position on all fours with knees wide and feet externally rotated.
Shift your hips in a controlled arc to the far left, hovering close to the floor.
Transition smoothly from left to right in a continuous rocking motion.
Increase the amplitude of the shift over successive repetitions.
Perform for the desired number of passes in each direction.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/mobility/advanced/0499 - advanced frog side-shift version 3.mp4

name: couch forward lunges
description: The couch forward lunge is a bodyweight split squat variation that elevates the rear foot on a couch or bench. The elevated rear foot increases the stretch on the hip flexors and increases the range of motion available to the front leg, intensifying the quad and glute activation.
instructions: Stand facing away from a couch or elevated surface.
Place one foot behind you on the couch, with the top of your foot resting on the surface.
Step the front foot forward far enough to allow a lunge with good form.
Bend your front knee and lower your hips toward the floor in a lunge.
Return to standing and repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0500 - couch forward lunges.mp4

name: couch hip stretch
description: The couch hip stretch is a bodyweight hip flexor stretch that uses a couch or bench to elevate the rear foot and deepen the stretch at the front of the hip. This is a popular mobility exercise for loosening tight hip flexors from prolonged sitting.
instructions: Stand facing away from a couch and place one foot behind you on the seat.
Step your front foot forward to create space for a deep stretch.
Lower your hips toward the floor until you feel a stretch through the front of the hip of the back leg.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0501 - couch hip stretch.mp4

name: couch hip stretch hold
description: The couch hip stretch hold is a sustained version of the couch hip stretch that holds the stretched position for an extended duration. The prolonged hold allows the hip flexor and quad muscles to progressively relax and lengthen, making it effective for improving hip mobility.
instructions: Stand facing away from a couch and place one foot behind you on the seat.
Step forward with your front foot and lower your hips into the stretch position.
Tuck your pelvis slightly under and hold the deepest comfortable stretch position.
Sustain the hold for 30–60 seconds without allowing your hips to rotate outward.
Release and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0502 - couch hip stretch hold.mp4

name: fire hydrants
description: The fire hydrant is a bodyweight glute exercise performed on all fours that targets the glute medius through a lateral leg raise. The movement improves hip abductor strength, hip joint mobility, and pelvic stability, and is commonly used in warm-up and rehabilitation routines.
instructions: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keeping your knee bent at 90 degrees, lift one leg out to the side as high as comfortable.
Pause at the top and squeeze the glute of the raised leg.
Lower the leg back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching to the other leg.

name: fire hydrate 1
description: Fire hydrate 1 is a bodyweight glute and hip abductor exercise that combines the fire hydrant with a leg extension at the top. This addition engages the glutes and hamstrings through a greater range of motion, increasing the difficulty and muscle activation compared to the standard fire hydrant.
instructions: Begin on all fours with a neutral spine and core engaged.
Lift one bent leg out to the side in the fire hydrant position.
From the raised position, extend the leg straight outward.
Return the leg to the bent raised position, then lower back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.

name: frog glute bridge
description: The frog glute bridge is a bodyweight glute exercise that performs the glute bridge with the feet together and knees open in a frog position. This foot placement increases adductor engagement alongside the primary glute activation, making it a comprehensive inner thigh and glute exercise.
instructions: Lie on your back and bring the soles of your feet together, allowing your knees to fall open to the sides in a frog position.
Press your feet together and drive your hips upward into a glute bridge.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of the movement.
Lower your hips back to the floor in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0505 - frog glute bridge.mp4

name: frog glute leg lift
description: The frog glute leg lift is a prone bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes in the frog leg position. Lifting both bent knees off the floor while lying face down activates the glute muscles and posterior hip rotators, improving hip extension strength and glute engagement.
instructions: Lie face down with your forehead resting on your hands.
Bring the soles of your feet together and allow your knees to fall outward in a frog position.
Engage your glutes and lift both knees off the floor simultaneously.
Hold the lifted position briefly, squeezing the glutes.
Lower your knees back to the floor and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0506 - frog glute leg lift.mp4

name: frog rock to knee lift
description: The frog rock to knee lift is a dynamic hip mobility and stability exercise. It combines the rocking motion of the frog stretch with an alternating knee lift, challenging hip flexor strength, adductor flexibility, and balance in the frog position.
instructions: Begin in a frog position on all fours with your knees wide and feet turned out.
Gently rock your hips backward toward your heels.
Rock forward and at the end of the forward rock, lift one knee off the floor.
Return the knee to the floor and rock back again.
Continue alternating knee lifts for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/intermediate/0507 - frog rock to knee lift.mp4

name: frog stretch
description: The frog stretch is a foundational hip mobility exercise that opens the hips and stretches the adductors by spreading the knees wide while in a kneeling position. It is widely used in warm-up and cool-down routines to improve groin and inner thigh flexibility.
instructions: Begin on all fours and slowly walk your knees wide apart until you feel a stretch in the inner thighs.
Lower your forearms to the floor for a deeper stretch.
Keep your feet in line with your knees and your lower back neutral.
Hold the position for 30–60 seconds, breathing steadily.
Walk your knees back together to exit the stretch.

name: glute bridge
description: The glute bridge is a foundational bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings through hip extension. By lifting the hips from a supine position, it builds posterior chain strength and improves hip mobility, and is commonly used in both training and rehabilitation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms at your sides with palms facing down.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips off the floor.
Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.

name: glute bridge hand on chest
description: The glute bridge hand on chest is a variation of the glute bridge where the arms are crossed over the chest, removing hand support from the floor. This position slightly reduces stability, increasing the demand on the glutes and core to maintain proper alignment.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Cross your arms over your chest rather than placing them on the floor.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze the glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back down and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0510 - glute bridge hand on chest.mp4

name: glute bridge hold
description: The glute bridge hold is an isometric variation of the glute bridge that holds the top position for an extended duration. The sustained contraction builds glute endurance, posterior chain stability, and improves hip extension strength through time under tension.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Drive your hips upward into a glute bridge position.
Hold the elevated position, squeezing your glutes firmly.
Maintain the bridge for the desired duration without allowing your hips to drop.
Lower slowly to the floor and rest before repeating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0511 - glute bridge hold.mp4

name: glute bridge vertical
description: The glute bridge vertical is a bodyweight hip exercise performed with the legs extended straight upward. The vertical leg position increases the challenge to the glutes and hamstrings compared to the standard glute bridge, and requires greater core stability to maintain alignment.
instructions: Lie on your back and raise your legs straight up toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the floor.
Place your arms at your sides with palms pressing into the floor.
Engage your glutes and press your hips upward, lifting them off the floor.
Hold at the top for a moment, then lower your hips back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0512 - glute bridge vertical.mp4

name: glute bridge wide leg
description: The glute bridge wide leg is a variation of the glute bridge with a wider foot stance, which shifts some emphasis to the adductors and outer glutes. The wider position engages the inner thigh muscles more directly while still primarily targeting the glutes.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet spread wider than shoulder-width apart.
Point your toes slightly outward.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0513 - glute bridge wide leg.mp4

name: glute leg lift stretch
description: The glute leg lift stretch is a bodyweight exercise performed on all fours that combines a posterior leg extension with a lift to target the glutes. The movement stretches the hip flexor of the grounded leg while activating the glute of the working leg.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Extend one leg straight behind you, keeping it in line with your torso.
Lift the extended leg upward, squeezing the glute at the top.
Hold briefly, then lower the leg back to hip level.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0514 - glute leg lift stretch.mp4

name: glute rotate sit on knee
description: The glute rotate sit on knee is a seated hip mobility drill that rotates the hip joint in a circular motion from a split-leg position. It targets the glutes and hip rotators, improving deep hip flexibility and range of motion.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one knee bent in front of you and the other leg extended behind.
Place your hands on the floor for support.
Slowly rotate your hips in a circular motion, shifting your weight gently.
Continue rotating for the desired number of circles in each direction.
Repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/mobility/beginner/0515 - glute rotate sit on knee.mp4

name: glute and hips open stretch
description: The glute and hips open stretch is a seated bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and adductors through a forward-leaning butterfly position. It opens the hip joint and improves inner thigh and glute flexibility, commonly used in warm-up or cooldown routines.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together in a butterfly position.
Lean forward from the hips, lowering your chest toward your feet.
Place your hands on your feet or the floor to assist the stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the glutes and inner thighs.
Return to upright and repeat as desired.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/0516 - glute and hips open stretch.mp4

name: hip thrust bench
description: The hip thrust bench is a bodyweight glute exercise that uses a bench to elevate the upper body, allowing a greater range of hip extension than a floor glute bridge. It is one of the most effective movements for targeting the glutes and building posterior chain strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a bench, knees bent and feet flat.
Drive through your heels and press your hips upward toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Hold briefly at the peak, then lower your hips back toward the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0517 - hip thrust bench.mp4

name: hip thrust with hand on table
description: The hip thrust with hand on table is a bodyweight glute exercise that uses a table or elevated surface to support the upper back during the hip thrust. This accessible variation develops glute strength through hip extension with a greater range of motion than a floor bridge.
instructions: Sit on the floor and lean your upper back against the edge of a stable table.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor.
Drive your hips upward, pressing through your heels until your torso is parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back down and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0518 - hip thrust with hand on table.mp4

name: hips thrust on bench with bottle
description: The hips thrust on bench with bottle is a modified hip thrust using a water bottle as light resistance across the hips. It targets the glutes while introducing a minimal load, bridging the gap between the bodyweight hip thrust and weighted variations.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and a water bottle placed across your hips.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor.
Drive through your heels to thrust your hips upward, pressing the bottle up.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back toward the floor and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0519 - hips thrust on bench with bottle.mp4

name: incline elbow plank with leg lift
description: The incline elbow plank with leg lift is a bodyweight exercise combining an elevated forearm plank with alternating rear leg raises. The leg lift targets the glutes while the plank position engages the core, shoulders, and lower back for comprehensive posterior chain activation.
instructions: Place your forearms on an elevated surface such as a bench and extend your legs behind you into an incline plank position.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line.
Lift one leg off the ground, raising it until it is in line with your torso or slightly above.
Hold briefly, then lower the leg back to the start.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0520 - incline elbow plank with leg lift.mp4

name: pigeon pose hips stretch version
description: The pigeon pose hips stretch version is a yoga-inspired bodyweight exercise that delivers a deep stretch to the glutes and external hip rotators. By crossing one leg forward and sinking the hips toward the floor, the piriformis and glute complex are targeted for improved hip flexibility.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position and bring one knee forward, placing it behind your wrist.
Allow the shin to rest at an angle across the mat and extend the opposite leg straight back.
Lower your hips toward the floor and sink into the stretch.
Hold for 30–60 seconds, feeling the stretch deep in the glute of the front leg.
Return to plank and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/intermediate/0521 - pigeon pose hips stretch version.mp4

name: reverse bridge hold with one leg lift
description: The reverse bridge hold with one leg lift is a bodyweight exercise that holds a reverse bridge position while raising one leg to increase the glute and core challenge. The single-leg variation demands greater hip stability and glute strength to maintain level hips.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your hands behind your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Press through your hands and feet to lift your hips into a reverse tabletop position.
Hold the bridge, then lift one leg straight up toward the ceiling.
Keep your hips level throughout the single-leg hold.
Lower the leg, then switch sides and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0522 - reverse bridge hold with one leg lift.mp4

name: reverse glute bridge
description: The reverse glute bridge is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes from a seated position using hand support behind the body. It develops glute and hamstring strength through hip extension, making it accessible for beginners or those working on foundational posterior chain strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your hands placed behind your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor.
Press through your hands and feet to lift your hips off the ground.
Squeeze your glutes at the top, forming a tabletop shape with your body.
Lower your hips back to the floor and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0523 - reverse glute bridge.mp4

name: seated in-and-outs hand support hold
description: The seated in-and-outs hand support hold is a bodyweight core exercise performed from a seated position with hand support behind the hips. The alternate drawing in and extending of the legs targets the abs and hip flexors through a dynamic range of motion.
instructions: Sit on the floor and place your hands behind your hips for support.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor with knees bent toward your chest.
Extend your legs straight out in front of you, then draw them back in.
Continue the in-and-out motion for the desired repetitions.
Keep your core engaged and maintain control throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0524 - seated in-and-outs hand support hold.mp4

name: standing inner thigh raises
description: Standing inner thigh raises is a bodyweight exercise targeting the adductors through a standing leg raise across the body. The movement improves inner thigh strength and hip adduction mobility while engaging the core and glutes for stability.
instructions: Stand upright and hold onto a wall or chair for balance.
Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the opposite leg slightly off the ground.
With the raised leg, swing it inward across your body as high as comfortable.
Return the leg to the starting position and repeat.
Complete the desired reps on one side before switching.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0525 - standing inner thigh raises.mp4

name: seated on hand support lift her hip
description: The seated on hand support hip lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes from a seated position. By lifting the hips off the floor while supported on one hand and both feet, the exercise develops glute and core strength with a lateral emphasis.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands placed beside your hips for support.
Lean onto one hand and press through your support hand and feet.
Lift your hips off the floor by engaging your glutes.
Hold the elevated position briefly, then lower back down.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0526 - seated on hand support lift her hip.mp4

name: seated one leg bent glute stretch
description: The seated one leg bent glute stretch is a bodyweight glute flexibility exercise performed while seated. By crossing one leg over the other and rotating slightly, a targeted stretch is achieved in the piriformis and glute complex, commonly used to relieve hip tightness.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one leg extended straight and the other bent, crossing the foot over the extended knee.
Sit tall and gently press the bent knee downward with your elbow.
Rotate slightly toward the bent leg, increasing the stretch in the glute.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then switch sides.
Repeat for the desired duration on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/0527 - seated one leg bent glute stretch.mp4

name: seated spinal
description: The seated spinal is a bodyweight posture exercise targeting the lower back and core stabilizers. By sitting upright with legs extended and focusing on spinal elongation, it promotes lumbar and thoracic alignment while gently engaging the core and back extensors.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
Place both hands behind you on the floor for light support.
Sit tall and elongate your spine by lifting through the crown of your head.
Hold the upright position for the desired duration.
Breathe steadily and focus on maintaining a long, neutral spine.

name: seated spine extension hip lift
description: The seated spine extension hip lift is a bodyweight exercise combining hip lifting with spinal extension from a seated position. It targets the glutes and lower back simultaneously, improving hip extension strength and thoracic mobility through the combined movement.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and hands placed behind your hips.
Press through your hands and heels to lift your hips off the floor.
As you lift, extend and arch your spine gently upward.
Hold the extended position briefly, squeezing your glutes.
Lower back down and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0529 - seated spine extension hip lift.mp4

name: seated spine extension hip lift beginner
description: The seated spine extension hip lift beginner is a modified version of the spine extension hip lift designed for those new to the movement. The knees are bent to reduce difficulty while the combination of hip lift and spinal extension still builds foundational glute and back strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat, hands placed behind your hips.
Press through your hands and feet to lift your hips slightly off the floor.
Gently extend your spine and lift your chest toward the ceiling.
Hold briefly, then lower back down.
Repeat for the desired repetitions at a slow, controlled pace.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0530 - seated spine extension hip lift beginner.mp4

name: single-leg glute bridge
description: The single-leg glute bridge is a unilateral bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes by performing a hip bridge on one leg. Removing the support of one leg significantly increases the demand on the working glute and requires greater core and hip stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Extend one leg straight out at knee height.
Drive through the heel of the grounded foot and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze the glute of the working leg at the top of the movement.
Lower your hips back down and repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0531 - single-leg glute bridge.mp4

name: single-leg hip thrust – table
description: The single-leg hip thrust using a table is a unilateral bodyweight glute exercise performed with one leg extended. The table provides upper back support while the single-leg position maximizes glute isolation and increases the strength and stability demands compared to bilateral variations.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against the side of a table.
Bend one knee with the foot flat on the floor and extend the opposite leg straight.
Drive through the planted foot and lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Keep the extended leg parallel to the floor throughout the movement.
Lower back down and repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0532 - single-leg hip thrust – table.mp4

name: sit on knee hip rotate
description: The sit on knee hip rotate is a kneeling bodyweight hip mobility exercise involving circular hip rotation while seated on the heels. It targets the glute and hip rotator muscles, improving joint mobility and releasing tension accumulated in the deep hip muscles.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and sit back on your heels.
Shift your weight to one side, leaning toward one knee.
Slowly rotate your hips in a circular motion.
Perform circles in one direction, then reverse.
Switch sides and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/mobility/beginner/0533 - sit on knee hip rotate.mp4

name: standing figure-4 glute stretch
description: The standing figure-4 glute stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and external hip rotators in a standing position. The figure-4 leg position combined with a partial squat delivers a deep stretch to the piriformis and glute complex without requiring floor work.
instructions: Stand upright and hold onto a wall or stable surface for balance.
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee, forming a figure-4 shape.
Slowly bend the standing leg and lower your hips as if sitting back into a chair.
Hold the lowered position for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the crossed leg's glute.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/0534 - standing figure-4 glute stretch.mp4

name: standing it band stretch – towel
description: The standing IT band stretch using a towel is a bodyweight lateral stretch targeting the iliotibial band and outer hip muscles. Crossing one foot behind the other and leaning with the towel overhead provides a controlled elongation of the IT band and surrounding hip structures.
instructions: Stand upright and hold a towel in both hands stretched above your head.
Cross one foot behind the other and lean your torso to the side of the rear leg.
Reach the towel overhead toward the same side to deepen the lateral stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch along the outer hip and thigh.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0535 - standing it band stretch – towel.mp4

name: aeroplan leg backward stretch
description: The aeroplan leg backward stretch is a bodyweight balance and flexibility exercise that combines a single-leg balance with a hamstring stretch. Hinging forward on one leg while extending the other back creates a deep stretch through the hamstrings of the planted leg.
instructions: Stand on one foot with your core engaged and your arms extended out to the sides.
Hinge forward at the hips, extending the free leg straight behind you.
Allow your torso to lower as your rear leg rises to maintain a horizontal line.
Hold the position for the desired duration, feeling the stretch in the hamstrings of the standing leg.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/intermediate/0536 - aeroplan leg backward stretch.mp4

name: bird dog leg lift
description: The bird dog leg lift is a foundational bodyweight core and back exercise performed on all fours. It targets the lower back and glutes through simultaneous opposite limb extension, improving lumbar stability, coordination, and anti-rotation core strength.
instructions: Begin on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Engage your core and extend one arm straight forward while simultaneously extending the opposite leg straight back.
Hold the position briefly, keeping your hips level and your spine neutral.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite arm and leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/strength/beginner/0537 - bird dog leg lift.mp4

name: bodyweight squats with bottle
description: The bodyweight squat with bottle is a beginner-friendly squat variation that uses a water bottle as a counterbalance held at chest height. The bottle helps maintain an upright torso position, improving squat mechanics and providing a light front load for the upper body.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a water bottle at chest height with both hands.
Engage your core and push your hips back as you bend your knees to lower into a squat.
Keep your chest up and the bottle steady throughout the descent.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0538 - bodyweight squats with bottle.mp4

name: bodyweight squats with water bottle hold (both hands)
description: The bodyweight squat with water bottle held in both hands is a squat variation that uses the forward arm extension with a bottle to counterbalance and cue thoracic extension. Holding the bottle at shoulder height challenges shoulder endurance and reinforces an upright squat posture.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a water bottle with both hands extended in front of you at shoulder height.
Keep your core engaged and push your hips back, bending your knees into a squat.
Maintain the arms extended forward throughout the movement.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to stand back up and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0539 - bodyweight squats with water bottle hold (both hands).mp4

name: bodyweight squatting with towel
description: The bodyweight squat with a towel uses a towel held at chest height to promote an upright torso and provide proprioceptive feedback during the squat. This variation is useful for beginners developing squat form and thoracic awareness.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel in both hands in front of you.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat.
Keep the towel taut at chest height as a posture cue.
Lower until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0540 - bodyweight squatting with towel.mp4

name: bodyweight squatting with towel version ii
description: Bodyweight squatting with towel version II holds the towel overhead throughout the squat, increasing the challenge to shoulder mobility and thoracic extension. The overhead position forces a more upright torso and helps expose limitations in shoulder or ankle mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel overhead with both hands, arms fully extended.
Engage your core and push your hips back while bending your knees into a squat.
Keep the towel extended overhead throughout the descent.
Lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your heels to stand back up and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0541 - bodyweight squatting with towel version ii.mp4

name: bulgarian half split squat
description: The Bulgarian half split squat is a modified version of the Bulgarian split squat with a reduced range of motion. The partial depth reduces joint stress while still challenging the quads, glutes, and hip flexors unilaterally, making it a good progression for those building toward the full variation.
instructions: Stand facing away from a bench or elevated surface and place one foot behind you on the surface.
Step forward with the front foot and lower your hips into a partial split squat.
Lower only halfway, stopping before the back knee reaches the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete the desired reps on one side before switching.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0542 - bulgarian half split squat.mp4

name: bulgarian split squat
description: The Bulgarian split squat is a unilateral bodyweight lower body exercise with the rear foot elevated. It places a high demand on the quads, glutes, and hip flexors of the front leg while also stretching the hip flexor of the elevated rear leg, making it one of the most effective single-leg exercises.
instructions: Stand facing away from a bench and place one foot behind you on the surface.
Step the front foot forward enough to allow a full lunge range of motion.
Bend both knees to lower your body until the back knee nearly touches the floor.
Keep your front knee aligned with your foot and your torso upright.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0543 - bulgarian split squat.mp4

name: clamshell leg bent
description: The clamshell leg bent is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glute medius through external hip rotation in a side-lying position. It is a foundational hip stability exercise commonly used in rehabilitation, warm-ups, and corrective training to address hip abductor weakness.
instructions: Lie on your side with your hips and knees bent at 45 degrees, feet stacked.
Keep your feet together and lift the top knee upward like a clamshell opening.
Raise the knee as high as possible without rotating the hips backward.
Lower the knee back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0544 - clamshell leg bent.mp4

name: clamshell leg raise
description: The clamshell leg raise is a variation of the clamshell performed with a straight leg rather than a bent knee. The extended leg increases the lever arm, placing greater demand on the glute medius and hip abductors, making it more challenging than the bent-knee variation.
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs stacked and extended straight.
Open your top leg upward in a wide arc, keeping the leg straight.
Raise the leg as high as comfortable without rolling the hip backward.
Lower the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0545 - clamshell leg raise.mp4

name: cossack squat
description: The Cossack squat is a deep bodyweight lower body exercise that stretches the adductors and strengthens the quads, glutes, and hamstrings unilaterally. Shifting to each side alternately trains both legs through a wide range of lateral hip and ankle mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet significantly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Shift your weight to one side and bend that knee, lowering your hips toward the floor.
Keep the opposite leg straight with the toes pointing up.
Go as low as your flexibility allows while maintaining a flat back.
Push back up to standing and repeat on the other side.

name: cossack squat slow
description: The Cossack squat slow is a tempo variation of the Cossack squat emphasizing a controlled descent and ascent. The reduced speed increases time under tension for the adductors and quads, improving mobility and strength simultaneously through a greater range of motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width and toes pointed slightly outward.
Slowly shift your weight to one side, bending the knee and lowering your hips at a 3–4 second count.
Pause at the bottom of the movement for a beat.
Slowly push back to center at a deliberate pace.
Repeat on the other side and continue alternating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/advanced/0547 - cossack squat slow.mp4

name: dead fly bodyweight squat
description: The dead fly bodyweight squat combines a standard squat with an arm fly movement. As the hips lower, the arms cross forward like a reverse chest fly, and they open back outward on the ascent. This adds a dynamic shoulder and chest element to a lower body squat exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended out to the sides.
Lower into a squat position while simultaneously bringing your arms forward to cross in front of you.
At the bottom of the squat, your arms should be crossed at chest height.
Drive through your heels to stand back up and open your arms out to the sides again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0548 - dead fly bodyweight squat.mp4

name: donkey kick
description: The donkey kick is a bodyweight glute exercise performed on all fours, targeting the glutes through hip extension. Pressing the bent leg upward against gravity activates the glute maximally at the top position, making it effective for isolated glute strengthening.
instructions: Begin on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Keep one knee bent at 90 degrees and press that foot toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement.
Lower the knee back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.

name: donkey kick 1
description: Donkey kick 1 is a glute variation of the donkey kick that adds a pulsing motion at the top of each repetition. The pulses increase time under tension at peak glute contraction, enhancing muscle activation and building greater endurance in the glute muscles.
instructions: Start on all fours with a neutral spine and core engaged.
Lift one bent leg upward as in a standard donkey kick, pressing the foot toward the ceiling.
At the peak, pause and pulse the leg two to three times before lowering.
Return the knee to the starting position and repeat.
Complete all reps on one side before switching legs.

name: foot arch squeeze
description: The foot arch squeeze is a rehabilitation and mobility exercise targeting the intrinsic muscles of the foot and the calf. Squeezing the arch activates the plantar foot muscles and calves, helping to strengthen the foot arch and prevent or address flat foot-related issues.
instructions: Sit on the floor or a chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Place a small rolled towel or ball under the arch of one foot.
Squeeze your foot downward to grip the object with your arch.
Hold the contraction for 3–5 seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching feet.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/calves/rehabilitation/beginner/0551 - foot arch squeeze.mp4

name: foot arch stretch
description: The foot arch stretch is a gentle bodyweight stretch targeting the plantar fascia, calves, and ankle stabilizers. Pulling the toes back toward the shin elongates the arch of the foot and the calf muscles, helping to relieve foot tension and improve ankle flexibility.
instructions: Stand or sit with one foot crossed over the opposite knee.
Gently pull your toes back toward your shin with your hand.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds, feeling it along the bottom of your foot and calf.
Release and repeat on the other foot.
Perform for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/calves/stretching/beginner/0552 - foot arch stretch.mp4

name: forward bent with one leg
description: The forward bent with one leg is a bodyweight single-leg hamstring stretch that hinges the torso forward while standing on one foot. It targets the hamstrings of the standing leg while also challenging balance and hip hinge mechanics.
instructions: Stand on one leg and shift your weight fully onto it.
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso toward the ground.
Reach your hands toward the floor while the free leg extends behind you for balance.
Hold the stretched position for a moment.
Return to standing and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/intermediate/0553 - forward bent with one leg.mp4

name: forward lunges 1
description: Forward lunges 1 is a foundational bodyweight lower body exercise that targets the quads and glutes through an alternating forward step lunge pattern. It develops leg strength, balance, and hip extension, making it a staple of lower body conditioning routines.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot forward into a wide lunge stance.
Lower your hips until both knees are at approximately 90 degrees.
Push off the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite leg and continue alternating for the desired reps.

name: forward lunges slightly hold
description: The forward lunge slightly hold is a lunge variation that incorporates a brief pause at the bottom of each lunge. The hold increases time under tension for the quads and glutes, improving strength and endurance compared to continuous alternating lunges.
instructions: Stand upright and step one foot forward into a lunge.
Lower your hips until both knees are bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Hold the bottom lunge position for 2–3 seconds.
Push off the front foot to return to standing.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0555 - forward lunges slightly hold.mp4

name: forward lunges stretch
description: The forward lunge stretch is a bodyweight hip flexor and quad stretch performed in a deep lunge position. Pressing the hips forward and allowing the back knee to drop elongates the hip flexor and quad of the rear leg, making it an effective mobility drill.
instructions: Stand upright and step one foot forward into a wide lunge.
Lower your hips toward the floor and allow the back knee to lower close to the ground.
Press the front hip forward slightly to deepen the stretch in the back hip flexor.
Hold the position for 20–30 seconds.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0556 - forward lunges stretch.mp4

name: forward lunges up & down
description: The forward lunge up and down is a lunge variation that adds a pulsing motion at the bottom of the lunge. The pulse increases the duration of quad and glute activation per repetition, building greater muscle endurance and creating additional training volume.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot forward and lower into a lunge until both knees are at 90 degrees.
From the bottom, rise halfway up and then lower back down for an additional pulse.
Return to standing and repeat on the opposite leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0557 - forward lunges up & down.mp4

name: frog wide leg squat
description: The frog wide leg squat is a bodyweight sumo-style squat with a wide foot stance and external rotation. The wide position targets the adductors alongside the quads and glutes, while the deep range of motion promotes hip flexibility and groin mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet significantly wider than shoulder-width and your toes turned outward.
Place your hands together in front of your chest or on your hips.
Bend your knees and lower your hips toward the floor in a deep squat.
At the bottom, allow your knees to flare outward in line with your toes.
Drive through your heels to return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0558 - frog wide leg squat.mp4

name: front kick squat
description: The front kick squat combines a bodyweight squat with an alternating front kick at the top of each repetition. The kicking motion engages the hip flexors and quads dynamically at the top while the squat builds lower body strength, making it an effective combined cardio and strength movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat, bending both knees until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive back up to standing and immediately kick one leg forward at hip height.
Lower back into the squat and kick the opposite leg on the next repetition.
Continue alternating kicks for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0559 - front kick squat.mp4

name: half cossack squat with one bent leg
description: The half Cossack squat with one bent leg is a modified version of the Cossack squat with a reduced range of motion at the bent knee. It serves as a progression for those developing adductor flexibility and lateral squat strength before attempting the full Cossack variation.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart.
Shift your weight to one side, bending that knee partially rather than going into a full squat.
Keep the opposite leg straight and the foot flat on the floor.
Hold the partial squat position briefly, then shift to the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0560 - half cossack squat with one bent leg.mp4

name: hand behind interlock high knee cardio
description: The hand behind interlock high knee cardio uses the interlaced hands behind the head to remove arm drive from the high knee drill, increasing core and hip flexor demand. The restricted arm position makes the lower body work harder to maintain the pace.
instructions: Stand upright and interlace your fingers behind your head with elbows wide.
Begin driving your knees up alternately toward your chest in a high knee pattern.
Maintain the interlocked hand position throughout without allowing the elbows to close in.
Keep a brisk pace to elevate the heart rate.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/intermediate/0561 - hand behind interlock high knee cardio.mp4

name: hips squats
description: The hips squat is a bodyweight squat variation emphasizing hip hinge mechanics and posterior engagement. By focusing on pushing the hips back and wide during the descent, the glutes and hamstrings are more directly targeted compared to a standard quad-dominant squat.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes turned slightly outward.
Push your hips back and wide as you bend your knees to lower into a squat.
Focus on hinging at the hips rather than simply bending the knees.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to standing and repeat.

name: inner thigh push-out
description: The inner thigh push-out is a standing bodyweight exercise that uses isometric-style resistance to activate the hip abductors and adductors. By pressing the knee outward and returning, the exercise trains the lateral hip stabilizers and adductors through a functional standing movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Push one knee outward against an imaginary resistance, squeezing the glute at the same time.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0563 - inner thigh push-out.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out - kneeling hip abduction
description: The inner thigh push-out kneeling hip abduction is performed from a kneeling position, lifting one knee out to the side. This targets the hip abductors and glute medius while challenging core stability and balance in the kneeling stance.
instructions: Kneel on both knees with your torso upright and core engaged.
Shift your weight to one side and lift the opposite knee off the floor, extending the leg out to the side.
Hold the raised position briefly, then return the knee to the floor.
Repeat on the same side for the desired reps before switching.
Maintain balance through your core throughout.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0564 - inner thigh push-out - kneeling hip abduction.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out - supine hold
description: The inner thigh push-out supine hold is a lying isometric adductor exercise that activates the inner thighs from a back-lying position with knees bent and spread. Pressing the knees together against resistance targets the adductors with minimal joint loading.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Allow your knees to fall outward toward the floor.
From the open position, press your knees inward against imaginary resistance.
Hold the contracted position for 3–5 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0565 - inner thigh push-out - supine hold.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out leg folded
description: The inner thigh push-out leg folded is a seated adductor exercise performed with one leg folded in front of the body. Pressing the knee downward activates the hip adductors and external rotators, targeting the inner thigh in a supported seated position.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one leg folded in front of you and the other extended to the side.
Press the folded knee toward the floor against gentle hand resistance.
Hold the pressed position for 2–3 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
Keep your spine tall throughout the exercise.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0566 - inner thigh push-out leg folded.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out – kneeling hip abduction
description: The inner thigh push-out kneeling hip abduction is a glute and hip abductor exercise performed from a kneeling position. Lifting the knee outward targets the glute medius and hip abductors while the kneeling position engages the core for stability.
instructions: Begin in a kneeling position with your hips stacked over your knees.
Abduct one knee outward, lifting it away from the midline of your body.
Pause at the top, feeling the activation through the outer hip and glute.
Return the knee to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0567 - inner thigh push-out – kneeling hip abduction.mp4

name: inner thigh push-out – supine
description: The inner thigh push-out supine is a lying adductor exercise that opens the legs wide and then actively presses them together. The closing movement targets the adductors concentrically, building inner thigh strength through a full range of motion from the supine position.
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended and feet together.
Open your legs outward to a comfortable wide position.
Engage your inner thighs and press your legs back together.
Hold the legs pressed together for a moment before releasing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0568 - inner thigh push-out – supine.mp4

name: internal half way
description: The internal halfway is a bodyweight lower body exercise targeting the adductors through an internally rotated squat stance. By rotating the foot inward and squatting to midpoint, the inner thigh and hip stabilizers are activated through a partial range of motion.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Rotate one foot inward so the toes point toward the midline of your body.
Bend the rotated knee and lower into a partial squat on that side.
Hold the halfway squat position briefly.
Return to standing and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/intermediate/0569 - internal half way.mp4

name: jump box 1
description: Jump box 1 is a plyometric bodyweight exercise involving an explosive jump onto a raised box. It develops lower body power, coordination, and cardiovascular fitness while engaging the quads, glutes, and hamstrings through the jump and landing mechanics.
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy box or elevated platform.
Bend your knees and swing your arms to prepare for the jump.
Explosively jump upward and forward, landing softly on the box with both feet.
Stand fully upright on the box, then step down one foot at a time.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jump box beginners
description: Jump box beginners is a low-impact box step-up variation for those new to box training. By stepping rather than jumping, it reduces plyometric impact while still developing coordination, leg strength, and the movement pattern required for full box jumps.
instructions: Stand in front of a low box or step.
Bend your knees slightly and step one foot onto the box.
Press through the elevated foot to step the other foot up.
Step back down one foot at a time.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating the leading foot.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0571 - jump box beginners.mp4

name: jump rope
description: Jump rope is a high-intensity cardio exercise that elevates the heart rate rapidly while engaging the calves, shoulders, and core. Whether performed with an actual rope or simulated, it improves cardiovascular endurance, coordination, and foot speed.
instructions: Stand upright and hold the handles of an imaginary or real jump rope at your sides.
Begin jumping both feet off the ground in small, quick hops.
Rotate your wrists in small circles as if turning the rope.
Keep your elbows close to your body and your jumps low and light.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: jump squat
description: The jump squat is a plyometric exercise that combines a bodyweight squat with an explosive upward jump. It develops lower body power, quads, glute strength, and cardiovascular endurance, making it an effective conditioning exercise for both athletic and general fitness goals.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Explosively drive upward from the bottom of the squat, jumping as high as possible.
Land softly on the balls of your feet, immediately absorbing the impact by bending the knees back into a squat.
Repeat immediately for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jump squat advanced
description: The jump squat advanced is a challenging plyometric variation that incorporates a knee tuck at the peak of the jump, increasing air time and explosive demand. It requires greater lower body power and coordination than the standard jump squat.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a full depth squat and explode upward as high as possible.
At the peak of the jump, tuck your knees toward your chest briefly.
Land softly with bent knees and immediately go into the next squat.
Repeat rapidly for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/advanced/0574 - jump squat advanced.mp4

name: jump squat beginners
description: The jump squat beginners is a low-intensity introductory version of the jump squat using a reduced squat depth and a minimal jump. This allows beginners to learn the mechanics of squatting and landing before progressing to full jump squats.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a partial squat by bending your knees slightly.
Rise onto your toes or make a small hop, extending your hips and knees.
Land gently by bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions at a controlled pace.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/beginner/0575 - jump squat beginners.mp4

name: jump squat version ii
description: Jump squat version II adds a pause at the bottom of the squat before the explosive jump, increasing the difficulty by eliminating the elastic rebound from continuous movement. The paused position demands greater quad and glute strength for the initial drive.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat and pause for a count at the bottom.
Explode upward from the held position and jump as high as possible.
Land softly and control the descent back into the bottom squat position.
Hold again before repeating for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/intermediate/0576 - jump squat version ii.mp4

name: knee tuck squat
description: The knee tuck squat combines a standard bodyweight squat with an alternating knee tuck at the top of each repetition. The knee drive engages the hip flexors and abs at the peak while the squat builds lower body strength, creating a combined leg and core exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat and drive back up to standing.
At the top of the movement, drive one knee upward toward your chest.
Lower the leg and immediately descend into the next squat.
Alternate the knee tuck each repetition for the desired count.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0577 - knee tuck squat.mp4

name: lateral shuffle 1
description: Lateral shuffle 1 is a side-to-side agility drill that elevates the heart rate and trains lateral movement patterns. It targets the cardiovascular system while engaging the glutes and upper legs, commonly used in athletic conditioning and warm-up routines.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance with your knees slightly bent and feet shoulder-width apart.
Take two to three quick shuffle steps to one side, staying low.
Plant the outside foot and immediately shuffle the same number of steps in the opposite direction.
Keep your hips low and your weight on the balls of your feet throughout.
Continue shuffling side to side for the desired duration.

name: lateral shuffle
description: The lateral shuffle is a bodyweight cardio drill performed by rapidly shuffling side to side in a low athletic stance. It develops lateral quickness, cardiovascular fitness, and lower body agility while engaging the glutes and leg muscles throughout the movement.
instructions: Begin in a low athletic stance with your knees bent and feet shoulder-width apart.
Push off your inside foot and shuffle laterally to one side.
Land softly and immediately push back the other direction.
Maintain a quick, continuous shuffling pace throughout.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: leg open stretch
description: The leg open stretch is a seated bodyweight exercise that targets the adductors and hamstrings through a wide-leg forward fold. Spreading the legs wide and folding forward improves inner thigh flexibility and hip mobility, commonly used in cooldown and flexibility routines.
instructions: Sit on the floor and open your legs as wide as comfortable.
Place your hands on the floor between or in front of you.
Gently hinge forward from your hips, lowering your torso toward the floor.
Hold the stretch for 30–60 seconds.
Slowly return to upright and repeat as desired.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0580 - leg open stretch.mp4

name: leg open with hand on chest cross stretch
description: The leg open with hand on chest cross stretch is a seated wide-leg forward fold performed with arms crossed over the chest. The arm position removes shoulder engagement and shifts focus entirely to the forward fold, deepening the adductor and hamstring stretch.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs spread wide apart.
Cross your arms over your chest and hinge forward from the hips.
Lower your torso toward the floor between your legs.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Return to upright and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0581 - leg open with hand on chest cross stretch.mp4

name: lunges knee dive
