description: The lunge knee dive is a forward lunge variation where the front knee is intentionally allowed to travel past the toes during the descent. This increases ankle and knee range of motion, engaging the quads and calf muscles more deeply in a controlled manner.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together.
Step forward with one foot into a lunge position.
As you lower, allow the front knee to travel forward over the toes for a knee dive.
Lower until the back knee nearly touches the floor.
Push back to standing and repeat on the opposite leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0582 - lunges knee dive.mp4

name: pulse sumo squat
description: The pulse sumo squat is a bodyweight inner thigh and quad exercise that holds the bottom of the sumo squat and performs small pulsing contractions. The constant tension at the bottom of the movement builds endurance and strength through the adductors and quads.
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width and your toes pointed outward.
Lower into a deep sumo squat position.
From the bottom, perform small pulsing movements up and down without fully rising.
Continue pulsing for the desired number of reps or duration.
Stand fully to complete the set.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/strength/beginner/0583 - pulse sumo squat.mp4

name: quadriceps stretch
description: The quadriceps stretch is a standing bodyweight exercise targeting the quad muscles through a heel-to-glute pull. It elongates the rectus femoris and hip flexors, making it a standard post-exercise or mobility stretch for the front of the thigh.
instructions: Stand upright and hold onto a wall or stable surface for balance.
Bend one knee and grasp the top of your foot with the same-side hand.
Draw your heel toward your glute, feeling the stretch through the front of the thigh.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, keeping your knees together and your torso upright.
Release and repeat on the other leg.

name: reverse lunges kickback
description: The reverse lunge kickback combines a reverse lunge with a rear leg kickback to create a compound glute and leg exercise. The kickback following the lunge maximizes glute activation at the top of the movement, building strength and muscle tone in the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together.
Step one foot backward into a reverse lunge, lowering the back knee toward the floor.
Push off the back foot to return to standing and continue the motion to kick that leg behind you.
Squeeze the glute at the top of the kickback.
Return to standing and repeat on the same side for the desired reps before switching.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0585 - reverse lunges kickback.mp4

name: slow prisoner marching knee lifts
description: Slow prisoner marching knee lifts is a controlled bodyweight exercise combining the prisoner arm position with deliberate high-knee marching. The slow tempo and sustained hold at peak knee height increases hip flexor engagement and core stability compared to brisk marching variations.
instructions: Stand tall with your hands placed behind your head and elbows wide.
Slowly lift one knee to hip height in a controlled marching motion.
Hold the raised knee for 1–2 seconds before lowering it.
Alternate legs at a deliberate, slow pace.
Continue marching for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0586 - slow prisoner marching knee lifts.mp4

name: seated bike hand on floor
description: The seated bike hand on floor is a bodyweight core exercise that simulates a bicycle motion from a seated floor position with hand support. It targets the hip flexors and abs through the continuous pedaling action, offering an accessible floor-based cardio and core drill.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your hands placed flat on the ground behind you for support.
Lift your feet off the floor and begin pedaling your legs in a smooth cycling motion.
Alternate extending and bending each leg in a continuous bicycle pattern.
Keep your core engaged and your torso from collapsing backward.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0587 - seated bike hand on floor.mp4

name: seated flutter kick 1
description: The seated flutter kick 1 is a bodyweight core and hip flexor exercise performed from a seated position with hand support. Rapidly alternating leg kicks while seated targets the lower abs and hip flexors, serving as an accessible variation of the lying flutter kick.
instructions: Sit on the floor and lean back slightly, placing your hands behind you for support.
Lift both feet off the floor with your legs extended.
Begin rapidly alternating small up and down kicks with each leg.
Keep the kicks controlled and the legs relatively straight.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0588 - seated flutter kick 1.mp4

name: seated leg bent flutter kick
description: The seated leg bent flutter kick is a seated core exercise that performs the flutter kick with a bent-knee starting position. The knee bend reduces the lever arm compared to a straight-leg variation, making it more accessible while still targeting the hip flexors and lower abs.
instructions: Sit on the floor and lean slightly back with hands behind you for support.
Lift your feet and bend your knees to 90 degrees.
Begin alternating kicks by extending one leg out straight while keeping the other bent.
Return and switch legs in a flutter kick pattern.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0589 - seated leg bent flutter kick.mp4

name: seated on knee high dive advanced
description: The seated on knee high dive advanced is a kneeling cardio and strength exercise that drives the knee upward explosively from a kneeling base. The movement targets the hip flexors and quads while challenging balance and coordination, with the advanced version performed at a higher speed.
instructions: Kneel upright on both knees with your core engaged and torso tall.
Drive one knee upward in a high knee motion while rising onto the opposite knee.
Return to the starting kneeling position and immediately repeat on the other side.
Increase the speed and height of the knee drive for the advanced variation.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/0590 - seated on knee high dive advanced.mp4

name: seated on knee lunges
description: Seated on knee lunges are performed from a kneeling starting position, transitioning into a half-kneeling lunge by stepping one foot forward. This variation reduces the balance demand of standing lunges and focuses more deeply on the hip flexor and quad of the front leg.
instructions: Begin in a kneeling position on both knees.
Step one foot forward into a half-kneeling lunge position.
Lower your hips toward the floor until the front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Return the front foot back to the kneeling position.
Repeat on the other side for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0591 - seated on knee lunges.mp4

name: seated straight-leg hold – towel assist
description: The seated straight-leg hold with towel assist is a hamstring flexibility exercise using a towel looped around the feet to maintain an upright torso while sitting with legs extended. The towel allows a comfortable long-hold stretch with controlled tension through the hamstrings.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended straight in front of you.
Loop a towel around the soles of your feet and hold both ends.
Gently pull the towel to draw your toes back toward you and sit tall.
Hold the position for the desired duration, feeling the hamstring stretch.
Release tension and repeat as needed.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0592 - seated straight-leg hold – towel assist.mp4

name: seated on knee high dive
description: The seated on knee high dive is a kneeling bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors and quads through an alternating knee drive. The kneeling base eliminates lower body momentum and isolates the hip drive, making it an effective drill for hip flexor activation.
instructions: Kneel upright on both knees with your torso tall and core engaged.
Drive one knee forward and upward in a controlled high knee motion.
Return the knee to the ground and repeat on the opposite side.
Alternate continuously at a steady pace.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0593 - seated on knee high dive.mp4

name: single leg roman deadlift
description: The single leg Romanian deadlift is a bodyweight unilateral exercise targeting the hamstrings through a hip hinge on one leg. The free leg extending behind provides counterbalance while the working leg hamstrings and glutes control the lowering and lifting phases.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the knee and your core engaged.
Hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso while extending the free leg behind you.
Lower until your torso is approximately parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your hamstring and glute to drive your hips forward and return to standing.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/intermediate/0594 - single leg roman deadlift.mp4

name: split squat hold
description: The split squat hold is an isometric bodyweight exercise that holds the bottom position of a split squat for an extended duration. Sustained tension through the quads, glutes, and hip flexors builds lower body endurance and strength through prolonged time under load.
instructions: Stand in a split stance with one foot forward and the other behind you.
Lower your hips until both knees are at approximately 90 degrees.
Hold the bottom split squat position for the desired duration.
Keep your front knee aligned with your toes and your torso upright.
Rise and switch legs before repeating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0595 - split squat hold.mp4

name: squat + bottle curl
description: The squat plus bottle curl combines a bodyweight squat with a simultaneous bicep curl using water bottles. This compound movement targets the quads and glutes in the squat phase while engaging the biceps during the curl, creating an efficient total body exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a water bottle in each hand at your sides.
Lower into a squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
As you rise from the squat, curl both bottles up toward your shoulders.
Lower the bottles back to your sides as you descend into the next squat.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0596 - squat + bottle curl.mp4

name: squat + bottle shoulder press 1
description: The squat plus bottle shoulder press 1 combines a bodyweight squat with an overhead press using water bottles. The combined movement targets the quads, glutes, and deltoids simultaneously, making it a time-efficient compound exercise for full body strength.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a water bottle in each hand at shoulder height.
Lower into a squat, keeping the bottles at shoulder height.
As you rise from the squat, press both bottles overhead.
Lower the bottles back to shoulder height as you descend into the next squat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0597 - squat + bottle shoulder press 1.mp4

name: squat bottle hold
description: The squat bottle hold is a bodyweight squat performed while holding a water bottle at chest height as a counterweight. The bottle position assists thoracic extension and reinforces an upright torso, making it a useful teaching tool for squat mechanics.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a water bottle at chest height with both hands.
Lower into a squat, keeping the bottle in front of your chest throughout.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to stand back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0598 - squat bottle hold.mp4

name: squat down bottle hold
description: The squat down bottle hold is a squat variation where a water bottle is held and lowered toward the floor at the bottom of each squat. The downward reach increases the forward hip hinge and deepens the posterior chain activation at the bottom of the movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a water bottle with both hands pointing downward.
Lower into a squat and tap the bottle to the floor at the bottom.
Drive through your heels to return to standing.
Keep your chest tall and back neutral throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0599 - squat down bottle hold.mp4

name: squat with high knee
description: The squat with high knee combines a bodyweight squat with an alternating single-leg knee drive at the top. This addition engages the hip flexors and core at the peak while the squat builds quad and glute strength, creating a combined lower body and core movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat and drive back up to standing.
At the top of the squat, drive one knee upward toward your chest.
Lower the leg and descend directly into the next squat.
Alternate the knee drive each repetition for the desired count.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0600 - squat with high knee.mp4

name: squat with knee dive
description: The squat with knee dive is a squat variation that deliberately allows the knees to travel forward over the toes to increase ankle dorsiflexion and quad engagement. This technique builds the quads through a greater range of motion and improves lower leg mobility.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat allowing your knees to travel forward over your toes intentionally.
Descend as low as your mobility allows, with emphasis on pushing the knees outward.
Drive through your full foot to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0601 - squat with knee dive.mp4

name: squats ii
description: Squats II is a standard bodyweight squat variation performed with the arms crossed over the chest. The arm position shifts the center of mass and increases the demand on the core and back extensors to maintain an upright posture throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms crossed over your chest.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing calf stretch
description: The standing calf stretch is a bodyweight exercise that targets the gastrocnemius and soleus muscles by pressing the heel into the floor in a staggered stance against a wall. It improves ankle flexibility and calf mobility, essential for running and general lower leg health.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place your hands on the wall at shoulder height.
Step one foot back and press the heel firmly into the floor.
Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch along the back of the lower leg.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then switch legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/calves/stretching/beginner/0603 - standing calf stretch.mp4

name: standing forward half bent
description: The standing forward half bent is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings and lower back through a hip hinge to 90 degrees. Holding the torso parallel to the floor develops the posterior chain and improves the hip hinge mechanics used in deadlifts and other movements.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is parallel to the floor.
Allow your arms to hang down or reach forward.
Hold the position, keeping your spine neutral and knees soft.
Return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0604 - standing forward half bent.mp4

name: standing hamstring calf stretch
description: The standing hamstring calf stretch combines a forward leg extension with a dorsiflexed foot to simultaneously stretch the hamstrings and calf muscles. The combined stretch is efficient for improving posterior chain flexibility.
instructions: Stand and extend one leg forward with the heel on the floor and toes pointing up.
Hinge your hips forward slightly and flex your foot firmly.
Feel the stretch running from the back of the knee up the hamstrings and down into the calf.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then switch legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0605 - standing hamstring calf stretch.mp4

name: standing hamstring towel calf stretch
description: The standing hamstring towel calf stretch uses a towel to increase foot dorsiflexion during a standing hamstring stretch, simultaneously targeting the calf. The towel provides more controlled tension and allows individuals with limited flexibility to maintain the stretch effectively.
instructions: Stand and extend one leg forward with the heel on the floor.
Loop a towel around the ball of the extended foot and hold both ends.
Gently pull the towel to dorsiflex the foot and lean your torso forward slightly.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/lower legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0606 - standing hamstring towel calf stretch.mp4

name: standing hand raise front glute stretch
description: The standing hand raise front glute stretch combines the figure-4 glute stretch position with arms raised overhead. The raised arms shift the center of gravity and help maintain balance while deepening the glute and external hip rotator stretch.
instructions: Stand on one foot and cross the opposite ankle over your standing knee.
Raise both arms straight overhead.
Slowly bend the standing knee to lower your hips, increasing the stretch in the crossed leg's glute.
Hold for 20–30 seconds.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/intermediate/0607 - standing hand raise front glute stretch.mp4

name: standing quad stretch (towel behind foot)
description: The standing quad stretch with a towel behind the foot uses a towel as an extension to bring the foot closer to the glute, allowing individuals with limited flexibility to achieve a deeper quad stretch. It targets the rectus femoris and hip flexors effectively.
instructions: Stand upright and place a towel behind one knee, looping it around your foot.
Hold the ends of the towel and pull your foot toward your glute.
Keep your knees together and maintain an upright posture.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0608 - standing quad stretch (towel behind foot).mp4

name: standing single leg upper body bent hold
description: The standing single leg upper body bent hold is a balance and hamstring exercise performed by holding a hinged forward torso position on one leg. It challenges posterior chain endurance, single-leg balance, and hip hinge stability through an isometric hold.
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend in the supporting knee.
Hinge forward at the hips and lower your torso until it is angled toward the floor.
Extend your arms forward for balance.
Hold the position for the desired duration without allowing your torso to rotate.
Return to standing and repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/balance/intermediate/0609 - standing single leg upper body bent hold.mp4

name: standing single-leg forward fold
description: The standing single-leg forward fold is a bodyweight hamstring stretch performed while balancing on one leg. It develops hamstring flexibility, hip hinge mechanics, and single-leg balance simultaneously, serving both as a stretch and a balance challenge.
instructions: Stand on one foot with your arms at your sides.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach your hands toward the floor.
Keep the standing leg straight or slightly bent.
Lower your torso as far as your flexibility allows.
Hold briefly, then return to standing and switch legs.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/intermediate/0610 - standing single-leg forward fold.mp4

name: standing single-leg forward fold (one leg up)
description: The standing single-leg forward fold with one leg up combines a forward fold with an elevated leg, increasing the hamstring stretch on the raised leg. The position also challenges hip flexor strength and balance in the standing leg.
instructions: Stand on one leg and raise the other leg in front of you.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach your hands toward the raised foot.
Keep the standing leg firm and your core engaged.
Hold briefly at the lowest point.
Return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/advanced/0611 - standing single-leg forward fold (one leg up).mp4

name: standing single-leg forward fold(leg up bent)
description: The standing single-leg forward fold with the opposite leg bent is a modified hamstring stretch that holds the raised knee bent rather than extended. The bent leg position reduces the hamstring stretch demand on the raised leg while still engaging the hip flexors and stretching the standing leg.
instructions: Stand on one leg and bend the opposite knee, holding it up in front of you.
Hinge forward at the hips and reach your hands toward the floor.
Keep the bent knee raised throughout the fold.
Hold at the bottom briefly, then return to standing.
Repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/intermediate/0612 - standing single-leg forward fold(leg up bent).mp4

name: vertical flutter kicks (lying)
description: The vertical flutter kick performed lying down targets the abs and hip flexors by executing rapid flutter kicks with the legs raised vertically. The vertical position changes the resistance angle compared to horizontal flutter kicks, shifting the emphasis to the lower abdominal region.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your hips for support.
Raise both legs straight up toward the ceiling, perpendicular to the floor.
Begin rapidly alternating small kicks, lowering and raising each leg a short distance.
Keep the movement controlled and the legs relatively straight throughout.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: zig zag walk
description: The zig zag walk is a low-impact agility exercise that involves walking or jogging in a zig zag pattern. It improves directional change mechanics, foot coordination, and provides a gentle cardiovascular stimulus, making it suitable for warm-ups or rehabilitation.
instructions: Mark out a zig zag path on the floor or visualize a series of cones.
Begin walking briskly in a zig zag pattern, stepping side to side with each change of direction.
Keep your steps controlled and your core engaged.
Increase your pace to a brisk walk or light jog as your coordination improves.
Continue through the zig zag pattern for the desired number of passes.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0614 - zig zag walk.mp4

name: one leg squats
description: The one leg squat is a demanding unilateral bodyweight exercise that targets the quads, glutes, and hamstrings on a single leg. It requires significant leg strength, core stability, and balance, and is a key exercise for identifying and addressing lower body strength imbalances.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other leg extended slightly in front of you.
Bend the standing knee and lower your hips toward the floor.
Keep your chest upright and your core engaged throughout.
Lower as far as your strength and mobility allow.
Drive through the standing heel to return to the starting position and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/0615 - one leg squats.mp4

name: reverse lunges hands up
description: Reverse lunges hands up is a bodyweight lunge variation performed with both arms raised overhead throughout. The raised arm position increases the challenge to core stability and thoracic extension, while the reverse step targets the quads and glutes of the front leg.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet together and both arms raised straight overhead.
Step one foot backward into a reverse lunge.
Lower your hips until both knees are at approximately 90 degrees.
Keep your arms raised throughout the movement.
Push back to standing and alternate legs for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0616 - reverse lunges hands up.mp4

name: isometric front neck press
description: The isometric front neck press is a bodyweight cervical strengthening exercise using hand resistance against the forehead. By pressing the head forward against the hand's resistance, the anterior neck muscles are activated isometrically, building neck strength and stability.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with a neutral spine.
Place your palm flat against your forehead.
Press your head forward against your hand while your hand resists the movement.
Hold the isometric contraction for 5–10 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/strength/beginner/0617 - isometric front neck press.mp4

name: neck press hands on behind head
description: The neck press with hands behind the head is an isometric neck strengthening exercise targeting the posterior neck muscles. By pressing the head backward against the resisting hands, the neck extensors and upper trapezius are strengthened through an isometric contraction.
instructions: Sit or stand with your spine neutral and core engaged.
Place both hands behind your head, interlacing your fingers.
Press your head backward against your hands while your hands provide resistance.
Hold the isometric contraction for 5–10 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/strength/beginner/0618 - neck press hands on behind head.mp4

name: neck press with hands
description: The neck press with hands is an isometric lateral neck strengthening exercise using hand resistance applied to the side of the head. Pressing the head sideways against resistance activates the lateral neck flexors and upper trapezius, improving cervical lateral strength.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine neutral.
Place one hand on the side of your head above the ear.
Press your head laterally against your hand while your hand resists.
Hold the isometric contraction for 5–10 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/strength/beginner/0619 - neck press with hands.mp4

name: neck press with one hands
description: The neck press with one hand is a unilateral isometric neck exercise using a single hand to provide resistance. Pressing the neck against one hand targets the neck muscles on the corresponding side, allowing focused strengthening of specific cervical muscle groups.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine in a neutral position.
Place one hand against your forehead or temple.
Press your head in the direction of the hand while the hand provides resistance.
Hold the isometric contraction for 5–10 seconds.
Release, switch directions, and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/strength/beginner/0620 - neck press with one hands.mp4

name: seated neck side stretch
description: The seated neck side stretch is a seated mobility exercise targeting the lateral neck muscles and upper trapezius. Tilting the head to each side while seated provides a controlled stretch to the cervical lateral flexors, relieving neck tightness and improving range of motion.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Tilt your head slowly to one side, bringing your ear toward your shoulder.
Use one hand to gently add pressure to the side of your head for a deeper stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch along the opposite side of the neck.
Return to center and repeat on the other side.

name: standing neck and shoulder stretch
description: The standing neck and shoulder stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lateral cervical muscles and upper trapezius. By tilting the head and reaching the same arm downward, the stretch is intensified along the side of the neck and across the shoulder.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Tilt your head to one side, dropping your ear toward your shoulder.
Reach the same-side hand down toward the floor to increase the lateral stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds.
Return to center and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/stretching/beginner/0622 - standing neck and shoulder stretch.mp4

name: standing neck extension (look up) + back stretch
description: The standing neck extension with back stretch combines a cervical extension stretch with a mild lumbar extension and chest opener. The combined movement targets the anterior neck, chest, and lower back, making it an effective full posterior chain counter-stretch.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Place both hands on your lower back.
Gently tilt your head back, looking up toward the ceiling.
Simultaneously lean slightly backward, opening the chest and stretching the lower back.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then return to neutral.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/stretching/beginner/0623 - standing neck extension (look up) + back stretch.mp4

name: standing neck rotate up down
description: The standing neck rotate up down combines cervical rotation with a vertical head nod from the rotated position. This compound mobility exercise targets the neck rotators and extensors, improving multi-directional cervical range of motion.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your shoulders relaxed.
Slowly rotate your head to one side as far as comfortable.
From the rotated position, gently tilt your chin downward and then upward.
Return to center and repeat the rotation to the opposite side.
Continue for the desired repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/neck/neck/mobility/beginner/0624 - standing neck rotate up down.mp4

name: standing neck stretch
description: The standing neck stretch is a simple bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lateral neck flexors and upper trapezius. Performed while standing, it can be used as a quick tension reliever at any point during the day or incorporated into a warm-up or cooldown routine.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Gently tilt your head to one side, ear toward shoulder.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, feeling the stretch along the opposite side of the neck.
Return to center and repeat on the other side.
Perform for the desired number of repetitions.

name: towel neck stretch
description: The towel neck stretch uses a towel as a prop to provide gentle cervical traction and a posterior neck stretch. The controlled forward pull of the towel elongates the posterior neck muscles and can help relieve neck stiffness and suboccipital tension.
instructions: Stand or sit upright and hold both ends of a towel, placing the middle of the towel behind your head at neck level.
Gently pull forward on the towel ends to create a mild traction force on the neck.
Simultaneously allow your chin to tilt slightly downward.
Hold for 20–30 seconds.
Release the tension and repeat as desired.

name: butter fly stretch
description: The butterfly stretch is a seated bodyweight exercise targeting the adductors and inner thighs. Bringing the soles together and gently pressing the knees toward the floor opens the hips and stretches the groin, making it a foundational hip flexibility drill.
instructions: Sit on the floor and bring the soles of your feet together, allowing your knees to fall open.
Hold your feet with both hands.
Gently press your knees toward the floor using your elbows.
Hinge forward slightly from the hips to deepen the stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds and repeat as desired.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0627 - butter fly stretch.mp4

name: dynamic chest stretch
description: The dynamic chest stretch is a bodyweight movement that dynamically opens and stretches the pectoral muscles through arm swings. The momentum-based movement warms up the chest, shoulders, and thoracic spine, making it ideal as a pre-workout mobility drill.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended out to the sides.
Swing your arms forward, crossing them in front of your chest.
Open them back out wide behind you.
Continue the swinging motion fluidly for the desired repetitions.
Gradually increase the range of motion with each swing.

name: external thigh out - seated floor
description: The external thigh out seated floor is a seated bodyweight exercise targeting the adductors and hip external rotators. By rotating one bent knee outward and pressing it toward the floor, the inner thigh and deep hip muscles are gently stretched.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend one knee and rotate it outward, placing the sole of the foot against the inner thigh of the extended leg.
Sit tall and gently press the bent knee toward the floor.
Hold for 20–30 seconds.
Repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0629 - external thigh out - seated floor.mp4

name: external thigh out- seated floor version i
description: External thigh out seated floor version I is a variation of the seated external thigh stretch with both knees bent and one knee allowed to fall outward. The hand pressure deepens the adductor and hip rotator stretch in this seated position.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both knees bent and feet flat.
Allow one knee to fall outward to the floor while keeping the other upright.
Gently press the dropped knee further toward the floor with your hand.
Hold for 20–30 seconds.
Switch sides and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0630 - external thigh out- seated floor version i.mp4

name: l-sit hip lift (toe touch variation)
description: The L-sit hip lift toe touch variation is a challenging bodyweight exercise combining an L-sit hold with an alternating toe touch. It targets the abs and hip flexors through the sustained L-sit while adding a dynamic reaching element that challenges balance and core stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and hands placed beside your hips.
Press through your hands to lift your hips off the floor into an L-sit position.
From the L-sit, reach one hand toward your toes while maintaining the lift.
Return the hand and lower your hips before repeating on the other side.
Continue alternating for the desired repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/advanced/0631 - l-sit hip lift (toe touch variation).mp4

name: l-sit leg on floor stretch
description: The L-sit leg on floor stretch is a seated hamstring and lower back flexibility exercise performed in the classic L-sit position with legs extended flat. Sitting upright with feet flexed and leaning forward gradually deepens the hamstring stretch through the posterior leg chain.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you.
Place your hands beside your hips on the floor for support.
Sit tall and flex your feet, pulling your toes toward you.
Gently hinge forward from the hips to increase the hamstring stretch.
Hold for 30–60 seconds.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0632 - l-sit leg on floor stretch.mp4

name: lying adductor stretch (supine inner thigh stretch)
description: The lying adductor stretch is a supine bodyweight exercise that stretches the inner thigh muscles by allowing the knees to fall outward while lying on the back. The relaxed position allows gravity to provide a gentle, sustained adductor stretch.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Allow one or both knees to fall outward toward the floor.
Rest in the open position and breathe steadily.
Hold for 30–60 seconds.
Slowly bring the knees back together and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0633 - lying adductor stretch (supine inner thigh stretch).mp4

name: lying adductor stretch – feet elevated
description: The lying adductor stretch with feet elevated positions the feet on a wall to provide support while gravity opens the hips and stretches the adductors. The wall support allows for a more relaxed and sustained inner thigh stretch compared to the standard supine variation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back near a wall and rest your feet on the wall with knees slightly bent.
Allow your knees to fall outward to the sides.
Let gravity gently open the hips and stretch the inner thighs.
Hold for 30–60 seconds.
Bring the knees back together and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0634 - lying adductor stretch – feet elevated.mp4

name: lying adductor stretch – feet elevated version 1
description: The lying adductor stretch feet elevated version 1 uses the wall as a surface to slide the feet apart progressively, allowing gravity to deepen the adductor stretch. The sliding motion lets practitioners ease into the stretch at their own pace.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips near a wall and feet resting on the wall.
Slowly slide your feet apart along the wall, letting gravity open the hips.
Allow the inner thigh to elongate as the legs spread.
Hold the widest comfortable position for 30–60 seconds.
Slide your feet back together and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0635 - lying adductor stretch – feet elevated version 1.mp4

name: lying bent leg scissor
description: The lying bent leg scissor is a bodyweight core exercise that targets the abs and adductors by opening and crossing the legs while lying on the back with knees slightly bent. The movement engages the hip adductors on the closing phase and the core throughout.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides.
Lift both legs off the floor with knees slightly bent.
Open your legs apart in a wide scissor position.
Close them back together, crossing one over the other.
Alternate which leg crosses on top with each repetition.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0636 - lying bent leg scissor.mp4

name: lying flutter kick
description: The lying flutter kick is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the lower abs and hip flexors through rapid alternating leg kicks while lying on the back. Keeping the legs low and straight increases the mechanical demand on the lower abdominal region.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your hips for support.
Raise your legs a few inches off the floor.
Begin rapidly alternating small up and down kicks with each leg.
Keep the legs straight and the movement controlled.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: lying flutter kick fast
description: The lying flutter kick fast is a high-intensity version of the lying flutter kick performed at maximum speed. The rapid pace elevates the cardiovascular demand and increases time under tension in the lower abs, making it effective for both core training and metabolic conditioning.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your hips.
Raise your legs just above the floor and begin flutter kicking at maximum speed.
Keep the kicks small and the legs as straight as possible.
Sustain the rapid pace for the desired duration.
Lower your legs to rest before repeating.

name: lying leg bent hold back stretch
description: The lying leg bent hold back stretch is a supine lower back stretch that gently decompresses the lumbar spine by drawing both knees toward the chest. It relieves lower back tension and stretches the glutes, making it a staple of post-exercise recovery routines.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Draw both knees toward your chest and wrap your arms around your shins.
Hold the knees close to the chest and gently rock side to side if comfortable.
Hold the stretched position for 20–30 seconds.
Release and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0639 - lying leg bent hold back stretch.mp4

name: lying leg bent stretch
description: The lying leg bent stretch is a supine hamstring flexibility exercise that draws one bent knee to the chest and then extends it upward for a sustained stretch. The progressive extension allows a controlled deepening of the hamstring stretch from a supported lying position.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both knees bent and feet flat.
Grasp one knee with both hands and draw it toward your chest.
Slowly extend that leg upward while keeping it held with your hands.
Hold the extended position for 20–30 seconds.
Lower the leg and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0640 - lying leg bent stretch.mp4

name: lying leg bent with hand raise straight stretch
description: The lying leg bent with hand raise straight stretch combines an arm raise with a leg extension from a supine position. The paired limb extension creates a diagonal stretch through the core and hip flexors, improving total body coordination and flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both knees bent.
Raise one arm straight up toward the ceiling.
Extend the same-side leg straight out, hovering above the floor.
Hold the extended arm and leg position for 20–30 seconds.
Return and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0641 - lying leg bent with hand raise straight stretch.mp4

name: lying leg raise straight flutter kick
description: The lying leg raise straight flutter kick performs the flutter kick from a high-leg position with legs raised toward vertical. The elevated starting position changes the resistance angle and shifts the work toward the upper abs and hip flexors compared to lower-leg variations.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your hips.
Raise both legs straight up toward the ceiling.
Begin performing small, rapid flutter kicks with the legs in the raised position.
Keep the kicks controlled and the legs straight.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0642 - lying leg raise straight flutter kick.mp4

name: lying leg raise up stretch
description: The lying leg raise up stretch is a supine bodyweight hamstring exercise that uses a leg raise to the ceiling followed by a gentle assisted pull. It targets the hamstrings through a straight-leg stretch and is a standard flexibility exercise for improving posterior leg range of motion.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended.
Raise one leg straight up toward the ceiling.
Grasp the back of the thigh or calf and gently pull the leg toward you.
Hold the stretch for 20–30 seconds, feeling the elongation through the hamstring.
Lower the leg and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0643 - lying leg raise up stretch.mp4

name: lying on floor marching
description: Lying on floor marching is a supine cardio and core exercise that simulates marching from a back-lying position. It targets the hip flexors and abs through alternating knee drives, offering a low-impact cardiovascular and core activation option for those with mobility limitations.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms at your sides.
Begin alternately lifting your knees toward your chest in a marching pattern.
Coordinate your arm swings with the opposite leg movements.
Maintain a steady, controlled pace.
Continue for the desired duration.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/cardio/cardiovascular system/cardio/beginner/0644 - lying on floor marching.mp4

name: lying quads stretch ii
description: Lying quads stretch II is a prone bodyweight stretch targeting the quadriceps and hip flexors. Bending one knee and pulling the heel toward the glute while face down provides a deep stretch through the front of the thigh in a supported, stable position.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with both legs extended.
Bend one knee and bring the heel toward your glute.
Reach back with the same-side hand and grasp the ankle.
Gently pull the ankle toward the glute to increase the quad stretch.
Hold for 20–30 seconds, then repeat on the other leg.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0645 - lying quads stretch ii.mp4

name: lying reverse bike
description: The lying reverse bike is a bodyweight core exercise that performs a cycling motion in the reverse direction while lying on the back. Reversing the standard cycling pattern challenges the abs and hip flexors differently, engaging the muscles through an altered range of motion.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise your legs to a 90-degree tabletop position.
Begin pedaling your legs backward in a reverse cycling motion.
Alternate extending one leg behind while the other bends toward the chest.
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor throughout.
Continue for the desired duration.

name: lying scissor leg
description: The lying scissor leg is a bodyweight core exercise that alternately opens and crosses the legs while lying on the back. The movement targets the lower abs and hip adductors, requiring sustained core engagement to keep the legs elevated throughout the exercise.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands under your hips.
Raise both legs off the floor to about 45 degrees.
Open your legs wide apart, then cross them over each other alternately.
Keep the movements slow and controlled.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying stretch wide leg bent
description: The lying stretch wide leg bent is a supine adductor stretch performed with bent knees falling open to the sides. The bent-knee version is more accessible than the straight-leg variation and provides a gentle inner thigh and hip stretch in a fully supported lying position.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Allow both knees to fall open to the sides as far as comfortable.
Breathe steadily and let gravity deepen the inner thigh stretch.
Hold for 30–60 seconds.
Bring the knees back together and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0648 - lying stretch wide leg bent.mp4

name: lying wide leg stretch
description: The lying wide leg stretch targets the adductors by spreading the legs wide from a supine position with legs raised. Gravity assists the deepening of the stretch, and the lying position removes lower back loading, making it accessible for those with limited flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and raise both legs up toward the ceiling.
Open your legs wide apart toward each side.
Use your hands on the inner thighs to gently press them further apart if needed.
Hold the wide position for 20–30 seconds.
Close your legs and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/intermediate/0649 - lying wide leg stretch.mp4

name: lying wide-leg adductor stretch
description: The lying wide-leg adductor stretch is a bodyweight floor exercise targeting the adductor muscles of the inner thigh. Performed on the back with legs extended and spread wide, it uses gravity to deepen the stretch and improve hip flexibility and groin mobility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms relaxed by your sides.
Extend both legs straight up toward the ceiling, then slowly open them out to the sides as wide as comfortable.
Allow gravity to gently pull your legs toward the ground, feeling a stretch along your inner thighs.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and relaxing into the stretch.
Slowly bring your legs back together and repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0650 - lying wide-leg adductor stretch.mp4

name: lying with holding her straight raise leg
description: The lying with holding her straight raise leg is a bodyweight stretch targeting the hamstrings. Performed lying on the back, one leg is raised straight and held in place with the hands to deepen the hamstring stretch and improve posterior leg flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended and arms resting by your sides.
Keeping your leg straight, slowly raise one leg toward the ceiling until you feel a stretch in the back of your thigh.
Grasp the raised leg with both hands just below the knee to hold it in place.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch along the hamstring.
Slowly lower the leg back to the floor and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0651 - lying with holding her straight raise leg.mp4

name: lying bike bent knee
description: The lying bike bent knee is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. Performed on the back, it mimics a slow cycling motion with bent knees to engage the abs and hip flexors while minimizing strain on the lower back.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed lightly behind your head.
Lift both feet off the ground and bend your knees to a 90-degree angle.
Begin a slow pedaling motion, drawing one knee toward your chest while extending the opposite leg.
Keep your lower back pressed into the floor and your core engaged throughout the movement.
Continue alternating legs in a controlled cycling motion for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0652 - lying bike bent knee.mp4

name: mermaid stretch
description: The mermaid stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the obliques and lateral torso. Performed in a side-seated position with one arm reaching overhead, it creates a deep lateral stretch through the waist, lats, and hip flexors, improving spinal mobility and flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs folded to one side, knees stacked and feet pointing behind you.
Place the hand closest to your feet on the floor for support.
Extend the opposite arm up and overhead, reaching toward the opposite side to create a lateral arc through the torso.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily.
Return to the starting position, switch your leg position to the other side, and repeat the stretch.

name: seated forward over crossed legs
description: The seated forward over crossed legs is a bodyweight stretch targeting the glutes and outer hip area. By folding the torso forward over crossed legs, it provides a deep stretch to the glutes and hip rotators while also engaging the lower back and hamstrings.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs crossed comfortably in front of you.
Sit tall, lengthening your spine, and place your hands on the floor in front of you.
Slowly walk your hands forward, hinging at the hips and folding your torso over your crossed legs.
Lower your chest as close to the floor as comfortable, feeling a deep stretch in your glutes and outer hips.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then slowly walk your hands back and return to an upright seated position.
Repeat with the opposite leg crossed on top.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/0654 - seated forward over crossed legs.mp4

name: seated iron cross stretch
description: The seated iron cross stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back through spinal rotation. Performed seated with one knee bent and crossed over, it uses the elbow as a lever to deepen the twist, releasing tension in the lower back, obliques, and glutes.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor with your legs extended straight out in front of you.
Bend your right knee and cross your right foot over your left leg, placing it flat on the floor outside your left knee.
Place your left elbow on the outside of your right knee and gently press to rotate your torso to the right.
Extend your right arm behind you for support and look over your right shoulder.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then slowly release and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0655 - seated iron cross stretch.mp4

name: seated iron cross stretch version1
description: The seated iron cross stretch version1 is a bodyweight variation of the seated iron cross stretch targeting the lower back. With the ankle crossed over the opposite thigh, the torso rotates gently toward the bent leg, creating a combined hip and spinal mobility stretch.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor with both legs extended straight out in front of you.
Cross your right ankle over your left thigh just above the knee, keeping your right foot flexed.
Place both hands on the floor beside your hips for support and sit tall.
Gently rotate your torso toward the bent knee, using your hands to maintain balance.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the rotation through your lower back and hips, then switch sides.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0656 - seated iron cross stretch version1.mp4

name: seated overhead reach & pull
description: The seated overhead reach and pull is a bodyweight shoulder and upper back exercise targeting the deltoids. Alternating between a full overhead reach and a downward pull engages the shoulders, traps, and lats, making it an effective mobility and activation drill for the upper body.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair or on the floor with your feet flat and your back straight.
Raise both arms overhead, extending them fully and reaching as high as possible.
Hold the top position for a moment, then pull both elbows down toward your sides as if pulling a bar to your chest.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom of the pull.
Extend your arms back overhead and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated shoulder mobility flow
description: The seated shoulder mobility flow is a bodyweight routine targeting the deltoids and rotator cuff. Combining shoulder circles, rotations, and cross-body stretches, this flow improves shoulder joint range of motion, reduces stiffness, and promotes overall shoulder health.
instructions: Sit upright with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Begin by rolling both shoulders forward in slow, controlled circles, completing 5 repetitions.
Reverse the direction, rolling your shoulders backward for 5 repetitions.
Next, raise both arms to shoulder height and perform small internal and external rotations at the shoulder joint.
Finish by reaching one arm across the chest and gently pressing it with the opposite hand, holding for 10 seconds each side.
Repeat the full flow for the desired number of rounds.

name: seated shoulder stretch
description: The seated shoulder stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the deltoids, particularly the posterior head. Performed seated with one arm pulled across the chest, it releases tension in the shoulder and upper arm and is commonly used as part of a warm-up or cool-down routine.
instructions: Sit upright in a chair or on the floor with your back straight and core engaged.
Extend your right arm straight across your chest at shoulder height.
Use your left hand or forearm to press the right arm gently closer to your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling tension in the rear deltoid and shoulder.
Release and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated upper body rotate
description: The seated upper body rotate is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the obliques and spinal rotators. Performed seated, the controlled torso rotation improves thoracic spine mobility, engages the obliques and lower back, and is suitable for all fitness levels.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor or in a chair with your feet flat and your back straight.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head with elbows wide.
Engaging your core, slowly rotate your torso to the right as far as comfortable.
Pause briefly at the end of the rotation, then rotate back to center and continue to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a slow and controlled manner for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated hamstring stretch
description: The seated hamstring stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed in a single-leg extended seated position, it involves a forward hip hinge to stretch the back of the thigh and calf, and is widely used to improve posterior chain flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one leg extended straight out in front of you and the other leg bent with the sole of the foot against the inner thigh.
Sit tall and place your hands on either side of the extended leg.
Hinging at the hips, slowly lean your torso forward over the extended leg, reaching toward your foot.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling tension in the back of the thigh.
Return to the upright position and switch legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0661 - seated hamstring stretch.mp4

name: senior-friendly burpee (low-impact, joint-safe)
description: The senior-friendly burpee is a low-impact, bodyweight variation of the traditional burpee designed to be safe for aging joints. By stepping rather than jumping, it maintains cardiovascular and full-body benefits while reducing impact stress on the knees, hips, and spine.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Slowly hinge at the hips and bend your knees, placing both hands on the floor in front of you.
Step one foot back at a time into a standing plank position, avoiding any jumping.
Hold the plank briefly, then step one foot at a time back toward your hands.
Place your hands on your knees and slowly push yourself back up to a standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions at a controlled pace.

name: single leg stretch seated
description: The single leg stretch seated is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed in a seated position, one leg is drawn in and extended while held with the hands, creating a focused stretch in the posterior thigh and hip, suitable for all fitness levels.
instructions: Sit upright on the floor with both legs extended in front of you.
Bend one knee and draw it toward your chest, placing both hands behind the thigh for support.
Gently extend the bent leg as straight as possible while holding it with your hands.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull along the back of the thigh.
Return to the starting position and repeat with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0663 - single leg stretch seated.mp4

name: single-leg head touch knee forward fold
description: The single-leg head touch knee forward fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed standing, it involves a deep hip hinge toward one extended leg, progressively deepening the stretch in the hamstrings and calf while challenging balance and lower back flexibility.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet together and your arms relaxed by your sides.
Shift your weight onto one foot and extend the opposite leg slightly forward, keeping it straight.
Hinge forward at the hips, reaching your hands toward the extended foot while keeping your back flat.
Continue folding until your head moves toward the knee of the extended leg.
Hold the bottom position for 15-20 seconds, then slowly return to standing.
Repeat on the opposite side for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/intermediate/0664 - single-leg head touch knee forward fold.mp4

name: sit on ankle ii
description: The sit on ankle ii is a bodyweight stretch targeting the quadriceps. A progression of the basic sit on ankle position, it incorporates a slight backward lean to intensify the stretch along the front of the thighs and ankles, improving quad and hip flexor flexibility.
instructions: Begin kneeling on a soft surface with your knees together and your toes pointed behind you.
Shift your weight back and slowly lower your hips toward your heels, sitting back onto your ankles.
Lean your torso slightly backward, supporting yourself with your hands placed on the floor behind you.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds, feeling a stretch through the quads and front of the ankles.
Slowly return to the kneeling position and repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0665 - sit on ankle ii.mp4

name: sit on ankle
description: The sit on ankle is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the quadriceps. Performed in a kneeling position with the hips lowered onto the heels, it gently stretches the quads, hip flexors, and front of the lower legs, and is widely used in mobility and warm-up routines.
instructions: Begin kneeling on a soft surface with your knees together and your toes pointed behind you.
Slowly lower your hips straight down toward your heels, sitting back onto your ankles.
Rest your hands on your thighs and sit upright, keeping your back straight.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling a gentle stretch through the quads and front of the lower legs.
Slowly rise back to the kneeling position and repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0666 - sit on ankle.mp4

name: standing hamstring
description: The standing hamstring stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed standing with one heel extended forward, a controlled forward hip hinge creates a focused stretch along the back of the thigh and calf, improving posterior chain flexibility and posture.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Extend one leg slightly forward and place the heel on the ground with the toes pointing up.
Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat, and reach both hands toward the toes of the extended leg.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling tension in the back of the thigh.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing shoulder rolls
description: Standing shoulder rolls is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the deltoids. Performed standing, the circular shoulder motion lubricates the shoulder joints, releases tension in the traps and neck, and is commonly used as part of an upper body warm-up or cool-down.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Slowly roll both shoulders upward, then backward, then downward, and finally forward in a full circular motion.
Perform 5-10 slow, controlled backward rolls, focusing on full range of motion.
Reverse the direction and perform 5-10 forward rolls.
Breathe steadily throughout and keep your head and neck relaxed.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: standing shoulder shrug
description: The standing shoulder shrug is a bodyweight exercise targeting the trapezius muscles. Performed standing with arms at the sides, it isolates the upper traps through a simple upward shrugging motion and is commonly used to build neck and shoulder stability or release upper body tension.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms hanging relaxed at your sides.
Keeping your arms straight, raise both shoulders as high as possible toward your ears.
Hold the shrugged position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing the trapezius muscles.
Slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: standing switch kick front
description: The standing switch kick front is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps and hip flexors. Alternating front kicks performed in a rhythmic switching pattern elevate the heart rate while activating the quads, glutes, and hip flexors, making it effective as a dynamic warm-up or cardio drill.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet together and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Shift your weight onto your left foot and swing your right leg forward in a controlled front kick motion.
Lower your right foot and immediately shift your weight to swing the left leg forward.
Continue alternating legs in a rhythmic, fluid switching motion.
Keep your core engaged and your torso upright throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or time.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/cardio/beginner/0670 - standing switch kick front.mp4

name: standing towel hamstring stretch
description: The standing towel hamstring stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings. Using a towel as an assist, one leg is raised and extended forward while the towel provides gentle traction to deepen the stretch, improving hamstring flexibility and posterior chain mobility.
instructions: Stand tall and loop a towel or resistance band around the sole of one foot.
Hold both ends of the towel and lift the foot forward, extending the leg as straight as possible.
Gently pull the towel toward you to increase the stretch along the back of the thigh.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, keeping your standing leg slightly bent for balance.
Lower the leg and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0671 - standing towel hamstring stretch.mp4

name: standing upper body rotation
description: The standing upper body rotation is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the obliques and thoracic spine. Performed standing with the hips stable, the controlled torso twist engages the obliques, lower back, and upper back, improving rotational mobility and core stability.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height, or cross them over your chest.
Engaging your core, slowly rotate your torso to the right as far as comfortable.
Return to center, then rotate to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a slow and controlled manner, keeping your hips facing forward throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: superman hold
description: The superman hold is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back. Performed lying face down, simultaneous lifting of the arms and legs engages the erector spinae, glutes, and upper back, making it an effective movement for building posterior chain strength and spinal stability.
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended straight overhead and your legs straight behind you.
Engaging your glutes and lower back, simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor.
Hold the raised position for 2-3 seconds, keeping your neck in a neutral position.
Slowly lower your arms, chest, and legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wide-leg bent forward fold
description: The wide-leg bent forward fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings and inner thighs. Performed with a wide stance and a forward hip hinge with slightly bent knees, it provides a deep stretch to the posterior and inner legs while reducing strain on the lower back.
instructions: Stand with your feet spread wide apart, approximately two to three times shoulder-width.
Turn your toes slightly outward and place your hands on your hips.
Hinge forward at the hips, bending your knees slightly as you lower your torso toward the floor.
Place your hands on the floor between your feet and allow your head to relax downward.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the inner thighs and hamstrings.
Slowly return to standing and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0674 - wide-leg bent forward fold.mp4

name: wide-leg forward fold with hand slide
description: The wide-leg forward fold with hand slide is a bodyweight stretch targeting the hamstrings and adductors. The hands sliding progressively down the legs guide a controlled forward fold, deepening the posterior and inner thigh stretch while cuing a flat-back hip hinge pattern.
instructions: Stand with your feet spread wide apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Place both hands on your thighs and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat.
Slowly slide your hands down your legs toward your feet as you deepen the forward fold.
Allow your hands to reach as far down as comfortable, feeling the stretch in the inner thighs and hamstrings.
Hold the bottom position for 20-30 seconds, then slide your hands back up your legs to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0675 - wide-leg forward fold with hand slide.mp4

name: wide-leg half forward fold
description: The wide-leg half forward fold is a bodyweight stretch targeting the hamstrings. By hinging to a torso-parallel position with a wide stance, it stretches the hamstrings and inner thighs while placing particular emphasis on maintaining a neutral, elongated spine.
instructions: Stand with your feet spread wide apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is parallel to the floor, maintaining a flat back.
Place your hands on your shins or on the floor directly beneath your shoulders for support.
Hold this half-fold position for 20-30 seconds, focusing on keeping the spine long.
Slowly return to standing and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0676 - wide-leg half forward fold.mp4

name: wide-leg sit on bent leg hand on floor
description: The wide-leg sit on bent leg hand on floor is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the adductors. Transitioning from a wide-leg seat to one bent knee, it creates an asymmetrical hip and inner thigh stretch, improving adductor flexibility and hip mobility.
instructions: Begin in a wide-leg seated position on the floor with both legs extended out to the sides.
Bend one knee and bring that foot in close to your groin, lowering your hips toward the bent-leg side.
Place both hands on the floor in front of you for support.
Gently shift your weight to deepen the stretch in the inner thigh of the straight leg.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0677 - wide-leg sit on bent leg hand on floor.mp4

name: wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch
description: The wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings. Combining a hip lift with a forward fold in a wide-leg seated position, it dynamically engages the glutes and adductors while deepening the hamstring stretch for improved posterior chain flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended wide apart and your hands placed on the floor behind your hips.
Press your hands into the floor and lift your hips slightly off the ground.
Hold the lifted position briefly, then slowly lower your hips back down.
Hinge your torso forward over one leg, reaching toward the foot to stretch the hamstring.
Hold the forward fold for 20-30 seconds, then return upright and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0678 - wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch.mp4

name: wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch 1
description: The wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch 1 is a bodyweight variation targeting the hamstrings and lower back. By adding a forward torso fold before the hip lift, it creates a deeper hamstring and adductor stretch and introduces a progressive challenge to hip extensor flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended wide and your back straight.
Place both hands on the floor in front of you between your legs.
Walk your hands forward to fold your torso toward the floor, keeping the spine long.
At the deepest point of the fold, press your hands into the floor and gently lift your hips off the ground.
Hold the lifted position for 5-10 seconds, then lower and return to upright.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0679 - wide-leg seated hamstring hip lift stretch 1.mp4

name: world's greatest stretch
description: The world's greatest stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors and providing a comprehensive full-body mobility sequence. Combining a deep lunge, thoracic rotation, and hamstring stretch in one fluid movement, it improves flexibility, mobility, and movement quality throughout the entire kinetic chain.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly beneath your shoulders.
Step your right foot forward to the outside of your right hand, lowering into a deep lunge.
Drop your right elbow toward the floor inside your right foot, holding briefly.
Rotate your torso and reach your right arm straight up toward the ceiling, following it with your gaze.
Return your hand to the floor, push your hips back to stretch the hamstring of the front leg, then step back to plank.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/intermediate/0680 - world's greatest stretch.mp4

name: wall arm stretch
description: The wall arm stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. Using a wall as a fixed anchor, the torso rotation stretches the chest and anterior shoulder, making it an effective tool for improving thoracic mobility and releasing tightness in the upper body.
instructions: Stand next to a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place one hand flat against the wall at shoulder height with your arm straight.
Slowly rotate your torso away from the wall while keeping your hand pressed firmly against it.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull across your chest and shoulder.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall arm stretch with touch knee
description: The wall arm stretch with touch knee is a bodyweight exercise targeting the pectorals. It combines the traditional wall chest stretch with a knee raise, adding a balance challenge while simultaneously stretching the chest, shoulder, and hip flexors for a more dynamic mobility drill.
instructions: Stand next to a wall and place one hand flat against the wall at shoulder height.
Rotate your torso away from the wall to open the chest, feeling an initial stretch.
Simultaneously lift the knee of your outside leg toward your chest to balance on one foot.
Hold the combined stretch and balance position for 10-15 seconds.
Lower the knee, return to the starting position, and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/chest/pectorals/stretching/beginner/0682 - wall arm stretch with touch knee.mp4

name: wall arms standing feet touch
description: The wall arms standing feet touch is a bodyweight stretch targeting the latissimus dorsi. Using the wall as a guide, a standing forward fold with arms extended elongates the lats and stretches the hamstrings and lower back, improving overall posterior chain and shoulder flexibility.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet together and your toes a few inches from the base of the wall.
Reach both arms up and place your hands on the wall as high as possible.
Slowly hinge forward at the hips, walking your hands down the wall and lowering your torso.
Continue until your chest faces the floor and your arms are extended along the wall.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull through the lats and hamstrings.
Slowly walk your hands back up the wall to return to standing and repeat.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/back/lats/stretching/beginner/0683 - wall arms standing feet touch.mp4

name: wall elbow plank elevated step-up
description: The wall elbow plank elevated step-up is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. Performed in a forearm plank on an elevated surface, the alternating foot step-ups challenge core stability, engage the quads and glutes, and increase the cardiovascular demand of a standard plank.
instructions: Place your forearms on a sturdy elevated surface such as a bench, with your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Step both feet back into an elbow plank position, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Keeping your core tight, step one foot up onto the elevated surface beside your hands.
Step the other foot up, holding briefly in the elevated plank, then step both feet back down one at a time.
Keep your hips level throughout the movement and avoid rotation in the torso.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0684 - wall elbow plank elevated step-up.mp4

name: wall elbow plank static hold
description: The wall elbow plank static hold is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominals. By performing a forearm plank against an elevated wall surface, it reduces load compared to a floor plank while still effectively engaging the core, shoulders, and lower back for stability and endurance.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place both forearms flat against the wall at shoulder height, elbows bent.
Step your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line from head to heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and thighs to maintain a rigid body position.
Hold the plank position for the desired amount of time, breathing steadily.
