To finish, step your feet forward and lower your arms from the wall.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/beginner/0685 - wall elbow plank static hold.mp4

name: wall facing squat
description: The wall facing squat is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. Performing a squat inches from a wall enforces an upright torso and proper knee tracking, making it an excellent drill for improving squat mechanics, ankle mobility, and quad activation.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your toes a few inches from the base, feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms forward and place your fingertips lightly on the wall for guidance.
Keeping your chest up and torso upright, slowly bend your knees and lower into a squat.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor or as low as comfortable without touching the wall.
Press through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0686 - wall facing squat.mp4

name: wall forward lunges
description: The wall forward lunges is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. Performing forward lunges with light wall support improves balance and allows the focus to remain on quad and glute engagement, making it suitable for beginners or those developing proper lunge mechanics.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart and your hands lightly touching the wall for balance.
Step one foot forward and lower your body into a lunge, bending both knees to approximately 90 degrees.
Keep your front knee aligned over your front foot and your torso upright.
Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite leg and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0687 - wall forward lunges.mp4

name: wall marching + heel drive
description: The wall marching with heel drive is a bodyweight cardio and mobility exercise targeting the hip flexors. Using the wall for support, the exaggerated knee raise and heel drive sequence activates the hip flexors, quads, and glutes, making it effective as a dynamic warm-up or low-impact conditioning drill.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place both hands flat on the wall at shoulder height.
Lean slightly forward, pressing into the wall with your arms straight.
Drive one knee up toward your chest as high as comfortable in a marching motion.
As the knee rises, actively drive the heel of the same leg upward toward the glutes on the return.
Lower the foot and repeat on the opposite leg, alternating in a rhythmic marching pattern.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or time.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/cardio/beginner/0688 - wall marching + heel drive.mp4

name: wall marching
description: Wall marching is a bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors. Using the wall for stability, alternating knee drives activate the hip flexors, quads, and core, and are commonly used as a low-impact warm-up drill or in rehabilitation programs to improve hip mobility and balance.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place both hands flat on the wall at shoulder height.
Lean slightly forward into the wall, keeping your arms extended.
Lift one knee up toward your chest in a controlled marching motion.
Lower the foot and lift the opposite knee.
Continue alternating legs in a steady marching rhythm for the desired number of repetitions or time.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/hip flexors/cardio/beginner/0689 - wall marching.mp4

name: wall one leg squats
description: The wall one leg squat is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. Performed with the back against a wall for support and one leg raised, it isolates each leg individually, challenging quad strength, glute activation, and single-leg stability with reduced risk of form breakdown.
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and your feet shoulder-width apart, slightly in front of you.
Extend one leg straight out in front of you and hold it off the floor.
Slowly bend the standing leg, sliding your back down the wall until your thigh approaches parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly, then press through the heel of your standing foot to return to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one leg, then switch and repeat on the other side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0690 - wall one leg squats.mp4

name: wall plank hold
description: The wall plank hold is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. Performed with hands on the wall instead of the floor, it provides a more accessible plank variation that effectively engages the core, shoulders, and lower back while reducing wrist and joint strain.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place both hands flat on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Step your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line from head to heels.
Engage your core, squeeze your glutes, and keep your hips level.
Hold the position for the desired amount of time, breathing steadily.
To finish, step your feet forward and release your hands from the wall.

name: wall plank one leg up down
description: The wall plank one leg up down is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominals. Adding alternating single-leg raises to a wall plank increases core stability demands, challenges the glutes and lower back, and develops unilateral coordination and anti-rotation strength.
instructions: Stand facing a wall and place both hands flat on the wall at shoulder height.
Step your feet back into a wall plank position, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and maintain a stable, neutral pelvis.
Slowly lift one leg off the floor, raising it as high as comfortable while keeping the hips level.
Lower the leg back to the floor with control and repeat with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0692 - wall plank one leg up down.mp4

name: wall sit arm stretch
description: The wall sit arm stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. By performing an overhead arm reach while holding a wall sit, it combines quad endurance with a shoulder and chest stretch, challenging lower body isometric strength and upper body flexibility simultaneously.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and slide down until your thighs are parallel to the floor in a wall sit position.
Hold your knees at 90 degrees and maintain the sitting position throughout the movement.
Raise both arms overhead, extending them fully along the wall behind you.
Hold the combined wall sit and overhead stretch for 20-30 seconds, keeping your lower back pressed against the wall.
Lower your arms and slowly slide back up the wall to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0693 - wall sit arm stretch.mp4

name: wall slide
description: The wall slide is a bodyweight shoulder mobility exercise targeting the deltoids. Performed with the back against a wall, the controlled upward arm slide challenges shoulder flexion, scapular stability, and rotator cuff function, making it a foundational drill for correcting poor shoulder mechanics.
instructions: Stand with your back, head, and heels flat against a wall and your feet about six inches from the base.
Press both arms against the wall in a goalpost position with elbows at 90 degrees and upper arms at shoulder height.
Slowly slide both arms upward along the wall, straightening them overhead while maintaining contact with the wall.
Pause at the top, then slide your arms back down to the goalpost position.
Keep your lower back, elbows, and wrists in contact with the wall throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall slide hips out
description: The wall slide hips out is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the adductors. Performed in a wall sit with the knees pressed outward, it creates a deep inner thigh stretch while the wall provides back support, making it effective for improving hip mobility and adductor flexibility.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and your feet about shoulder-width apart.
Slide down the wall until your knees are bent at approximately 90 degrees in a squat position.
From this position, push both knees outward away from each other as far as comfortable.
Hold the knees-out position for 10-15 seconds, feeling the stretch in the inner thighs.
Return knees to neutral, slide back up the wall, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/adductors/stretching/beginner/0695 - wall slide hips out.mp4

name: wall squat
description: The wall squat is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. Using the wall for back support, the squat movement isolates the quads and glutes while enforcing proper knee alignment and an upright torso, making it a safe and effective option for beginners and rehabilitation.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and your feet shoulder-width apart, about two feet in front of you.
Slowly slide your back down the wall, bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Ensure your knees are directly above your ankles and do not extend past your toes.
Hold the squat position for 2-3 seconds, then press through your heels to slide back up to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall squat + push ups
description: The wall squat plus push ups is a bodyweight compound exercise targeting the quadriceps and pectorals. Combining a lower body isometric squat hold with upper body push-ups performed against the wall creates a challenging full-body strength routine that improves muscular endurance and coordination.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands flat on the wall at chest height.
Step your feet back and lower into a squat position, bending your knees to approximately 90 degrees.
Holding the squat, perform a push-up by bending your elbows and lowering your chest toward the wall, then pressing back.
Complete the desired number of push-ups while maintaining the squat position.
Press through your heels to return to standing and rest briefly before repeating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0697 - wall squat + push ups.mp4

name: wall squat hold
description: The wall squat hold is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the quadriceps. By holding a seated squat position with the back supported against a wall, it builds sustained quad and glute endurance, improves knee stability, and is commonly used in rehabilitation and functional training programs.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and your feet shoulder-width apart, placed about two feet in front of you.
Slide your back down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the floor and your knees form a 90-degree angle.
Press your back firmly into the wall and keep your arms relaxed at your sides or extended in front of you.
Hold this static position for the desired amount of time, keeping your breathing steady.
Slowly slide back up the wall to the starting position and rest before repeating.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0698 - wall squat hold.mp4

name: wall squat hold hip out
description: The wall squat hold hip out is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps with added adductor stretch. Combining a wall squat isometric hold with outward knee pressure, it simultaneously builds quad endurance and stretches the inner thighs, improving hip mobility and lower body stability.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and your feet wider than shoulder-width, placed in front of you.
Slide down the wall until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor.
From the squat hold position, push both knees outward, pressing them away from each other.
Hold this knees-out squat position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the inner thighs and activation in the glutes.
Return the knees to neutral and slide back up the wall.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/0699 - wall squat hold hip out.mp4

name: wall squats (knee touch on wall)
description: The wall squats knee touch on wall is a bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps. By squatting with the knees making light contact with the wall, it promotes forward knee drive, improves ankle dorsiflexion, and enforces proper squat depth while developing quad strength and mobility.
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your toes nearly touching it and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Slowly bend your knees and lower into a squat, allowing your knees to gently make contact with the wall.
Keep your torso upright and your heels flat on the floor throughout the movement.
Pause at the bottom with knees touching the wall, then press through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0700 - wall squats (knee touch on wall).mp4

name: wall support plank leg on wall
description: The wall support plank leg on wall is a bodyweight core exercise targeting the abdominals. With one foot braced against the wall, the plank position gains additional stability, allowing for deeper core and glute engagement and making it an effective progression tool for building plank endurance and unilateral stability.
instructions: Position yourself near a wall and place both hands on the floor in a standard plank position.
Lift one leg and place the sole of the foot flat against the wall behind you for support.
Engage your core, keep your hips level, and maintain a straight line from head to the elevated foot.
Hold the position for the desired amount of time, breathing steadily.
Lower the foot, switch legs, and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: 400 Home Workout/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/0701 - wall support plank leg on wall.mp4

name: apple cheek lift
description: The apple cheek lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the cheek muscles. It involves lifting the cheeks upward toward the eyes to tone and firm the mid-face region.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight and shoulders relaxed.
Close your mouth and smile gently, then push the fleshy part of your cheeks upward toward your eyes using your facial muscles.
Hold the lifted position for 5 seconds, feeling the tension in your cheek muscles.
Slowly release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0913 - apple cheek lift.mp4

name: balloon cheek
description: The balloon cheek is a bodyweight facial exercise that targets the cheek muscles by inflating the cheeks with air and holding the position. It helps tone and strengthen the muscles of the mid-face.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your mouth closed.
Take a deep breath in through your nose and fill your cheeks with air as if inflating a balloon.
Hold the air in your puffed cheeks for 5–10 seconds.
Slowly release the air through a small opening in your lips.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: blinking resistance
description: Blinking resistance is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the muscles around the eyes. It involves closing the eyelids against gentle finger resistance to strengthen and tone the eye area.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed and eyes open.
Place your index fingers lightly on the outer corners of your eyes to create gentle resistance.
Slowly close your eyes against the resistance of your fingers, squeezing the eyelids shut.
Hold the closed position for 2–3 seconds, then slowly open your eyes.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: brow lift
description: The brow lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the forehead muscles. It involves raising the eyebrows against finger resistance to strengthen and lift the brow area.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your face in a neutral, relaxed position.
Place your index fingers just above your eyebrows, applying light downward pressure.
Using your forehead muscles, raise your eyebrows upward against the resistance of your fingers.
Hold the raised position for 5 seconds, then slowly lower your eyebrows back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cheek firming press
description: The cheek firming press is a bodyweight facial exercise that targets the cheek muscles by pressing the palms against the cheeks while smiling. It helps firm and tone the mid-face area.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed and face relaxed.
Place your palms flat against your cheeks with gentle pressure.
Smile as wide as possible while pressing your cheeks inward against your palms.
Hold the position for 5 seconds, feeling the contraction in your cheek muscles.
Release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0917 - cheek firming press.mp4

name: cheek lifter
description: The cheek lifter is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the cheek and zygomaticus muscles. It involves forming an 'O' shape with the lips and lifting the cheeks upward to firm and tone the mid-face.
instructions: Sit or stand with your spine straight and shoulders relaxed.
Open your mouth slightly and form an 'O' shape with your lips.
Smile while keeping the 'O' shape, lifting the cheek muscles upward toward your eyes.
Place your fingertips lightly on the top of your cheeks to feel the lift.
Hold for 5 seconds, then release to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cheek sculptor
description: The cheek sculptor is a bodyweight facial exercise that targets the cheek muscles by drawing them inward. It helps define and contour the cheekbones through repeated muscle engagement.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed and face relaxed.
Suck your cheeks inward toward your teeth to create a hollowed expression.
Hold the hollowed position for 5–10 seconds, engaging the cheek muscles.
Slowly release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cheekbone lift
description: The cheekbone lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the zygomaticus and cheek muscles. It involves lifting the cheeks toward the fingertips to define and elevate the cheekbone area.
instructions: Sit upright and relax your face completely.
Place your index and middle fingers on top of your cheekbones just below the outer corners of your eyes.
Smile as broadly as possible, pushing your cheek muscles upward toward your fingers.
Hold the lifted smile for 5 seconds.
Slowly relax and return to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chin lift jawline
description: The chin lift jawline is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and platysma muscles. It involves tilting the head back and puckering the lips to stretch and tone the chin and jawline area.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head back slowly, directing your gaze toward the ceiling.
Pucker your lips as if kissing the ceiling, extending your chin upward.
Hold the position for 5 seconds, feeling the stretch along your chin and neck.
Slowly return your head to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chewing gum jawline
description: The chewing gum jawline is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter and jaw muscles. It mimics an exaggerated chewing motion to strengthen and define the jawline.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight.
Close your mouth and simulate a chewing motion by moving your jaw up and down in an exaggerated manner.
Exaggerate each chewing movement to fully engage the jaw muscles.
Continue the chewing motion for 30–60 seconds.
Rest briefly, then repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0922 - chewing gum jawline.mp4

name: crow's feet smoother
description: The crow's feet smoother is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the muscles around the outer corners of the eyes. It involves controlled squinting to tone the orbicularis oculi and reduce the appearance of fine lines.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Place your index fingers at the outer corners of each eye, applying very light pressure.
Slowly squint by gently bringing the lower eyelid upward without moving your forehead.
Hold the squint for 3 seconds, then slowly release.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0923 - crow's feet smoother.mp4

name: duck face hold
description: The duck face hold is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oris and surrounding lip muscles. It involves extending the lips outward in a puckered position to tone and strengthen the mouth area.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight.
Pucker your lips outward as far as possible, creating a 'duck face' expression.
Hold the puckered position for 5–10 seconds, engaging the muscles around your mouth.
Slowly release and return your lips to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0924 - duck face hold.mp4

name: eye socket massage
description: The eye socket massage is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the muscles and tissue around the eye area. It involves applying gentle circular pressure along the orbital bone to relieve tension and improve circulation around the eyes.
instructions: Sit comfortably with your back straight and face relaxed.
Place the tips of your index fingers on the inner corners of your eyebrows.
Apply gentle circular pressure, moving along the brow bone toward the outer corners of the eyes.
Continue the circular massage under the eyes along the lower orbital bone back toward the nose.
Repeat the full circuit for 30–60 seconds.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0925 - eye socket massage.mp4

name: fish face
description: The fish face is a bodyweight facial exercise that targets the cheek and lip muscles. It involves sucking the cheeks inward while smiling to tone and strengthen the mid-face region.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight.
Suck your cheeks inward and purse your lips to create a fish-like expression.
Try to smile while maintaining the sucked-in cheek position.
Hold the expression for 5 seconds, feeling the tension in your cheek muscles.
Relax and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: forehead smoother
description: The forehead smoother is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the frontalis muscle. It involves raising the eyebrows against hand resistance to strengthen the forehead muscles and reduce the appearance of horizontal lines.
instructions: Sit upright and place both palms flat on your forehead, fingers pointing toward each other.
Apply gentle downward pressure with your palms against your forehead.
While maintaining the pressure, try to raise your eyebrows upward, working against your hands.
Hold the raised position for 5 seconds.
Slowly lower and relax your eyebrows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: forehead wrinkle eraser
description: The forehead wrinkle eraser is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the frontalis and corrugator muscles. It uses finger resistance to strengthen the forehead while minimizing the formation of horizontal wrinkles.
instructions: Sit upright with your face completely relaxed.
Place your fingertips horizontally across your forehead, applying light pressure.
Slowly raise your eyebrows as high as possible while your fingertips resist the upward movement.
Hold for 3–5 seconds at the top.
Gently lower your eyebrows back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0928 - forehead wrinkle eraser.mp4

name: full face stretch
description: The full face stretch is a bodyweight facial exercise that targets all major facial muscle groups simultaneously. It involves opening the mouth, eyes, and extending the tongue to perform a comprehensive stretch of the face.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your shoulders relaxed.
Open your mouth as wide as possible and simultaneously open your eyes as wide as you can.
Extend your tongue downward as far as comfortable.
Hold the fully stretched position for 5 seconds.
Slowly release all muscles back to a neutral, relaxed expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/stretching/beginner/0929 - full face stretch.mp4

name: giraffe neck stretch
description: The giraffe neck stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and platysma muscles. It involves tilting the head back while pulling the collarbone skin downward to stretch and tone the front of the neck.
instructions: Sit upright with your shoulders pulled back and relaxed.
Place your fingertips on the collarbone and gently pull the skin downward.
Tilt your head back slowly, directing your chin toward the ceiling.
Hold the stretch for 5–10 seconds, feeling the elongation along the front of the neck.
Slowly return your head to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hollow cheeks
description: Hollow cheeks is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the buccinator and cheek muscles. It involves drawing the cheeks firmly inward to sculpt and define the cheekbone area.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed and face relaxed.
Draw your cheeks inward toward your teeth as strongly as possible.
Hold the hollowed position for 5–10 seconds while breathing through your nose.
Slowly release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: horizontal forehead
description: The horizontal forehead exercise is a bodyweight facial movement targeting the frontalis muscle. It involves pressing the fingers across the forehead to resist upward wrinkling, helping to tone and smooth horizontal forehead lines.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed and eyes open.
Place both index fingers horizontally across your forehead just above the eyebrows.
Press lightly with your fingers and attempt to wrinkle your forehead upward against the resistance.
Hold the contraction for 5 seconds.
Slowly relax the forehead muscles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jaw & platysma stretch
description: The jaw and platysma stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the platysma and neck muscles. It combines a head tilt with a downward lip pull to stretch and tone the front of the neck and lower face.
instructions: Sit upright with your spine straight and shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head back gently until you are looking at the ceiling.
Pull the corners of your mouth downward and outward to engage the platysma muscle along your neck.
Hold this stretched position for 5–10 seconds.
Slowly return your head to the neutral position and relax.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jaw & tongue stretch
description: The jaw and tongue stretch is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the jaw and tongue muscles. It involves opening the mouth wide and extending the tongue downward to stretch and tone the lower face and neck area.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed and face relaxed.
Open your mouth wide, then extend your tongue downward toward your chin as far as comfortable.
Simultaneously tilt your head slightly upward to increase the stretch in the jaw and neck.
Hold the stretched position for 5 seconds.
Slowly close your mouth and return to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/stretching/beginner/0934 - jaw & tongue stretch.mp4

name: jaw clench release
description: The jaw clench release is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter and temporalis muscles. It involves alternating between clenching and releasing the jaw to strengthen the muscles along the jawline.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth gently closed.
Slowly clench your teeth together firmly, engaging the masseter muscles on both sides of your jaw.
Hold the clenched position for 3–5 seconds.
Slowly release and allow the jaw to relax completely.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0935 - jaw clench release.mp4

name: jaw protrusion
description: Jaw protrusion is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the jaw muscles and platysma. It involves pushing the lower jaw forward to stretch and strengthen the muscles along the jaw and lower face.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight.
Close your mouth gently and relax your jaw.
Slowly push your lower jaw forward as far as comfortable, so your lower teeth are in front of your upper teeth.
Hold the protruded position for 5 seconds, feeling the stretch in the jaw and neck.
Slowly return your jaw to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jaw resistance open-close
description: The jaw resistance open-close is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter and temporalis muscles. It uses hand resistance while opening and closing the mouth to strengthen the jaw muscles.
instructions: Sit upright and place your fist under your chin.
Apply upward pressure with your fist while slowly opening your mouth against the resistance.
Open your mouth as wide as comfortable, maintaining resistance throughout.
Slowly close your mouth, still pressing lightly with your fist.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0937 - jaw resistance open-close.mp4

name: jaw sculpt hold
description: The jaw sculpt hold is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter and jaw muscles. It combines jaw protrusion with a chin tilt to sculpt and define the jawline.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth slightly open.
Push your lower jaw forward slightly and tilt your chin upward at a 45-degree angle.
Clench your teeth lightly and hold the position for 5–10 seconds.
Slowly relax the jaw and return to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0938 - jaw sculpt hold.mp4

name: jaw squeeze
description: The jaw squeeze is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter muscles. It involves clenching the back teeth while smiling to isolate and strengthen the jaw muscles along the jawline.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth gently closed.
Smile broadly, keeping your lips together.
Clench your back molars firmly together while maintaining the smile.
Hold the squeeze for 5 seconds.
Slowly release the clench and relax the jaw.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jawline lift
description: The jawline lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and platysma muscles. It involves tilting the head back and pushing the lower jaw forward to tighten and define the jawline and chin area.
instructions: Sit upright with your shoulders relaxed and spine straight.
Tilt your head back until you are looking at the ceiling.
Close your mouth and move your lower jaw forward slightly.
Hold this position for 5–10 seconds, feeling the stretch beneath the chin.
Slowly lower your head back to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: jawline stretch
description: The jawline stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and jaw muscles. It involves lateral head tilting combined with jaw protrusion to stretch and lengthen the muscles along the jawline.
instructions: Sit upright with your back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head gently to the right side toward your shoulder.
Move your lower jaw forward and slightly upward to feel a stretch along the left side of the jaw and neck.
Hold the stretch for 10–15 seconds.
Return to the neutral position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lip corner lift
description: The lip corner lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the zygomaticus and orbicularis oris muscles. It involves lifting the corners of the mouth upward to tone and firm the lower cheek and lip area.
instructions: Sit upright with your face in a neutral, relaxed position.
Place your index fingers at the outer corners of your mouth.
Using your facial muscles, lift the corners of your mouth upward into a gentle smile.
Hold the lifted position for 5 seconds.
Slowly release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0942 - lip corner lift.mp4

name: lip press
description: The lip press is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oris muscle. It involves pressing the lips firmly together to tone and strengthen the muscles surrounding the mouth.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Press your lips firmly together without clenching your teeth.
Hold the pressed position for 5 seconds, engaging the muscles around the mouth.
Slowly release the tension and relax your lips.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lip pucker (kiss face)
description: The lip pucker (kiss face) is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oris muscle. It involves puckering the lips outward to strengthen and tone the muscles around the mouth.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your spine straight.
Close your mouth and pucker your lips outward as if preparing to give a kiss.
Hold the puckered position for 5 seconds, squeezing the lip muscles firmly.
Release and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0944 - lip pucker (kiss face).mp4

name: lip stretch smile
description: The lip stretch smile is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the lip and zygomaticus muscles. It involves holding a wide, closed-lip smile to stretch and tone the muscles across the lower face.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Smile as wide as possible while keeping your lips closed together.
Hold the wide smile for 5 seconds, feeling the stretch across your lips and cheeks.
Slowly relax and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/stretching/beginner/0945 - lip stretch smile.mp4

name: lips stretch
description: The lips stretch is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oris and surrounding lip muscles. It involves stretching the lips outward to increase flexibility and tone the muscles around the mouth.
instructions: Sit upright with your face in a relaxed, neutral position.
Open your mouth slightly and stretch your lips outward as wide as possible, as if saying 'eee'.
Hold the stretched position for 5 seconds.
Slowly relax and return to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lower eyelid tightener
description: The lower eyelid tightener is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oculi muscle. It involves raising only the lower eyelid upward to strengthen and tone the under-eye area.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed and eyes open.
Look straight ahead and keep your upper eyelids still.
Slowly raise your lower eyelids upward toward the iris, as if squinting from below.
Hold the position for 3 seconds.
Slowly release and return to a relaxed expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0947 - lower eyelid tightener.mp4

name: lower lip lift
description: The lower lip lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the mentalis and orbicularis oris muscles. It involves raising the lower lip upward to tone and strengthen the muscles around the lower mouth and chin.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Keeping your upper lip still, push your lower lip upward as far as possible toward your upper lip.
Hold the raised position for 5 seconds, engaging the chin and lower lip muscles.
Slowly return your lower lip to the resting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0948 - lower lip lift.mp4

name: move jaw fastly
description: Move jaw fastly is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the masseter and temporalis muscles. It involves rapid lateral jaw movements to dynamically engage and strengthen the jaw muscles.
instructions: Sit upright with your spine straight and shoulders relaxed.
Open your mouth slightly and begin moving your jaw from side to side in a rapid, controlled motion.
Keep the movements small but quick, engaging the jaw muscles with each repetition.
Continue for 20–30 seconds.
Rest briefly, then repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/cardio/beginner/0949 - move jaw fastly.mp4

name: neck circle
description: The neck circle is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck muscles and sternocleidomastoid. It involves slow, controlled circular head rotations to improve neck mobility and release tension.
instructions: Sit or stand upright with your shoulders relaxed.
Slowly lower your chin toward your chest.
Gently roll your head in a full circular motion, moving to the right shoulder, then back, then left shoulder, then back to the chest.
Keep the movements slow and controlled throughout the full circle.
Repeat the circle in the opposite direction.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: neck jaw stretch
description: The neck jaw stretch is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and jaw muscles. It combines a lateral head tilt with a jaw protrusion to stretch the sternocleidomastoid and surrounding neck musculature.
instructions: Sit upright with your back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head to the right, bringing your right ear toward your right shoulder.
Gently push your lower jaw forward and slightly upward to feel a stretch on the left side of the neck and jaw.
Hold the stretch for 10–15 seconds.
Return to the neutral position and repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: neck lift
description: The neck lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the sternocleidomastoid and anterior neck muscles. It involves lifting the head while lying down to strengthen the muscles at the front of the neck.
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a mat with your legs extended.
Place your arms flat at your sides.
Slowly raise your head off the mat, bringing your chin toward your chest.
Hold the lifted position for 5 seconds, engaging the neck muscles.
Slowly lower your head back to the mat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: neck resistance press
description: The neck resistance press is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck muscles using isometric contraction. It involves pressing the head against the resistance of the hand in multiple directions to strengthen the neck.
instructions: Sit upright with your back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Place your palm flat against your forehead.
Press your head forward into your palm while resisting the movement with your hand.
Hold the isometric contraction for 5–10 seconds.
Release and repeat, alternating the hand placement to the back of the head and each side.
Perform the desired number of repetitions in each direction.

name: neck tightener
description: The neck tightener is a bodyweight exercise targeting the platysma and neck muscles. It combines a head tilt with a chewing motion to tighten and tone the muscles along the front of the neck.
instructions: Sit upright with your back straight.
Tilt your head back until you are looking at the ceiling.
Open and close your mouth in a chewing motion while in this position, engaging the neck and chin muscles.
Continue for 20–30 seconds.
Return your head to the neutral position and rest briefly.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.

name: neck tilt hold
description: The neck tilt hold is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and trapezius muscles. It involves holding a lateral neck tilt to stretch and lengthen the muscles on the side of the neck.
instructions: Sit upright with your shoulders relaxed and spine straight.
Slowly tilt your head to the right, bringing your right ear toward your right shoulder.
Hold the tilted position for 15–20 seconds, feeling the stretch on the left side of the neck.
Slowly return to the neutral position.
Repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: nose shortening
description: Nose shortening is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the nasalis and muscles surrounding the nose. It involves pressing the nose tip upward with a finger while contracting the muscles downward to tone the nasal area.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Place your index finger on the tip of your nose, applying very light upward pressure.
Flex your nose downward using the muscles above the upper lip, working against your finger.
Hold the contracted position for 3–5 seconds.
Release and return to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: nostril flare control
description: Nostril flare control is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the nasalis muscle. It involves alternating between flaring and contracting the nostrils to improve muscular control and tone the nose area.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed and breathing normally.
Focus on the muscles around your nostrils and attempt to flare them outward.
Hold the flared position for 3–5 seconds.
Slowly contract the nostrils back inward as much as possible.
Hold the contracted position for 3–5 seconds.
Repeat the flare and contract cycle for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0957 - nostril flare control.mp4

name: platysma lift
description: The platysma lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the platysma muscle along the front of the neck. It involves tensing and lifting the platysma bands to tone and firm the neck and lower face.
instructions: Sit upright with your spine straight and shoulders pulled back.
Tilt your head back slightly and pull the corners of your mouth downward and outward.
Tense the muscles along the front of your neck so the platysma bands become visible.
Hold the tension for 5 seconds.
Slowly release and return to a neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: puffy cheeks hold
description: The puffy cheeks hold is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the cheek and buccinator muscles. It involves holding air in the puffed cheeks and transferring it side to side to tone and strengthen the mid-face muscles.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed.
Inhale deeply through your nose and fill both cheeks with air.
Hold the air in your puffed cheeks for 10 seconds without releasing.
Transfer the air from one cheek to the other by pressing one cheek inward.
Hold on each side for 3 seconds before releasing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0959 - puffy cheeks hold.mp4

name: say vowels
description: Say vowels is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the lip, jaw, and cheek muscles. It involves exaggerated vocalization of vowel sounds to engage and tone multiple muscle groups across the face.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Open your mouth wide and slowly and exaggeratedly say each vowel: A, E, I, O, U.
Exaggerate the lip and jaw movements for each sound to fully engage the facial muscles.
Pause briefly between each vowel to feel the muscle engagement.
Repeat the vowel sequence for the desired number of repetitions.

name: scalp massage
description: The scalp massage is a bodyweight exercise targeting the scalp and frontalis muscles. It involves applying circular pressure across the scalp to improve circulation, relieve tension, and promote relaxation.
instructions: Sit comfortably with your spine straight.
Place all fingertips on your scalp and apply gentle but firm pressure.
Move your fingertips in small circular motions across the scalp, starting from the hairline and working toward the crown.
Continue the circular massage over the sides and back of the scalp.
Repeat the full circuit for 30–60 seconds.

name: smile resistance hold
description: The smile resistance hold is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the zygomaticus and cheek muscles. It uses finger resistance against a wide smile to strengthen and tone the muscles of the mid and lower face.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Place your index fingers at the outer corners of your mouth.
Smile as wide as possible while pressing your fingertips inward to resist the movement.
Hold the smile against the resistance for 5 seconds.
Slowly relax back to a neutral expression.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0962 - smile resistance hold.mp4

name: surprise look (wide eyes lift)
description: The surprise look (wide eyes lift) is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the frontalis and orbicularis oculi muscles. It involves opening the eyes as wide as possible to strengthen and tone the muscles of the upper face.
instructions: Sit upright with your face in a neutral, relaxed expression.
Open your eyes as wide as possible while simultaneously raising your eyebrows.
Hold the wide-eyed expression for 5 seconds without wrinkling your forehead.
Slowly relax your eyes and eyebrows back to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0963 - surprise look (wide eyes lift).mp4

name: temple press
description: The temple press is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the temporalis muscle. It involves placing fingers at the temples and clenching the teeth to feel and strengthen the muscles along the side of the head.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Place the fingertips of both hands on your temples, applying gentle pressure.
Slowly clench your back teeth together to engage the temporalis muscles beneath your fingertips.
Hold the clenched position for 5 seconds.
Slowly release the clench and relax the muscles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: tongue circle
description: The tongue circle is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the tongue and surrounding facial muscles. It involves rotating the tongue in circular motions inside the mouth to tone and strengthen the muscles of the jaw and cheeks.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth closed.
Press your tongue against the inside of your cheek and move it in a slow, circular motion.
Complete a full circle along the inside of your upper lip, then across the upper teeth, down the inside of the opposite cheek, and back.
Perform 5 circles in one direction, then reverse.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: tongue rotate side to side stretch
description: The tongue rotate side to side stretch is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the tongue and cheek muscles. It involves moving the extended tongue from side to side to stretch and strengthen the muscles of the mouth.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth slightly open.
Extend your tongue outward from your mouth.
Slowly move your tongue to the right corner of your lips, then across to the left corner.
Keep the movement controlled and deliberate, feeling the stretch in the tongue and cheek muscles.
Continue alternating side to side for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/stretching/beginner/0966 - tongue rotate side to side stretch.mp4

name: tongue stretch forward
description: The tongue stretch forward is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the tongue and jaw muscles. It involves extending the tongue downward as far as possible to stretch and tone the muscles of the mouth and lower face.
instructions: Sit upright with your spine straight.
Open your mouth wide and extend your tongue outward as far as possible toward your chin.
Hold the fully extended position for 5 seconds.
Slowly retract your tongue and close your mouth.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/stretching/beginner/0967 - tongue stretch forward.mp4

name: tongue to roof
description: The tongue to roof is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the tongue and jaw muscles. It involves pressing the tongue firmly against the palate to strengthen the tongue and promote jaw alignment.
instructions: Sit upright with your mouth gently closed.
Press the tip of your tongue firmly against the roof of your mouth just behind the upper front teeth.
Apply as much upward pressure as possible with your tongue.
Hold the position for 5–10 seconds.
Release and relax the tongue.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0968 - tongue to roof.mp4

name: tongue touch to hard palate
description: The tongue touch to hard palate is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the tongue and palate muscles. It involves pressing the tongue flat against the hard palate to improve muscle control and tone the oral region.
instructions: Sit upright with your back straight and mouth gently closed.
Open your mouth slightly and press the tongue flat against the hard palate at the roof of your mouth.
Apply firm, even pressure with the full surface of the tongue.
Hold the position for 5 seconds.
Release the tongue and relax.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0969 - tongue touch to hard palate.mp4

name: upward neck kiss
description: The upward neck kiss is a bodyweight exercise targeting the neck and platysma muscles. It involves tilting the head back and puckering the lips upward to tone the muscles under the chin and along the front of the neck.
instructions: Sit upright with your shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head back slowly until you are looking at the ceiling.
Pucker your lips as if kissing upward toward the ceiling.
Hold the kissing position for 5 seconds, feeling the contraction under the chin and along the neck.
Slowly return to the neutral position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: vertical forehead lift
description: The vertical forehead lift is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the frontalis muscle. It uses finger resistance to work the forehead muscles vertically, helping to tone the brow area and reduce glabellar lines.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed.
Place both index fingers vertically between your brows, applying light inward pressure.
Using your forehead muscles, try to push your brows apart and upward against the resistance of your fingers.
Hold the lifted position for 5 seconds.
Slowly release and relax the forehead.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/rehabilitation/beginner/0971 - vertical forehead lift.mp4

name: wide eye opener
description: The wide eye opener is a bodyweight facial exercise targeting the orbicularis oculi and surrounding eye muscles. It involves opening the eyes as wide as possible without engaging the forehead to isolate and strengthen the muscles around the eyes.
instructions: Sit upright with your face relaxed and eyes in a neutral position.
Without raising your eyebrows, open your eyes as wide as possible using only the muscles around the eyes.
Hold the wide-open position for 5 seconds.
Slowly relax the eye muscles back to a normal position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Face Exercise/neck/facial muscles/strength/beginner/0972 - wide eye opener.mp4

name: seated interlaced back
description: The seated interlaced back is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated in a chair. It involves interlacing the fingers and pressing the arms forward while rounding the upper back to stretch the muscles between the shoulder blades, promoting upper back mobility and tension relief.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Interlace your fingers and extend both arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height, palms facing outward.
Round your upper back and push your palms forward, separating your shoulder blades and feeling a stretch through the middle of your back.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Release and return to the upright starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated interlaced fingers behind back 1
description: The seated interlaced fingers behind back 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids. It involves clasping the hands behind the back and drawing them downward to open the chest and engage the upper back muscles, counteracting the forward posture common with desk work.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Reach both arms behind your back and interlace your fingers together with your palms facing inward.
Gently pull your clasped hands downward and slightly away from your back, lifting your chest and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily and feeling the stretch across your chest and upper back.
Release your hands and return to the starting position. Repeat as desired.

name: seated interlaced fingers behind back stretch
description: The seated interlaced fingers behind back stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves interlacing the hands behind the body and extending the arms to open the chest and stretch the pectoral and upper back muscles simultaneously.
instructions: Sit tall in your chair with your core engaged and your feet flat on the floor.
Bring both arms behind your back and interlace your fingers with your palms facing inward.
Straighten your arms fully and gently pull them downward while drawing your shoulder blades firmly together and lifting your chest upward.
Hold this stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply into your chest.
Release the hands and relax back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/rhomboids/stretching/beginner/0704 - seated interlaced fingers behind back stretch.mp4

name: seated interlaced fingers behind back version 1
description: The seated interlaced fingers behind back version 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids and chest, performed while seated. It involves clasping the hands behind the back and fully extending the arms with a slight head tilt to achieve a deeper chest and upper back opening.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your spine tall and your feet flat on the ground.
Clasp both hands behind your lower back with fingers interlaced and palms facing each other.
Extend your arms fully, gently pressing your knuckles toward the floor while lifting your sternum toward the ceiling.
Tilt your head slightly back to deepen the chest opening and hold for 20-30 seconds.
Lower your chin, release your hands, and return to the starting position. Repeat as needed.
video_path: Office/back/rhomboids/stretching/beginner/0705 - seated interlaced fingers behind back version 1.mp4

name: seated shoulder blade clock
description: The seated shoulder blade clock is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves moving the shoulder blades in a controlled circular pattern to improve upper back mobility, reduce tension, and enhance scapular stability.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Engage your core and begin moving your shoulder blades in a slow circular motion, as if tracing the face of a clock.
Move both shoulder blades upward toward 12 o'clock, then out to 3 o'clock, down to 6 o'clock, and back through 9 o'clock.
Complete the full circular motion slowly and with control, feeling the muscles around your shoulder blades engage throughout.
Reverse the direction and repeat the clock movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated shoulder butterfly stretch
description: The seated shoulder butterfly stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated. It involves placing the fingertips on the shoulders and alternating between bringing the elbows together and pulling them back to stretch and mobilize the shoulder girdle and upper back.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Place your fingertips on your shoulders with your elbows pointing out to the sides at shoulder height.
Slowly bring your elbows together in front of your chest, rounding your upper back slightly.
Then pull your elbows back and wide, squeezing your shoulder blades together as you open your chest.
Continue alternating between the closed and open positions in a smooth, controlled motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated arms stretch
description: The seated arms stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the triceps, performed while seated in a chair. It involves raising the arms overhead and bending the elbows to stretch the back of the upper arms, promoting flexibility in the triceps and shoulder region.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Raise both arms overhead and bend your elbows, lowering your hands behind your head.
Use one hand to gently press the opposite elbow downward and backward, deepening the triceps stretch.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, keeping your spine tall and your head neutral.
Switch arms and repeat on the other side. Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated arms up down stretch
description: The seated arms up down stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated. It involves raising both arms overhead and lowering them back down in a controlled motion to stretch and mobilize the shoulder girdle, trapezius, and triceps.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your core engaged.
Raise both arms straight overhead, reaching as high as possible and feeling a full stretch along the sides of your body.
Hold the raised position briefly, then slowly lower your arms back down to your sides.
At the lowered position, gently press your arms down and slightly behind your hips to feel a stretch through your anterior shoulder.
Repeat the upward and downward movement in a fluid, controlled motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated cat cow stretch
description: The seated cat cow stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated in a chair. It involves alternating between arching and rounding the spine in coordination with the breath, promoting spinal flexibility, core engagement, and tension relief in the back and neck.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your knees.
Inhale deeply and arch your lower back, pushing your chest forward and lifting your head slightly to enter the cow position.
Exhale and round your spine, tucking your chin toward your chest and drawing your navel toward your spine to enter the cat position.
Continue flowing between the two positions in sync with your breathing, moving slowly and with control.
Repeat for 8-10 cycles or the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0710 - seated cat cow stretch.mp4

name: seated chair forward fold
description: The seated chair forward fold is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated. It involves hinging forward from the hips to drape the torso over the thighs, releasing tension in the lower back, hamstrings, and glutes.
instructions: Sit near the edge of your chair with your feet flat on the floor, slightly wider than hip-width apart.
Inhale to lengthen your spine, then exhale and slowly hinge forward from your hips, lowering your torso toward your thighs.
Let your hands hang down toward the floor or rest them lightly on your shins.
Relax your head and neck, allowing your upper body to drape forward and feeling a deep stretch through your lower back.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply, then slowly roll back up to seated. Repeat as desired.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0711 - seated chair forward fold.mp4

name: seated chest opener
description: The seated chest opener is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed while seated in a chair. It involves clasping the hands behind the back and drawing the shoulder blades together to open the chest, counteracting the effects of prolonged sitting and forward posture.
instructions: Sit upright at the edge of your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your spine tall.
Clasp your hands behind your back or interlace your fingers at your lower back.
Gently draw your shoulder blades together and lift your chest upward, feeling an opening sensation across the front of your chest.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply into your chest.
Release your hands and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated deep breath relax
description: The seated deep breath relax is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the abdominal breathing muscles, performed while seated. It involves slow, deliberate diaphragmatic breathing to reduce stress, lower muscular tension, and promote mental and physical relaxation.
instructions: Sit comfortably in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your thighs.
Close your eyes and bring your attention to your natural breathing rhythm.
Inhale slowly through your nose for a count of four, allowing your belly to expand fully outward.
Hold the breath briefly at the top, then exhale slowly through your mouth for a count of six, releasing all tension from your body.
Continue this deep breathing pattern for 5-10 repetitions, allowing your body to relax progressively with each exhale.

name: seated dynamic chest stretch
description: The seated dynamic chest stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed while seated. It involves sweeping the arms forward and back in a dynamic motion to open the chest, improve shoulder mobility, and reduce upper body tension.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your core engaged.
Extend both arms out to your sides at shoulder height with your palms facing forward.
Sweep your arms forward and cross them in front of your chest in a hugging motion.
Sweep them back to the starting position, squeezing your shoulder blades together as you open your chest.
Continue the dynamic sweeping motion in a controlled manner for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining an upright posture throughout.

name: seated dynamic stretch
description: The seated dynamic stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated in a chair. It involves rhythmically rounding and extending the spine in sync with the breath to improve spinal mobility, release tension, and gently engage the core.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your knees.
Inhale and lengthen your spine, sitting as tall as possible.
Exhale and slowly round your back, drawing your shoulders forward and pulling your navel toward your spine.
Inhale again and return to the tall seated position, extending through the crown of your head.
Continue this dynamic movement in coordination with your breath for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated eyes close deep breath
description: The seated eyes close deep breath is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the diaphragm and abdominal muscles, performed with eyes closed while seated. It involves deliberate nasal breathing with focused attention on the belly to encourage relaxation, improve breath awareness, and reduce stress.
instructions: Sit comfortably in your chair with your back supported and your feet flat on the floor.
Gently close your eyes and place your hands on your abdomen to feel the movement of your breath.
Inhale slowly through your nose, expanding your belly outward against your hands for a count of four.
Exhale gently through your nose or mouth for a count of six, allowing your abdomen to fall inward and release all tension.
Continue breathing in this manner for 5-10 cycles, focusing your attention fully on the sensation of each breath.

name: seated fast leg swing
description: The seated fast leg swing is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while seated. It involves swinging one leg forward and backward at a brisk pace to dynamically warm up the hip joint, improve range of motion, and engage the upper leg muscles.
instructions: Sit near the edge of your chair with your back straight and your core lightly engaged.
Place both hands on the sides of the chair seat for stability.
Swing one leg forward and backward in a controlled but brisk motion, keeping the knee soft.
Allow the hip joint to move freely through its range of motion, increasing the pace slightly while maintaining control.
Continue for the desired number of swings, then switch legs and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/beginner/0717 - seated fast leg swing.mp4

name: seated forward fold hips lift
description: The seated forward fold hips lift is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated. It combines a forward fold with a hip lift to simultaneously stretch and activate the lower back, glutes, and hamstrings, providing effective relief from spinal tension.
instructions: Sit at the edge of your chair with your feet flat on the floor and slightly wider than hip-width apart.
Hinge forward from your hips, lowering your torso toward your thighs with your hands reaching toward the floor.
From the folded position, engage your glutes and press through your feet to lift your hips slightly upward off the seat.
Hold the lifted position for a moment, feeling the stretch in your lower back and hamstrings.
Lower your hips back to the seat, sit up slowly, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0718 - seated forward fold hips lift.mp4

name: seated glute squeeze
description: The seated glute squeeze is an isometric bodyweight strength exercise targeting the gluteal muscles, performed while seated in a chair. It involves contracting the glutes as firmly as possible and holding the contraction to strengthen and activate the glute muscles without any external movement.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Place your hands lightly on your thighs and engage your core.
Squeeze both glute muscles as tightly as possible, holding the contraction for 5-10 seconds.
Release the squeeze completely and rest briefly before initiating the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on a strong contraction with each squeeze.

name: seated knee extension
description: The seated knee extension is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed while seated. It involves extending one leg to a straight position and holding briefly to strengthen the quads and improve knee joint stability, making it ideal for rehabilitation and office-based conditioning.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Engage your core and slowly lift one foot off the ground, extending your leg out straight in front of you.
Hold the extended position for 2-3 seconds, squeezing your quadriceps firmly at the top.
Slowly lower your foot back toward the floor without fully resting it between repetitions.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

name: seated leg raise straight
description: The seated leg raise straight is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed while seated in a chair. It involves lifting one straight leg to a horizontal position and holding briefly to engage the quads, hip flexors, and core muscles.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your thighs.
Engage your core and straighten one leg, lifting it up until it is approximately parallel to the floor.
Hold the raised position for 2-3 seconds, keeping your foot flexed and your quadriceps engaged.
Slowly lower your leg back toward the floor without fully resting it.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.
video_path: Office/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0721 - seated leg raise straight.mp4

name: seated leg swing
description: The seated leg swing is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while seated. It involves swinging one leg in a controlled pendulum motion to gently mobilize the hip joint, warm up the upper leg muscles, and improve flexibility through dynamic movement.
instructions: Sit at the edge of your chair with your back straight and your hands placed on the sides of the seat for support.
Allow one leg to hang freely with your knee slightly bent and your foot relaxed.
Gently swing the leg forward and backward in a pendulum motion, keeping the movement slow and controlled.
Allow the hip joint to move freely through its natural range of motion with each swing.
Continue for the desired number of swings, then switch to the other leg and repeat.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/beginner/0722 - seated leg swing.mp4

name: seated lift both legs
description: The seated lift both legs is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while seated in a chair. It involves simultaneously lifting both legs off the ground and holding the position to engage the hip flexors, abdominal muscles, and quadriceps.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands gripping the sides of the seat.
Engage your core and press down through your hands for stability.
Simultaneously lift both feet off the ground, bending your knees at approximately a 90-degree angle.
Hold both legs raised for 2-5 seconds, keeping your back straight and your core tight.
Slowly lower both feet back to the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/0723 - seated lift both legs.mp4

name: seated march
description: The seated march is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while seated in a chair. It involves alternating knee lifts in a marching motion to elevate the heart rate, activate the hip flexors, and engage the core without leaving the seated position.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Engage your core and begin lifting your right knee upward toward your chest in a marching motion.
Lower your right foot back to the floor and simultaneously lift your left knee to the same height.
Continue alternating legs in a brisk marching rhythm, keeping your back straight and your movements controlled.
March for the desired duration or number of repetitions on each leg.

name: seated neck and back stretch
description: The seated neck and back stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the neck muscles, performed while seated. It combines a chin tuck with an upper back rounding motion to simultaneously release tension in the neck, trapezius, and upper back.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your thighs.
Slowly lower your chin toward your chest, feeling a stretch along the back of your neck.
From this position, gently round your entire upper back, deepening the combined neck and back stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and allowing gravity to deepen the stretch with each exhale.
Slowly lift your head and return to an upright position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/neck/neck/stretching/beginner/0725 - seated neck and back stretch.mp4

name: seated neck lift up down
description: The seated neck lift up down is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the neck muscles, performed while seated. It involves slowly lowering and lifting the head in a controlled motion to stretch the anterior and posterior neck muscles and release built-up tension from prolonged screen use.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your shoulders relaxed.
Slowly lower your chin toward your chest, feeling a gentle stretch along the back of your neck.
Hold the lowered position for a brief moment, then slowly lift your head and tilt it gently backward, looking toward the ceiling.
Hold the extended position briefly, then return your head to the neutral starting position.
Repeat the upward and downward movement for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/neck/neck/stretching/beginner/0726 - seated neck lift up down.mp4

name: seated neck side to side rotate
description: The seated neck side to side rotate is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the neck muscles, performed while seated. It involves slowly rotating the head from side to side to improve cervical range of motion, reduce stiffness, and release tension in the neck and upper shoulder region.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your shoulders relaxed and your feet flat on the floor.
Slowly rotate your head to the right, bringing your chin toward your right shoulder as far as comfortably possible.
Hold for a brief moment, then slowly rotate your head to the left, bringing your chin toward your left shoulder.
Continue rotating side to side in a slow, controlled manner without forcing the range of motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: Office/neck/neck/mobility/beginner/0727 - seated neck side to side rotate.mp4

name: seated neck stretch
description: The seated neck stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the neck muscles, performed while seated. It involves tilting the head to one side to stretch the lateral neck and upper trapezius, effectively relieving tension associated with prolonged desk work.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Tilt your head to the right, bringing your right ear toward your right shoulder.
Optionally, place your right hand gently on the left side of your head to apply light pressure and deepen the stretch.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull along the left side of your neck.
Return your head to center and repeat on the left side. Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated overhead arms stretch
description: The seated overhead arms stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated. It involves raising both arms overhead and reaching upward to stretch the shoulders, triceps, and trapezius while promoting upper body mobility and spinal lengthening.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your core engaged.
Raise both arms straight overhead with your palms facing each other and your fingers extended.
Reach as high as possible, feeling a full stretch through your arms, shoulders, and the sides of your torso.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and lengthening your spine with each inhale.
Slowly lower your arms back to your sides and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated overhead hand
description: The seated overhead hand is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the triceps, performed while seated in a chair. It involves raising one arm overhead and bending the elbow to stretch the back of the upper arm, with gentle assistance from the opposite hand to deepen the stretch.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Raise one arm overhead and bend the elbow, lowering your hand toward the back of the same shoulder.
Use your opposite hand to gently press the raised elbow further back and downward, deepening the triceps stretch.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, keeping your spine tall and your head neutral.
Release and switch arms, repeating the stretch on the other side for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated scapular squeeze
description: The seated scapular squeeze is an isometric bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves drawing the shoulder blades firmly together and holding the contraction to strengthen the upper back muscles and improve postural alignment.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms bent at 90 degrees by your sides.
Draw your shoulder blades toward each other as firmly as possible, as if trying to hold a pencil between them.
Hold the contraction for 5-10 seconds, keeping your chest lifted and your core engaged.
Release the squeeze completely and allow your shoulder blades to return to the resting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated self hug
description: The seated self hug is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves wrapping the arms across the chest in a self-embrace to stretch the upper back muscles, improve thoracic mobility, and relieve tension between the shoulder blades.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Wrap both arms across your chest, placing each hand on the opposite shoulder as if hugging yourself.
Gently pull your arms inward, rounding your upper back and feeling a stretch between your shoulder blades.
Hold the hugged position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and allowing your upper back to relax.
Release your arms and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated shoulder circle
