description: The seated shoulder circle is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated. It involves rotating the shoulders in full circular motions in both directions to warm up the shoulder girdle, improve mobility, and release tension in the neck and upper back.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Begin rotating both shoulders simultaneously in a forward circular motion, lifting them up toward your ears and rolling them forward and down.
Complete 5-8 forward rotations in a slow, controlled manner, feeling full range of motion with each circle.
Reverse the direction and perform 5-8 backward circles, rolling the shoulders back and down.
Repeat for the desired number of cycles, maintaining a relaxed and upright posture throughout.

name: seated shoulder rolls up down
description: The seated shoulder rolls up down is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the trapezius, performed while seated. It involves raising the shoulders toward the ears and rolling them back and down in a deliberate motion to release tension, improve shoulder mobility, and engage the upper back muscles.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms relaxed by your sides.
Slowly raise both shoulders up toward your ears as high as possible, feeling a shrug-like contraction.
Hold the raised position for a brief moment, then roll the shoulders back and press them firmly downward.
Pause at the lowered position to feel the muscles engage before initiating the next repetition.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions, moving slowly and with full range of motion.

name: seated shoulder rotate
description: The seated shoulder rotate is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the deltoids, performed while seated. It involves rotating the extended arms in circular motions to warm up the shoulder joints, improve rotator cuff mobility, and release tension in the upper body.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height.
With your arms straight, rotate them forward in small circles, gradually increasing the size of the rotation.
After 10-15 forward rotations, reverse the direction and rotate your arms backward.
Keep your core engaged and your posture upright throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions in each direction.

name: seated shoulder shurg
description: The seated shoulder shurg is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the trapezius muscles, performed while seated. It involves lifting both shoulders upward toward the ears in a controlled shrugging motion to activate the upper trapezius, neck stabilizers, and deltoids.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Engage your core and simultaneously raise both shoulders as high as possible toward your ears.
Hold the elevated position for 2-3 seconds, squeezing your upper trapezius muscles.
Slowly release and lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated side stretch
description: The seated side stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the obliques, performed while seated in a chair. It involves leaning the torso to one side with one arm raised overhead to stretch the lateral torso, engaging the obliques, lower back, and abdominal muscles.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your spine tall.
Place one hand on the side of your chair seat for stability and raise the opposite arm straight overhead.
Slowly lean your torso to the side of the grounded hand, reaching your raised arm up and over in an arc.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply into the lengthened side.
Return to the upright position and repeat on the other side. Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated side to side toe touch
description: The seated side to side toe touch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the obliques, performed while seated. It involves rotating the torso and reaching toward the opposite foot to stretch and engage the obliques, abdominals, and hamstrings in a dynamic cross-body movement.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height.
Rotate your torso to the right and reach your left hand down toward your right foot or ankle.
Hold for a brief moment, feeling the stretch through your left oblique and the back of your right leg.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side, rotating left and reaching your right hand toward your left foot.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/waist/obliques/stretching/beginner/0738 - seated side to side toe touch.mp4

name: seated spine rocking
description: The seated spine rocking is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated at the edge of a chair. It involves rhythmically tilting the pelvis forward and backward to mobilize the lumbar spine, release tension, and gently engage the abdominal and glute muscles.
instructions: Sit upright at the edge of your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your knees.
Begin by tilting your pelvis forward, arching your lower back slightly and sitting up very tall.
Then tilt your pelvis backward, rounding your lower back and tucking your tailbone under.
Continue rocking forward and backward through the pelvis in a slow, rhythmic motion.
Repeat for 10-15 rocking cycles or the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated thoracic twist version 1
description: The seated thoracic twist version 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the thoracic region of the spine, performed while seated. It involves rotating the upper body with the hands clasped behind the head to stretch the spinal rotators, obliques, and trapezius.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands clasped behind your head.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your upper body to the right, bringing your right elbow back as far as comfortably possible.
Hold the twisted position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the stretch through your thoracic spine.
Return to the center and repeat the rotation to the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0740 - seated thoracic twist version 1.mp4

name: seated thoracic twist
description: The seated thoracic twist is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the thoracic spine and lower back, performed while seated. It involves rotating the upper body side to side with the arms crossed to improve thoracic mobility, stretch the spinal rotators, and engage the obliques.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Cross your arms over your chest, placing each hand on the opposite shoulder.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your upper body to the right as far as comfortably possible, keeping your hips facing forward.
Hold the twisted position for 2-3 seconds, then rotate back through center and continue to the left.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated toe raise
description: The seated toe raise is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the tibialis anterior, performed while seated. It involves lifting the front of the feet off the ground while keeping the heels planted to strengthen the shin muscles and improve ankle mobility and stability.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Keeping your heels firmly planted on the ground, lift the front of both feet upward as high as possible.
Hold the raised position for 2-3 seconds, feeling the contraction in the muscles along the front of your shins.
Slowly lower your feet back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/lower legs/tibialis anterior/rehabilitation/beginner/0742 - seated toe raise.mp4

name: seated torso stretch
description: The seated torso stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated. It involves reaching the arms forward while rounding the entire back to stretch the spinal column and engage the core, providing effective relief from the compression of prolonged sitting.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your hands resting on your thighs.
Inhale and lengthen your spine, sitting as tall as possible.
Exhale and reach both arms forward at shoulder height, rounding your upper and lower back.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch through your entire back.
Return to the upright position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated torso twist
description: The seated torso twist is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the obliques, performed while seated. It involves rotating the torso side to side with the arms extended to engage the obliques, improve spinal rotation, and release tension in the lower back.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms extended in front of you at shoulder height.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your torso to the right, following your arms as they move to the right side.
Hold the rotated position for 2-3 seconds, keeping your hips square and facing forward.
Return to the center and rotate to the left side.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated upper back and shoulder stretch
description: The seated upper back and shoulder stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves clasping the hands and pushing them forward while rounding the upper back to stretch the muscles between the shoulder blades and the rear deltoids.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Extend both arms forward at shoulder height and clasp your hands together with your palms facing outward.
Round your upper back and push your clasped hands forward, separating your shoulder blades as wide as possible.
Simultaneously allow your shoulders to round forward slightly, deepening the stretch across the upper back.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply, then release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/rhomboids/stretching/beginner/0745 - seated upper back and shoulder stretch.mp4

name: seated upper back stretch version 1
description: The seated upper back stretch version 1 is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated. It involves reaching the arms forward with interlaced fingers and a lowered chin to stretch the upper back and neck simultaneously, providing relief from postural tension.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your spine tall.
Reach both arms forward at chest height with your fingers interlaced and palms facing forward.
Push your hands forward as you round your upper back, drawing your shoulder blades apart.
Lower your chin slightly toward your chest to add a gentle neck stretch to the movement.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated upper back stretch
description: The seated upper back stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed while seated in a chair. It involves wrapping the arms around the torso to round the upper back and stretch the muscles between the shoulder blades, offering effective relief from upper back tension.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor.
Wrap both arms around your torso in a self-hug and grasp opposite shoulders.
Round your upper back and pull your shoulders gently forward, feeling a stretch between and around your shoulder blades.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing evenly.
Release and return to the upright starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated upper body side to side stretch
description: The seated upper body side to side stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed while seated. It involves leaning the upper body to each side with one arm raised overhead to stretch the lateral torso, engaging the obliques, deltoids, and lower back muscles.
instructions: Sit upright in your chair with your feet flat on the floor and your arms relaxed at your sides.
Place one hand on your hip and raise the opposite arm straight overhead.
Lean your torso gently toward the side of the hand on your hip, creating a lateral stretch through your upper body.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing into the lengthened side.
Return to the upright position and repeat on the other side. Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0748 - seated upper body side to side stretch.mp4

name: desk close grip push ups
description: The desk close grip push up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the triceps, performed using a desk as an elevated surface. The close hand placement emphasizes the triceps over the pectoral muscles, making it an effective upper arm exercise suitable for office environments.
instructions: Stand facing a desk or sturdy surface and place your hands on the edge closer than shoulder-width apart with fingers pointing forward.
Walk your feet back until your body forms a straight diagonal line from head to heels, with your core engaged.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the desk, keeping your elbows tucked close to your sides throughout the movement.
Pause when your chest nearly touches the desk surface, then press back up to the starting position by fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0749 - desk close grip push ups.mp4

name: desk half bent with straight hands raise stretch
description: The desk half bent with straight hands raise stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed in a hinged position at a desk. It involves raising the arms from a bent-over stance to stretch the lower back, hamstrings, and shoulders simultaneously.
instructions: Stand about one arm's length away from your desk with your feet hip-width apart.
Hinge forward at your hips until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor, resting your hands lightly on the desk surface.
Keep your back flat, your arms straight, and your head in a neutral position aligned with your spine.
From this position, lift both hands slightly off the desk and raise your arms forward, maintaining straight arms.
Hold the raised position for 10-20 seconds, then lower your hands back to the desk. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0750 - desk half bent with straight hands raise stretch.mp4

name: desk incline plank hold
description: The desk incline plank hold is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, performed using a desk as an elevated surface. It involves maintaining a rigid plank position with the hands elevated to engage the core, shoulders, and lower back in a modified and accessible variation.
instructions: Stand facing a desk and place both hands on the edge, shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet backward until your body forms a straight diagonal line from your head to your heels.
Engage your core, squeeze your glutes, and ensure your hips are aligned with your body, neither sagging nor raised.
Hold this incline plank position for the desired amount of time, breathing steadily throughout.
Walk your feet forward to return to the starting position. Rest and repeat as desired.

name: desk palm of hands stretch
description: The desk palm of hands stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the forearm muscles, performed at a desk. It involves pressing the palms flat against the desk surface and leaning gently forward to stretch the wrist flexors and forearm muscles, relieving tension from repetitive hand and typing movements.
instructions: Stand or sit in front of your desk and place both palms flat on the desk surface with your fingers pointing toward your body.
Gently lean your weight slightly forward, pressing your palms down and feeling the stretch across the inside of your forearms and wrists.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
To vary the stretch, rotate your hands so the fingers point outward and repeat the gentle lean.
Release and shake out your hands before repeating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/lower arms/forearms/stretching/beginner/0752 - desk palm of hands stretch.mp4

name: desk push ups standard
description: The desk push up standard is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed using a desk as an elevated surface. It involves the standard push up movement at a comfortable incline, engaging the chest, triceps, and deltoids while remaining accessible for all fitness levels.
instructions: Stand facing a desk and place both hands on the edge, shoulder-width apart with your fingers pointing forward.
Walk your feet backward until your body forms a straight diagonal line from your head to your heels.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the desk in a controlled manner, keeping your core tight and your body straight.
Pause when your chest is close to the desk surface, then push back up by fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a straight body line throughout.

name: desk push ups
description: The desk push up is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the pectoral muscles, performed using a desk as an inclined surface. It offers a beginner-friendly modification of the traditional push up, engaging the chest, triceps, and front deltoids while reducing the load compared to floor-based variations.
instructions: Stand facing a desk and place your hands on the edge slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step your feet back to create a comfortable incline angle with your body straight from head to heels.
Engage your core and bend your elbows to lower your chest toward the desk, keeping your body in a straight line.
Push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms fully.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: desk standing back stretch
description: The desk standing back stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed using a desk for support. It involves hinging at the hips with hands on the desk to create a deep spinal stretch, releasing tension in the lower back, trapezius, and shoulders.
instructions: Stand facing your desk with your feet hip-width apart and your arms extended at shoulder height.
Place both hands flat on the desk surface and step your feet backward until your arms are straight and your torso is roughly parallel to the floor.
Allow your head to drop between your arms and let gravity lengthen your spine, feeling a deep stretch through your back.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and relaxing further with each exhale.
Walk your feet forward and slowly roll back up to standing. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0755 - desk standing back stretch.mp4

name: desk standing leg stretch
description: The desk standing leg stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings, performed using a desk as an elevated surface. It involves placing one leg on the desk and hinging forward to stretch the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back, making it an effective standing flexibility exercise.
instructions: Stand facing your desk and extend one leg, placing your heel on the desk surface with your leg straight.
Stand tall with your other foot flat on the floor and your hands resting on the desk for support.
Gently hinge forward at the hips toward the raised leg, keeping your back flat and your chest lifted.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the pull along the back of the raised leg.
Carefully lower your leg and repeat on the other side. Continue for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0756 - desk standing leg stretch.mp4

name: desk upper body and arms stretch
description: The desk upper body and arms stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the rhomboids, performed using a desk as a prop. It involves reaching forward with both arms and drawing the upper body back to stretch the upper back, deltoids, and triceps simultaneously.
instructions: Stand facing your desk and reach both arms forward, placing your hands flat on the surface.
Walk your feet backward and lower your chest below the level of the desk, allowing your arms to stretch forward.
Round your upper back and actively pull your hips away from the desk, feeling a stretch through your upper back and arms.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and letting your shoulders relax.
Walk your feet forward and return to standing. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/back/rhomboids/stretching/beginner/0757 - desk upper body and arms stretch.mp4

name: desk upper body bent hands on desk
description: The desk upper body bent hands on desk is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back, performed using a desk for support. It involves hinging forward at the hips with hands on the desk to decompress and stretch the lumbar spine, hamstrings, and shoulder girdle.
instructions: Stand about one arm's length away from your desk with your feet hip-width apart.
Hinge forward at the hips and place both hands flat on the desk, allowing your upper body to form approximately a 90-degree angle with your legs.
Keep your back flat, your arms straight, and your knees soft.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply and allowing your lower back to decompress.
Slowly stand back up by engaging your core and returning to the upright position. Repeat as desired.
video_path: Office/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/0758 - desk upper body bent hands on desk.mp4

name: desk wrist stretch
description: The desk wrist stretch is a bodyweight rehabilitation exercise targeting the forearm muscles, performed at a desk. It involves bending the wrist in both flexion and extension directions to stretch the wrist flexors and extensors, providing relief from repetitive strain and improving wrist mobility.
instructions: Stand or sit in front of your desk and extend one arm forward with your palm facing upward.
Use your other hand to gently pull back on the fingers of the extended hand, bending the wrist downward.
Hold the stretch for 15-20 seconds, feeling the tension along the underside of your forearm.
Flip your hand so the palm faces downward and gently press the fingers toward the ground for the opposite stretch.
Hold for 15-20 seconds, then switch to the other hand and repeat.

name: supported glute kickback
description: The supported glute kickback is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the gluteal muscles, performed while holding a desk or chair for support. It involves extending one leg backward and upward in a controlled motion to isolate and strengthen the glutes and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand behind your desk or chair and hold onto it with both hands for support.
Shift your weight onto your left leg and engage your core.
Keeping your right knee slightly bent, swing your right leg backward and upward, squeezing your glute firmly at the top of the movement.
Hold the raised position briefly, then slowly return your right foot to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on the right leg, then switch to the left.

name: supported hamstring curl
description: The supported hamstring curl is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the hamstrings, performed while holding a desk or chair for balance. It involves curling one heel upward toward the glutes in a standing position to isolate and strengthen the hamstring muscles.
instructions: Stand behind your desk or chair and hold onto it lightly with both hands for balance.
Shift your weight onto your left leg, keeping it slightly bent at the knee.
Bend your right knee and curl your right heel upward toward your glutes as far as comfortably possible.
Hold the curled position for 1-2 seconds, squeezing your hamstring at the top.
Slowly lower your right foot back to the floor. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

name: supported hip hinge
description: The supported hip hinge is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower back, performed with light support from a desk or chair. It involves hinging forward at the hips with a neutral spine to stretch the hamstrings and glutes, reinforce proper hip mechanics, and decompress the lumbar spine.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart in front of your desk, holding lightly onto the surface for balance.
Engage your core and slightly soften your knees.
Hinge forward at your hips, pushing them backward while keeping your back flat and your spine neutral.
Lower your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch through your hamstrings and glutes.
Drive through your hips to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: supported mini squats
description: The supported mini squat is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed while holding a desk or chair for support. It involves performing a shallow squat movement to strengthen the quads, glutes, and hamstrings, making it ideal for beginners and those with limited mobility.
instructions: Stand facing your desk or chair with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold lightly onto the surface for support.
Engage your core and keep your chest lifted and your back straight.
Slowly bend your knees and lower your hips as if sitting into a shallow squat, going no deeper than a 45-degree knee bend.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press through your heels to return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: supported place jog running
description: The supported place jog running is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while holding a desk or chair for balance. It involves jogging in place with support to elevate the heart rate, engage the lower body muscles, and improve cardiovascular fitness in a confined space.
instructions: Stand behind your desk or chair and hold onto it lightly with both hands for support.
Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees alternately as if running, at a comfortable pace.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with each step, keeping the movement light and controlled.
Gradually increase your pace while maintaining proper form and keeping your core engaged.
Continue for the desired duration, then slow down gradually and come to a full stop.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hip flexors/cardio/beginner/0764 - supported place jog running.mp4

name: supported quad stretch
description: The supported quad stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed while holding a desk or chair for balance. It involves pulling one heel toward the glute while standing to stretch the front of the thigh and hip flexors, improving lower body flexibility.
instructions: Stand beside your desk or chair and hold onto it with one hand for balance.
Shift your weight onto your left foot and bend your right knee, bringing your right heel toward your right glute.
Grasp your right ankle with your right hand and gently pull your heel closer to your body.
Keep your knees together, your standing leg slightly bent, and your torso upright.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs and repeat on the other side.

name: supported side leg raise
description: The supported side leg raise is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the hip abductors, performed while holding a desk or chair for balance. It involves raising one leg out to the side in a controlled motion to strengthen the outer thigh, glutes, and hip stabilizers.
instructions: Stand beside your desk or chair and hold onto it with one hand for support.
Shift your weight onto your inner leg and engage your core.
Keeping your outer leg straight, slowly raise it out to the side as high as comfortably possible while maintaining an upright posture.
Hold the raised position for 1-2 seconds, then slowly lower your leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Office/upper legs/abductors/strength/beginner/0766 - supported side leg raise.mp4

name: supported side leg stretch
description: The supported side leg stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hip abductors, performed while holding a desk or chair for support. It involves shifting the hips laterally to stretch the outer thigh and hip region, improving abductor flexibility and relieving tension from prolonged sitting or standing.
instructions: Stand beside your desk or chair and hold onto it with one hand for support.
Cross your outer leg in front of the standing leg, resting it lightly on the ground.
Shift your hips away from the desk, leaning into the supported side to feel a stretch along the outer hip and thigh.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper legs/abductors/stretching/beginner/0767 - supported side leg stretch.mp4

name: supported single-leg balance
description: The supported single-leg balance is a bodyweight balance exercise targeting the calf muscles, performed with light support from a desk or chair. It involves standing on one leg to challenge the stabilizing muscles of the lower leg, engage the glutes and core, and develop proprioception and balance.
instructions: Stand behind your desk or chair and hold onto it lightly with both hands for initial stability.
Shift your weight onto one foot and slowly lift the other foot off the floor, bending the knee slightly.
Gradually reduce your grip on the support until you are balancing primarily on the standing leg.
Hold the single-leg balance for 20-30 seconds, focusing your gaze on a fixed point ahead to maintain stability.
Return both feet to the floor, rest briefly, then switch legs and repeat.

name: supported slightly jump squat
description: The supported slightly jump squat is a bodyweight plyometric exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed with light support from a desk or chair. It involves performing a small explosive jump from a shallow squat position to develop lower body power, engage the glutes and calves, and elevate the heart rate.
instructions: Stand facing your desk or chair with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding lightly onto the surface for support.
Engage your core and lower into a shallow squat by bending your knees to approximately 45 degrees.
From the squat position, push explosively through your feet and perform a small, controlled jump upward.
Land softly on the balls of your feet, absorbing the impact by bending your knees back into the squat position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining control and using the support as needed.
video_path: Office/upper legs/quads/plyometrics/beginner/0769 - supported slightly jump squat.mp4

name: supported standing march
description: The supported standing march is a bodyweight cardio exercise targeting the hip flexors, performed while holding a desk or chair for balance. It involves alternating knee lifts in a marching motion to improve cardiovascular fitness, activate the hip flexors, quads, and core while maintaining an upright, stable posture.
instructions: Stand behind your desk or chair with your feet together and hold onto the surface with both hands for support.
Engage your core and stand tall with your weight evenly distributed.
Lift your right knee upward toward hip height in a controlled marching motion.
Lower your right foot and immediately lift your left knee to the same height.
Continue alternating legs in a steady marching rhythm for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper legs/hip flexors/cardio/beginner/0770 - supported standing march.mp4

name: supported wide leg mini squat
description: The supported wide leg mini squat is a bodyweight strength exercise targeting the quadriceps, performed with support from a desk or chair. The wide stance places additional emphasis on the inner thighs and glutes alongside the quads, promoting hip mobility and inner thigh activation.
instructions: Stand facing your desk or chair with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your toes pointed slightly outward.
Hold lightly onto the surface for support and engage your core.
Slowly lower your hips toward the floor by bending both knees outward over your toes, going no deeper than a 45-degree knee bend.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, then press through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Office/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/0771 - supported wide leg mini squat.mp4

name: bicycle (side-lying)
description: The side-lying bicycle is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and obliques. Performed on the side, it involves a controlled pedaling motion of the legs that challenges core stability and hip flexibility.
instructions: Lie on your side with your body in a straight line and your head resting on your bottom arm.
Bend both knees to a 90-degree angle, stacking your hips and shoulders.
Engage your core and draw your top knee toward your chest while extending your bottom leg forward.
Reverse the movement, bringing the bottom knee in while extending the top leg back.
Continue alternating in a smooth pedaling motion for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: criss cross
description: The criss cross is a classic Pilates bodyweight exercise that targets the abdominal muscles, particularly the obliques. It involves a rotational movement pairing elbow-to-knee contact with leg extension, building core strength and rotational control.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed behind your head and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Lift your head, neck, and shoulders off the mat, engaging your core.
Rotate your right elbow toward your left knee while simultaneously extending your right leg straight out.
Return to center and immediately rotate your left elbow toward your right knee, extending your left leg.
Continue alternating sides in a controlled, rhythmic motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: double leg scissor
description: The double leg scissor is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves lowering and raising both legs simultaneously while keeping the core engaged and the lower back pressed to the mat.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended overhead and your legs raised to a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and press your lower back firmly into the mat.
Simultaneously lower both legs toward the ground in a controlled arc, stopping just above the mat.
Raise both legs back to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a stable pelvis throughout.

name: leg scissor
description: The leg scissor is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves alternating leg lowering movements performed with the head and shoulders lifted, challenging core control and lower body coordination.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended toward the ceiling.
Place your hands behind your head and lift your head and shoulders off the mat.
Lower one leg toward the ground in a controlled motion while keeping the other raised.
Switch legs in a scissoring motion, alternating smoothly.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions, keeping your core engaged and lower back pressed to the mat.

name: teaser pilate advanced hold
description: The Pilates teaser advanced hold is a challenging bodyweight exercise that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves balancing in a V-sit position for an extended hold, demanding exceptional core strength, stability, and spinal control.
instructions: Sit on the mat with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lean back slightly and lift both legs to a 45-degree angle, creating a V-shape with your torso.
Extend your arms forward, parallel to your legs.
Hold this position, maintaining a stable spine and engaged core.
While holding, gently pulse your arms up and down or reach forward to deepen the challenge.
Hold for the desired duration, then slowly lower and return to the starting position.

name: teaser pilate beginner
description: The Pilates teaser beginner is an introductory bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. It involves a controlled spinal roll-up from a bent-knee position, helping to build the core strength and spinal articulation needed for more advanced teaser variations.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Extend your arms overhead and engage your core.
Slowly roll up through your spine, reaching your arms forward as you lift your torso.
Stop when your torso is upright and your hands reach toward your knees.
Slowly roll back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: teaser pilate
description: The Pilates teaser is a fundamental bodyweight exercise that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves simultaneously rolling the torso up and lifting the legs into a V-sit position, requiring significant core strength and body control.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and arms overhead.
Engage your core and simultaneously lift your legs to a 45-degree angle and roll your torso up.
Reach your arms forward, parallel to your legs, balancing on your sit bones.
Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your torso and legs back to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: the hundred
description: The hundred is a foundational Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. It involves a rhythmic arm-pumping motion while holding a curl-up position, combining core strengthening with breath control for 100 total pulses.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent at a 90-degree angle and your feet lifted off the mat.
Lift your head, neck, and shoulders off the mat, extending your arms long by your sides.
Pump your arms up and down in small, controlled pulses.
Inhale for 5 pumps and exhale for 5 pumps, completing 10 full breath cycles for a total of 100 pumps.
Keep your core engaged and your lower back pressed to the mat throughout.

name: bird dog
description: The bird dog is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and core stabilizers. It involves simultaneously extending opposite arm and leg from an all-fours position, improving balance, coordination, and spinal stability.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Engage your core and keep your spine in a neutral position.
Simultaneously extend your right arm forward and your left leg back, keeping both parallel to the floor.
Hold for a moment at full extension, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side, extending your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: child's pose
description: Child's pose is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the lower back and glutes. It involves folding the torso forward over the knees in a kneeling position, providing a gentle, restorative stretch for the spine, hips, and thighs.
instructions: Begin kneeling on the mat with your knees hip-width apart and your toes together.
Sit back onto your heels and fold your torso forward over your thighs.
Extend your arms straight forward along the mat, palms facing down.
Allow your forehead to rest on the mat and relax your entire body.
Hold the position for the desired duration, breathing deeply to release tension in the back and hips.

name: cobra push ups
description: Cobra push ups are a bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles and triceps, performed from a low, cobra-like position. This movement combines a chest press with spinal extension, engaging the shoulders and lower back while building upper body strength.
instructions: Begin lying face down with your hands placed under your shoulders and elbows close to your body.
Press into your palms and extend your arms to lift your chest off the mat, keeping your hips low.
Pause at the top of the press, then slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest back toward the mat.
Before touching the mat, press back up again without fully lowering your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping your core engaged throughout.

name: mountain climber
description: The mountain climber is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves a running motion from a plank position, alternately driving the knees toward the chest, building core strength, hip flexibility, and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and drive your right knee toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs, driving your left knee forward while returning your right leg back.
Continue alternating legs at a brisk, controlled pace.
Keep your hips level and your shoulders stable throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: plank leg open
description: The plank leg open is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and glutes. Performed from a forearm plank, it involves lifting and opening each leg to the side, challenging core stability and hip abductor strength.
instructions: Start in a forearm plank position with your elbows under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line.
Engage your core and keep your hips level.
Lift one leg off the mat and open it out to the side as far as comfortable.
Hold for a moment, then return the leg to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rocker with open legs
description: Rocker with open legs is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and abdominal muscles. It involves rocking along the spine while holding the legs in a wide V-shape, developing spinal massage, core control, and hamstring flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the mat with your knees bent and feet lifted off the floor.
Hold your ankles and straighten your legs out into a V-shape, balancing on your sit bones.
Engage your core and round your lower back slightly.
Inhale and rock backward, rolling onto your upper back while maintaining the V-shape.
Exhale and rock back up to the balanced position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Pilates/back/lower back/strength/intermediate/1120 - rocker with open legs.mp4

name: star balance
description: The star balance is an advanced Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and obliques. Performed as an elevated side plank with the top arm and leg raised, it challenges lateral core stability, balance, and full-body coordination.
instructions: Begin in a side plank position with your supporting hand or forearm on the mat and your body in a straight line.
Stack your feet and lift your hips off the mat.
Raise your top arm toward the ceiling and lift your top leg, forming a star shape.
Hold this position, engaging your core and obliques to maintain balance.
Lower your arm and leg with control and return to the side plank position.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: swimming pilate
description: Swimming Pilate is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and glutes. Performed lying face down, it involves alternating arm and leg lifts in a flutter motion, strengthening the posterior chain and improving spinal extension and coordination.
instructions: Lie face down on the mat with your arms extended overhead and your legs straight.
Engage your core and lift your arms, chest, and legs slightly off the mat.
Alternately lift your right arm and left leg higher while lowering the opposite limbs.
Continue in a fluttering swimming motion, inhaling for 5 counts and exhaling for 5 counts.
Keep your gaze down to maintain a neutral neck position throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of breath cycles.

name: wall sit pilate
description: The wall sit Pilate is a bodyweight isometric exercise targeting the quadriceps and glutes. It involves holding a seated position with the back against a wall, building lower body endurance and core stability through sustained muscle engagement.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall and your feet hip-width apart, about two feet from the wall.
Slide your back down the wall until your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
Ensure your knees are directly above your ankles and your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Engage your core and press your back firmly into the wall.
Hold this position for the desired duration, breathing steadily throughout.

name: wall slide pilate
description: The wall slide Pilate is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and shoulder stabilizers. Performed against a wall, it involves sliding the arms up and down while maintaining full spinal contact, improving posture, shoulder mobility, and back muscle activation.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against a wall, feet hip-width apart and a few inches from the wall.
Press your lower back, upper back, and head against the wall.
Raise your arms to a goalpost position, pressing the backs of your hands and forearms against the wall.
Slowly slide your arms up the wall overhead, keeping contact throughout.
Slide your arms back down to the goalpost position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: clamshell legs up
description: The clamshell legs up is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteal muscles and hip abductors. This variation of the clamshell is performed with both feet elevated, increasing the demand on the lateral hip and obliques for stabilization.
instructions: Lie on your side with your hips and knees bent at a 45-degree angle and your feet stacked.
Lift both feet off the mat, keeping them together and level with your hips.
Keeping your feet lifted, rotate your top knee upward like a clamshell opening.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your knee back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

name: clamshell single leg raise
description: The clamshell single leg raise is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteal muscles and hip abductors. It combines the traditional clamshell motion with a leg extension at the top, increasing the range of motion and muscle engagement.
instructions: Lie on your side with your hips stacked and knees bent at a 45-degree angle.
Open your top knee upward like a clamshell, rotating from the hip.
At the top of the clamshell, extend your top leg straight out before closing.
Bend the knee back in and lower it to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1126 - clamshell single leg raise.mp4

name: clamshell
description: The clamshell is a foundational Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteal muscles and hip abductors. Performed side-lying with knees bent, it involves rotating the top knee upward while keeping the feet together, building hip stability and lateral glute strength.
instructions: Lie on your side with your knees bent at a 45-degree angle and your feet stacked together.
Keep your feet together and rotate your top knee upward as far as comfortable without rolling your hips.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your knee back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: double straight leg stretch
description: The double straight leg stretch is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings and abdominal muscles. It involves lowering and raising both straight legs while in a curl-up position, challenging core control and hamstring flexibility simultaneously.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs raised to a 90-degree angle.
Lift your head and shoulders off the mat and place your hands behind your head.
Engage your core and lower both legs toward the mat in a slow, controlled descent.
Stop when your lower back begins to lift, then raise both legs back to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a stable pelvis throughout.

name: frog pilate advanced
description: The Pilates frog advanced is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hip flexors. This variation incorporates an upper body curl-up and arm pulses while performing the frog leg press, significantly increasing abdominal engagement and overall challenge.
instructions: Lie on your back and lift both legs to a 90-degree angle, turning your toes out and heels together.
Lift your head and shoulders off the mat, engaging your core.
Press your legs out at a diagonal angle as you exhale, keeping the heels together.
Bend your knees and draw them back into the starting position.
Simultaneously pulse your arms forward to deepen the core engagement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: frog pilate
description: The Pilates frog is a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and inner thighs. It involves pressing the legs out in a diagonal direction from a frog-legged position, developing hip stability, glute activation, and inner thigh engagement.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent, feet together, and knees turned out to the sides in a frog position.
Engage your core and press your lower back gently into the mat.
Extend both legs out at a diagonal angle, squeezing your heels together.
Bend your knees and draw them back to the starting frog position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hamstring stretch single leg
description: The hamstring stretch single leg is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hamstrings. Performed supine, it involves gently extending one leg toward the ceiling while supporting it with the hands, providing a focused stretch for the posterior thigh and improving overall leg flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs extended.
Bend one knee and place a strap or your hands behind the thigh of the opposite leg.
Gently straighten the raised leg, extending it toward the ceiling.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling a lengthening sensation in the back of the leg.
Lower the leg and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1131 - hamstring stretch single leg.mp4

name: heel beat advanced
description: The heel beat advanced is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the calves and glutes. This variation incorporates a chest lift while rapidly beating the heels together in a prone position, increasing the demand on the posterior chain and core extensors.
instructions: Lie face down with your forehead resting on your hands and your legs extended.
Engage your glutes and lift both legs off the mat.
Turn your toes out slightly so your heels face each other.
Rapidly beat your heels together in a quick, percussive rhythm.
Simultaneously lift your chest off the mat to increase the challenge.
Continue for the desired number of beats or duration.

name: heel beat
description: Heel beat is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the calves and gluteal muscles. Performed in a prone position, it involves rapidly clicking the heels together with the legs lifted, strengthening the glutes, hamstrings, and lower leg muscles.
instructions: Lie face down on the mat with your forehead resting on your hands.
Engage your core and glutes, and lift both legs slightly off the mat.
Turn your toes out slightly so your heels face each other.
Rapidly beat your heels together in a small, controlled motion.
Continue for the desired number of beats or duration, breathing steadily.

name: hips flexor
description: The hip flexor stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the hip flexors and quadriceps. Performed in a kneeling lunge position, it involves shifting weight forward to lengthen the front of the hip, improving hip mobility and relieving tightness from prolonged sitting.
instructions: Kneel on one knee with your opposite foot forward, creating a 90-degree angle at both knees.
Engage your core and keep your torso upright.
Gently shift your weight forward, driving your front knee over your toes.
Feel a stretch along the front of the hip of the kneeling leg.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.

name: jackknife beginners
description: The jackknife beginners is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. This entry-level variation focuses on controlled leg lifting and a gentle hip raise, building the core strength needed for the full jackknife movement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms by your sides and legs extended.
Engage your core and slowly lift your legs to a 90-degree angle.
Press your palms into the mat for support as you gently lift your hips off the mat.
Slowly lower your hips and legs back to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: leg circle
description: The leg circle is a foundational Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors and stabilizing the core. It involves drawing controlled circles with one leg while keeping the pelvis and spine completely still, developing hip mobility and core stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with one leg extended on the mat and the other raised toward the ceiling.
Engage your core and stabilize your hips against the mat.
Draw a large circle with your raised leg, moving it across your body, down, out, and back up.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Switch legs and repeat on the other side.

name: legs flutter
description: Legs flutter is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors and abdominal muscles. It involves lying on the back and performing rapid, alternating flutter kicks with the legs held low to the ground, challenging core endurance and lower body coordination.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the mat.
Lift both legs a few inches off the mat.
Alternately raise and lower each leg in small, rapid flutter kicks.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or duration, breathing steadily.

name: lower body lifted
description: Lower body lifted is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. It involves raising and lowering both legs from a supine position, requiring deep core engagement and spinal stability to control the movement safely and effectively.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended by your sides, palms facing down.
Raise both legs to a 90-degree angle directly above your hips.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the mat.
Slowly lower both legs toward the mat without letting your lower back arch.
Raise them back to vertical and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lying double leg bent
description: Lying double leg bent is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hamstrings. It involves lifting both bent legs from a supine position and drawing the knees toward the chest, developing lower body awareness and core stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat, hip-width apart.
Engage your core and press your feet firmly into the mat.
Lift both feet off the mat, drawing your knees toward your chest.
Hold the position for a moment, then slowly lower your feet back to the mat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1139 - lying double leg bent.mp4

name: lying leg cycle
description: The lying leg cycle is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip flexors and quadriceps. Performed on the back, it simulates a cycling motion with both legs, building hip mobility, core endurance, and lower body coordination.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed beneath your hips for support.
Lift both legs off the mat and draw one knee in toward your chest.
Extend that leg out while drawing the opposite knee in, creating a smooth cycling motion.
Continue pedaling in a forward direction for the desired number of repetitions.
Reverse the direction and repeat.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/1140 - lying leg cycle.mp4

name: outer thigh lift
description: The outer thigh lift is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip abductors and glutes. Performed side-lying, it involves lifting the top leg to work the outer thigh and lateral hip, improving hip stability and lower body alignment.
instructions: Lie on your side with your body in a straight line and your head resting on your bottom arm.
Engage your core and keep your hips stacked.
Slowly raise your top leg as high as comfortable without rotating your hip.
Pause at the top, then lower your leg with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/hip abductors/strength/beginner/1141 - outer thigh lift.mp4

name: piriformis
description: The piriformis stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise targeting the deep gluteal muscles, particularly the piriformis. Performed supine in a figure-four position, it releases tension in the outer hip, reducing tightness that can contribute to sciatic discomfort.
instructions: Lie on your back with both knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Cross your right ankle over your left knee, creating a figure-four shape.
Flex your right foot to protect the knee.
Gently press your right knee away from your body, or draw both legs toward your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the release in the outer hip and glute.
Switch sides and repeat.

name: reverse plank
description: The reverse plank is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and glutes. It involves lifting the hips to form a straight line facing upward, engaging the posterior chain and core stabilizers to maintain proper alignment.
instructions: Sit on the mat with your legs extended and your hands placed on the mat behind your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Engage your core and press your palms and heels into the mat.
Lift your hips off the mat until your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Hold this position, keeping your hips elevated and your glutes engaged.
Lower your hips back to the mat with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions or hold for the desired duration.

name: reverse single leg march
description: The reverse single leg march is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteal muscles and hamstrings. It involves a controlled single-leg hip extension from a standing position, developing posterior chain strength, balance, and pelvic stability.
instructions: Stand facing a wall or chair for light support, with feet hip-width apart.
Shift your weight onto one leg and engage your glutes.
Hinge slightly forward at the hip and lift the opposite leg behind you.
Hold for a moment, then lower the leg with control.
Repeat on the other side, alternating in a marching rhythm.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions on each side.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/glutes/balance/beginner/1144 - reverse single leg march.mp4

name: rocking pilate
description: Rocking Pilate is a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and glutes. Performed prone while holding the ankles, it involves a rocking motion along the front of the body, strengthening the spinal extensors and improving spinal flexibility and body awareness.
instructions: Lie face down on the mat with your knees bent and feet raised toward your glutes.
Reach back and grasp your ankles with both hands.
Engage your core and lift your chest and thighs off the mat simultaneously.
Rock forward onto your chest, then rock back onto your thighs in a controlled arc.
Continue rocking back and forth for the desired number of repetitions.

name: roll over
description: The roll over is an advanced Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and lower back. It involves rolling the legs overhead from a supine position, providing a deep spinal stretch and building significant core and abdominal strength.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms by your sides, palms pressing into the mat.
Raise both legs to a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and use your abdominals to roll your hips off the mat.
Continue rolling until your legs are parallel to the floor overhead.
Open your legs slightly and slowly roll your spine back down to the mat, lowering your legs to 90 degrees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: scissor (single straight leg stretch)
description: The scissor, also known as the single straight leg stretch, is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hamstrings and abdominal muscles. It involves alternating straight leg pulls in a curl-up position, improving hamstring flexibility and core control simultaneously.
instructions: Lie on your back and lift both legs to a 90-degree angle.
Lift your head and shoulders off the mat, engaging your core.
Lower one leg toward the mat while holding the other raised leg with both hands.
Pull the raised leg gently toward your face in two short pulses.
Switch legs in a scissoring motion and repeat.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: shoulder bridge kick
description: The shoulder bridge kick is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes and hamstrings. Performed in a bridge position, it involves extending and kicking one leg while maintaining hip elevation, developing unilateral glute strength and pelvic stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat, hip-width apart.
Press your feet into the mat and lift your hips into a bridge position.
Engage your core and straighten one leg, extending it toward the ceiling.
Lower the extended leg toward the mat in a controlled kick, then raise it back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch legs.
Lower your hips back to the mat to complete the set.

name: side lying double legs up
description: Side lying double legs up is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip abductors and obliques. Performed side-lying, lifting both legs together increases the demand on the lateral hip and core compared to single-leg variations.
instructions: Lie on your side with your body in a straight line and legs stacked.
Place your bottom hand on the mat for stability and rest your head on your arm.
Engage your core and keep your hips stacked.
Lift both legs together off the mat as high as comfortable.
Hold for a moment at the top, then lower with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/hip abductors/strength/intermediate/1149 - side lying double legs up.mp4

name: side lying single leg up
description: Side lying single leg up is a foundational Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the hip abductors. Performed side-lying, it involves raising the top leg while maintaining hip and pelvic stability, building lateral hip strength and lower body alignment.
instructions: Lie on your side with your body in a straight line and legs stacked.
Place your bottom hand on the mat for stability and rest your head on your arm.
Keep your hips stacked and engage your core.
Slowly raise your top leg as high as comfortable without rotating your hip.
Lower it with control back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/hip abductors/strength/beginner/1150 - side lying single leg up.mp4

name: single leg press
description: The single leg press is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps and glutes. It involves an isometric pressing action of one leg against hand resistance, activating the hip flexors and core while developing neuromuscular control and single-leg stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent, foot flat on the mat, and the other leg extended toward the ceiling.
Place both hands behind your raised thigh for support.
Engage your core and gently press your raised leg against your hands while your hands resist.
Hold the isometric contraction for 2-3 seconds.
Release and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch legs and repeat on the other side.

name: squat with arm reach
description: Squat with arm reach is a Pilates-inspired bodyweight exercise targeting the quadriceps and glutes. The simultaneous arm reach counterbalances the squat and improves posture awareness, building lower body strength and shoulder mobility in one integrated movement.
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms by your sides.
Engage your core and begin to lower into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
As you descend, reach both arms forward at shoulder height.
Pause at the bottom of the squat with your thighs parallel to the floor.
Press through your heels and return to standing while lowering your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Pilates/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1152 - squat with arm reach.mp4

name: supine legs lift toe touch
description: Supine legs lift toe touch is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. It involves lifting the torso to reach toward the raised legs, combining core flexion with hip flexor engagement for a challenging abdominal strengthening movement.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both legs raised to a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms toward the ceiling.
Engage your core and curl your head and shoulders off the mat.
Reach your hands up toward your toes, lifting as high as comfortable.
Slowly lower your torso back down with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: supine toe tap
description: Supine toe tap is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves alternately tapping each foot to the floor from a tabletop position, challenging the deep core stabilizers to maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent at 90 degrees and shins parallel to the floor.
Place your hands behind your head and engage your core.
Slowly lower one foot toward the mat and tap your toes lightly on the ground.
Return your foot to the starting position with control.
Repeat on the opposite side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: swim dive
description: Swim dive is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and posterior shoulder muscles. Performed prone, it involves sweeping the arms from an overhead to a hip position while the chest and legs are lifted, strengthening the spinal extensors and improving shoulder mobility.
instructions: Lie face down with your arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Engage your core and lift your arms, chest, and legs off the mat.
Simultaneously sweep your arms out to the sides and back toward your hips, like a diving motion.
Return your arms overhead and repeat.
Keep your gaze downward and your neck in a neutral position throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: the saw pilate
description: The saw is a fundamental Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the obliques and abdominal muscles. It combines spinal rotation with a forward reach, stretching the hamstrings and outer hips while building rotational core strength and spinal mobility.
instructions: Sit upright with your legs extended wide and arms stretched out to the sides at shoulder height.
Engage your core and sit tall through your spine.
Inhale and rotate your torso to the right.
Exhale and reach your left hand toward your right little toe in a sawing motion, pulsing three times.
Inhale and return to the center upright position.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: arms circle
description: Arms circle is a bodyweight warm-up exercise targeting the deltoids and shoulder stabilizers. It involves rotating the arms in controlled circles at shoulder height, increasing blood flow to the shoulder joint, improving rotator cuff activation, and preparing the upper body for exercise.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height.
Engage your core and keep your shoulders relaxed.
Begin making small circles forward with both arms simultaneously.
Gradually increase the size of the circles over several repetitions.
Reverse the direction and perform circles backward.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions in each direction.

name: arms raise
description: Arms raise is a bodyweight warm-up exercise targeting the deltoid muscles. It involves a controlled forward raise of both arms to an overhead position, activating the shoulders and improving range of motion in preparation for a full-body Pilates session.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms resting by your sides.
Engage your core and keep your spine in a neutral position.
Slowly raise both arms forward and overhead, keeping them straight and palms facing down.
Pause when your arms are fully extended overhead.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cat-cow stretch
description: The cat-cow stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise targeting the lower and upper back. It involves alternating spinal flexion and extension on all fours, synchronized with the breath, to warm up the spine, improve flexibility, and release tension throughout the back.
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Inhale and drop your belly toward the mat, lifting your head and tailbone toward the ceiling (cow position).
Exhale and round your spine toward the ceiling, tucking your chin to your chest and pelvis under (cat position).
Continue flowing between cat and cow positions in rhythm with your breath.
Repeat for the desired number of breath cycles.

name: control balance
description: Control balance is an advanced Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and posterior chain. Performed in an inverted position with one leg overhead and one extended toward the ceiling, it demands exceptional core control, balance, and hamstring flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back and roll your legs overhead until your toes touch the mat behind your head.
Support your lower back with your hands or press your arms into the mat.
Raise one leg vertically toward the ceiling while the other remains extended behind your head.
Hold for a moment, then switch legs in a controlled scissoring motion.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions, then slowly roll back down to the mat.

name: imprint & release
description: Imprint and release is a foundational Pilates bodyweight warm-up exercise targeting the deep abdominal muscles and lower back. It trains awareness of spinal positioning by alternating between a neutral spine and an imprinted lower back, building core activation and spinal control.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Find your neutral spine position, allowing a natural curve in your lower back.
Gently exhale and engage your deep abdominals, pressing your lower back softly into the mat (imprint).
Hold for a breath, then release back to neutral spine.
Continue alternating between imprint and neutral for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pelvic curl (glute bridge)
description: The pelvic curl, also known as the glute bridge, is a fundamental Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the gluteal muscles and hamstrings. It involves peeling the spine off the mat with articulation, strengthening the posterior chain while developing spinal mobility and core stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat, hip-width apart.
Engage your core and gently press your lower back into the mat.
Exhale and peel your spine off the mat one vertebra at a time, beginning with your tailbone.
Continue lifting until your hips are fully elevated and your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
Slowly roll back down through your spine, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: seated upper body bent
description: Seated upper body bent is a Pilates bodyweight warm-up exercise targeting the lower back and hamstrings. It involves a seated forward hinge from an upright position, gently lengthening the posterior chain and preparing the spine for more demanding Pilates movements.
instructions: Sit upright on the mat with your legs extended straight in front of you.
Flex your feet and engage your core.
Inhale to prepare, then exhale and slowly hinge forward from your hips.
Reach your hands toward your feet, keeping your spine long.
Hold the forward position for a breath, then slowly return upright.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Pilates/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/1163 - seated upper body bent.mp4

name: shoulder roll
description: Shoulder rolls are a bodyweight warm-up exercise targeting the trapezius and deltoid muscles. This simple rotational movement helps release shoulder tension, improve joint mobility, and activate the muscles of the upper back and shoulders in preparation for exercise.
instructions: Stand or sit upright with your arms relaxed by your sides.
Slowly lift both shoulders toward your ears.
Roll your shoulders back and down in a smooth, circular motion.
Pause at the bottom and repeat the forward motion.
After several repetitions, reverse the direction and roll your shoulders forward and down.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions in each direction.

name: spine twist (seated)
description: The seated spine twist is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the obliques and abdominal muscles. It involves rotating the torso from a seated position with arms outstretched, developing spinal mobility, waist definition, and rotational core strength.
instructions: Sit tall on the mat with your legs extended and feet flexed, slightly wider than hip-width.
Extend your arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
Inhale to lengthen your spine, then exhale and rotate your torso to the right.
Pulse twice to the right, deepening the rotation without collapsing the spine.
Return to center and repeat on the left side.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.

name: boxing
description: Boxing is a bodyweight Pilates exercise targeting the pectorals and deltoids. It involves performing alternating punch movements from a standing position, engaging the core with each rotation and building upper body endurance, coordination, and cardiovascular capacity.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Bring your hands to guard position near your chin.
Engage your core and extend one arm forward in a controlled punch, rotating slightly through the torso.
Return that arm and alternate with the other arm.
Increase the pace as your coordination improves, maintaining a stable, engaged core.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions or duration.

name: chest extension
description: Chest extension is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back and spinal extensors. Performed prone with arms at the sides, it involves lifting the chest off the mat to strengthen the posterior chain and improve thoracic extension and posture.
instructions: Lie face down on the mat with your arms extended by your sides, palms facing up.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes.
Lift your chest and upper body off the mat as high as comfortable, keeping your neck in a neutral position.
Hold for a moment at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your chest back to the mat.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: coordination
description: Coordination is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. It challenges the core with a multi-step sequence of arm, leg, and torso actions performed in precise order, developing body awareness, core strength, and neuromuscular control.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent at 90 degrees and fists at your chest.
Exhale and curl your head and shoulders off the mat while extending your arms forward and your legs straight.
Open your legs to shoulder-width apart.
Close your legs back together.
Bend your knees back to 90 degrees and return arms to chest simultaneously.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: hug-a-tree
description: Hug-a-tree is a Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. It involves a wide arc of the arms from an outstretched position to a hugging position in front of the chest, building chest strength and shoulder stability without equipment.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Engage your core and keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Slowly arc your arms forward and toward each other as if wrapping around a large tree.
Pause when your hands are about to meet in front of your chest.
Slowly return your arms to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: knee push up
description: The knee push up is a beginner bodyweight exercise targeting the pectoral muscles and triceps. Performed with the knees on the mat, it reduces the load compared to a standard push up while still developing upper body strength and core stability.
instructions: Start on your hands and knees with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Walk your hands slightly forward and lower your hips so your body forms a straight line from head to knees.
Bend your elbows and slowly lower your chest toward the mat, keeping your core engaged.
Push back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: neck pull
