description: The neck pull is an advanced Pilates bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves a full roll-up from a supine position with hands clasped behind the head, demanding significant core strength, spinal articulation, and hamstring flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and hands placed behind your head, elbows wide.
Engage your core and slowly curl your head, neck, and upper body off the mat.
Continue rolling up until you are sitting upright, then hinge slightly forward from the hips.
Round your spine and slowly roll back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: triceps dips
description: Triceps dips are a bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps. Performed using a chair or elevated surface, they involve lowering and raising the body using arm strength, effectively isolating the back of the upper arm while also engaging the shoulders and chest.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair or mat with your hands gripping the edge beside your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your hips off the edge and extend your legs out in front of you.
Bend your elbows and slowly lower your body toward the floor, keeping your back close to the chair.
Stop when your elbows are at approximately 90 degrees.
Press through your palms and straighten your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall bent hamstring stretch
description: The wall bent hamstring stretch is a bodyweight stretching exercise that uses a wall for support while targeting the hamstrings. One leg remains bent against the wall while the other is kept straight, creating a focused stretch along the back of the leg.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips close to a wall and your legs extended up against it.
Bend one knee slightly and press your foot flat against the wall.
Keep the other leg straight against the wall and flex your foot to feel the stretch in your hamstring.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1173 - wall bent hamstring stretch.mp4

name: wall bent leg reverse lying
description: The wall bent leg reverse lying exercise is a bodyweight movement performed lying on the back with bent legs resting against the wall. It targets the hamstrings and glutes while promoting lower body flexibility and core stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips near the base of the wall and your legs bent, feet flat against the wall.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the floor.
Slowly slide your feet up the wall, straightening your legs as much as comfortable.
Hold briefly, then return to the bent position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1174 - wall bent leg reverse lying.mp4

name: wall decline elbow plank hold
description: The wall decline elbow plank hold is a bodyweight core exercise where the feet are elevated on a wall in a forearm plank position. The decline angle increases the challenge on the abdominal muscles, shoulders, and lower back compared to a standard plank.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and position your feet elevated against the wall.
Align your elbows directly under your shoulders and form a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and shoulders to maintain the declined plank position.
Hold the position for the desired duration without allowing your hips to sag or rise.
Lower your feet from the wall to return to the starting position.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1175 - wall decline elbow plank hold.mp4

name: wall dog kick hold leg raise
description: The wall dog kick hold leg raise is a bodyweight exercise using a wall for balance support. It targets the glutes through a controlled rear leg raise, while the hamstrings, lower back, and shoulders assist in stabilization throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Shift your weight onto one leg and lift the opposite leg behind you in a straight line with your torso.
Hold the raised leg position briefly, squeezing the glute at the top.
Slowly lower the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1176 - wall dog kick hold leg raise.mp4

name: wall donkey kick leg bent
description: The wall donkey kick leg bent is a bodyweight glute exercise performed with a bent knee while using a wall for balance. The 90-degree knee angle isolates the glutes and engages the hamstrings and lower back as supporting muscles.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Shift your weight onto one leg and lift the opposite knee to hip height.
Keeping the knee bent at 90 degrees, drive the heel up and back toward the ceiling.
Squeeze the glute at the top of the movement, then lower the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1177 - wall donkey kick leg bent.mp4

name: wall donkey kick leg up
description: The wall donkey kick leg up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes through a straight-leg rear lift. The wall provides balance support, allowing greater focus on controlled glute contraction and hip extension throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Shift your weight onto one leg and extend the other leg straight behind you.
Keeping the leg straight, lift it upward as high as comfortable while squeezing the glute.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower the leg back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1178 - wall donkey kick leg up.mp4

name: wall elbow pike hold plank
description: The wall elbow pike hold plank is a bodyweight core and shoulder exercise where the feet are elevated on the wall and the hips are raised into a pike position while supported on the forearms. This movement intensifies core and shoulder activation compared to a flat plank.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and walk your feet up the wall until your hips are raised and your body forms an inverted V shape.
Keep your elbows directly under your shoulders and press firmly into the forearms.
Engage your core and hold the pike position with a straight line from elbows to hips.
Maintain the position for the desired duration, breathing steadily.
Carefully walk your feet back down the wall to return to the starting position.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1179 - wall elbow pike hold plank.mp4

name: wall elbow plank mountain climber
description: The wall elbow plank mountain climber is a bodyweight core exercise combining a forearm plank with alternating knee drives. The feet remain against the wall for support while the mountain climber motion intensifies abdominal and hip flexor engagement.
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor and position your feet against the wall in a forearm plank.
Engage your core and ensure your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Drive one knee toward your chest in a controlled motion, then return it to the wall.
Immediately repeat with the opposite knee, alternating in a steady climbing motion.
Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1180 - wall elbow plank mountain climber.mp4

name: wall frog glute inner thigh join stretch
description: The wall frog glute inner thigh join stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise performed lying on the back with feet together and knees out against the wall. It provides a combined stretch for the glutes and inner thighs while the wall aids in positioning and depth.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips close to the wall and bring the soles of your feet together against the wall.
Allow your knees to fall out to the sides in a frog position, forming a diamond shape with your legs.
Press your lower back gently into the floor and relax your hips toward the ground.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Bring your knees back together to release the stretch.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1181 - wall frog glute inner thigh join stretch.mp4

name: wall frog pose inner thigh stretch
description: The wall frog pose inner thigh stretch is a bodyweight flexibility movement targeting the inner thighs. Performed on the back with knees open and feet resting on the wall, it uses gravity and wall support to deepen the stretch through the adductor muscles.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips close to the base of the wall.
Bend your knees and place the soles of your feet against the wall, feet together.
Allow your knees to drop open to the sides, pressing toward the wall in a frog position.
Gently press your lower back into the floor and feel the stretch along the inner thighs.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then slowly bring your knees back together.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/inner thighs/stretching/beginner/1182 - wall frog pose inner thigh stretch.mp4

name: wall frog pose stretch hold
description: The wall frog pose stretch hold is a sustained bodyweight flexibility exercise targeting the inner thighs, glutes, and hips. Holding the frog position against the wall allows gravity to progressively deepen the stretch while the wall provides structural support.
instructions: Lie on your back and scoot your hips up to the base of the wall.
Bend your knees outward and place the soles of your feet flat against the wall.
Let your knees fall open toward the wall in a frog position.
Relax your arms by your sides and allow gravity to deepen the stretch.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing steadily throughout.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/inner thighs/stretching/beginner/1183 - wall frog pose stretch hold.mp4

name: wall glute bridge
description: The wall glute bridge is a bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings. Using a wall to position the feet at a fixed height, this variation increases the range of hip extension and provides a stable base for controlled glute contraction.
instructions: Lie on your back and place your feet flat against the wall with knees bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Place your arms flat on the floor at your sides for stability.
Engage your core and drive your hips upward by pressing through your feet against the wall.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Slowly lower your hips back to the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1184 - wall glute bridge.mp4

name: wall glute bridge hand folded
description: The wall glute bridge hand folded is a bodyweight exercise that targets the glutes with hands folded across the chest to remove upper body support. This variation increases core and stabilizer engagement while maintaining the wall-supported foot position for consistent hip extension.
instructions: Lie on your back with feet flat against the wall and knees bent at approximately 90 degrees.
Fold your hands across your chest and keep your arms off the floor throughout the movement.
Engage your core and press your feet firmly into the wall to drive your hips upward.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back to the floor with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1185 - wall glute bridge hand folded.mp4

name: wall glute bridge no hands support
description: The wall glute bridge no hands support is a bodyweight glute exercise where no arm contact with the floor is used, maximizing core and stabilizer activation. The feet remain against the wall throughout, providing a consistent platform for hip extension and glute contraction.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet pressed flat against the wall and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Extend your arms straight up toward the ceiling or alongside your body without touching the floor.
Press through your feet into the wall and lift your hips off the ground by engaging your glutes.
Hold the top position briefly, keeping your core tight and hips level.
Lower your hips with control and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1186 - wall glute bridge no hands support.mp4

name: wall glute stretch hand raise front
description: The wall glute stretch hand raise front is a bodyweight flexibility exercise combining a wall-supported figure-four position with arms raised forward for balance. It effectively targets the glutes while the wall provides postural support to deepen the stretch.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet a few inches from the base.
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee to form a figure-four shape with your legs.
Slowly slide your back down the wall, bending the supporting leg until you feel a stretch in the raised glute.
Raise both arms straight out in front of you for balance.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1187 - wall glute stretch hand raise front.mp4

name: wall hand touch forward lunges version 1
description: The wall hand touch forward lunges version 1 is a bodyweight lower body exercise that uses light wall contact for balance guidance during forward lunges. It targets the quadriceps while engaging the glutes, hamstrings, and calves as supporting muscles.
instructions: Stand facing the wall at arm's length with your feet hip-width apart.
Place your fingertips lightly on the wall at shoulder height for balance support.
Step one foot forward into a lunge position, lowering your back knee toward the floor.
Touch your fingertips to the wall as you descend into the lunge.
Push through the front heel to return to standing, then repeat on the opposite side for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1188 - wall hand touch forward lunges version 1.mp4

name: wall high knee pilate
description: The wall high knee pilate is a bodyweight Pilates-style exercise performed with the back against the wall. It targets the hip flexors and core through controlled high knee lifts, using the wall to encourage proper spinal alignment and postural awareness.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against the wall and feet a few inches from the base.
Press your lower back into the wall and engage your core.
Lift one knee up toward your chest as high as comfortable while maintaining back contact with the wall.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower the leg back to the starting position.
Alternate legs in a controlled marching motion for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/1189 - wall high knee pilate.mp4

name: wall hundred pilate
description: The wall hundred pilate is a classic Pilates core exercise adapted with the feet resting against the wall for guided leg positioning. It targets the abdominal muscles through sustained contraction and rhythmic arm pumping, building core endurance and breath control.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet pressed flat against the wall and legs at a 90-degree angle.
Lift your head, neck, and shoulders off the floor and extend your arms straight by your sides.
Pump your arms up and down in small, controlled pulses while breathing in for five counts and out for five counts.
Continue the pumping motion for 100 counts total, maintaining a stable core throughout.
Lower your head and legs to the floor to complete the set.

name: wall lunges
description: Wall lunges are a bodyweight lower body exercise that uses the wall for balance and postural support during the lunge movement. This variation allows greater focus on quad and glute activation by reducing the need for balance stabilization.
instructions: Stand facing the wall with your hands placed flat against it at shoulder height.
Step one foot forward into a lunge stance, keeping the back leg extended.
Lower your back knee toward the floor, maintaining an upright torso supported by the wall.
Press through the front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

name: wall lying leg and hips lift up
description: The wall lying leg and hips lift up is a bodyweight core exercise performed on the back with legs resting against the wall. The movement involves lifting the hips off the floor using abdominal strength, with the wall providing foot support throughout the motion.
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms flat on the floor at your sides and your legs extended up the wall.
Press your lower back gently into the floor and engage your core.
Using your abdominal muscles, lift your hips off the floor and press your feet into the wall for stability.
Hold the lifted position briefly, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1192 - wall lying leg and hips lift up.mp4

name: wall lying leg straight lower body slide up
description: The wall lying leg straight lower body slide up is a bodyweight flexibility and strength exercise performed lying on the back with straight legs against the wall. Sliding the legs upward and returning engages the hamstrings and glutes while the wall guides the range of motion.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips close to the wall and your legs extended straight up against it.
Place your arms flat on the floor at your sides for stability.
Slowly slide both legs apart down the wall in a controlled motion, lowering them toward the floor.
Hold the stretched position briefly, then slide the legs back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1193 - wall lying leg straight lower body slide up.mp4

name: wall lying straight leg on wall hold
description: The wall lying straight leg on wall hold is a bodyweight stretching exercise where both legs are extended straight up against the wall. This sustained hold targets the hamstrings and calves through passive elongation while the wall provides consistent positioning.
instructions: Lie on your back and position your hips close to the base of the wall.
Extend both legs straight up against the wall, keeping your heels pressed to the surface.
Flex your feet and press your lower back gently into the floor.
Hold the position for the desired duration, breathing steadily.
Lower your legs from the wall to release.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1194 - wall lying straight leg on wall hold.mp4

name: wall marching hand on wall
description: The wall marching hand on wall is a bodyweight exercise that uses a wall for balance support while performing alternating high knee marches. It targets the hip flexors and engages the abs and quads, making it suitable for warm-up routines and lower body activation.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite knee up to hip height.
Hold the raised position briefly, then lower the leg back to the floor.
Immediately lift the opposite knee in a marching pattern.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/hip flexors/strength/beginner/1195 - wall marching hand on wall.mp4

name: wall one leg sit glute stretch hand raise
description: The wall one leg sit glute stretch hand raise is a bodyweight flexibility exercise combining a wall sit with a figure-four glute stretch. The arms are raised forward for balance while the wall provides back support, allowing a deep and controlled stretch of the glute muscles.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and slide down until your supporting leg is at a 90-degree angle.
Cross the opposite ankle over the supporting knee to form a figure-four shape.
Raise both arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height for balance.
Hold the wall sit position and feel the stretch in the raised leg's glute.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs and repeat.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/intermediate/1196 - wall one leg sit glute stretch hand raise.mp4

name: wall pilates double leg stretch new
description: The wall pilates double leg stretch new is a Pilates-inspired core exercise adapted with the wall for foot guidance. It targets the abdominal muscles through a coordinated leg and arm extension while the upper body remains lifted, building core strength and coordination.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet flat against the wall and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Lift your head, neck, and shoulders off the floor and hug your knees toward your chest.
Extend both legs straight while simultaneously reaching your arms overhead, maintaining core engagement.
Circle your arms back around and return your knees to your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/abs/strength/intermediate/1197 - wall pilates double leg stretch new.mp4

name: wall plank hold
description: The wall plank hold is a bodyweight core exercise where the feet are elevated against the wall in a standard plank position. The wall provides stability and elevation, increasing the demand on the abdominal muscles, shoulders, and lower back.
instructions: Place your hands flat on the floor and walk your feet up the wall until your body is parallel to the floor.
Align your wrists directly under your shoulders and engage your core.
Maintain a straight line from head to heels with your feet pressed firmly against the wall.
Hold the position for the desired duration without allowing your hips to sag or rise.
Walk your feet back down the wall to return to the starting position.

name: wall push up leg on wall
description: The wall push up leg on wall is a bodyweight chest exercise where one or both feet are elevated against the wall during a push-up. The elevated position shifts more load onto the upper chest and shoulders while engaging the core for stabilization.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor at shoulder width and walk one foot up the wall behind you.
Keep the other foot on the floor or raise it to meet the first, forming an elevated push-up position.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Push back up to the starting position by extending your arms fully.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1199 - wall push up leg on wall.mp4

name: wall push up with step up leg
description: The wall push up with step up leg is a bodyweight compound exercise combining a push-up with a single-leg wall step-up. It targets the chest while adding dynamic movement that engages the shoulders, core, and glutes with each repetition.
instructions: Begin in a push-up position with your hands on the floor and feet near the base of the wall.
Perform one push-up, lowering your chest toward the floor and pressing back up.
At the top of the push-up, step one foot up onto the wall behind you.
Hold briefly, then lower the foot back to the floor.
Alternate the stepping leg with each push-up repetition for the desired number of reps.
video_path: Wall Pilates/chest/pectorals/strength/intermediate/1200 - wall push up with step up leg.mp4

name: wall reverse crunch
description: The wall reverse crunch is a bodyweight core exercise performed lying on the back with feet pressed against the wall. The wall provides resistance and foot support as the hips are curled upward using abdominal strength, targeting the lower abs effectively.
instructions: Lie on your back and extend your legs straight up against the wall.
Place your arms flat on the floor at your sides for support.
Engage your core and curl your hips off the floor, pressing your feet into the wall.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall reverse hand squat
description: The wall reverse hand squat is a bodyweight squat variation where the hands are placed on the wall behind the body for postural support during the descent. It targets the quadriceps while allowing the back to remain upright throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand facing away from the wall and reach both hands behind you to rest against the wall at waist height.
Walk your feet slightly forward and position them hip-width apart.
Keeping your hands on the wall for support, slowly lower your hips into a squat position.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then press through your heels to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1202 - wall reverse hand squat.mp4

name: wall reverse leg touch on wall
description: The wall reverse leg touch on wall is a bodyweight balance and hamstring exercise that involves reaching back with one heel to touch the wall. The movement stretches the hamstrings and glutes of the reaching leg while engaging the stabilizing muscles of the standing leg.
instructions: Stand facing away from the wall with your feet a few inches from the base.
Shift your weight to one leg and bend the opposite knee, reaching back to touch the wall behind you with your heel.
Maintain an upright torso and keep the standing leg slightly bent.
Hold the touch briefly, then lower the foot back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/strength/beginner/1203 - wall reverse leg touch on wall.mp4

name: wall single leg bent stand straight hold
description: The wall single leg bent stand straight hold is a bodyweight balance exercise performed with the back against the wall. One leg is lifted while the standing leg remains straight, targeting quad endurance and ankle stability while the wall ensures proper upright posture.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against the wall and feet hip-width apart.
Bend one knee and lift that foot off the floor, holding it in front of you.
Press your back firmly against the wall and keep the standing leg straight.
Hold the single-leg standing position for the desired duration.
Lower the foot and repeat on the opposite leg.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/balance/beginner/1204 - wall single leg bent stand straight hold.mp4

name: wall single leg glute
description: The wall single leg glute is a bodyweight exercise that isolates the glutes using a single-leg wall glute bridge. The elevated wall position and unilateral stance increase the challenge on the working glute while engaging the core and hamstrings for stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with both feet flat against the wall and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Extend one leg straight while pressing the other foot firmly into the wall.
Engage your core and drive your hips upward by pressing through the foot on the wall.
Squeeze your glute at the top and hold briefly.
Lower your hips back to the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching legs.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1205 - wall single leg glute.mp4

name: wall single leg hamstring stretch
description: The wall single leg hamstring stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise where one leg is extended up the wall while the other rests on the floor. This isolated stretch targets the hamstring of the elevated leg, with the wall providing a fixed surface for consistent positioning.
instructions: Lie on your back with your hips close to the wall and both legs extended upward against it.
Lower one leg to the floor while keeping the other pressed straight against the wall.
Flex the foot of the wall leg and feel the hamstring stretch along the back of that leg.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1206 - wall single leg hamstring stretch.mp4

name: wall single leg stand hold
description: The wall single leg stand hold is a bodyweight balance exercise using the wall for light support while maintaining a single-leg stance. It targets quad endurance and improves lower body stability, suitable for rehabilitation and balance training.
instructions: Stand sideways next to the wall and place one hand lightly against it for balance.
Shift your weight onto the leg closest to the wall and lift the opposite foot off the floor.
Keep the standing leg straight or slightly bent and hold the balanced position.
Maintain the single-leg stance for the desired duration.
Lower the foot and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/balance/beginner/1207 - wall single leg stand hold.mp4

name: wall single leg standing hold
description: The wall single leg standing hold is a bodyweight balance and stability exercise using the wall for light hand support. It targets the quadriceps and stabilizing muscles of the lower body, developing single-leg balance and postural control.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands lightly on it at shoulder height.
Lift one foot off the floor and hold it behind you, keeping the standing leg straight.
Engage your core and maintain an upright posture throughout.
Hold the single-leg position for the desired duration.
Lower the foot back to the floor and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/balance/beginner/1208 - wall single leg standing hold.mp4

name: wall single leg glute twist
description: The wall single leg glute twist is a bodyweight exercise targeting the glutes through a rotational knee movement with wall support. The twisting motion engages the hip external rotators and obliques in addition to the primary glute muscles.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite knee to hip height.
Rotate the raised knee outward to the side in a controlled twisting motion, engaging the glute.
Return the knee to the forward position, then lower the leg back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1209 - wall single leg glute twist.mp4

name: wall sit glute stretch hand raise hold
description: The wall sit glute stretch hand raise hold is a bodyweight exercise combining a wall sit with a figure-four glute stretch, performed while holding the arms raised for balance and posture. It simultaneously challenges quad endurance and provides a deep glute stretch.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and slide down until your knees are at 90 degrees.
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee into a figure-four position.
Raise both arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
Hold the wall sit with the figure-four glute stretch for the desired duration.
Return to standing and switch legs to repeat on the other side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/intermediate/1210 - wall sit glute stretch hand raise hold.mp4

name: wall sit on one leg forward fold stretch
description: The wall sit on one leg forward fold stretch is a bodyweight hamstring and lower back flexibility exercise. One foot rests against the wall while the opposite leg is extended and the torso folds forward, creating a focused unilateral hamstring stretch.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one leg extended straight out and the opposite foot flat against the wall.
Sit tall and engage your core, then hinge forward from the hips toward the extended leg.
Reach your hands toward your foot and hold the forward fold at maximum comfortable depth.
Keep the extended leg straight and foot flexed throughout the stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch legs and repeat.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1211 - wall sit on one leg forward fold stretch.mp4

name: wall sliding
description: Wall sliding is a bodyweight lower body exercise where the back slides along the wall during a squat movement. The wall guides proper alignment and reduces hip and knee strain, making it effective for quad strengthening and rehabilitation.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet shoulder-width apart, a step away from the base.
Slowly slide your back down the wall by bending your knees, lowering into a squat position.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then slide back up to standing.
Keep your core engaged and your back flat against the wall throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: wall sliding hip thrust
description: The wall sliding hip thrust is a bodyweight glute exercise where the back is supported against the wall during a hip thrust motion. The wall keeps the upper body anchored, allowing maximum focus on hip extension and glute contraction with each repetition.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your back against the wall and your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Press your back against the wall and drive your hips forward and upward, squeezing your glutes.
Hold the thrust position briefly at the top, then lower your hips back toward the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/1213 - wall sliding hip thrust.mp4

name: wall squat pose hold
description: The wall squat pose hold is a static bodyweight exercise where the back is pressed against the wall in a deep squat position. This isometric hold builds quad endurance and lower body strength while the wall ensures consistent upright posture.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against the wall and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Slowly slide down the wall until your thighs are parallel to the floor and knees are at 90 degrees.
Keep your back pressed into the wall and your arms resting at your sides or held in front.
Hold the squat position for the desired duration.
Slide back up the wall to return to standing.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1214 - wall squat pose hold.mp4

name: wall standard plank hold
description: The wall standard plank hold is a bodyweight core exercise using the wall to elevate the feet in a standard plank position. The elevated feet increase the demand on the abdominals and shoulders compared to a floor plank, making it an effective core stability exercise.
instructions: Place your hands on the floor directly under your shoulders and walk your feet up the wall behind you.
Ensure your body forms a straight, rigid line from head to heels.
Engage your core, glutes, and shoulders, pressing firmly through your palms.
Hold the plank position for the desired duration without allowing your hips to drop.
Walk your feet back down the wall to release the position.

name: wall standing calf raise hold
description: The wall standing calf raise hold is a bodyweight exercise targeting the calves through a sustained raised-heel position. The wall provides light balance support, allowing full focus on calf contraction and ankle stability at the peak of the movement.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands lightly against it at shoulder height.
Position your feet hip-width apart with toes pointing forward.
Rise up onto the balls of your feet, lifting your heels as high as possible.
Hold the raised position for a moment, squeezing the calves at the top.
Slowly lower your heels back to the floor and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/lower legs/calves/strength/beginner/1216 - wall standing calf raise hold.mp4

name: wall standing calf raise stretch
description: The wall standing calf raise stretch is a bodyweight exercise combining calf raises with a downward heel stretch. Using the wall for balance, this movement targets the calves through the full range of motion, building both strength and flexibility in the lower leg.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Step one foot back and press the heel firmly into the floor.
Rise up on the toes of the back foot, then lower the heel toward the floor for a stretch.
Alternate between raising and lowering the heel in a controlled calf raise and stretch motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: Wall Pilates/lower legs/calves/stretching/beginner/1217 - wall standing calf raise stretch.mp4

name: wall standing calf stretch
description: The wall standing calf stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise targeting the calves using the wall for balance and leverage. The back leg's heel is pressed into the floor while leaning forward, creating a sustained stretch through the gastrocnemius and soleus.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at chest height.
Step one foot back and press the heel firmly into the floor, keeping the leg straight.
Lean your body slightly toward the wall to deepen the calf stretch on the back leg.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat.
video_path: Wall Pilates/lower legs/calves/stretching/beginner/1218 - wall standing calf stretch.mp4

name: wall standing calf stretch version 1
description: Wall standing calf stretch version 1 is a bodyweight flexibility exercise where the toes are elevated against the wall base while the heel remains on the floor. This positioning provides an intense calf stretch, particularly targeting the gastrocnemius and Achilles tendon.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place your toes against the base of the wall, keeping your heel on the floor.
Lean your body toward the wall with hands placed flat against it for support.
Feel the stretch in your calf and hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Step back and repeat with the other foot.
Continue for the desired number of sets on each side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/lower legs/calves/stretching/beginner/1219 - wall standing calf stretch version 1.mp4

name: wall standing cat pose stretch
description: The wall standing cat pose stretch is a bodyweight mobility exercise adapted from the yoga cat pose to a standing position against the wall. It targets the lower and upper back through spinal flexion and extension, improving mobility and relieving tension along the spine.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Walk your feet back until your arms are fully extended and your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Engage your core and round your upper back upward like a cat pose, tucking your chin to your chest.
Hold the rounded position for a moment, then gently arch your back in the opposite direction.
Repeat the alternating rounding and arching motion for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/lower back/mobility/beginner/1220 - wall standing cat pose stretch.mp4

name: wall standing glute bride hold hand folded
description: The wall standing glute bridge hold hand folded is a bodyweight isometric exercise where the glute bridge position is held with hands folded across the chest. The wall supports the feet, and the absence of arm floor contact increases core engagement throughout the sustained hold.
instructions: Lie on your back with feet flat against the wall and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Fold your hands across your chest and drive your hips upward by pressing through both feet.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold the bridge position for the desired duration.
Maintain core engagement and keep your arms folded throughout.
Lower your hips back to the floor to complete the hold.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/1221 - wall standing glute bride hold hand folded.mp4

name: wall standing glute stretch
description: The wall standing glute stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise performed in a partial wall sit with a figure-four leg position. The wall provides back support, allowing a controlled and progressive stretch of the glutes and external hip rotators.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee, forming a figure-four shape.
Slowly bend the standing knee and slide your back down the wall until you feel a stretch in the raised glute.
Place your hands on the raised knee for a gentle press to deepen the stretch if needed.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/beginner/1222 - wall standing glute stretch.mp4

name: wall standing glute stretch advanced
description: The wall standing glute stretch advanced is a deeper variation of the wall glute stretch, performed with a full wall sit descent in the figure-four position. The increased depth provides a more intense stretch through the glutes and hip external rotators for flexible individuals.
instructions: Stand with your back flat against the wall and your feet hip-width apart.
Cross one ankle over the opposite knee in a figure-four position.
Slide your back fully down the wall into a deep squat position, maximizing the glute stretch.
Press the raised knee gently outward to intensify the stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds at the deepest comfortable position, then switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/glutes/stretching/intermediate/1223 - wall standing glute stretch advanced.mp4

name: wall standing hamstring stretch
description: The wall standing hamstring stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise where one heel is elevated against the wall to create a targeted hamstring stretch in a standing position. The wall supports the foot and provides leverage to deepen the stretch safely.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands on it at shoulder height.
Step one foot forward toward the wall and place the heel on the ground with toes raised and resting on the wall.
Keep the front leg straight and lean your torso slightly forward until you feel a stretch in the hamstring.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1224 - wall standing hamstring stretch.mp4

name: wall standing hamstring stretch single leg hand
description: The wall standing hamstring stretch single leg hand is a bodyweight unilateral hamstring stretch using the wall for foot support and one hand for balance. Leaning forward with the raised leg extended targets the hamstring with greater precision and control.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and raise one foot, placing the heel against the wall at a comfortable height.
Place one hand on the wall for support and the other on the raised leg for gentle guidance.
Keep the raised leg straight and lean gently forward from the hips to deepen the stretch.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily.
Lower the leg and switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1225 - wall standing hamstring stretch single leg hand.mp4

name: wall standing leg circle stretch
description: The wall standing leg circle stretch is a bodyweight hip mobility exercise using the wall for balance support during controlled leg circles. It targets the hip flexors and surrounding muscles through a full circular range of motion, improving hip joint mobility and flexibility.
instructions: Stand sideways next to the wall and place one hand against it for balance.
Lift the outer leg slightly off the floor and begin making slow, controlled circular motions with the leg.
Circle the leg forward, out, back, and around in a smooth arc, engaging the hip throughout.
Complete the desired number of circles in one direction, then reverse.
Switch sides and repeat with the other leg.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hip flexors/mobility/beginner/1226 - wall standing leg circle stretch.mp4

name: wall standing leg lift up
description: The wall standing leg lift up is a bodyweight exercise using the wall for balance while performing a straight-leg front raise. It targets the hip flexors and engages the quads and core, suitable for hip flexor strengthening and lower body activation.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands lightly against it at shoulder height.
Stand tall and shift your weight onto one leg.
Lift the opposite leg straight up in front of you as high as comfortable, keeping the knee extended.
Hold briefly at the top, then slowly lower the leg back to the floor.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/1227 - wall standing leg lift up.mp4

name: wall standing leg raise
description: The wall standing leg raise is a bodyweight lower body exercise using the wall for lateral balance support during a front leg raise. It strengthens the hip flexors and quads while engaging the core for stabilization throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand with your side to the wall and place one hand on it for balance.
Stand tall and engage your core.
Lift the outer leg straight up in front of you as high as comfortable.
Slowly lower the leg back to the floor with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then turn and switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hip flexors/strength/beginner/1228 - wall standing leg raise.mp4

name: wall standing lower body stretch
description: The wall standing lower body stretch is a comprehensive bodyweight stretching routine targeting the quadriceps, hip flexors, and calves in a standing position. The wall provides balance support, making it easier to maintain an upright posture during the full lower body stretch.
instructions: Stand facing the wall with your feet hip-width apart and hands placed against it for support.
Bend one knee and lift your heel toward your glute, grasping the ankle with the hand on the same side.
Stand tall and keep your knees together as you hold the quad stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch through the front of the thigh.
Lower the foot and switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/stretching/beginner/1229 - wall standing lower body stretch.mp4

name: wall standing neck and back stretch hold
description: The wall standing neck and back stretch hold is a bodyweight flexibility exercise performed with the back against the wall. The wall supports proper spinal alignment while the chin tuck creates a targeted stretch along the cervical spine and upper back.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet a few inches from the base.
Press your entire back and head against the wall and relax your arms at your sides.
Slowly tuck your chin toward your chest, feeling the stretch along the back of the neck.
Hold the tucked position for 20-30 seconds.
Return your head to neutral and repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Wall Pilates/neck/neck/stretching/beginner/1230 - wall standing neck and back stretch hold.mp4

name: wall standing shoulder and hips stretch
description: The wall standing shoulder and hips stretch is a bodyweight combined flexibility exercise targeting the shoulders and hip flexors simultaneously. Using the wall for one arm's support, the body rotates to open both the shoulder and hip in a single integrated stretch.
instructions: Stand sideways next to the wall and place the near hand on it at shoulder height.
Step the inside foot slightly forward and rotate your body away from the wall.
Simultaneously open your hips and reach the free arm overhead or back to stretch the shoulder and hip.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch across the shoulder and hip.
Turn around and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/shoulders/shoulders/stretching/beginner/1231 - wall standing shoulder and hips stretch.mp4

name: wall standing single leg twist hamstring stretch
description: The wall standing single leg twist hamstring stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise that adds a spinal rotation component to a traditional hamstring stretch. The wall supports balance while the twisting motion targets both the hamstring and the lower back and obliques.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place one foot slightly forward with the heel on the floor and toes raised.
Place both hands on the wall for support and keep the front leg straight.
Slowly rotate your torso away from the raised-toe leg, adding a twisting component to the stretch.
Hold the twisted position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in the hamstring and through the lower back.
Return to neutral and switch sides.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1232 - wall standing single leg twist hamstring stretch.mp4

name: wall standing squat
description: The wall standing squat is a bodyweight lower body exercise where the back slides along the wall during the squat movement. This variation promotes proper squat mechanics and reduces spinal load, targeting the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings effectively.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet shoulder-width apart, a step away from the base.
Place your arms at your sides or extended in front of you for balance.
Slowly bend your knees and slide your back down the wall into a squat position.
Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then press through your heels to slide back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/quads/strength/beginner/1233 - wall standing squat.mp4

name: wall standing upper back stretch version 1
description: Wall standing upper back stretch version 1 is a bodyweight flexibility exercise targeting the upper back and shoulders. With hands pressed into the wall and the torso hinged forward, the stretch lengthens the muscles along the thoracic spine and shoulder girdle.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands flat against it at shoulder height.
Walk your feet back until your arms are fully extended and your torso is bent at approximately 45 degrees.
Press your hands into the wall and allow your chest to sink toward the floor, rounding your upper back.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds.
Walk your feet back toward the wall and return to standing.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/1234 - wall standing upper back stretch version 1.mp4

name: wall standing upper body bent stretch
description: The wall standing upper body bent stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise where the torso is bent forward with both hands on the wall. This position creates a decompressive stretch along the lower back and spine while engaging the upper back and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand facing the wall with your feet hip-width apart and place both hands flat on it at waist height.
Walk your feet back until your torso is bent parallel to the floor with your arms fully extended.
Press your hands into the wall and lengthen your spine by pulling your hips away from the wall.
Hold the elongated position for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply.
Walk your feet back in to return to standing.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/1235 - wall standing upper body bent stretch.mp4

name: wall support squat hand raise
description: The wall support squat hand raise is a bodyweight isometric exercise combining a wall squat hold with arms raised forward. The absence of arm support increases core activation and postural demand, while the wall-supported squat position targets the quadriceps and glutes.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and slide into a squat until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Once in the squat position, raise both arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
Hold the squat and arms raised position for the desired duration.
Lower your arms and slide back up the wall to return to standing.
Repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/quads/strength/intermediate/1236 - wall support squat hand raise.mp4

name: wall upper back reverse bent squat
description: The wall upper back reverse bent squat is a bodyweight exercise that combines a wall squat with an upper back rounded position. It targets the upper back musculature during the squat hold while the legs perform the primary strengthening work.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and feet shoulder-width apart.
Slide your back down the wall into a squat position until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Round your upper back slightly against the wall and reach your arms forward.
Hold the position while engaging your upper back and core.
Slide back up to standing and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/upper back/strength/intermediate/1237 - wall upper back reverse bent squat.mp4

name: wall upper back and hips stretch
description: The wall upper back and hips stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise that targets the upper back and hip flexors simultaneously. With hands against the wall and the torso bent forward, this combined stretch decompresses the spine and opens the hip flexors in one position.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands on it at shoulder height, arms extended.
Walk your feet back until your torso is hinged forward and your arms are fully extended.
Simultaneously push your hips back and press your chest toward the floor to create a stretch through the upper back and hips.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Walk your feet forward and return to standing.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/upper back/stretching/beginner/1238 - wall upper back and hips stretch.mp4

name: wall upper body bent back stretch
description: The wall upper body bent back stretch is a bodyweight flexibility movement targeting the lower and upper back. Bending forward from the wall while maintaining slight contact with the lower back creates a progressive decompressive stretch along the entire spine.
instructions: Stand with your back against the wall and your feet about a foot from the base.
Slowly bend your upper body forward and away from the wall while keeping your lower back lightly in contact.
Reach your arms down toward the floor and feel the stretch through your upper and lower back.
Hold the stretched position for 20-30 seconds.
Return to an upright position against the wall.
video_path: Wall Pilates/back/lower back/stretching/beginner/1239 - wall upper body bent back stretch.mp4

name: wall upper body bent hamstrings stretch
description: The wall upper body bent hamstrings stretch is a bodyweight standing hamstring stretch using the wall for arm support while the torso is bent forward. The straight-leg forward hinge creates effective lengthening of the hamstrings and lower back.
instructions: Stand facing the wall and place both hands on it slightly below shoulder height.
Walk your feet back and hinge at the hips until your torso is at approximately 45-90 degrees.
Keep your legs straight and press your heels into the floor to feel the hamstring stretch.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Walk your feet back in and return to standing.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/1240 - wall upper body bent hamstrings stretch.mp4

name: wall upper body side bent hand overhead
description: The wall upper body side bent hand overhead is a bodyweight side-bending stretch targeting the obliques and lateral torso. The wall provides support for the lower arm while the overhead arm and lateral lean create a focused stretch through the waist and shoulders.
instructions: Stand sideways next to the wall with your feet together and your near hand placed on the wall for support.
Raise the outer arm overhead and lean your body toward the wall in a lateral side bend.
Feel the stretch along the obliques and side of the torso.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily.
Return to upright and repeat on the opposite side.
video_path: Wall Pilates/waist/obliques/stretching/beginner/1241 - wall upper body side bent hand overhead.mp4

name: wall wide leg open bent stretch
description: The wall wide leg open bent stretch is a bodyweight flexibility exercise where the legs are spread wide against the wall in a supine position with a slight knee bend. It provides a progressive inner thigh and groin stretch using gravity and the wall for guided positioning.
instructions: Lie on your back and position your hips at the base of the wall with your legs extended straight up against it.
Slowly allow your legs to slide apart into a wide V position, bending your knees slightly as comfortable.
Let gravity pull your legs wider as you relax your inner thighs.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds at the comfortable maximum width.
Bring your legs back together and repeat for the desired number of sets.
video_path: Wall Pilates/upper legs/inner thighs/stretching/beginner/1242 - wall wide leg open bent stretch.mp4

name: airplane pose (dekasana)
description: Airplane pose is a standing balance posture that targets the lower back while engaging the glutes, hamstrings, and shoulders. It involves balancing on one leg while extending the torso and opposite leg parallel to the floor, improving stability and posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand tall with your feet together and arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height.
Shift your weight onto your right foot and hinge forward at the hips, lifting your left leg straight behind you.
Continue tilting your torso forward until your body forms a straight line parallel to the floor.
Keep your arms extended wide and your gaze slightly forward to help with balance.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then slowly return to standing and repeat on the other side.

name: anantasana sleeping vishnu pose
description: Anantasana is a reclining yoga posture targeting the hamstrings and inner thighs while requiring core engagement for lateral balance. It involves lying on one side and extending one leg upward, promoting flexibility and stability.
instructions: Lie on your right side with your body in a straight line, supporting your head with your right hand.
Bend your right elbow and prop your head up, placing your right foot flat on the mat for stability.
Raise your left leg toward the ceiling, keeping it straight, and hold your left big toe with your left hand if possible.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, feeling the stretch along the inner thigh and hamstring.
Slowly lower your leg, roll to the other side, and repeat.

name: ashtanga namaskar
description: Ashtanga namaskar, or eight-limbed pose, is a yoga posture in which eight points of the body make contact with the floor. It targets the chest and engages the triceps, lower back, and abs, and is commonly used as a transitional pose in sun salutation sequences.
instructions: Begin in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Lower your knees, chest, and chin to the floor simultaneously, keeping your hips slightly elevated.
Ensure your palms, chin, chest, and knees are all touching the mat while your elbows stay close to your sides.
Hold this position briefly, pressing the tops of your feet into the mat.
Inhale and transition into cobra or upward-facing dog to continue the sun salutation sequence.

name: bhekasana frog pose
description: Bhekasana is an advanced prone yoga posture that deeply stretches the quadriceps and hip flexors while opening the chest and shoulders. It involves bending both knees and pressing the feet toward the floor, requiring significant quad and shoulder flexibility.
instructions: Lie face down on your mat with your legs extended and arms by your sides.
Bend both knees and reach back with your hands to grasp the tops of your feet.
Press your feet toward the floor beside your hips while lifting your chest and thighs off the mat.
Draw your shoulder blades together and keep your gaze forward.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily, then release and rest.

name: boat pose (navasana) both hands front raise
description: Boat pose with both hands front raise is a yoga balance posture that intensely targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves balancing on the sit bones with legs and arms extended, building core strength and stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
Straighten your legs to a 45-degree angle and extend both arms forward, parallel to the floor.
Keep your spine long and chest open while engaging your core to maintain balance.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, breathing steadily, then lower your legs and rest.
video_path: Yoga/waist/abs/balance/intermediate/0776 - boat pose (navasana) both hands front raise.mp4

name: boat pose (navasana)
description: Boat pose is a foundational yoga posture that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves balancing on the sit bones with the legs and torso lifted in a V-shape, building core strength and body awareness.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
Straighten your legs to a 45-degree angle and extend your arms forward alongside your legs, palms facing up.
Engage your core, lengthen your spine, and keep your chest lifted.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then lower your legs and rest before repeating.

name: bow pose (dhanurasana)
description: Bow pose is a prone backbend yoga posture that targets the lower back and stretches the chest, quads, and hip flexors. It involves grasping the ankles and lifting the chest and thighs simultaneously, creating a bow-like shape in the body.
instructions: Lie face down on your mat with your legs extended and arms by your sides.
Bend your knees and bring your heels toward your glutes, then reach back and grasp your ankles with both hands.
Inhale and simultaneously lift your chest and thighs off the mat by pressing your feet into your hands.
Keep your knees hip-width apart and your gaze forward.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, breathing deeply, then exhale and gently lower back to the mat.

name: bridge pose (setu bandhasana)
description: Bridge pose is a supine yoga posture that targets the glutes and hamstrings while opening the chest and strengthening the lower back. It involves lifting the hips off the floor with feet flat on the mat, promoting spinal flexibility and posterior chain activation.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms flat by your sides with palms facing down.
Press your feet into the floor and lift your hips toward the ceiling, engaging your glutes and core.
Roll your shoulders underneath you and interlace your fingers below your back if comfortable.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then slowly lower your spine back to the mat vertebra by vertebra.

name: butterfly pose (baddha konasana)
description: Butterfly pose is a seated yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and inner thighs through a sustained groin stretch. It involves sitting with the soles of the feet together and knees falling outward, promoting hip mobility and flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your spine tall and bring the soles of your feet together in front of you.
Let your knees drop out to the sides and hold your feet with both hands.
Gently press your knees toward the floor using your elbows while keeping your back straight.
Hinge forward slightly from the hips to deepen the stretch if comfortable.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply, then slowly release.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0780 - butterfly pose (baddha konasana).mp4

name: camel pose (ustrasana)
description: Camel pose is a kneeling backbend yoga posture that deeply opens the chest and stretches the hip flexors and quadriceps. It involves arching the back and reaching for the heels, promoting thoracic extension and front-body flexibility.
instructions: Kneel on the floor with your knees hip-width apart and your thighs perpendicular to the floor.
Place your hands on your lower back with fingers pointing down, and begin to arch your back.
Slowly reach your hands back one at a time to grasp your heels, pressing your hips forward.
Drop your head back gently and open your chest toward the ceiling.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then bring your chin to your chest and carefully return to upright.

name: chair pose (utkatasana)
description: Chair pose is a standing yoga posture that targets the quadriceps and glutes while engaging the core and lower back. It involves bending the knees and lowering the hips with arms raised overhead, building lower body strength and endurance.
instructions: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Inhale and raise your arms overhead, palms facing each other.
Exhale and bend your knees, lowering your hips as if sitting in an imaginary chair.
Keep your thighs as parallel to the floor as comfortable and your knees behind your toes.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, then inhale and straighten your legs to return to standing.

name: child's pose
description: Child's pose is a restorative yoga posture that gently stretches the lower back, hips, and shoulders. It involves folding the torso over the thighs with arms extended forward, providing a calming and decompressive stretch for the spine.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees in a tabletop position.
Bring your big toes together and spread your knees wide, about hip-width apart or wider.
Exhale and sink your hips back toward your heels, extending your arms forward along the mat.
Rest your forehead on the mat and allow your chest to melt toward the floor.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply into your back body, then slowly return to tabletop.

name: corpse pose (shavasana)
description: Corpse pose is a restorative yoga posture used primarily for deep relaxation at the end of a practice. It involves lying completely still on the back, releasing all muscular tension and focusing on conscious relaxation of the body and mind.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and slightly apart.
Let your arms rest at your sides, palms facing up, slightly away from your body.
Close your eyes and allow your feet to fall naturally outward.
Release all muscular effort and breathe naturally, allowing your entire body to relax.
Remain in this position for 5-10 minutes, then slowly deepen your breath and gently roll to one side before sitting up.

name: cow pose (bitilasana)
description: Cow pose is a foundational yoga posture typically paired with cat pose to warm up the spine. It targets the lower back by encouraging lumbar extension while opening the chest and engaging the core, promoting spinal mobility and flexibility.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees in a tabletop position with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
Inhale deeply and drop your belly toward the mat, allowing your spine to arch.
Lift your chest and tailbone toward the ceiling, opening the front of your body.
Keep your shoulders relaxed and away from your ears.
Hold briefly, then exhale and transition into cat pose, repeating the sequence for 5-10 breath cycles.

name: crane pose (bakasana)
description: Crane pose is an arm balance yoga posture that requires core strength and upper body stability. It involves balancing the knees on the upper arms with feet lifted off the floor, engaging the triceps, shoulders, and abdominal muscles.
instructions: Begin in a low squat with your feet close together and hands flat on the floor in front of you.
Bend your elbows slightly and place your knees on the backs of your upper arms.
Lean your weight forward into your hands and lift one foot, then the other, off the floor.
Engage your core and round your back slightly to maintain the tuck position.
Hold the pose for 10-20 seconds, then slowly lower your feet back to the floor.

name: crow pose (kagasana)
description: Crow pose is an arm balance yoga posture closely related to crane pose, distinguished by a more bent-arm position. It targets the lower back and engages the core, triceps, and shoulders, building upper body and core strength for arm balancing.
instructions: Squat down and place your palms flat on the floor about shoulder-width apart.
Bend your elbows and place your shins or inner knees against the backs of your upper arms.
Shift your weight forward onto your hands until your feet naturally lift off the ground.
Keep your gaze slightly forward and engage your core to hold the position.
Hold for 10-20 seconds, then gently lower your feet to the floor.

name: dance pose (natarajasana)
description: Dance pose is an advanced standing balance posture that stretches the chest, shoulders, and quadriceps while strengthening the lower back and improving balance. It involves standing on one leg and extending the opposite leg behind the body in a graceful backbend arc.
instructions: Stand tall in mountain pose with your weight evenly distributed on both feet.
Shift your weight onto your left foot and bend your right knee, lifting your right foot behind you.
Grasp your right ankle or foot with your right hand and begin to tilt your torso forward.
Extend your left arm straight forward and press your right foot into your hand to lift the leg higher.
Hold the balance for 20-30 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

name: dandayamana janu sirsasana
description: Dandayamana janu sirsasana is a standing head-to-knee yoga posture that targets the hamstrings and challenges balance and concentration. It involves extending one leg forward while standing, then folding the torso over the raised leg.
instructions: Stand on your left foot and lift your right knee, clasping your hands beneath the right thigh.
Slowly extend your right leg forward until it is straight and parallel to the floor.
Flex your right foot and reach forward, holding the foot or ankle with both hands.
Keep your standing leg straight and your spine long as you fold toward your extended leg.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

name: dolphin pose
description: Dolphin pose is a forearm-based inversion prep that targets the deltoids and upper back while stretching the hamstrings and lower back. It involves lifting the hips from a forearm plank position to form an inverted V-shape, building shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees, then lower your forearms to the mat with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Interlace your fingers or keep your hands flat on the mat.
Tuck your toes under and lift your hips toward the ceiling, straightening your legs.
Press your forearms firmly into the mat and draw your shoulder blades together on your back.
Hold the inverted V-shape for 20-30 seconds, then lower your knees back to the mat.

name: downward-facing dog (adho mukha svanasana)
description: Downward-facing dog is a foundational yoga posture that targets the latissimus dorsi and stretches the hamstrings, shoulders, and lower back. It involves forming an inverted V-shape by pressing the hands and feet into the floor with hips lifted, serving as both a strength-builder and restorative stretch.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees in a tabletop position with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
Tuck your toes under and exhale as you lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Straighten your arms and legs as much as possible, forming an inverted V-shape with your body.
Press your hands firmly into the mat, spread your fingers wide, and draw your shoulder blades down your back.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, pedaling your heels alternately to stretch your calves, then lower back to tabletop.

name: eka hasta vyagrasana
description: Eka hasta vyagrasana, or one-handed tiger pose, is a kneeling balance and backbend posture targeting the lower back and glutes. It involves extending one leg back while grasping the foot with the opposite hand, requiring core stability and hip flexibility.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees in a tabletop position.
Extend your right leg straight back and bend your knee, lifting your right foot toward the ceiling.
Reach your left hand back to grasp the right foot, creating a backbend.
Maintain balance on your right arm and left knee while opening your chest upward.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then release and repeat on the opposite side.

name: eka pada galavasana
description: Eka pada galavasana is an advanced arm balance yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and glutes of the crossed leg while requiring significant core and upper body strength. It involves hooking one shin on the upper arms and extending the opposite leg backward while balancing on the hands.
instructions: Begin standing and shift your weight to your left foot, bringing your right ankle to rest on your left thigh.
Bend your left knee and hinge forward, placing your hands on the mat.
Hook your right shin on the backs of your upper arms and shift your weight onto your hands.
Lean forward and lift your left foot off the floor, balancing entirely on your hands.
Hold for 10-20 seconds, then slowly lower and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hip flexors/balance/advanced/0793 - eka pada galavasana.mp4

name: extended side angle
description: Extended side angle is a standing yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and obliques while stretching the entire side body. It involves a wide-legged stance with one knee bent and the torso extending laterally, promoting hip opening and lateral body flexibility.
instructions: Stand with your feet wide apart and turn your right foot out 90 degrees, aligning your right heel with your left arch.
Bend your right knee to a 90-degree angle, keeping it directly over your ankle.
Lower your right forearm to rest on your right thigh or place your right hand on the mat beside your right foot.
Extend your left arm overhead with the palm facing down, forming a long diagonal line from left heel to left fingertips.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then press through your right heel to return to standing and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0794 - extended side angle.mp4

name: firefly pose (tittibhasana)
description: Firefly pose is an advanced arm balance that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors while demanding significant upper body strength. It involves balancing on the hands with the legs extended horizontally to the sides, requiring core engagement and tricep stability.
instructions: Squat with feet slightly wider than hip-width, then slide your upper arms behind your calves as low as possible.
Plant your palms flat on the floor slightly behind your heels.
Lean your weight forward onto your hands and begin to straighten your legs out to the sides.
Lift your hips to approximately arm height and extend both legs straight, parallel to the floor.
Hold the position for 10-20 seconds, engaging your core and keeping your gaze forward, then lower with control.

name: fish pose (matsyasana)
description: Fish pose is a supine backbend yoga posture that targets the lower back and opens the chest and throat. It involves arching the upper back and placing the crown of the head on the floor, promoting thoracic spine extension and chest flexibility.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and arms by your sides.
Slide your hands under your glutes with palms facing down.
Press your forearms and elbows into the mat and lift your chest toward the ceiling, arching your upper back.
Gently tilt your head back and lower the crown of your head lightly to the floor.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then lift your head, release your hands, and lower your back to the mat.

name: forearm plank
description: Forearm plank is a foundational isometric yoga and fitness posture that targets the abdominal muscles and core stabilizers. It involves holding a rigid body position supported by the forearms and toes, building endurance in the core, shoulders, and glutes.
instructions: Begin face down on your mat, then place your forearms on the floor with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Interlace your fingers or keep your hands flat and parallel.
Tuck your toes under and lift your body off the floor, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core by pulling your navel toward your spine and squeeze your glutes.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing steadily, then lower your knees to the mat.

name: frog pose (mandukasana)
description: Frog pose is a deep hip-opening yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and inner groin. It involves kneeling with the legs spread wide and hips lowering toward the floor, providing an intense stretch for the groin and inner thighs.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees in a tabletop position.
Slowly slide your knees apart as wide as comfortable while keeping your ankles in line with your knees.
Lower your forearms to the mat and let your hips sink toward the floor.
Keep your feet flexed with the inner edges of your feet and knees touching the mat.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply into your hips, then slowly walk your knees back together.

name: garland pose (malasana)
description: Garland pose is a deep squat yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and groin while stretching the lower back and ankles. It involves a wide-stance squat with hands in prayer position, promoting hip mobility and lower body flexibility.
instructions: Stand with your feet slightly wider than hip-width apart and turn your toes out to about 45 degrees.
Bend your knees and lower into a deep squat, bringing your hips toward the floor.
Bring your palms together at your chest in prayer position and press your elbows against the insides of your knees.
Keep your spine long and chest lifted, using your elbows to gently push your knees wider.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, then press through your feet to return to standing.

name: half-pigeon pose (ardha kapotasana)
description: Half-pigeon pose is a deep hip-opening yoga posture that primarily targets the glutes and piriformis of the front leg while stretching the hip flexors of the back leg. It involves folding the torso over a bent front leg to facilitate a deep external hip rotation stretch.
instructions: Begin in a downward-facing dog position, then bring your right knee forward and place it behind your right wrist.
Extend your left leg straight back, lowering your hips toward the mat.
Square your hips toward the front of your mat and flex your right foot to protect the knee.
Walk your hands forward and lower your torso over your right shin, resting your forehead on the mat.
