Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, then press back into downward dog and repeat on the other side.

name: hand-to-big-toe pose (utthita hasta padangusthasana)
description: Hand-to-big-toe pose is a standing balance posture that targets the hamstrings through an active standing stretch. It involves grasping the big toe and extending one leg forward while maintaining balance on the standing leg, improving hamstring flexibility and core stability.
instructions: Stand in mountain pose with your weight balanced on both feet.
Shift your weight to your left foot and bend your right knee, lifting it toward your chest.
Grasp your right big toe with your right index and middle fingers.
Slowly extend your right leg forward until it is as straight as comfortable.
Hold the balance for 20-30 seconds, then bend the knee and release, repeating on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hamstrings/balance/intermediate/0801 - hand-to-big-toe pose (utthita hasta padangusthasana).mp4

name: fold piriformis relief
description: Fold piriformis relief is a seated forward fold variation that targets the glutes and piriformis muscle to release deep hip tension. It combines a figure-four position with a forward fold, intensifying the external hip rotation stretch compared to the supine version.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and cross your right ankle over your left thigh just above the knee.
Flex your right foot to protect the knee joint.
Hinge forward from the hips, folding your torso over both legs until you feel a deep stretch in your right glute.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply into the hip, then slowly return to seated and repeat on the other side.

name: hanuman's pose
description: Hanuman's pose, or full splits, is an advanced yoga posture that deeply targets the hip flexors of the back leg and hamstrings of the front leg. It involves lowering into a full front-to-back split with hips squared, demanding exceptional hip and hamstring flexibility.
instructions: Begin in a low lunge with your right foot forward and left knee on the mat.
Start to slide your right foot forward and your left knee back simultaneously.
Lower your hips toward the floor as you work both legs toward a full split position.
Keep your hips square and your torso upright, using your hands on the floor for support.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply, then press back into the lunge to release.

name: janusirsasana (head to knee forward fold)
description: Janusirsasana is a seated forward fold yoga posture that targets the hamstrings of the extended leg. It involves bending one knee out to the side and folding over the straight leg, providing a deep stretch to the posterior chain and promoting spinal flexibility.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and place the sole of your right foot against your left inner thigh.
Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and hinge forward from the hips over your extended left leg.
Reach your hands toward your left foot, holding the ankle, shin, or foot.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, then slowly return to seated and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0804 - janusirsasana (head to knee forward fold).mp4

name: king pigeon pose (rajakapotasana)
description: King pigeon pose is an advanced yoga backbend that targets the lower back and opens the chest and hip flexors simultaneously. It involves a deep spinal extension combined with hip opening, requiring considerable flexibility in the spine, shoulders, and hip flexors.
instructions: Begin in pigeon pose with your right shin forward and left leg extended back.
Bend your left knee and reach your left hand back to grasp your left foot.
Raise your right arm overhead and reach it back to clasp your left foot as well.
Open your chest to the ceiling and gently deepen the backbend by pressing your foot toward your head.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then release the foot carefully and repeat on the other side.

name: knee to chest (apanasana)
description: Knee to chest pose is a gentle restorative yoga posture that targets the lower back and hip flexors through a supine compression stretch. It involves drawing both knees to the chest while lying on the back, releasing tension in the lumbar spine and glutes.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and arms by your sides.
Exhale and draw both knees toward your chest, wrapping your arms around your shins.
Gently hug your knees in and rock lightly side to side to massage the lower back.
Keep your head flat on the mat and your shoulders relaxed.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, then slowly release your legs back to the mat.

name: legs up the wall (viparita karani)
description: Legs up the wall is a restorative inverted yoga posture that gently stretches the hamstrings and lower back while promoting circulation and relaxation. It involves lying on the back with legs extended vertically against a wall, offering a passive and calming inversion.
instructions: Sit sideways next to a wall and swing your legs up as you lower your back to the floor.
Scoot your glutes as close to the wall as comfortable and extend your legs straight up the wall.
Rest your arms out to the sides with palms facing up.
Relax your entire body and allow your lower back to soften into the floor.
Hold the position for 5-10 minutes, breathing deeply, then bend your knees and roll to one side to come out.

name: lion's breath (simhasana)
description: Lion's breath is a pranayama and yoga posture targeting the muscles of the face, neck, and throat. It involves a forceful exhalation with tongue extended and eyes wide, releasing tension in the face and neck while stimulating the throat region.
instructions: Sit in a comfortable kneeling or cross-legged position with your hands on your knees.
Inhale deeply through your nose, spreading your fingers wide and sitting tall.
Open your mouth wide and exhale forcefully, extending your tongue down toward your chin.
Simultaneously open your eyes wide and direct your gaze upward between your eyebrows.
Repeat the breath 3-5 times, then return to normal breathing.

name: locust pose (salabhasana)
description: Locust pose is a prone yoga posture that targets the lower back by lifting the chest and legs simultaneously off the floor. It strengthens the posterior chain including the glutes, hamstrings, and spinal extensors, and is a foundational backbend for building back body strength.
instructions: Lie face down on your mat with your legs extended and arms alongside your body, palms facing up.
Rest your forehead gently on the mat.
Inhale and simultaneously lift your head, chest, arms, and legs off the floor.
Keep your gaze forward and downward, draw your shoulder blades together, and squeeze your glutes.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then exhale and slowly lower everything back to the mat.

name: marichyasana
description: Marichyasana is a seated spinal twist yoga posture that targets the abdominal muscles and obliques while stretching the lower back and hamstrings. It involves twisting the torso with one knee bent, encouraging thoracic rotation and core activation.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and place your right foot flat on the floor close to your right hip.
Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and twist your torso to the right.
Bring your left elbow to the outside of your right knee and place your right hand on the floor behind you.
Hold the twist for 20-30 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

name: headstand flex (sirsasana)
description: Headstand flex is an advanced inverted yoga posture targeting the deltoids and requiring full-body engagement. It involves balancing on the crown of the head and forearms with legs extended vertically, building shoulder endurance, core strength, and spatial awareness.
instructions: Kneel on the mat and interlace your fingers, placing your forearms on the floor to form a triangular base with your hands.
Place the crown of your head on the mat, cradled by your interlaced hands.
Walk your feet in toward your head, lifting your hips until they stack over your shoulders.
Engage your core and slowly bring one knee, then the other, toward your chest before extending both legs fully overhead.
Hold the inverted position for 20-60 seconds with legs actively extended, then lower with control and rest in child's pose.

name: mountain pose (tadasana)
description: Mountain pose is the foundational standing yoga posture from which all other standing poses are built. It targets whole-body alignment and postural awareness, engaging the legs, core, and back to promote upright stability and grounding.
instructions: Stand with your feet together or hip-width apart and arms resting at your sides.
Distribute your weight evenly across both feet and all four corners of each foot.
Engage your thighs slightly, lift your kneecaps, and lengthen your tailbone toward the floor.
Lift your chest, roll your shoulders back and down, and extend the crown of your head toward the ceiling.
Hold the pose for 30-60 seconds, breathing steadily, focusing on full-body alignment and awareness.

name: piriformis relief
description: Piriformis relief is a supine yoga and rehabilitation posture that targets the glutes and piriformis muscle to alleviate tension and sciatic discomfort. It involves the figure-four position while drawing the legs toward the chest to create a deep external hip rotation stretch.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with both knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Cross your right ankle over your left thigh, flexing your right foot.
Lift your left foot off the floor and draw both legs toward your chest.
Interlace your hands behind your left thigh and gently pull your legs closer to your torso.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

name: piriformis
description: The piriformis stretch is a seated yoga and mobility posture targeting the piriformis muscle and deep external hip rotators. It involves crossing one knee over the extended leg and rotating the hip to generate a focused stretch in the outer glute region.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and cross your right foot over your left thigh, placing it flat on the floor.
Sit up tall and place your right hand on the floor behind your right hip.
Hug your right knee with your left arm and gently draw it toward your left shoulder.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, feeling the release in the right glute, then switch sides.

name: plough pose (halasana)
description: Plough pose is an inverted yoga posture that targets the lower back and stretches the hamstrings, shoulders, and entire posterior chain. It involves bringing the legs overhead until the toes touch the floor behind the head, creating deep spinal flexion and shoulder opening.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms alongside your body and palms pressing into the floor.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the floor, bringing your legs overhead.
Lower your toes toward the floor behind your head, keeping your legs straight.
Interlace your fingers on the mat beneath your back for additional support.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then slowly roll your spine back down to the mat vertebra by vertebra.

name: plank pose
description: Plank pose is a foundational yoga and fitness posture that isometrically targets the abdominal muscles and core stabilizers. It involves holding a push-up position with a straight body line, building strength and endurance in the core, shoulders, and glutes.
instructions: Begin on your hands and knees, then walk your feet back until your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Place your hands directly under your shoulders with fingers spread wide.
Engage your core by drawing your navel toward your spine and squeeze your glutes.
Keep your neck in a neutral position by gazing at the floor slightly in front of your hands.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing steadily, then lower your knees to the mat.

name: pyramid pose (parsvottanasana)
description: Pyramid pose is a standing forward fold yoga posture that deeply targets the hamstrings of the front leg. It involves a staggered stance with a deep forward hinge over the front leg, promoting posterior chain flexibility and hip alignment.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, then step your right foot back about 3-4 feet.
Square your hips toward the front of your mat and keep your right foot turned in at about 45 degrees.
Place your hands on your hips or bring your palms together behind your back.
Inhale to lengthen your spine, then exhale and hinge forward at the hips over your left leg.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, then inhale to lift your torso and repeat on the other side.

name: raised hands pose (urdhva hastasana)
description: Raised hands pose is a foundational standing yoga posture that targets the deltoids and stretches the side body and triceps. It involves standing tall with arms extended overhead, promoting shoulder mobility and upright postural alignment.
instructions: Stand in mountain pose with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Inhale and sweep both arms out to the sides and overhead, palms facing each other.
Reach your fingertips actively toward the ceiling and gently tilt your head back to gaze at your thumbs.
Keep your shoulders relaxed away from your ears and your core engaged.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then exhale and lower your arms back to your sides.

name: reverse plank
description: Reverse plank is a yoga posture that targets the abdominal muscles and posterior chain in an inverted tabletop position. It involves lifting the hips with hands behind the body, engaging the glutes, hamstrings, and shoulders while building full-body isometric strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands placed flat on the mat behind your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Press through your palms and lift your hips off the floor until your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Squeeze your glutes and engage your core to maintain the plank position.
Let your head tilt back slightly and keep your feet flexed or pointed.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then slowly lower your hips back to the mat.

name: reverse warrior (viprit virabhadrasana)
description: Reverse warrior is a standing yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and stretches the side body and obliques. It involves a warrior II base with a lateral backbend arc of the front arm, opening the chest and promoting lateral spinal flexibility.
instructions: Begin in warrior II with your right knee bent and arms extended parallel to the floor.
Flip your right palm to face the ceiling and inhale as you arc your right arm up and back.
Lower your left hand to rest lightly on your left thigh or shin.
Keep your right knee bent at 90 degrees and your hips open to the side.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then return to warrior II and switch sides.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hip flexors/stretching/beginner/0820 - reverse warrior (viprit virabhadrasana).mp4

name: revolved triangle (parivrtta trikonasana)
description: Revolved triangle is a standing twist yoga posture that targets the obliques and challenges hamstring flexibility. It involves rotating the torso over a straight front leg while reaching the opposite hand to the floor, promoting spinal rotation and lateral body strength.
instructions: Stand with your feet about 3-4 feet apart and extend your arms to the sides.
Turn your right foot out 90 degrees and your left foot in 45-60 degrees.
Exhale and rotate your torso to the right, placing your left hand on the floor outside your right foot or on a block.
Extend your right arm toward the ceiling and open your chest, stacking your shoulders vertically.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then return to standing and repeat on the other side.

name: king pigeon pose (rajakapotasana)
description: King pigeon pose is an advanced yoga backbend that targets the lower back while simultaneously opening the chest and deeply stretching the hip flexors and quadriceps. It combines a deep hip-opening base with a full spinal extension, requiring considerable flexibility throughout the front body.
instructions: Begin in pigeon pose with your right shin forward and left leg extended straight back on the mat.
Bend your left knee and reach your left hand back to grasp your left foot or ankle.
Raise your right arm overhead and reach it back to meet your left hand, clasping the foot with both hands.
Open your chest toward the ceiling and gently press your foot toward your head to deepen the backbend.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then carefully release the foot and repeat on the other side.

name: seated forward bend (paschimottanasana)
description: Seated forward bend is a foundational yoga posture that deeply targets the hamstrings and lower back. It involves folding the torso over extended legs from a seated position, promoting posterior chain flexibility and calming the nervous system.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended straight in front of you and spine tall.
Flex your feet and engage your quadriceps, pressing the backs of your legs into the mat.
Inhale and lengthen your spine, then exhale and hinge forward from the hips over your legs.
Reach your hands toward your feet, holding your ankles, shins, or feet.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, maintaining length in your spine, then inhale and slowly return to seated.

name: sheershasana (headstand)
description: Sheershasana, or headstand, is an advanced inverted yoga posture that targets the deltoids and upper body while requiring significant core strength and balance. It involves balancing the entire body on the head and forearms in a fully inverted position, building shoulder endurance and mental focus.
instructions: Kneel on the mat and place your forearms on the floor, interlacing your fingers to form a triangular base.
Place the crown of your head on the mat inside your interlaced hands.
Walk your feet in toward your head until your hips are over your shoulders.
Engage your core and slowly lift both feet off the floor, stacking your hips and legs vertically.
Hold the inverted position for 20-60 seconds, then lower your legs with control and rest in child's pose.

name: mermaid stretch
description: Mermaid stretch is a seated lateral yoga posture that targets the lower back and obliques while opening the hip flexors and chest. It involves a z-sit base with a side body arc, offering both a lateral spinal stretch and an optional shoulder and hip flexor opening.
instructions: Sit on the floor with both legs bent to the right side in a z-sit position, right shin in front and left shin behind.
Place your right hand on the floor beside your right hip for support.
Inhale and raise your left arm overhead, then exhale and arc it to the right, creating a lateral bend.
For a deeper variation, bend your left elbow and reach your left hand back to clasp your right foot.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, feeling the opening along the left side body, then release and repeat on the other side.

name: single leg lifted balance
description: Single leg lifted balance is a standing yoga posture that targets the quadriceps of the standing leg while challenging overall balance and stability. It involves lifting one knee to hip height and holding the position, engaging the core and hip flexors.
instructions: Stand tall in mountain pose with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Shift your weight onto your right foot and engage your core.
Slowly lift your left knee toward your hip level, keeping your lifted thigh parallel to the floor.
Extend your arms out to the sides or hold them at your hips for balance.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then lower your foot and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/lower legs/quads/balance/beginner/0826 - single leg lifted balance.mp4

name: sphinx pose (bhujangasana)
description: Sphinx pose is a gentle prone backbend yoga posture that targets the lower back through passive extension. It involves lying face down and propping the torso up on the forearms, encouraging lumbar extension while remaining gentle on the spine.
instructions: Lie face down on your mat with your legs extended and tops of the feet pressing into the floor.
Place your forearms flat on the mat with elbows directly under your shoulders.
Inhale and gently press your forearms into the mat to lift your chest and upper belly off the floor.
Keep your pelvis and lower abdomen resting on the mat and your gaze forward.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, breathing deeply, then lower your chest back to the mat.

name: standing forward fold (uttanasana)
description: Standing forward fold is a foundational yoga posture that targets the hamstrings and lower back through a gravity-assisted stretch. It involves hinging forward from standing with a long spine, allowing the torso to hang toward the floor to release tension in the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
Inhale to lengthen your spine, then exhale and hinge forward at the hips, folding your torso toward your legs.
Allow your head and neck to relax and let gravity draw your torso toward the floor.
Reach your hands to the floor, your shins, or your ankles depending on your flexibility.
Hold the position for 30-60 seconds, then press your feet into the floor and slowly roll up to standing.

name: standing split (urdhva prasarita eka padasana)
description: Standing split is an advanced standing balance yoga posture that targets the hamstrings through an active and gravity-assisted vertical stretch. It involves standing on one leg and lifting the other leg as high as possible while folding the torso toward the standing leg.
instructions: Begin in a standing forward fold with both hands on the floor.
Shift your weight onto your left foot and begin to lift your right leg straight up behind you.
Keep your hips as square as possible and raise your right leg as high as your flexibility allows.
Walk your hands toward your left foot or grip the ankle for a deeper stretch.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then lower your right leg and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hamstrings/balance/advanced/0829 - standing split (urdhva prasarita eka padasana).mp4

name: supine spinal stretch
description: Supine spinal stretch is a restorative yoga posture that targets the lower back through a lying spinal rotation. It involves crossing one knee to the opposite side while keeping the shoulders grounded, releasing tension in the lumbar spine, glutes, and obliques.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended out to the sides in a T-shape.
Bend your right knee and draw it toward your chest.
Exhale and guide your right knee across your body to the left side, lowering it toward the floor.
Turn your gaze to the right and keep both shoulders flat on the mat.
Hold the twist for 30-60 seconds, then return your knee to center and repeat on the other side.

name: supported glute bridge
description: Supported glute bridge is a yoga and rehabilitation posture that targets the glutes and hamstrings in a supine hip-lifting position. It involves pressing the feet into the floor to raise the hips while maintaining core engagement, building posterior chain strength and stability.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms flat by your sides with palms pressing into the mat.
Engage your core and press through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold the position, keeping your knees aligned with your feet.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then slowly lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.

name: thunderbolt pose (vajrasana)
description: Thunderbolt pose is a kneeling yoga posture that targets the quadriceps and provides a gentle stretch to the ankles and lower legs. It is commonly used for pranayama, meditation, and post-meal relaxation, promoting upright spinal alignment and mindful stillness.
instructions: Kneel on the mat with your knees together and sit back so your glutes rest on your heels.
Place your hands on your thighs with palms facing down.
Sit tall, lengthen your spine, and relax your shoulders away from your ears.
Keep your gaze forward or gently close your eyes and breathe naturally.
Hold the position for 1-5 minutes, then place your hands on the floor beside you and gently extend your legs to release.

name: tree pose (vrikshasana)
description: Tree pose is a foundational standing balance yoga posture that targets the quadriceps and engages the glutes and core. It involves balancing on one leg with the opposite foot placed on the inner thigh or calf, cultivating focus, stability, and lower body strength.
instructions: Stand in mountain pose with your weight evenly on both feet.
Shift your weight to your left foot and bend your right knee, lifting your right foot off the floor.
Place the sole of your right foot against your left inner calf or thigh, avoiding the knee joint.
Bring your palms together at your chest or extend your arms overhead.
Hold the balance for 30-60 seconds, gazing at a fixed point, then lower your foot and repeat on the other side.

name: triangle pose (trikonasana)
description: Triangle pose is a standing yoga posture that targets the hip flexors and inner thighs while stretching the hamstrings and obliques. It involves a wide-legged stance with a lateral bend, creating length through the entire side body and promoting hip opening.
instructions: Stand with your feet wide apart and turn your right foot out 90 degrees, your left foot in slightly.
Extend your arms out to the sides parallel to the floor.
Inhale to lengthen your spine, then exhale and extend your torso to the right, lowering your right hand to your shin, ankle, or the floor.
Extend your left arm toward the ceiling and open your chest, stacking your shoulders.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then inhale to come back up and repeat on the other side.

name: trivikaramasana
description: Trivikaramasana is an advanced standing split variation that deeply targets the hamstrings and hip flexors. It involves standing on one leg and raising the other leg alongside the torso toward a vertical position while grasping the lifted foot, demanding extreme hamstring and hip flexibility.
instructions: Begin standing in mountain pose and shift your weight to your left foot.
Lift your right leg and grasp your right foot or ankle with your right hand.
Slowly raise your right leg upward alongside your torso, extending it as high as possible.
Keep your standing leg straight and hold your right foot above your head if flexibility allows.
Hold for 10-20 seconds, then lower the leg with control and repeat on the other side.

name: utthita janu sirsasana (extended head to knee)
description: Extended head to knee pose is a standing yoga posture that targets the hamstrings through an active lateral leg extension. It involves balancing on one leg while extending the other to the side and reaching toward the foot, building hamstring flexibility and lateral core stability.
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and shift your weight to your right foot.
Lift your left knee and extend your left leg out to the side at hip height.
Hold your left foot with your left hand and straighten your left leg as much as possible.
Extend your right arm overhead and laterally bend toward your raised leg.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, then lower the leg and repeat on the other side.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hamstrings/balance/advanced/0836 - utthita janu sirsasana (extended head to knee).mp4

name: warrior i (virabhadrasana i)
description: Warrior I is a foundational standing yoga posture that targets the quadriceps and hip flexors. It involves a lunge-based stance with arms raised overhead, combining lower body strengthening with an opening of the chest and hip flexors.
instructions: Stand in mountain pose, then step your right foot back about 4 feet, turning it out to about 45 degrees.
Bend your left knee to 90 degrees, keeping it directly over your ankle.
Square your hips toward the front of your mat as much as possible.
Inhale and raise both arms overhead, palms facing each other, and lift your chest.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then step forward and repeat on the other side.

name: warrior ii (virabhadrasana ii)
description: Warrior II is a foundational standing yoga posture that targets the quadriceps and hip flexors while opening the hips and chest. It involves a wide-legged stance with the front knee bent and arms extended to the sides, building lower body strength and endurance.
instructions: Stand with your feet wide apart and turn your right foot out 90 degrees, left foot in slightly.
Bend your right knee to 90 degrees, keeping it aligned over your right ankle.
Extend your arms out to the sides parallel to the floor, palms facing down.
Gaze out over your right hand and keep your torso upright over your hips.
Hold the pose for 20-30 seconds, then straighten your right leg and repeat on the other side.

name: wide-legged boat pose (upavistha konasana balance)
description: Wide-legged boat pose is a yoga balance posture that targets the abdominal muscles and hip flexors. It involves balancing on the sit bones with legs extended wide in a V-shape, intensifying the core challenge of standard boat pose through the wider leg position.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the mat.
Lean back slightly and lift both feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
Open your legs wide apart, extending them toward a V-shape while keeping them as straight as possible.
Extend your arms forward between your legs, parallel to the floor.
Hold the position for 20-30 seconds, engaging your core, then slowly bring your legs together and lower your feet.
video_path: Yoga/waist/abs/balance/intermediate/0839 - wide-legged boat pose (upavistha konasana balance).mp4

name: wide-legged forward bend (prasarita padottanasana)
description: Wide-legged forward bend is a standing yoga posture that targets the hamstrings and inner thighs through a wide-stance forward fold. It involves hinging forward between widely spaced legs with the torso hanging toward the floor, promoting deep posterior chain flexibility and shoulder opening.
instructions: Stand with your feet wide apart, about 4-5 feet, with toes pointed slightly inward.
Place your hands on your hips and inhale to lengthen your spine.
Exhale and hinge forward from the hips, lowering your torso between your legs.
Place your hands flat on the floor shoulder-width apart and allow the crown of your head to reach toward the mat.
Hold the stretch for 30-60 seconds, then place your hands on your hips and inhale to rise back up.
video_path: Yoga/upper legs/hamstrings/stretching/beginner/0840 - wide-legged forward bend (prasarita padottanasana).mp4

name: scorpion pose (vrschikasana)
description: Scorpion pose is an advanced inverted backbend yoga posture that intensely targets the lower back and spinal extensors. It involves balancing on the forearms in an inverted position while deeply arching the spine to bring the feet toward the head, requiring exceptional spinal flexibility and shoulder strength.
instructions: Begin in dolphin pose with your forearms flat on the mat and hips lifted high.
Walk your feet in until your hips are stacked over your shoulders in a forearm headstand.
Once balanced, slowly bend both knees and lower your feet toward the back of your head.
Arch your lower back and lift your chest to create a deep spinal curve as your feet descend.
Hold the pose for 10-20 seconds, then carefully lower your feet back to the mat and rest in child's pose.

name: side plank (vasisthasana)
description: Side plank is a yoga posture that targets the obliques and lateral core stabilizers. It involves balancing on one hand and the outer edge of one foot with the body forming a straight diagonal line, building lateral core strength and shoulder stability.
instructions: Begin in a standard plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders.
Shift your weight onto your right hand and rotate your entire body to the right, stacking your left foot on top of your right.
Extend your left arm straight toward the ceiling, keeping your body in a straight diagonal line from head to heels.
Engage your obliques and lift your hips to prevent them from sagging toward the floor.
Hold for 20-30 seconds, then return to plank and repeat on the other side.

name: ab roll workout
description: The ab roll workout is a barbell or ab wheel exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, with secondary emphasis on the obliques, lower back, and shoulders. It involves rolling forward from a kneeling position to challenge core stability and strength.
instructions: Kneel on the floor and grip an ab wheel or barbell with both hands, shoulder-width apart.
Position the wheel directly beneath your shoulders with your arms fully extended.
Slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body towards the ground while keeping your core tight.
Pause when your body is nearly parallel to the floor, then engage your abs to roll back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable crunch (kneeling)
description: The cable crunch (kneeling) is a cable machine exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. Performed kneeling in front of a high pulley, it uses constant cable tension to challenge the abs through a controlled crunching motion.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to the high pulley of a cable machine and kneel facing the machine.
Grasp the rope with both hands and bring it to the sides of your head, keeping your elbows bent.
Engage your core and crunch your torso downward, bringing your elbows towards your knees.
Pause at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: captain's chair leg raises
description: Captain's chair leg raises are a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, with secondary emphasis on the hip flexors and obliques. Performed on a captain's chair, this exercise challenges core strength by lifting the legs against gravity.
instructions: Mount a captain's chair apparatus and grip the handles firmly with your arms bent at 90 degrees.
Allow your legs to hang straight down and engage your core.
Keeping your legs together, raise them upward until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions without swinging your body.

name: hanging leg raise
description: The hanging leg raise is a bodyweight exercise targeting the abdominal muscles. Performed while hanging from a bar, it engages the full core and hip flexors to lift the legs against gravity, building significant abdominal strength.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms fully extended and feet off the floor.
Engage your core and keep your body stable to minimize swinging.
Raise your legs upward by flexing at the hips, keeping your legs straight or slightly bent.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the floor or higher if flexibility allows.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: kettlebell sit-ups to press
description: Kettlebell sit-ups to press is a compound exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, combining a full sit-up with an overhead press. This movement engages the core, hip flexors, and shoulders, providing both strength and conditioning benefits.
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a kettlebell at chest level with both hands.
Engage your core and perform a full sit-up, rising to an upright seated position.
At the top of the sit-up, press the kettlebell overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the kettlebell back to your chest and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: plank hold weighted
description: The weighted plank hold is a bodyweight-based core exercise targeting the abdominal muscles, with secondary engagement of the lower back, shoulders, and glutes. Adding weight increases the demand on the core, making it an advanced progression of the standard plank.
instructions: Place a weight plate on your upper back and assume a forearm plank position with your elbows directly beneath your shoulders.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, engaging your core and glutes.
Maintain a neutral spine and avoid letting your hips sag or rise.
Hold the position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Carefully remove the weight and rest before repeating.

name: russian twist weighted
description: The weighted russian twist is a core exercise targeting the abdominal muscles and obliques. Performed in a seated position with the feet raised, this movement involves rotating the torso side to side with added resistance to build rotational strength.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly elevated, holding a weight plate or dumbbell with both hands.
Lean back slightly to create tension in your core, keeping your spine straight.
Rotate your torso to the right, bringing the weight towards the floor beside your hip.
Return to center and rotate to the left, repeating the movement on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

name: band pushups
description: Band push-ups are a resistance band exercise targeting the triceps, with secondary emphasis on the chest and shoulders. The band adds progressive resistance at the top of the push-up, increasing the challenge on the pressing muscles.
instructions: Loop a resistance band across your upper back and hold each end in your hands, placing your palms flat on the floor.
Position yourself in a standard push-up stance with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest towards the floor by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position, working against the band resistance.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell curl standard
description: The standard barbell curl is a fundamental upper arm exercise targeting the biceps. Performed standing with a barbell, it isolates the biceps through a full range of motion to build arm strength and muscle mass.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Curl the barbell upward by contracting your biceps until the bar is at shoulder level.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0009 - barbell curl standard.mp4

name: barbell hold
description: The barbell hold is a grip strength exercise targeting the forearms. Performed by holding a loaded barbell at the sides for time, it develops crushing grip strength and forearm endurance essential for many compound movements.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a loaded barbell at your sides with an overhand grip.
Allow your arms to hang straight down, fully extending your fingers around the bar.
Squeeze the bar as hard as possible and hold for the desired duration.
Focus on maintaining grip strength without bending your arms.
Set the bar down in a controlled manner and rest before repeating.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/lower arms/lower arms/strength/beginner/0010 - barbell hold.mp4

name: cable curl (straight bar)
description: The cable curl with a straight bar is a cable machine exercise targeting the biceps. The constant cable tension throughout the range of motion makes it effective for building bicep strength and muscle definition.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley of a cable machine and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows at your sides.
Curl the bar upward by contracting your biceps until your forearms are parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0011 - cable curl (straight bar).mp4

name: cable wood chop
description: The cable wood chop is a cable machine exercise targeting the obliques and core. It simulates a chopping motion across the body, developing rotational strength and power in the core and shoulders.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the high pulley of a cable machine and stand sideways to the machine.
Grip the handle with both hands and position your feet shoulder-width apart.
In one sweeping motion, pull the handle diagonally downward across your body towards the opposite hip, rotating your torso.
Control the movement as you return the handle to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/obliques/strength/intermediate/0012 - cable wood chop.mp4

name: close-grip bench press barbell
description: The close-grip bench press is a barbell exercise targeting the triceps. By using a narrower grip than a standard bench press, it shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps, making it a key exercise for building arm pressing strength.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grip a barbell with your hands approximately shoulder-width apart or slightly closer.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the bar to your lower chest by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Press the bar back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0013 - close-grip bench press barbell.mp4

name: concentration curl (seated)
description: The seated concentration curl is a dumbbell exercise targeting the biceps. By bracing the upper arm against the thigh, it isolates the bicep and limits momentum, making it highly effective for building peak bicep strength and definition.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a dumbbell in one hand.
Rest the back of your upper arm against the inside of your thigh for support.
Curl the dumbbell upward towards your shoulder by contracting your bicep.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your bicep.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat, then switch arms.

name: dumbbell curl
description: The dumbbell curl is a fundamental upper arm exercise targeting the biceps. Using dumbbells allows for independent arm movement and a natural wrist rotation, promoting balanced bicep development and strength.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms hanging by your sides and palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms stationary and close to your torso.
Curl the dumbbells upward by contracting your biceps until they reach shoulder level.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell hammer curl
description: The dumbbell hammer curl is an upper arm exercise targeting the biceps with a neutral grip. This grip variation places greater emphasis on the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles, contributing to overall arm thickness and strength.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Curl the dumbbells upward while maintaining the neutral grip, bringing them to shoulder level.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps and forearms.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0016 - dumbbell hammer curl.mp4

name: dumbbell overhead press version 1
description: The dumbbell overhead press version 1 is a seated shoulder exercise targeting the deltoids. Pressing dumbbells overhead from shoulder height builds shoulder strength and size, with secondary activation of the triceps and traps.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Keep your feet flat on the floor and your back pressed against the pad.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell spider curl
description: The dumbbell spider curl is an upper arm exercise targeting the biceps. Performed face-down on an incline bench, this variation eliminates body movement and keeps constant tension on the biceps throughout the entire range of motion.
instructions: Set an incline bench to approximately 45 degrees and lie face down with your chest against the pad.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms hanging straight down and palms facing forward.
Curl the dumbbells upward by contracting your biceps, keeping your upper arms perpendicular to the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0018 - dumbbell spider curl.mp4

name: incline dumbbell curl
description: The incline dumbbell curl is an upper arm exercise targeting the biceps. The inclined position places the biceps in a stretched position at the bottom, increasing the range of motion and placing greater tension on the long head of the bicep.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench set to approximately 60 degrees and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Allow your arms to hang straight down behind the plane of your body, fully stretching the biceps.
Curl the dumbbells upward by contracting your biceps while keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0019 - incline dumbbell curl.mp4

name: landmine press (single arm)
description: The single arm landmine press is a barbell exercise targeting the shoulders, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and chest. The angled pressing path of the landmine reduces shoulder joint stress while building unilateral pressing strength.
instructions: Secure one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment or wedge it into a corner.
Stand facing the barbell and grasp the free end with one hand at shoulder height.
Stagger your stance for balance and keep your core braced.
Press the barbell upward and forward in an arcing motion until your arm is fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position and repeat, then switch arms.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/shoulders/strength/intermediate/0020 - landmine press (single arm).mp4

name: overhead triceps extension (dumbbell)
description: The overhead dumbbell triceps extension is an upper arm exercise targeting the triceps. Performed with a single dumbbell held overhead, it stretches the long head of the tricep for maximum engagement and builds arm extension strength.
instructions: Stand or sit upright and hold a single dumbbell with both hands, gripping the top plate.
Raise the dumbbell overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head and elbows pointing forward, lower the dumbbell behind your head.
Pause at the bottom, then extend your arms back to the starting position by contracting your triceps.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0021 - overhead triceps extension (dumbbell).mp4

name: preacher curl ez bar
description: The preacher curl with an ez bar is a bicep isolation exercise performed on a preacher bench. The angled pad locks the upper arms in place, eliminating momentum and placing maximum tension on the biceps for focused growth.
instructions: Sit at a preacher curl bench and place the back of your upper arms flat against the angled pad.
Grip an ez curl bar with an underhand grip using the inner angled handles.
Starting with your arms nearly fully extended, curl the bar upward by contracting your biceps.
Pause at the top of the movement and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar back to the near-extended starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/intermediate/0022 - preacher curl ez bar.mp4

name: single arm pull rope
description: The single arm pull rope is a cable exercise targeting the biceps. Using a single handle attachment allows for unilateral training, helping to correct strength imbalances and improve bicep peak development with constant cable tension.
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley of a cable machine and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand using an underhand grip and step back slightly.
Keeping your elbow close to your side and your upper arm stationary, curl the handle upward towards your shoulder.
Pause at the top and squeeze your bicep, then slowly lower back to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching arms.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/biceps/strength/beginner/0023 - single arm pull rope.mp4

name: skull crushers (ez bar)
description: Skull crushers with an ez bar are a barbell exercise targeting the triceps. Performed lying on a bench, the movement isolates the triceps by flexing and extending the elbow, building size and strength in the back of the upper arm.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and hold an ez curl bar with an overhand grip, arms extended directly above your chest.
Keeping your upper arms vertical and elbows pointing toward the ceiling, slowly lower the bar toward your forehead.
Pause just above your forehead, then extend your arms back to the starting position by contracting your triceps.
Keep your elbows tucked and avoid flaring them outward throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0024 - skull crushers (ez bar).mp4

name: triceps pushdown (cable - straight bar)
description: The cable triceps pushdown with a straight bar is a cable machine exercise targeting the triceps. The straight bar allows both arms to work simultaneously, providing constant tension throughout the range of motion for effective tricep development.
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and tuck your elbows close to your sides.
Push the bar downward by extending your arms until they are fully straight.
Pause at the bottom and squeeze your triceps.
Slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/beginner/0025 - triceps pushdown (cable - straight bar).mp4

name: weighted push up
description: The weighted push-up is a bodyweight exercise targeting the triceps and chest, with added resistance from a weight plate. This progression of the standard push-up increases demand on the pressing muscles, promoting strength and muscle development.
instructions: Place a weight plate on your upper back and assume a standard push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels and engage your core.
Lower your chest towards the floor by bending your elbows, keeping them at approximately 45 degrees from your body.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/triceps/strength/intermediate/0026 - weighted push up.mp4

name: wide dumbbell push up
description: The wide dumbbell push-up is an exercise targeting the chest, with secondary emphasis on the triceps and shoulders. Gripping dumbbells in a wide position increases the range of motion and recruits the outer chest fibers more effectively than a standard push-up.
instructions: Place two dumbbells on the floor wider than shoulder-width apart and grip each handle.
Assume a push-up position with your arms fully extended, hands gripping the dumbbells.
Lower your chest towards the floor between the dumbbells by bending your elbows.
Push through the dumbbells to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a straight body line throughout.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper arms/chest/strength/intermediate/0027 - wide dumbbell push up.mp4

name: barbell bent over row
description: The barbell bent over row is a compound back exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Performed in a hinged position, it builds thickness in the upper and middle back while also developing grip and lower back endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hinge at the hips to lean your torso forward to approximately 45 degrees, keeping your back flat.
Let the barbell hang with your arms extended, then row it upward towards your lower chest.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell shrug
description: The barbell shrug is a resistance exercise targeting the trapezius muscles. By elevating the shoulders with a loaded barbell, it builds upper trap strength and thickness, which is important for overall neck and upper back development.
instructions: Stand upright with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Allow your arms to hang straight down, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Elevate your shoulders as high as possible towards your ears in a shrugging motion.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze your traps.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable face pull
description: The cable face pull is a cable machine exercise targeting the trapezius and rear deltoids. It improves upper back strength and shoulder health by training the external rotators and retractors of the scapula, making it valuable for postural correction.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a cable machine set at approximately face height and stand facing the machine.
Grip the rope with both hands using an overhand grip and step back until your arms are extended.
Pull the rope towards your face by driving your elbows outward and back.
Separate the rope ends at your face level, bringing your hands to either side of your head.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: chin up leg folded
description: The chin-up with legs folded is a bodyweight exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. The underhand grip emphasizes bicep involvement, while folding the legs shifts the body's center of gravity and reduces the use of momentum.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Cross your ankles and bend your knees to bring your legs up behind you in a folded position.
Engage your core and pull your body upward until your chin clears the bar.
Pause at the top, squeezing your lats and biceps.
Slowly lower yourself back to the hanging position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: deadlift barbell
description: The barbell deadlift is a compound exercise targeting the lower back, with significant engagement from the glutes, hamstrings, and traps. It is one of the most fundamental strength movements for building overall posterior chain power.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart and a loaded barbell over your mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and bend your knees to grip the bar with hands just outside your legs.
Take a deep breath, brace your core, and keep your chest up with a flat back.
Drive through your heels to lift the barbell, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Stand tall at the top, then hinge at the hips and bend your knees to lower the bar back to the floor in a controlled manner.

name: deadlift sumo barbell
description: The sumo barbell deadlift is a compound exercise targeting the lower back and glutes. The wide stance reduces the range of motion and places greater emphasis on the hips and inner thighs compared to a conventional deadlift.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart with toes pointed outward, and a barbell over your mid-foot.
Grip the bar with hands inside your legs using an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core and keep your chest up, then push your knees out in line with your toes.
Drive through your heels to lift the bar, extending your hips and knees at the same rate.
Stand fully upright at the top, then lower the bar back to the floor in a controlled manner.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/back/lower back/strength/intermediate/0033 - deadlift sumo barbell.mp4

name: dumbbell shrug
description: The dumbbell shrug is a back exercise targeting the trapezius muscles. Holding dumbbells at the sides allows for a natural arm path and equal loading on both sides, making it effective for building upper trap strength and thickness.
instructions: Stand upright holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip.
Allow your arms to hang straight down with your shoulders relaxed.
Elevate your shoulders as high as possible towards your ears in a controlled shrugging motion.
Pause briefly at the top, squeezing your trapezius muscles.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: good morning barbell
description: The barbell good morning is a compound exercise targeting the lower back and hamstrings. By hinging at the hips under a barbell load, it strengthens the entire posterior chain and improves hip hinge mechanics essential for deadlifts and squats.
instructions: Place a barbell across your upper traps as if performing a back squat and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
With your knees slightly bent, hinge forward at the hips, pushing them back as you lower your torso.
Continue lowering until your torso is approximately parallel to the floor or until you feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings.
Pause at the bottom, then drive your hips forward to return to the upright starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/back/lower back/strength/intermediate/0035 - good morning barbell.mp4

name: hyperextensions (back extensions)
description: Hyperextensions, or back extensions, are a bodyweight exercise targeting the lower back. Performed on a hyperextension bench, this movement isolates and strengthens the erector spinae muscles, improving spinal stability and posterior chain endurance.
instructions: Position yourself face down on a hyperextension bench with your hips at the edge of the pad and feet secured.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Lower your torso toward the floor in a controlled manner, bending at the waist.
Raise your torso back up by contracting your lower back and glutes until your body forms a straight line.
Pause at the top and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: lat pull down wide grip
description: The wide-grip lat pulldown is a cable machine exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. The wider grip increases lat stretch at the top and places greater emphasis on the outer back, helping to build a wider back appearance.
instructions: Sit at a lat pulldown machine and grip the bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder-width apart.
Adjust the knee pad to secure your legs and lean back slightly.
Pull the bar downward towards your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/beginner/0037 - lat pull down wide grip.mp4

name: pull up
description: The pull-up is a foundational bodyweight exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Using an overhand grip, it builds upper body pulling strength across the back, biceps, and shoulders, and is a key measure of relative body strength.
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and avoid swinging your body.
Pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down and back until your chin clears the bar.
Pause at the top and squeeze your lats.
Slowly lower yourself back to the full hanging position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: rear delt fly with dumbbell
description: The rear delt dumbbell fly is an exercise targeting the trapezius and rhomboids, with emphasis on the posterior deltoid. Performed bent over, it trains the muscles responsible for scapular retraction and rear shoulder development.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench and hinge forward at the hips with your chest near your thighs.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip, allowing them to hang below your knees.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arcing motion, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause at the top when your arms are parallel to the floor.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/back/traps/strength/beginner/0039 - rear delt fly with dumbbell.mp4

name: seated chest supported machine
description: The seated chest-supported row machine is a machine exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. The chest pad eliminates lower back involvement, allowing full focus on the back muscles and making it an effective isolation option for upper back development.
instructions: Sit facing a chest-supported row machine and press your chest firmly against the pad.
Grip the handles with both hands using a neutral or overhand grip.
Pull the handles back towards your body by retracting your shoulder blades and driving your elbows back.
Pause at the peak of the contraction, squeezing your lats and rhomboids.
Slowly release the handles back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/back/lats/strength/beginner/0040 - seated chest supported machine.mp4

name: t-bar row
description: The t-bar row is a compound back exercise targeting the latissimus dorsi. Straddling the bar provides a stable base for heavy rowing, making it one of the most effective exercises for building mid-back thickness and strength.
instructions: Load one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment and straddle the bar.
Grip the bar end with both hands using a neutral grip handle or cupping the plates.
Hinge at the hips to lean your torso forward at approximately 45 degrees, keeping your back flat.
Row the bar upward towards your chest by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell bench press flat
description: The barbell flat bench press is the foundational compound chest exercise. Pressing a barbell from a flat bench activates the entire pectoral muscle, along with the triceps and front deltoids, making it a cornerstone of upper body strength development.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench and grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the bar to your mid-chest by bending your elbows at approximately 45 degrees.
Press the bar back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell incline bench press
description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound chest exercise targeting the upper pectoral muscles. The inclined angle shifts emphasis to the upper chest and front deltoids, making it a key movement for building a full and well-developed chest.
instructions: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees and lie back, gripping the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and hold it above your upper chest with arms extended.
Lower the bar to your upper chest in a controlled manner.
Press the bar back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: cable crossover (high to low)
description: The cable crossover from high to low is a cable exercise targeting the lower and inner chest. The downward arc emphasizes the lower pectoral fibers and provides constant tension, making it effective for chest shaping and definition.
instructions: Set both pulleys on a cable machine to the high position and attach single handles to each.
Stand in the center of the machine and grasp a handle in each hand.
Step forward slightly with one foot for stability and lean forward slightly at the hips.
With a slight bend in your elbows, pull the handles downward and inward in an arcing motion until they meet in front of your hips.
Slowly return the handles to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dips (chest focus - leaning forward)
description: Dips with a forward lean are a bodyweight exercise targeting the chest. By leaning the torso forward and allowing the elbows to flare, greater emphasis is placed on the pectoral muscles rather than the triceps, making it a highly effective chest builder.
instructions: Grip the parallel bars of a dip station and lift yourself up to the starting position with arms extended.
Lean your torso forward at approximately 30-45 degrees and allow your elbows to flare slightly outward.
Lower yourself by bending your elbows until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell bench press (bench)
description: The dumbbell bench press is a compound chest exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. Using dumbbells allows for a greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared to a barbell, promoting balanced chest development.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells back to chest level in a controlled arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell bench press (decline)
description: The decline dumbbell bench press is a chest exercise targeting the lower pectoral muscles. The declined angle shifts emphasis to the lower chest fibers while reducing shoulder involvement, helping to build a complete and defined chest.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured at the top and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Begin with the dumbbells at chest level, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell bench press incline
description: The incline dumbbell bench press is a compound exercise targeting the upper chest. The inclined angle emphasizes the upper pectoral fibers, and the use of dumbbells allows for a natural pressing path and greater range of motion.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench set to 30-45 degrees and hold a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to chest level in a controlled arc.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then press back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell flys flat
description: Dumbbell flat flys are an isolation chest exercise targeting the pectoral muscles. The wide arcing motion stretches and contracts the chest through a large range of motion, making it effective for developing chest width and muscle definition.
instructions: Lie flat on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended directly above your chest.
With a slight bend in your elbows, lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc.
Continue lowering until you feel a deep stretch across your chest.
Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up in the same arcing path until they meet above your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: dumbbell flys incline
description: Incline dumbbell flys are an isolation exercise targeting the upper chest. The inclined angle directs the stretch and contraction toward the upper pectoral fibers, complementing pressing exercises to build a fuller upper chest.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench set to 30-45 degrees holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest.
With a slight bend in your elbows, lower the dumbbells outward in a wide arc.
Continue lowering until you feel a deep stretch in your upper chest.
Bring the dumbbells back up in the same arcing motion to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: pec deck machine fly
description: The pec deck machine fly is a machine exercise targeting the chest. The guided range of motion isolates the pectoral muscles and maintains constant tension throughout, making it effective for chest development with reduced risk of shoulder strain.
instructions: Sit on the pec deck machine and adjust the seat so your arms are parallel to the floor.
Place your forearms against the arm pads or grip the handles with elbows bent at 90 degrees.
Press the arm pads together in front of your chest by contracting your pectoral muscles.
Pause at the peak of the contraction and squeeze your chest.
Slowly allow the arm pads to return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: svend press chest
description: The svend press is a chest exercise using weight plates to create medial force on the pectoral muscles. By pressing the plates together while extending and retracting the arms, it effectively isolates the inner chest and improves mind-muscle connection.
instructions: Stand upright and hold a pair of weight plates pressed together at chest level with both palms.
Press the plates outward from your chest until your arms are nearly fully extended.
Squeeze the plates together throughout the entire movement to engage the inner chest.
Slowly pull the plates back towards your chest in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: air bike sprint
description: The air bike sprint is a high-intensity cardio exercise targeting the entire body. Using both the pedals and arm handles simultaneously, it elevates heart rate rapidly and burns a high number of calories, making it effective for conditioning and fat loss.
instructions: Sit on the air bike and adjust the seat so your legs are slightly bent at full extension.
Grip the moving handles with both hands.
Begin pedaling and pumping the handles simultaneously at maximum effort.
Sprint at full intensity for the desired interval duration.
Slow down gradually and rest before repeating.

name: barbell back squat
description: The barbell back squat is the foundational lower body compound exercise targeting the quadriceps. With a barbell on the upper back, it trains the entire leg and builds functional strength, muscle mass, and athletic power.
instructions: Position a barbell on your upper traps and step back from the rack with feet shoulder-width apart.
Point your toes slightly outward and keep your chest up and core braced.
Descend by bending your knees and pushing your hips back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to extend your knees and hips and return to the standing position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0054 - barbell back squat.mp4

name: barbell bulgarian split squat
description: The barbell Bulgarian split squat is a unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps. The elevated rear leg increases the range of motion and demands greater balance, making it an excellent movement for building leg strength and correcting imbalances.
instructions: Stand in front of a bench and hold a barbell across your upper traps.
Extend one leg behind you and rest the top of your foot on the bench.
Lower your back knee toward the floor by bending your front knee, keeping your torso upright.
Descend until your front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position and repeat, then switch legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/0055 - barbell bulgarian split squat.mp4

name: barbell front squat
description: The barbell front squat is a compound lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps. Holding the bar in a front rack position shifts the torso more upright, placing greater emphasis on the quads and core compared to the back squat.
instructions: Position a barbell in a front rack across the front of your shoulders with your elbows raised high.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly pointed out.
Keeping your elbows high and torso upright, descend by bending your knees and hips.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or below.
Drive through your heels to return to the standing position and repeat.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/advanced/0056 - barbell front squat.mp4

name: barbell hip thrust
description: The barbell hip thrust is a compound exercise targeting the gluteal muscles. Performed with the upper back elevated on a bench, it allows for a full range of hip extension under heavy load, making it the most effective exercise for glute development.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and a padded barbell across your hips.
Plant your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart with knees bent.
Drive through your heels to thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Lower your hips back toward the floor in a controlled manner and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/intermediate/0057 - barbell hip thrust.mp4

name: barbell march
description: The barbell march is a loaded unilateral exercise targeting the quadriceps and core. Marching with a barbell on the back challenges single-leg stability, hip flexor strength, and core bracing under load.
instructions: Hold a barbell across your upper traps as in a back squat position with feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core and maintain an upright posture.
Lift one knee upward to hip height in a marching motion while keeping your stance foot flat.
Lower your foot and repeat the movement with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating legs in a controlled marching motion for the desired number of repetitions.

name: barbell reverse lunges
description: The barbell reverse lunge is a compound lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps. Stepping backward reduces shear force on the knee compared to a forward lunge, making it effective for building leg strength and stability.
instructions: Stand upright with a barbell across your upper traps and feet together.
Step one foot directly backward and lower your back knee toward the floor.
Continue descending until your front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Push through your front heel to bring your back foot forward to the starting position.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/quads/strength/intermediate/0059 - barbell reverse lunges.mp4

name: barbell romanian deadlift
description: The barbell Romanian deadlift is a hip hinge exercise targeting the hamstrings and glutes. By maintaining a soft knee bend and hinging at the hips, it stretches and loads the posterior chain through a long range of motion.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Keep a soft bend in your knees and your back flat throughout the movement.
Hinge at the hips and push them back as you lower the bar along your legs.
Continue lowering until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to return to the upright position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/upper legs/strength/intermediate/0060 - barbell romanian deadlift.mp4

name: cable leg kickback
description: The cable leg kickback is an isolation exercise targeting the gluteal muscles. Using a cable machine provides constant resistance throughout the range of motion, making it effective for glute activation, shaping, and strength.
instructions: Attach an ankle strap to the low pulley of a cable machine and secure it to one ankle.
Face the machine and hold the frame for support, keeping your body slightly inclined.
Keeping your knee straight, kick your strapped leg backward and upward as far as possible.
Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement.
Slowly return your leg to the starting position and repeat, then switch legs.
video_path: 100 Gym Workouts/upper legs/glutes/strength/beginner/0061 - cable leg kickback.mp4

name: cycling
description: Cycling is a low-impact cardio exercise targeting the quadriceps. Whether performed on a stationary bike or outdoors, it improves cardiovascular endurance and lower body muscular endurance while being gentle on the joints.
instructions: Mount a stationary bike and adjust the seat height so your leg has a slight bend at full pedal extension.
Place your feet in the pedal straps and grip the handlebars.
Begin pedaling at a comfortable cadence, maintaining an upright or forward-leaning posture.
Adjust the resistance as needed to reach your target intensity level.
Continue for the desired duration, then gradually slow to a cool down pace.

name: dumbbell bulgarian split squat
description: The dumbbell Bulgarian split squat is a unilateral lower body exercise targeting the quadriceps. Holding dumbbells allows for a natural arm position while the elevated rear foot increases range of motion and unilateral leg strength.
